>
> A thousand thank yous Dr. Kruse.

[@Kari Dunlop](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25265/) thank you for sharing and i can see you dancing under the stars with immense health improvements.

I think many people who have found Jack were at their wits end with poor health from the many stories I have heard that will all empathise with how you feel at this moment.

I hope my sharing some of my journey below over the last twelve months helps you a little, it won't help you do what you need to, but i still hope it gives you courage and energy to get your journey on track and know you aren't alone.

Personally I know where you are coming from as I came across Jack about four years ago and knew what he was saying to be true but it was too hard and there were always reasons why I didn’t implement any of this work, or constantly told myself I would ‘come back to it when I had time’, wow, I never realised what those words would mean in years to come, however the universe works in mysterious ways and a health crisis resulted in our first consult with Jack twelve months ago, meaning I could no longer ignore any of his teachings.

His advice to my partner and myself were along similar lines to your rx – ‘go north and see how he goes in a better environment and when you can then 'get the hell out of Australia as soon as they open the borders'!

Very daunting prospect, we have a small business and commitment to employees who rely on us and we have a blended family of six adult children and five grandchildren so no easy task for Vince to leave his daughters and grandchildren, what about my children, my mum, i'm so tired how can i do all of this …………blah blah blah!

I also found the thought of taking a very unwell man half way around the world where, like you, we don't speak the language nor know anything about the culture and we would be alone to be a monster task I didn’t have the energy to undertake (not realising I had my own health challenges) yet the more I thought about it the more the idea seemed not so daunting. I was making it harder than it should have been. The gauntlet was thrown down, was I up to the challenge?

I thought about all the excuses I would bring up - it was difficult to leave family, friends and a life I believed was good me, a city I loved…..and the more the excuses kept coming, the more I analysed everything, the more I realised I was sabotaging myself & both our recoveries, so for the first time in 56 years (yay for 1966 babies) I decided to put myself first and Vince needed to do the same.

Jacks words again rattled around in my head ‘who are you good for if you’re not good enough for yourself!’

None of the family, friends, acquaintances, business, staff and everything else we used as excuses not to leave or change in the past would bring back our health. It was harder to stay here as we were than to make these changes and leave.

I realised I needed to take a leap of faith and believe in myself, and that I am exactly where I am meant to be. I took Jacks advice to do this for myself and my partner to allow him to have his journey as well.

I also realised its only as hard as I make it and the little voice inside my head always told me it would be too hard to do this, that people relied on me, that I couldn’t leave but that was fear speaking and through all of this I found my voice and courage within. I realised my thyroid cancer and now pituitary tumour were because of a lifetime of this sort of thinking, sabotage, stress and more that i had allowed to fester. It’s time to tackle this. I drew my line in the sand.

I decided to look at everything it in small chunks to lessen overwhelm and now am so excited by an adventure that we will undertake for the next 12-18 months. Take it in small periods of time and small tasks.

I started with decluttering the house, one room at a time, sometimes just one drawer or one cupboard was all i could manage. Sometimes i would go for a week and not be able to do anything and when i felt like it i achieved what i could. It was all moving in the right direction and i told myself that something was better than nothing and that it was all moving me forward towards my goal. The biggest thing troubling me was how was I going to pack up and move this whole house in the state I was in, so thinking outside the box I decided that I don’t need to pack it, the solution is that I would hire packers & movers (less work and stress for me). I have enlisted children to help after we leave so I really don’t have to worry about it. I have donated half my house to charity and many of the ‘kids’ got first dibs to the artwork, exercise equipment and furniture while many friends and cousins have all adopted my plants, expensive clothes, more furniture and china that are going to good homes. They were all weighing me down.

The more I did this the more weight I felt lift off my shoulders and I felt better knowing everything I never used that was a burden to me had gone to good homes where they were needed and appreciated. So, I kept going and it got easier, it will for you too.

As far as our business I spoke with two of my senior staff and offered them a significant pay increase to undertake all the physical work for our business that I took on once Vince got too unwell to do it, explaining we didn’t want to shut it down but would lean on them to manage part of it and I would do the rest at a distance, and they were so excited to step up and take it on board and help us while benefitting themselves, tick, another mammoth obstacle out of the way and a feeling of empowerment to our team and a huge relief for me – I can now either do the admin or handball that too but the big advantage is that I can work from anywhere, now there is freedom and excitement for the journey ahead.

I am just about done!

And I did it and survived, and the benefits of this journey have been numerous. So many happy people who feel part of the journey and part of the solution helping us to get out of here, so much love, faith and trust in ourselves and each other and everyone around us has bought us all closer. So many who are so excited to come and stay with us in FNQ and Central America – sounds like we have helped so many to become excited about life again and travel to be with us to create new memories and undertake new adventures.

So, we are booked in to leave Melbourne May 30th. We fly to Far North Queensland to a resort at the 17th latitude for 6-8 weeks.

We will both rest and destress and focus on light, water and magnetism and see how we both go, this is our precursor to heading over to Miami, Mexico and El Salvador for an extended period of time all dependant on how Vince’s health holds up. We will get there in better shape having done all of this when the time is right for us.

I believe it will be amazing and we will both thrive, and I am so excited about this new adventure.

As per my own training and Jacks words the truth that ‘you can’t get better in the place that made you sick!’ can’t be denied. I knew that but chose to ignore it at my own peril.

I can’t wait to get to the other side of the world sometime later in the year around September/October all things going well. I have learned to pivot and find ways to solve problems, because they can always be solved.

I see this journey as a gift, and look at it as an adventure and new beginnings and a different life than the one we were told was how we had to live via societal and cultural norms. I am so, so very grateful for covid and the great reset for highlighting this all as bullshit and to Jack and others for helping me to see this and for a future I had never envisioned. I have started listening and laid the foundations for our health and wealth.

I really hope I get to meet you over there one of these days and give you a big hug, along with the rest of the cult members (haha) relocating down to that part of the world that sound like my people, the people I always needed rather than what I allowed myself to settle for.

Reach out for support and motivation when the little voice says it’s too hard, rest when you are tired as it may take longer but you will cope far better, because I can tell you that there are great lessons in all of this, you have to stop for long enough to realise them and there are so many people who want to support you, if you only allow them to. I can now say this from the other side of this experience, I learnt so much about myself and others around me, I did it, I survived and I am continuing to do it with renewed energy and excitement.

You are going to be great, you can do this, we are all here cheering you on.

### 5G Canary — 2022-05-08T15:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-312812>

Love this ^^^ y’all got this!!

I am the rip the bandaid off type person. We moved three weeks after our consult with Dr. Kruse while I didn’t end up in Mexico but instead farther south and in a low population area... about 3 years in I had reversed about 6 autoimmune conditions and no longer suffer from severe EHS!! All thanks to Dr. Kruse teaching me to follow Mother Nature’s rules! Since today is Mother’s Day.... I encourage everyone to spend sometime loving Mother Nature she is truly amazing! What we give her is mirrored back in us... meaning the more time we spend with her the healthier we become and the more time disconnected from her the more unhealthy we will be. Yes... it is that easy!

### caroline — 2022-05-09T18:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-312840>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=312808)
>
> [@Kari Dunlop](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25265/) thank you for sharing and i can see you dancing under the stars with immense health improvements.
>
> I think many people who have found Jack were at their wits end with poor health from the many stories I have heard that will all empathise with how you feel at this moment.
>
> I hope my sharing some of my journey below over the last twelve months helps you a little, it won't help you do what you need to, but i still hope it gives you courage and energy to get your journey on track and know you aren't alone.
>
> Personally I know where you are coming from as I came across Jack about four years ago and knew what he was saying to be true but it was too hard and there were always reasons why I didn’t implement any of this work, or constantly told myself I would ‘come back to it when I had time’, wow, I never realised what those words would mean in years to come, however the universe works in mysterious ways and a health crisis resulted in our first consult with Jack twelve months ago, meaning I could no longer ignore any of his teachings.
>
> His advice to my partner and myself were along similar lines to your rx – ‘go north and see how he goes in a better environment and when you can then 'get the hell out of Australia as soon as they open the borders'!
>
> Very daunting prospect, we have a small business and commitment to employees who rely on us and we have a blended family of six adult children and five grandchildren so no easy task for Vince to leave his daughters and grandchildren, what about my children, my mum, i'm so tired how can i do all of this …………blah blah blah!
>
> I also found the thought of taking a very unwell man half way around the world where, like you, we don't speak the language nor know anything about the culture and we would be alone to be a monster task I didn’t have the energy to undertake (not realising I had my own health challenges) yet the more I thought about it the more the idea seemed not so daunting. I was making it harder than it should have been. The gauntlet was thrown down, was I up to the challenge?
>
> I thought about all the excuses I would bring up - it was difficult to leave family, friends and a life I believed was good me, a city I loved…..and the more the excuses kept coming, the more I analysed everything, the more I realised I was sabotaging myself & both our recoveries, so for the first time in 56 years (yay for 1966 babies) I decided to put myself first and Vince needed to do the same.
>
> Jacks words again rattled around in my head ‘who are you good for if you’re not good enough for yourself!’
>
> None of the family, friends, acquaintances, business, staff and everything else we used as excuses not to leave or change in the past would bring back our health. It was harder to stay here as we were than to make these changes and leave.
>
> I realised I needed to take a leap of faith and believe in myself, and that I am exactly where I am meant to be. I took Jacks advice to do this for myself and my partner to allow him to have his journey as well.
>
> I also realised its only as hard as I make it and the little voice inside my head always told me it would be too hard to do this, that people relied on me, that I couldn’t leave but that was fear speaking and through all of this I found my voice and courage within. I realised my thyroid cancer and now pituitary tumour were because of a lifetime of this sort of thinking, sabotage, stress and more that i had allowed to fester. It’s time to tackle this. I drew my line in the sand.
>
> I decided to look at everything it in small chunks to lessen overwhelm and now am so excited by an adventure that we will undertake for the next 12-18 months. Take it in small periods of time and small tasks.
>
> I started with decluttering the house, one room at a time, sometimes just one drawer or one cupboard was all i could manage. Sometimes i would go for a week and not be able to do anything and when i felt like it i achieved what i could. It was all moving in the right direction and i told myself that something was better than nothing and that it was all moving me forward towards my goal. The biggest thing troubling me was how was I going to pack up and move this whole house in the state I was in, so thinking outside the box I decided that I don’t need to pack it, the solution is that I would hire packers & movers (less work and stress for me). I have enlisted children to help after we leave so I really don’t have to worry about it. I have donated half my house to charity and many of the ‘kids’ got first dibs to the artwork, exercise equipment and furniture while many friends and cousins have all adopted my plants, expensive clothes, more furniture and china that are going to good homes. They were all weighing me down.
>
> The more I did this the more weight I felt lift off my shoulders and I felt better knowing everything I never used that was a burden to me had gone to good homes where they were needed and appreciated. So, I kept going and it got easier, it will for you too.
>
> As far as our business I spoke with two of my senior staff and offered them a significant pay increase to undertake all the physical work for our business that I took on once Vince got too unwell to do it, explaining we didn’t want to shut it down but would lean on them to manage part of it and I would do the rest at a distance, and they were so excited to step up and take it on board and help us while benefitting themselves, tick, another mammoth obstacle out of the way and a feeling of empowerment to our team and a huge relief for me – I can now either do the admin or handball that too but the big advantage is that I can work from anywhere, now there is freedom and excitement for the journey ahead.
>
> I am just about done!
>
> And I did it and survived, and the benefits of this journey have been numerous. So many happy people who feel part of the journey and part of the solution helping us to get out of here, so much love, faith and trust in ourselves and each other and everyone around us has bought us all closer. So many who are so excited to come and stay with us in FNQ and Central America – sounds like we have helped so many to become excited about life again and travel to be with us to create new memories and undertake new adventures.
>
> So, we are booked in to leave Melbourne May 30th. We fly to Far North Queensland to a resort at the 17th latitude for 6-8 weeks.
>
> We will both rest and destress and focus on light, water and magnetism and see how we both go, this is our precursor to heading over to Miami, Mexico and El Salvador for an extended period of time all dependant on how Vince’s health holds up. We will get there in better shape having done all of this when the time is right for us.
>
> I believe it will be amazing and we will both thrive, and I am so excited about this new adventure.
>
> As per my own training and Jacks words the truth that ‘you can’t get better in the place that made you sick!’ can’t be denied. I knew that but chose to ignore it at my own peril.
>
> I can’t wait to get to the other side of the world sometime later in the year around September/October all things going well. I have learned to pivot and find ways to solve problems, because they can always be solved.
>
> I see this journey as a gift, and look at it as an adventure and new beginnings and a different life than the one we were told was how we had to live via societal and cultural norms. I am so, so very grateful for covid and the great reset for highlighting this all as bullshit and to Jack and others for helping me to see this and for a future I had never envisioned. I have started listening and laid the foundations for our health and wealth.
>
> I really hope I get to meet you over there one of these days and give you a big hug, along with the rest of the cult members (haha) relocating down to that part of the world that sound like my people, the people I always needed rather than what I allowed myself to settle for.
>
> Reach out for support and motivation when the little voice says it’s too hard, rest when you are tired as it may take longer but you will cope far better, because I can tell you that there are great lessons in all of this, you have to stop for long enough to realise them and there are so many people who want to support you, if you only allow them to. I can now say this from the other side of this experience, I learnt so much about myself and others around me, I did it, I survived and I am continuing to do it with renewed energy and excitement.
>
> You are going to be great, you can do this, we are all here cheering you on.

well said my very dear friend. You and Vince are amazing humans.
We have all learned so much on this journey.
We have learned we are more resilient than we thought.
We have learned that we can do the hard stuff - one baby step at a time.
We have learned to trust our instincts.
We have learned that our openness and willingness to move forward attracts people to us and also inspires others to do better.

The other day we were shopping in a department store in San Salvador. Generally the staff don't speak English at all - but they always race around to find someone on the staff that does speak English.
A lovely young woman joined us - we are generally like a travelling side show in this store because we need so many things....
Andrea asked us where we are from and we said we are moving to ES from Australia.

Her first question was "because of bitcoin?" We said yes! and because of being closer to the equator - for health
She knows about magnetism and light and the benefits of the sun ...and she loves bitcoin!
She is young but she has just bought a house. She is a graphic designer and also designs handbags and clothes.
She is so open to everything so she can improve the conditions of her existence.
Andrea was an absolute pleasure - she radiated positive energy and kindness.

### Kari Dunlop — 2022-05-09T19:31:39+0300

Thank you ALL for your support and insights. Reading your comments is like building a new 'soul'er callus  My stumbling blocks will become stepping stones because you have been so kind and generous to share your experiences.

@Loudy Cincotta - Thank you for the very touching and tender message. I'm so excited to hear more as you keep moving forward. A most excellent adventure it is!! And I know it doesn't feel that way all of the time. I do look forward to that hug  A definite tissue warning for anyone else who has the good fortune of reading your post.

I was hoping to actually galvanize and get to Mexico a bit sooner than later but the gal who I was going to meet up with is heading out of town for the summer so I'll just keep trusting and breathing my way towards the next baby step!

[@ND Hauf](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25376/), [@Laudy Cincotta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24082/) [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), [@Ravi Sandhu](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25364/), [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/), [@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/), [@EWO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25426/) Again, thank you all so very much. It helps more than you know.

### 5G Canary — 2022-05-10T17:34:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-312866>

Thank you! “It is easy”... I would love to say food, exercise, supplements or my hard work healed me but it didn’t work that way. It was the complete opposite. It wasn’t hard work at all... it was easy. First and most importantly you need to move to an optimal environment. In an optimal environment you should only see improvements in your health... if you don’t then it is still likely something in the environment. Next is the fun part- literally reconnecting with mother nature... following her rules.

We are being brainwashed into believing this is fun and making life easier... living optimal!

### Kari Dunlop — 2022-05-10T19:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-312882>

So true [@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/) and [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) .... people don't know what they don't know but I'm already doing what I can to start planting some seeds. I'm already seeing that I'm less interested doing all the things that I was so addicted to....working long hours, checking emails (thinking the harder I work the more money I make, all of it being used to pay for labs, supplements, appointments, IVs, Rxs and so on) and also waaaaay too much tv. I've been too tired to do anything else but now I drag my behind outside to lie out on my balcony. Having been sick for 3 decades now, the tech addiction was the only way I felt connected to anyone and anything outside of my symptoms. I did a lotta living vicariously through other people.

My 14 year old nephew is so addicted to his video games and now he's on antidepressants. Hurts my heart. The good news is that my sister is taking him to a local quantum biology practitioner.

I'm starting to notice mother (nature)'s hunger percolate a little louder. She whispers more and more to me. Super subtle right now but definitely sensing the pull to want to spend more time outside.

I'm also flirting with an new idea, a new belief that the more time I 'spend' in the sun/play/outdoors the more money I make. I'll keep you posted as to how that all goes!!!!

I just got my ethernet installed so that's one thing off of my list  Still sorting out a trip south but celebrating the baby steps in the mean time.

[@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/) so very relevant and so true, and once you open that door you realise how beautiful it is and that you had things around the wrong way, i believe we use these as coping mechanisms until we can no longer ignore symptoms or unrest within, i know i certainly did that and like [@Kari Dunlop](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25265/) said, i believed it was how i stayed connected too, once i went to out and connected with mother nature i could clearly see how wrong my thinking was that actually the more time i spent outside and away from technology the more i really wasn't interested in it anymore.

i now really only look at a few sites and emails in the morning and i only really skim over them and then don't really go back there, i get told off by everyone now that i don't answer my phone, i actually never take it off silent and its never around me, i carry it when i go out but don't even see the need to have it near, only checking at certain intervals (if i remember) just in case there is someone really trying to get through to me.

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) watching young adults and kids is heartbreaking. My beautiful 26 year old son is so addicted to tech and is so blue light toxic and i cant get through to him, tech is his whole world, he is studying game design and thinks technology is magnificent, he thinks AI is amazing and has been working at events pre covid using VR tech which he loves .

i try and keep connection with him and keep him engaged so if he needs help he can reach out but he wont listen to reason and thinks i am a crackpot that hates mainstream ways, he will however call when he's sick though and ask me for 'whatever hippie shit' i think he could use so whilst he won't admit my 'hippie shit' works he acknowledges it by asking when he is in need of it.

He had covid about a month ago, sadly he is terrified of it and triple vaxxed, interestingly the only one of my three children like that and he called and asked if i could bring him any 'hippie shit' i thought might help him, so armed with my 'weird and wonderful' supplements, fresh juices, fresh fruit and vegetables, a pot of chicken noodle and vegetable soup, essential oils and herbal teas and anti virals, i delivered them to him with instructions and a few days later feeling so much better he was grateful but told me it was less severe because he had just had his booster shot!!! His fear of the virus broke my heart and knowing its driven by his lifestyle and addiction to social media had him believing in the mainstream narrative as opposed to what he should instinctively know was good for him.........so i hope many others with family addicted to technology can just keep lines of communication open and support their loved ones. My hope is one day he will see the light (literally) and cross over to the dark side with the rest of us who have done so before him.

He has committed to coming to Mexico while we are there later this year and i do honestly believe being away from here and doing outdoorsy things and sightseeing with us sneakily getting him into the light and away from tech might help him to see how much better he will feel........fingers crossed!

### Kari Dunlop — 2022-07-17T22:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-314446>

I would love to know if anyone has resolved their SIBO and what tips you'd be willing to share?

My frustration levels are at an all time high. A good friend of mine suicided last week and her services are on Thursday. It's been a very hard loss for me. I know this sounds really superficial and ridiculous but I truly don't have anything to wear. Nada. Zilch. Nothing. The family has asked that we wear bright colours, including something blue, and all I have is a closet full of black casual wear. I don't really go out much due to my health and so I've just always focused on comfort. My fatigue is pretty bad right now so the thought of going out to try and find something is a little daunting. I think I would just have to shop at maternity stores because the bloating is really bad. I thought I'd stick to just protein to see if that would help to minimize the bloating but it's not working; I know this sounds like a really ridiculous thing to be complaining about. I feel like I'm in a major food fight at the best of times but this has amplified everything.

I may just have to surrender and watch from home via livestream but there are so many people who will be there that I haven't seen in such a long time and yet, getting to the mall and then facing myself in a dressing room mirror feels a little much.

I fee so stressed about this. And maybe I should just waive the white flag and watch from home? And, if anyone has any tips or tricks to deal with bloating, I do have a couple of days to try and see if I can get something to shift.

### caroline — 2022-07-20T22:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/operation-sunrise-medicine-karis-journal.26985/post-314522>

C/T is fantastic! I used to put bags of ice on my tummy too.

Go to your friends funeral - it truly doesn't matter what you wear. Grab something/anything out of your closet and just go.

Kari- have you done the leptin reset yet?

I always had huge problems with bloating ....it was mostly from wheat.

When I did the leptin reset I was eating protein mostly with a small amount of veg...like a lettuce leaf or a carrot and found all my issues getting a lot better.

Have a look at 100% carnivore and beyond ......there are lots of helpful people over there. Phil Escott wrote a book and Jack wrote the forward for it.

I used to cook a roast beef or lamb or pork in my crockpot over nite and then have 50gr protein with some eggs sometimes for a BAB breakfast.

I would not be hungry the rest of the day. I would then have an early dinner - 4:30- 5pm with a little more protein and maybe a bit of sweet potato.

Hi Caroline
I did end up going to the service. It was lovely as far as those things can be. I haven't eaten wheat in years. I have been eating a lot of eggs which were in the red zone on my last Cyrex food sensitivity panel so I think I'll cut those out and see if that makes a difference. I've also had some pain in my liver/gall bladder (I do have NAFLD, I don't drink but I'm guessing it's from the 100s of supplements a day I've taken for 15+ years and/or SIBO), so I'm not sure if that contributes to bloating as well. My visceral massage therapist said I had a sluggish liver. My liver enzymes have normalized so that's something! I just don't recognize my body anymore.

I've been working at the Leptin reset and the BAB but as I mentioned I was eating eggs and a little dairy but I think I'll just try meat and some fat and see how things shift. I love sweet potatoes!! but they are high fodmap and I'm not able to tolerate them. They turn me into a larger than life puffer fish  I'm also working with a low amylose diet so no root veggies.

Is 100% carnivore and beyond a FB or IG group? I will definitely check it out and also the book

Thank you for always sharing your own valuable tips and experiences!!

# Thread: John's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johns-journal.27102/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Warner — 2022-07-20T02:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johns-journal.27102/post-314504>

Hello, I'm 51, started my quantum journey Christmas time 2019. life was rolling along. I work as a horseshoer, on the far northwest side of Houston and live in the country north east of houston. I thought I was relatively healthy most of my life, had a head injury, 2012, and horse smashed left knee 2017. Family history is another thing, Father died at 51, Cancer, many aunts and uncles also and mother had in 2017. All this lead me to start to question what Drs were saying in hospital, you would ask "why" and they didn't know or didn't say. I've always been somewhat of a skeptic, So I wasn't buying it.
Health journey Started goin to the gym, you know body builders are fit and know how to eat... Well that wasn't working, bit over weight and inflamed. Was not building muscle or loosing weight, so found fasting. Lost some weight, and was feeling bit better. Listening To a Ben Geenfield podcast, Dr Kruse said live like a polar bear, and eat like a great white shark? So I started to listen more, but more importantly I stared the protocols. Then things started to change. Lost extra weight, exercise outside, felt better, sleep improved, and food timing helped digestive issues. I had serious back pain in Jan. It was gradually better, then would flare up, on a consult was told needed a MRI.
Thanks Dr Kruse. In May had 2 MRI's, Cat scan, and surgery. Real Cell Carcinoma. Surgery was on May 24, my Dads birthday. He died at 51, I am 51. Took cancer and kidney. All is well now. Since I've been a Mito, I have driven to much, 2 to 4 hours a day. I have since resolved that when I work, I stay 2 or 3 nights away, has drastically reduced driving time. I also know I have done things in the past that has prob started this. Where I live Is a good spot in the country, so always thought driving was better than living around population. Changes have been made, and more to come, but the major one's have been made. By the way surgeon said of when I asked him "why" Shitty Luck! Have started to teach him about light, don't know if he gets it, you can tell he's thinking when I explain the health benefits of light, and he ask every time about the blue blockers. More on the past later. I know is has its place, moving forward here. I'm thankful for everyone in this space without, things may have turned out different.

# Thread: Jaden's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healingfromwithin — 2022-07-28T21:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/post-314674>

When I was 16 my health got my attention for the first time. Someone close to me mentioned that I don’t seem like my “old self” anymore. It made me realize that things were changing in my body and my life force was feeling a bit more dull. Wanting that “old self” back led to a daily, relentless search on my part for answers.

What started with cursory google searches directing me to badly-written articles by mis-informed bloggers (which I was unaware of at the time given I had only begun my journey) led to deeper, more profound discoveries as well as a refined ability to discern.

For thirteen years now, I dove deeper and deeper into biochemistry, trying to find the culprit - trying to discover what went wrong, why and when.

Several years ago I ended up following the thinking of a brilliant biologist who focused heavily on genetics, optimal functioning of the methylation cycle, and energy production via the Krebs cycle.

Well, I never could get myself even starting her protocol, because no matter what I tried regarding diet or supplements, inflammation would rear its’ ugly head. And so I continued to deteriorate, still relentlessly looking for answers, but feeling like I wasn’t getting anywhere.

This way of thinking, however, has changed for me these past couple of months as I discovered the Kruse community and had my eyes opened. When I hear truth, it resonates within my entire being, and I crave more.

As a small child, I knew only that I sought truth and that somehow, the entire world around me was a lie. On a physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual level, I have been navigating this lie from the moment I entered this world.

Thus, in this now moment I have arrived here, writing this story, and with a pivoted point of view, I search anew for that which I have sought for so long.

━━━━━━━━━▲━━━━━━━━━​

### JanSz — 2022-07-28T21:55:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/post-314676>

The more testing you do, the more information you will get, and the better your guessing will be.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) , the owner of this board, test himself at least every 3 months.
....

### caroline — 2022-07-29T02:10:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/post-314684>

welcome to this optimal health journey Jaden!

Please give Dr. K. some health history ....also your Mom's and Gran's

Where do you live?
what do you do for work?

### healingfromwithin — 2022-08-05T20:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/post-314880>

**FAMILY HISTORY:**

**Mother** - Born 1957, resided mostly in Massachusetts. Moved around a lot growing up due to my grandfather being on active air force duty. The family lived at different bases, most notably in Okinawa, Japan for a while. Known health issues: weight struggles, fibromyalgia, always looked “toxic” from the outside, bad skin etc. Did not have good health habits.

**Grandmother** - Born 1929, resided mostly in Massachusetts, of Irish heritage. Also struggled with weight issues. Birthed 2 children: my mother, and 3 years later, a boy with many birth defects (cerebral palsy, brain abnormalities). Grandmother’s brother developed schizophrenia in early adult life. However, the second brother is very sound of mind, as is my grandmother herself.

**Sister** - 6 years older than me. Took shots early in life to help stop so much hormone production. Bipolar, anxiety, violent, depressed. Lifelong lithium user. Lifelong oral contraceptive user (hormone regulation). Plus many other meds. Struggled with eating way too much all the time (I assume from low dopamine from the lithium). Went up to 320 lbs at largest. Very artistic and imaginative, but a constant struggle with health.

**Other familial notes:**

-   **Father** - Angry (seems like a liver issue to me), gout, hypertension, kidney stones
-   **Maternal Uncle** - Static cerebral palsy, missing the dividing tissue between parts of the brain, very extreme case, taken care of constantly his whole life
-   **Maternal Great Uncle** - Schizophrenia (deceased, 81)
-   **Maternal Great Aunt** - Multiple sclerosis (deceased, 76)
-   **Maternal Great Grandfather** - Died from heart attack at 55
-   **Paternal Grandmother** - Hypertension, heart attack (deceased, 74)
-   **Paternal Grandfather** - Died in sleep at 65

**MY HISTORY:**

**Vaccinations:** HepB, DTP, Hib, Polio, MMR, Var, Meningitis, Flu, HPV (Gardasil)
**Age 3** - Dx severe eczema (still have it writing this)
**Age 6** - Parents officially divorced, massive stress started
**Age 8** \- Prescribed steroids for eczema (prednicarbate 0.01%) Used for 10 years
**Age 9** \- Entered puberty, depression began
**Age 11** - First menstrual cycle (they were immediate very heavy, devastating)
**Age 13** - Discovered libido, depression, internal & external stress worsening. Got our first wi-fi router.
**Age 15** - Got my first cell phone. Network & wifi constantly on. Slept with it under my head for a year.
**Age 16** - Started oral contraceptives to control the bleeding
**Age 17** - Noticed I was starting to feel much more flat. I was calmer, less anxious, but also dispassionate and my libido almost completely vanished (Still nothing to this day writing this - oh the misery it has caused me in life!)
**Age 18** - Left home (massively freeing) and rented a tiny apartment by myself
**Age 19** - Mental symptoms really worsening. Tried an even lower dose oral contraceptive, had a psychotic episode a week later so I stopped completely. I felt a tiny bit better, but my “normalcy” has never returned to me. I’ve just been worsening ever since.
**Age 20** - Moved in with my husband-to-be. Finally got a doctor. All she “found” was low vitamin D. Crazy reaction when I started supplementing (anxiety, heart palps etc. from low magnesium / or possibly high calcium) Used magnesium every day and tolerated it for the next 8 years (I recently stopped completely).
**Age 21** - Moved to a different house that had a basement which was full of so much junk and everything was coated in this gray “dust” that always seemed to me like it was alive. There was no black mold or water damage. Just this living dust. It made us feel sick going down there. We cleaned it very well and made part of it “livable” and cordoned off the “toxic” stuff. But we both felt it was not healthy for us over the 7 years we stayed.
**Age 22** - Got married. My personality continued to change as my health got worse. We both agreed we’d keep “working on it” and expected things to get better and return to the way things “used” to be when we first became friends (when I was much more my true self)

Also, I tried low dose progesterone cream at this time. Exacerbated my high testosterone and increased estrogen. Felt a lot worse. Switched to HIGH dose for a long while. Felt calmer, it stopped my cycles again, and I eventually just felt worse and worse. It was all converting to cortisol (I have been constantly stressed out my entire life). So I stopped (weaned off slowly).
**
Age 26** - Anger, violence, health issues just worsening all the time. Getting sicker and sicker. Food sensitivities growing. Started really going downhill after getting some illness which was either a really bad cold or possibly strep. Felt great when it was over, but my immune system seemed different. The “infection” never quite left my tonsils. I kept trying to kill it with oregano and things like that but it kept coming back (I wasn’t doing it right). I noticed from there on out my health was rapidly reclining even more, my mineral levels were getting progressively lower, my food sensitivities were growing, I started having new levels of leaky gut where I could “taste” everything throughout my body. For example, If I had some lemon, my whole body, head to toe, could “taste” it. I can’t explain it. But I still have that today. I also started noticing I couldn’t tolerate sodium anymore. It would make my nerves CRAZY and I would get violent, like I couldn’t control my nervous system. I would become emotionally violent too. So I stayed away from sodium. (Still cannot tolerate it at ALL today - WTF?)
**
Age 28** - Down to 95 lbs again. Can barely eat. Whatever I eat causes excitotoxicity (glutamate). Drinking water causes me to cry. Adrenaline rushes. Very low electrolytes. Body so starved and stressed out.

I started using liquid RNA therapy from HHI (Inflammation support) and I feel that helped over time. I also started spending more time outside, just sitting. I was so dehydrated. The sun would make it worse. But I felt less stressed outside. I was so weak, but we started going for long walks. I noticed I could tolerate water better while walking. I also got so hungry that I decided I had to start just eating again. It was working for a while. I was getting stronger. I mostly ate right before bed. It was the only time I could tolerate it without anxiety / cortisol.

**We moved to Maine** to a tiny trailer on a piece of land we found ourselves the owners of. This was freedom on so many levels. We had no running water, no AC, and a lot of work to do on the house / yard for the first summer. I found myself spending more time outside and in the sun than I ever had before. I also was getting more oxygen after being extremely oxygen deprived during the whole pandemic. I was feeling better. Eating way too many things I wasn’t doing well with, but just enjoying it for a while. I got tired of feeling awful after certain foods so I started being more strict again. I was getting a lot of psychotic episodes again. And I regained my menstrual cycles. A lot of the mental stuff was revolving around my cycle. But I also noticed it has a lot to do with sodium / potassium. Everything is worse when I have sodium. I become a monster. Taking potassium just makes me thirsty and acts like a diuretic. I also have been a chronic magnesium user for years. It’s one of the ONLY things I can do to help me feel calm (gaba activation, NMDA receptor blocking, calcium regulation, etc.). But I always need more.

As winter came, and we went indoors, (and didn’t have heat for months) and started using electric heaters (oh the EMFs!). We also didn’t have hot water until just before winter. We had a wood stove put in in December. It was so warm. But there was soot everywhere, carbon monoxide, and the aroma from the wood (that has to be processed by the liver too!). I started feeling worse. I had a rash erupt all over my body. Still have it today.

I got worse and worse. My cycles stopped again. I became so psychotic and delusional I thought I needed my marriage to end. It made no sense to me. I just knew I was so sick, I couldn’t eat, I was so stressed out, if I could just be completely alone, maybe I could save my life. (That’s what my body was telling me). But we worked it all out. No divorce. But we’re both suffering with our health.

**Age 29** \- Fast forward to the moment I’m writing this…

Doing pretty badly but making a lot of “discoveries” in the past few months. Still not recovering. But continuing to learn.

Turned off the wi-fi and phone network recently. Discovered a massive grounding problem with our hot water tank. Every time it would come on, the farthest reaches of the house would read 10 milligauss! And in my room next to the tank it would read 20 milligauss. And right near the tank, 300 milligauss. Every time it would come on, for almost a year.

Started going outside with bare feet and trying to get some sun on my skin. BUT the sun makes me even more dehydrated (by biggest issue - I CANNOT hydrate!) and it gives me a rash on my arms, and the intensity of the sun fires all my nerves and can lead to a psychotic attack. My nerves are so overstimulated all the time. WHY?

I’ve had some good suggestions from the various communities I research through (Jack Kruse, STTM, MTHFR groups etc.) One was to look into mold toxicity. I have been around mold during my life and it wouldn’t surprise me. I will test for that soon. I will also be ruling out Lyme (I live in the northeast, have never been bitten as far as I know), I will also be ruling out hyperparathyroidism. My recent blood calcium was kind of high and I can’t tolerate calcium or I get cramps, mental issues, and my stomach feels like a brick.

**My current constant symptoms (as of August 2022):**

-   Severe dehydration
-   Struggling to eat
-   Inflammation
-   Always tingling, fuzzy senses, overstimulated nerves
-   Anger and violent tendencies if I am not super strict with my diet / fasting
-   Leaky gut
-   Very low hormones
-   Practically non-existent sense of well-being

### healingfromwithin — 2022-08-23T21:10:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jadens-optimal-journal.27113/post-315215>

​

I’ve just completed the first 6 of 12 weeks of the RT3 clearing protocol (following STTM guidelines) and I’m feeling prompted today to write an update. A lot has changed and not necessarily in regards to T3 therapy. I don’t feel the benefits of it yet. I have a good amount of RT3 to clear out and it takes a full 12 weeks. I calculated the FT3 / RT3 ratio the other day from my labs 5 weeks ago before I started. I was shocked to see how non-optimal it was. For example, people report feeling healthy and well with a ratio of at least 20:1 in favor of FT3. Mine was 0.019.

So I suppose I’m feeling at least some hope that there could be progress in that department in the near future. Progress is not something I’m used to… I have not seen it in over a decade. I’ve actually just been deteriorating my whole life (physically). Meanwhile, my spirit, my perspective, my understanding has grown exponentially. It has not been fun for a moment feeling not even human in my weakened body. But I would not trade my life for any idea of something better. Who I am at this moment is what is worth more to me than anything. Therefore, it has been just the right experience for me.​

━━━━━━━━━▲━━━━━━━━━

​

For the longest time I was attempting to subsist on mostly carbs, a good amount of fat, and very low protein. Following my body signals and also the advice of people following other protocols, specifically for addressing MTHFR issues. I was getting sicker all the time though. And I (recently) found myself down to just carrots, MCT and some sweet potato.

I could not have imagined how sick it was making me at the time. And what I’ve learned about my body and my current condition since adopting a zero-carb carnivore diet has been mind-blowing. The following is a brief recap of the previous 6 weeks of my life.​

▲​

**WEEK 1 -** Starting T3 therapy. Feeling a little nervous at first. Noticing that the sulfate additive in the pills is causing issues. I’ve struggled with sulfur for a long time. I notice stress signals coming from my gut, excess gas, the feeling that my blood is full of a toxin, and I can taste sulfur in my mouth.

Within a few days I was prompted to begin a major change in my diet. It’s possible I have vitamin A toxicity from the ridiculous amount of carrots, sweet potato and squash I’ve consumed over the years. Also, learning the principles behind the 100% carnivore lifestyle and having it really resonate with me. Deciding to go for it. Just leap in. I’m only getting worse with my current diet.

**WEEK 2 -** I have no idea what to eat. I haven’t eat meat in a long time. Trying a number of new foods. Going with what sounds right to me. Making a lot of discoveries about what does and does not agree with me. (Mostly things that don’t…) Having a lot of gut struggles in this process. But the sheer lack of fiber is feeling oh so right to my poor tummy! And I’m finding I can “hear” my gut better without all those pain signals from vegetable roughage.

Trying salmon…a little hard to digest but seems very agreeable. Trying canned tuna…first few cans tasted incredible, now I’m getting nauseated. Trying beef…making me feel low and depressed. Trying eggs with the yolk…still can’t tolerate that much cholesterol / choline at once. Trying egg whites alone… doing ok. Trying roast beef… the tiny specs of black pepper creating major pain signals. Anything with added sodium making my nervous system explode with severe stress and violent behavior the next day (this has been going on for many years. Why the issue with sodium?)

Experiencing severe dehydration… worse than it’s ever been. Feeling desperate… unable to even stand up… barely breathing. My instinct is to eat watermelon. Just eat it so I don’t die. Immediate relief and rehydration. Absolutely crazy from the sugar and phenols later. Making me irritable and my rash flare up even more. No entering a period of alternating diarrhea and dehydration.

Mental symptoms are bad.. Fasting. Finally ate tuna with MCT. Diarrhea four times after… can’t figure this out.

**WEEK 3 -** I think the MCTs are causing the violent gut movement and dehydration… stopping for while. Tried pork rinds. Holy sh\*\* anxiety. 1000mg of (added) sodium at once. Nerves, muscles, brain going to sh\*\*. Next couple days resulting in severe depression, violence, intense stress, high cortisol, feeling like ending my life.

Realizing that it has to be related to low potassium and magnesium. Trying to add them in orally (which never goes well). Just causing more dehydration and nerve issues. I’ve always just soaked my feet in magnesium to get it (2x day for many years otherwise horrible nervous symptoms).

Tingling… itching… dehydrated… irritable… all the time…

Dehydration and high stress making me desperate for carbs. Specifically potato chips and watermelon. Every time I’ve felt extreme electrolyte issues it seems like my body overrides my better judgment and goes for potato chips. The salt, potassium, fat and carbs… I always feel so sick the next few days. So dehydrated, itchy, and stressed out. Not working… And the sugar from the watermelon inflaming my whole body, especially my airways, nose and throat. This has to stop.

Back on low carb… Trying cucumber (raw, peeled and deseeded). Seems to help a little, but feeling the diuretic effect and there’s a “sappiness” to it that all squash-type veggies have. I always get a leg cramp from sap… After a few days and a half-dozen cucumbers later, it’s definitely not working out and my tummy is too stressed out from the vegetable matter.

Trying to take potassium again, just getting tight shoulders. Still too dehydrated and inflamed to take electrolytes orally.

Incessantly experiencing pounding heart since low carb. Dehydration, low blood volume, switching to a new fuel source…

**WEEK 4 -** Attempting to switch to butter as main fat source since the MCT were causing trouble. Getting the same “greasy” feeling throughout my gut and feeling inflammation / depression. I’ve always had such trouble processing fat (not a gallbladder issue, but one of inflammation).

Feeling desperate for carbs again, feeling very off and in trouble… had sweet potato and watermelon. Wow, energy is crazy, mood better, actually feeling a little dopamine. Couldn’t sleep. Took 2mg lithium. Feel horrible. Very depressed in the morning, dehydrated. That didn’t go well. Tried potato again tonight. Feel horrible. Shaking like crazy. Way too much inflammation from the glucose. Tummy is so stressed out. Ok, done with potato. And still way too low in dopamine, hormones, thyroid to tolerate any amount of lithium (even though I’m seriously deficient).

Went on a small road trip for our anniversary. Felt decent during the trip, I was happy we were actually doing something. Decided for the celebration we would have some ice cream (an extremely rare occasion). I got my “safe” kind made from coconut with very few ingredients. Ok, the sugar (coconut sugar) made me so f\*in sick the next day. Wow.

Cloudy for a couple days. Lack of sunlight always makes me feel dead. Depressed as hell.

Been trying to figuring out the following for years - whenever food, especially fiber or any irritating bulk moves past my transverse colon, I get serious inflammation, crazy mental symptoms, my whole torso feels like a brick and cortisol is so high. It goes away once it goes past the splenic flexure. Must be damage in there.

Also been trying to figure out for years - the right side of my head gets a “full” feeling. It impairs my ability to think and my stress level gets really high. It’s like a swelling in my brain, making glutamate fire. Just straight down the middle of my head. My eye feels full, my mouth feels heavy, my ear is slightly numb, my hearing a little less. Seems electrolyte related. Always made so much worse when I try something with added sodium. Cannot get my electrolytes balanced no matter what.

I’m hoping (cautiously, because I’m so sick of hoping) that improving T3 levels will be a rising tide that helps everything…

Decided to try MCT after 4 full day off (first break in months). Holy crap. A bunch of symptoms came back right away. Sour stomach, sour mouth, heart pounding, dehydration (it moves all the water in my body into my gut). So violently dehydrated the next day I near passed out. Emergency watermelon saved me. But I feel awful from it. So happy to have figured out that MCT was dehydrating me so much further for months. It stops right now.

**WEEK 5 -** Sugar always means glutamate issues the next day. That’s the last sugar I’ll ever eat. Decided I need to start zinc again (even though it makes my mood so flat. It makes me feel so distant and grumpy. But it helps my nerves, my stomach, my appetite, my calmness so much. It’s like a salve on my gut. It helps to heal leaky gut).

Realizing that as much water as I already drink - it’s NOT ENOUGH! I just need so much more. I can drink 2 gallon a day.

Struggling with feeling no emotional connection to my husband or my world around me. I’m just so weak, so tired, I feel like just a shell with no “human-ness” inside at all. Like an observer just passing through this world but not even living.

Tried a little bit of dark meat from a chicken. Felt so incredibly greasy an nauseous after. I just want omega 3s now.. Fish every day. After enough omega 3s, I start to crave the others, like chicken, butter, etc. My body tells me which fats are needed next. If I go overboard on one, I crave the other. Fish, chicken, beef repeat basically.

Trying one single possible safe vegetable - spaghetti squash. It has always been the most gentle to me. I ate it every day for a couple years. Next day, stressed out gut. Ok, now it’s obvious that my gut does NOT want any plant material in it whatsoever.

Grateful for the ever-increasing knowledge as I feel my way forward. I know I can find my way out of hell…

Been having a lot of issues with constipation since going zero-carb… another thing I’ve been trying to figure out for years is this - immediately after a bowel movement I feel relief in my gut. But then my electrolytes shift hard, like I just ditched a lot of magnesium. I get brain swelling and the nerve issues start. After a day, or two, my electrolytes feel a little better, but as I approach any more time, I get really uncomfortable and my tummy is unhappy.

Just plain flat out electrolyte issues for years… magnesium is all that helps. Can’t get enough (but not orally).

Zinc really helps my nerves and my appetite, helps keep cortisol and sodium in check. I really enjoy how it makes me hungry, stimulates the desire to eat and helps me tolerate it. I’m so weak and emaciated, I need to eat as much as I can tolerate. I look like a skeleton. Even bonier and scarier than when I was 95 lbs. I don’t want to know… I took photos because it shocked me so much (I don’t often look in the mirror, not part of my lifestyle).

So… zinc stays. Even though it makes my mood crap.

I get really hungry before bed. I have been trying to follow the circadian protocol and not eat at least 4 hours before bed, but it’s not what my body wants right now. If that’s when I can tolerate more calories, that’s when I need it. If I don’t eat, I feel like I’m dying. Some nights I cry near bedtime because I feel so starved. Cortisol / blood pressure is at its lowest. And my body says, now is time to eat.

Realizing that a lot of my tummy issues are also because of hydrogen sulfide (sulfate reducing bacteria in the gut). I notice that carbs at all feeds them and makes gas. I’m miserable. No carbs, I’m able to tolerate sulfur so much better. I just have to keep drinking water constantly.

Eating a lot of butter (unsalted)… getting most of my calories from it. Was using kerrygold but noticed they list “skimmed milk” in the ingredients. Possibly contaminated with more protein / sugar. Switching to Vermont Creamery cultured butter. Wow it tastes great. Can’t get enough. Eating a lot…

**WEEK 6 -** The electric company has been working on the power lines near my house for a few months… an ongoing project. I live near a small substation about 100ft away. I tested the EMFs with the Alpha Labs UHS2 and the EMF are no worse than the lines themselves, and the lines give off a large ambient field. Right under them, 3.5 to 4.5mg. The farthest I can get on our land it goes down to 0.6mg. Still totally unacceptable for an AMBIENT reading. Inside out house starts at 0.6 and goes up to 1.1 the side closest to the wires. BUT the electric company is moving some of the wires for some reason. I wash shocked one day this week (as I take a 10 second reading every day just for reference) to find that it was only 0.2 inside the house. I ran outside with my meter and walked everywhere. I couldn’t believe it. They took half the wires away and the EMFs are way down! WOW. Feeling so grateful…

I soak my feet in magnesium chloride flakes every night. Been doing this for many years to get my mag. But I notice that dry flakes vs liquid (pre-dissolved in water for at least 12 hrs) is different. The liquid is way more powerful. Too powerful if it’s done right. I’m wondering if: dry flakes dissolving at the same time I’m soaking are using MY water to hydrate themselves, thus dehydrating me slightly. And also if the pre-dissolved mag already has all the water it can absorb and doesn’t require any of my water. Trying to go liquid only this week and see what happens..

Every day I battle dehydration, grumpiness, tiredness, anxiety, brain swelling, very high stress (physical discomfort), heart pounding from electrolyte imbalance and lack of carbs, very low mood, incessant thoughts of what I’m going to eat at my one meal today, and in the morning and especially before bed, I can’t stop thinking of ALL the food I want and it drives me nuts. I’m so hungry. I’m so thirsty. I’m so tired. I just want this hell to end. I haven’t been human in so long… I don’t even know what that means anymore.

Humidity makes me feel so much worse. The brain swelling is worse, I’m more tired, grumpy.

Getting a lot less potassium on zero-carb carnivore… adding some K chloride to my foot soaks.. Never sure if it’s helping or further dehydrating me.. Pushing out sodium.. But I can’t eat anything with added sodium or I become violent. I just can’t figure it out. Driving me crazy for years. So dehydrated, but I can’t tolerate sodium at all? It makes the brain swelling so much worse too. It’s like my glutamate issues are half because my brain is so stressed all the time from improper electrolyte balance.

Gut is still feeling very stressed out… haven’t had carbs in days. I think it’s all the butter. I must be getting way too much residual casein and whey. DUH obviously. Switching to ghee only…

Fortunately I know how to make my own ghee. But gee, it tastes like crap compared to the creamy, delicious cheese-like flavor and texture of cultured butter. Ugh.. even less to look forward to when I eat…

Just so much depression from the physical imbalances all the time… this is my life. I might as well not exist. Sometimes I wish it would just end. But I know I need to keep going. I always know that…

Trying homemade ghee infused with garlic, oregano, rosemary, then filtering out the pieces. Garlic caused that “heady” onion feeling. Forget it…

So stressed out… stomach so stressed out.

This is the list I’ve ended up with after 6 weeks of figuring out what I can and can’t eat (and it’s always changing): ground beef, chicken breast, egg white, unsalted sardines, wild salmon, ghee, and trying little bits of coconut oil again (watching for MCT dehydration though).

And by the way, zinc is out again. Causing me to be a grumpy monster, as if I could be any worse.

My gut is so stressed out no matter what. I feel like it’s from 2 things: that it is LEAKY but also the bad bacteria and their byproducts. I wish I could work on the bacteria. But I guess I’m beginning to do that by taking carbs away.

Now at 62.5mcg of T3. Endocrinologist appointment in a few days… she’s going to love me (NOT). I don’t do anything conventionally. I only “use” the western medical system to get some things I don’t want to pay for myself, mainly lab work. None of them know anything. Pure ignorance.

━━━━━━━━━▲━━━━━━━━━​

So there’s a 6-week update. Still miserable as hell. But moving forward. I guess.​

**Onwards as always…

**​

### Donald Shrump Jr. — 2022-11-20T01:13:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donald%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.27312/post-317697>

Hello,

I started my journey at 270 lbs and my kids making fun of my belly, energy, and my lack of patience. Now I am 218 lbs almost a six pack, tons of energy, and patience without real workout program (I am Chiropractor/Strength Coach so that was huge not to mess with my AMPK pathway).

I use deep CT at or before sunrise, redlight, and my vitamin d was in the 30’s and now in 80. I am hoping to get more sun and DHA in 2023 (I live in NJ so season closed). I am buckling down this winter to CT, sleep, and get my head around Ubiquitination.

So much to learn and read. I will check in with my latest labs as soon as I get them back.

### John Schumacher — 2022-11-20T06:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donald%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.27312/post-317706>

### Ravi Sandhu — 2023-01-03T15:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donald%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.27312/post-318668>

Awesome Donald!

Man, can you tell me more about your journey with CT?

I mean, I do it a lot, too, and it's powerful. But I haven't been one to do daily sessions for like a hr at a time, lets say, and something in my intuition does tell me, Ravi, you'll evolve when you can get in the tub and do CT 1hr a day.....call me crazy but I am sensing that from within

But this is your thread. So excuse me for the digression. Hearing you've got a tonne of energy is just AWESOME. I came down from 275 too, now 187lbs, but don't have anywhere near the energy or function I seek, lol.

But I am determined and just keep showing up ;-)

Ravi

### Donald Shrump Jr. — 2024-06-20T14:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/donald%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.27312/post-335929>

I had a great 2023 and winter, but spring 2024 kicked my ass. I was so diligent about Leptin Rx no food hours before bed, but that feel off. I got labs back in April my Vitamin D dropped so much and my iron is high which didn’t happen last winter. I buckled down with the UVA-B coming back to my Latitude. Feeling good again, but I swear something is stealing my tan everyday. I am trying to get to the bottom of it.
The ferromagnetic effect of RBCs has to be an issue and I am not collecting (sun) or making enough light (vUV mitochondria redox).

# Thread: Mell's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mells-journal.27733/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mellanie Parrish — 2023-05-25T22:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mells-journal.27733/post-321724>

I just got a round of blood tests done and want to post here in the hopes for feedback to help me "translate" what the test results are telling me. Here's some background info: I started eating Epi-Paleo Rx on April 15, 2023. I was eating red meat for breakfast, sometimes lunch too, nearly every day with veggies and 25g-50g of carbs. I started noticing an increase in energy levels after Week 2 (but not all the signs for improved leptin were present), and during Week 4, I felt my energy dip down again. During week 5, I sampled some yucca root, tomatoes, and a sauce made with canola oil to see if they were triggers, and I think that made me feel even worse. So here I am approaching Week 6, back on the wagon. I got the blood tests done after 4 weeks of Epi-Paleo eating, and the biggest thing to note was that my LDL cholesterol went up by 30+ points. It has freaked me out b/c I don't know if that is normal/safe/ok, or if I'm doing something wrong. I've decided to shift my protein to more fish, chicken, turkey. My functional medicine provider thought I had adrenal fatigue and had me drink electrolytes daily, which I continued to do since starting Epi-Paleo Rx. The brand I use (Ultima) has Stevia as the sweetener. I watch the sun as soon as I wake up and put on blue-light blockers at sunset. I try to get out in the sun during the day as much as possible.

Regarding my C-Reactive Protein results: My doctor order CRP (not HS-CRP), and I'm curious how big a difference does this make. Both reflect the level of inflammation in the body. My CRP level went from 5.25 in 9/2022 to 2.3 in 5/2023.

My TSH levels are fluctuating. In one test it's 3+ (3/2023) and 2 months later, it's 1.55 (5/2023). How do I get this to stabilize? (The T3 and T4 tests do not seem to fluctuate much.)

My Vitamin B12 and Folate levels seem really high. B12=1176 and Folate is >20. What would cause this?

There are probably more details to share, but I'm not quite sure what is pertinent and what isn't. I don't eat any grains or refined sugars at all, so my diet is protein and veggies (avoiding carbs), and I'm still not losing weight. It seems like the inflammation is going down quite a bit, though, so that's something good. I take a few supplements daily: Glutathione, aloe juice, a prenatal multivitamin (i'm still breastfeeding), Vitamin D3+K2, Omega-3s, Zinc picolinate, Magnesium, and the electrolytes.

So here is the data from my May 2023 blood draw:

# Thread: Endo/ Adeno and gut issues in Ottawa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endo-adeno-and-gut-issues-in-ottawa.27810/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leaf — 2023-06-13T22:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endo-adeno-and-gut-issues-in-ottawa.27810/post-322370>

Hi, I'm excited to join the health revolution. Thanks Dr Jack Kruse for your curiosity. I've been diving deep into your podcasts and each is filled with so much information.

My journey: riddled with gut issues all my life; Premature birth by a few weeks (no finger prints). The Montreal paediatrician put a sugar cap on me and I was in an incubator. Breast fed, but bad gut as a young child and then hospitalized and given antibiotics while in hospital due to incessant vomiting around age 5. I used to vomit a lot during my youth. (I had temper tantrums as a kid. I always had cold clammy hands and feet and was always cold.) Every winter as a child I would get sick with a cold and insomnia. Diet was filled with AM sugary cereals as a kid but apart from that ate meat and local veg. I played outdoors and loved sports. Sprained most body parts as a tomboy. I was a TV addict as a kid. (So interesting to know about blue light). In my teens after getting my period (age 13) with very heavy monthly bleeding I started to have mysterious pelvic pain attacks. It seems they were triggered by a fire in the house (I was looking for an acute stress point after diagnosed with endo). Pain attacks kept brining me to the emergency room. Nothing was ever discovered. I was given toradol for the pain and told to take them before the pain got too bad. Needless to say I ate them in the middle of the night before knowing I had to eat food with anti-inflammatories. (I had bad acne so was prescribed tetracycline for two years.) After uni, I started to work in travel so I was outside A LOT, hiking and biking and then designing the trips. My pain attacks disappeared and then the gut issues became more acute. I was diagnosed with H Pylori and given double antibiotics. My period was still very heavy, regular and 7 days long with acute pain for the first three days. I would have bleeding episodes when I was rushed to hospital to stop the bleeding. A uterine polyp was found. It was removed and I was put on birth control to build my iron levels. My 7 day heavy bleeding period was considered normal but numerous MDs since I was based out of London at this point and had a new GP, who said the same as my GP in Ottawa. I was also told I was anaemic but never told what to do about it. Irregular pab smears were my normal.

With time I tried giving up certain foods to see if it would help my gut pain. I gave up dairy for 10 years and finally felt better. During that time I started rhythmic breath work and after a few years my gut improved. I was living /working in NYC during the attacks so invariably ingested a lot of asbestos. I am a big cook, I attend local farmers markets wherever I am and cook. I continuously experiment with food to see what my gut will tolerate. I reintroduced dairy but found I could only tolerate hard cheeses and butter.

Five years later I was in Australia living in Melbourne. Right after arriving I stubbed my toe and lost feeling in my lower leg. My partner and family were all MDs but none told me about the neurological issues that might be happening. I looked for an osteopath. That helped a little. I had an MRI and a friend who is a neurologist told me I had a perfect 10 mm prolapse on the L5/S1 disc. I could barely walk. When I saw the neurological surgeon his compute was not working so he did a strength test on my legs and told me it would improve after 6 weeks. It took 2 years to fully get my movement back and I have been an iyengar yoga practitioner ever since. ( I am now an iyengar yoga teacher). Perimenopause started in my late 30s and by my early 40s I had insane monthly blood loss. Investigations showed three fibroids. I gave up red meat and all estrogen triggers and followed FODMAP diet to decrease their size and ease stomach issues. Stress was high as my father was diagnosed with vascular dementia. I gave up most wheat products. After 11 years I decided to return to Canada. On the way I passed through Pune to finalized yoga studies. I fell and twisted my body in a hot tub and two weeks later had my first adult pelvic pain attack. I did not connect the dots. I was told by local MDs it might be caused by perimenopause then another doctor looked for a kidney stone and did scans then another dr said it was uterine cancer and I had three growths, not fibroids. I started to haemorrhage incessantly and have utterly insane pain attacks. Dull lower pelvic grew to be normal. Three consuslting surgeons wanted to remove uterus and ovaries. I wanted to keep m uterus so pushed back. After continued acute blood loss, chemically induced menopause from Lurpon (pls don't take it) anxiety attacks post-operertively from exploratory procedures, I could no longer live trying to save my body parts. I never thought I had cancer but 5 rounds of "unknown" pathology lead all surgeons to tell me "it could become cancer, if its not already". I had my uterus removed in 2021 in the middle of covid. After far too many hospital visits during a very unpleasant period. I did get to keep my ovaries after three surgeons said it was impossible who also said I would end up with a colostomy bag. Happily I did not.

2023\. Two years post op. I started to watch the sun rise last summer with cold plunges pre 2021 surgery for the pain. Little did I realize the true benefits. Now blood work is looking funny and they are trying to figure out why my platelttes are decreasing and my white and red blood cells are low. WBC 3.7, RBC 3.65, Hct 0.345,, Platelets 1.35
Post hysterectomy I was diagnoses with endometriosis and adenomyosis. I'd like to avoid it becoming another inflammatory issue so now married to sun/grounding/water and eating local.

I hope this insight might prove helpful to others. My key findings- breath work, mediation and yoga keep my calm and my mind from going off the rails. Morning dawn walks before surgery with acute nausea from the estrogen excess seemed to have done more for me than I knew at the time.

### Leaf — 2023-06-16T00:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endo-adeno-and-gut-issues-in-ottawa.27810/post-322451>

Update 27 days into Leptin Px.
When I started I was on 200 mg of progesterone, 700 of mag citrate and glycenate, NAC 500 mg, Vit C 2000 mg, Vit D 4000 ui, Choline 3000, and a mixture of herb tinctures: SJW, Withania, and Black Cohosh. I stoppped all of it. I restarted with Magnesium 200mg and MB (4 drops) and Vit E in MTC oil and Vit K. Pain in my lower lymph node has returned after not feeling any pain for 1. 5 years. Slightly disconcerting. Anyone else had experience with Endo/Ando and had return of pain while starting the Leptin Px?

# Thread: Hashimoto’s, Alopecia Universalis, Autoimmune Oophoritis (Premature Ovarian Failure)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashimoto%E2%80%99s-alopecia-universalis-autoimmune-oophoritis-premature-ovarian-failure.27954/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### juliemarie — 2023-07-13T07:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashimoto%E2%80%99s-alopecia-universalis-autoimmune-oophoritis-premature-ovarian-failure.27954/post-323638>

Hi friends! I am new and am happy to be here. My name is Julie and I am 32 years old and have 3 autoimmune diseases… I was born and raised in San Diego, CA and still live here today!

I was diagnosed with Hashimoto’s when I was 10/11 years old which is really young apparently! Had a couple of small bald spots around this time but they cleared up on their own and I was never concerned... Around 12 years old I noticed that I sprouted a VERY large amount (hundreds) of cherry angiomas on my body… I’ve heard that can be a bromine issue which would make sense because our jacuzzi used bromine as its cleaning agent. Had pretty good health until I was 19… I came back from a trip to Argentina and had the flu for a month. When it finally cleared up, my menstrual cycle stopped and my hair started falling out again. A year later I was completely bald and diagnosed with alopecia universalis and autoimmune oophoritis aka premature ovarian failure/primary ovarian insufficiency. So I pretty much went into menopause at 19 and the effect of having no estrogen has been so rough. Horrible anxiety, vaginal pain/atrophy, weak bones. I took Estrace + Prometrium for about 10 years and felt great… now that I’m in a place of wanting to get to the root cause of my issues, I have stopped taking the hormones. I still take Armour Thyroid. Naturopathic physician wants me on LDN, but I haven’t decided how I feel about that yet… sometimes the shame and agony over having alopecia is so strong that I just want to take whatever I’m given to not be disfigured but I’m not giving up yet.

San Diego has lots of sun right now and I’ve felt confident that I’m in a good place latitude-wise but after watching July’s Q+A I’m starting to think Dr. Kruse would want me in Costa Rica  I’m pretty diligent about getting up for sunrise but need to be better about getting my skin in the game every single day because I don’t. I‘m pretty tan but my vitamin D level is only 24 ng/ml so I know I have major work to do. Is that going to be helpful though if I’m living in CA and there are EMFs everywhere? I was so confident that watching sunrise and nailing down a perfect sun routine and grounding in San Diego would be the answer for me but after watching July’s Q+A I’m afraid that just being in California will make all of my efforts pointless even if I’m outside 8 hours a day! Am I doomed just by being in California?

I’m starting back on an AIP/grain elimination diet because I’ve read past blogs where Dr, Kruse has stated that if you have alopecia you should be off all grains… is there any other information that I could provide about my history that would be helpful? I was on Doxycycline for 6 months in high school for acne when I didn’t know better… also received the Gardasil shots in high school…

Thank you for taking the time to read this… I hope it wasn’t all over the place and I made a little bit of sense.
