# Thread: Barbara's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barbaras-journal.27963/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barbara Mac — 2023-07-14T19:42:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barbaras-journal.27963/post-323752>

Hello, everyone! I’m new and happy to have found this site and all of you. I’m 59 years old and feeling the best I can remember since I was a little kid. For most of my adult life I have been overweight, more than 50 pounds over most of the time. I would lose a few pounds by sheer effort and deprivation, then gain it all back. Yet always trying to what we’re all told to do: move more, eat less, eat low fat, no saturated fat, use heart-healthy oils, eat plenty of fruits and vegetables, stay out of the sun, use sunscreen. Even all those years ago, none of that seemed right on some level, but I didn’t look deeper until about 5 years ago.

A combination of stress, menopause and some other issues that happened in 2018 caused me to have some kind of complete system-wide crash. All at the same time, I developed severe scalp psoriasis with an overlying seborrheic dermatitis, lichen sclerosis, SIBO, my hair started falling out, I had a yeast infection and severe itching in the peri-anal region. And the skin on my forearms was so fragile it would rip off if I brushed against anything too hard and my arms were always covered with purpura. My sex hormones tanked to the point that sex was horribly painful and I had zero libido.

So I go to the dermatologist for the psoriasis/dermatitis/purpura. “There is no cure, here’s some steroids. Stay out of the sun” Then I go to the gynecologist for the lichen sclerosis/hormone issue, “It just happens with age, here’s some steroids.” I asked both of them, could these symptoms be related? “Oh, no, they’re not related.” I go to other doctors for other issues but the main takeaway I get is pretty much the same. “We don’t know what causes it, (or age causes it, or the sun causes it) but there is no cure.” Really?? Something caused it because I wasn’t born with it. And if you can’t even figure out what causes it, how the hell do you know there is no cure? They're not related but you give me the same treatment for them – steroids. How is that supposed to work? To me, this made absolutely no sense. I was very angry and I was determined to do everything in my power to get a better answer and not lose hope.

Even as a kid, it seemed to me we should be just like any other animal on the planet. The sun doesn’t hurt wild animals, why would it hurt us? Animals in the wild eating their natural diet don’t get fat. Why do humans? These questions, along with my refusal to accept stupid answers from my doctors, led me on a long journey of doing massive amounts of research. My scalp psoriasis was so bad I was a walking snow globe and as someone who always loved wearing dark colors, I had to completely change my wardrobe. Everything I bought had to contain a wild pattern with flecks of white so the flakes wouldn’t show. I had to be careful at work because all the conference room chairs were black and I would leave a blanket of snow behind. It was horribly embarrassing, but even more devastating was being told there was no cure. Even with the steroids, I still had severe itching and wanted to rip my hair out of my head ( what was left of it.)

Over the course of last few years, I did a lot of research. First I learned how to read and analyze journal articles and when it comes to nutrition research, I learned that most of it is worthless. That was not the answer. Then I read hundreds of blogs, listened to a crap-ton of podcasts and I started to question everything I had ever learned. I finally landed on Dr. Kruse. Now that made sense! I always wanted to know how things work in the body and why. Why did I always feel better when I went to the beach, got out in the sun and lay on the sand? Now I know—it’s what we’re supposed do, it’s how we evolved.

I tried a lot of different things over the past five years but it wasn’t until about a year and a half ago that I pretty much incorporated all of these protocols: Sun exposure, a.m. and p.m, grounding, blue light blockers, CT, Epi-Paleo diet, nnEMF mitigation. (When it comes to seeing the sunrise, up until a year and a half ago, I don’t think I had ever seen more than a couple of dozen sunrises in my life!)

Current status: Psoriasis, seborrheic dermatitis, lichen sclerosis, (gone since Jan 2023) SIBO and peri-anal itching, (gone for over a year). I lost 30 pounds without even having to try, only need to lose about 10 more. I can now tan, whereas before I always burned, and for the last few weeks, I’ve noticed the skin on my forearms seems better, so doing the very thing my dermatologist told me not to do, is apparently healing that as well! I used to be on thyroid meds (Synthroid and Cytomel) for hypothyroidism and weaned off that as of April. (My doctors tried to discourage me but I insisted.) I noticed many other benefits as well, joint pain and fatigue gone, overall happy mood, etc.

I think for me, the biggest change came in January of this year when I stopped eating all fruits and vegetables, I just ate meat, fish, eggs and the occasional poultry for a while. About 4 to 6 weeks after doing that, I noticed the psoriasis had just completely disappeared. I will now eat a few blueberries or blackberries in summer (they happen to grow in my backyard) and I eat some vegetables, but if it won’t grow at my latitude, I don’t eat it.

I know this is long-winded, but I feel it’s important to give others hope and to encourage you not to give up, and especially not to let doctors or other health care providers try to take hope away from you. Remember, unless you are a cadaver, there is always hope!

Also, I want to say thank you, Dr. Kruse! I admire your willingness to teach others and the amount of time you devote to it. I am truly grateful!

### Sandra — 2024-02-29T23:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barbaras-journal.27963/post-332081>

Thank you for sharing this! I hope all continues to go well for you.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-03-01T15:45:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barbaras-journal.27963/post-332098>

Fantastic post, glad Sandra bumped it. What type of nnEMF mitigation and CT did you incorporate?

Very inspiring in any case, congratulations.

### Barbara Mac — 2025-10-10T22:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barbaras-journal.27963/post-360848>

> [doctorwu85 said:](/goto/post?id=332098)
>
> Fantastic post, glad Sandra bumped it. What type of nnEMF mitigation and CT did you incorporate?
>
> Very inspiring in any case, congratulations.

For CT, I do cold showers or ice packs in summer about 3 times per week and don't wear a coat when I walk the dog in the winter. For nnEMF's mitigation, I do everything I can think of. I got rid of all LED's and fluorescent bulbs at home, keep all electronics unplugged. I don't have internet or WiFi at home and keep my phone on airplane mode most of the time. I also stay outside most of the time, rather than indoors, and keep as far away from buildings, powerlines, cell towers, anything. I found a place to go camping where I don't even get phone reception so we do that a lot.

I didn't realize its been over 2 years since I posted, but I've since lost the last 10 pounds and my eyesight has improved to the point that I see better than 20/20 and no longer need glasses for distance or closeup. If I have to be indoors for any reason, I always have blue blockers on. I am amazed at the body's ability to heal itself if you just stop doing the wrong thing.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Mariana's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marianas-journal.28009/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Awakenotwoke — 2023-07-25T00:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/marianas-journal.28009/post-324267>

I'm Mariana, (the one on the white shirt). I'm a very young 50 y.o. in body and spirit. I've always been long, lean and small boned but also super sporty since childhood. No dolls for me, pass me a ball/raquet! Always prone to colds. tonsils out at 9 y.o. Bronchitis here and there as a child. Odd fact: I don't get fevers. If I do, it's straight to 102-104 but that's only happened 2 or 3x in my life. My body doesn't seem to be able to create its own heat much.
My father got Parkinsons disease at around 50 y.o. and died at 72.
My mother is alive at 90 but had a stroke after the 4th jab even when I warned her not to get any. She's now at death's door.
My ancestors were Europeans who moved to Peru in the 1800's before WW1. At 17 right after high school I moved to L.A., then Maui where I got my first pair of sunglasses at 21 y.o., then Geneva and Lausanne (Switzerland) and now Florida. I spent every summer growing up on the beach near Lima. No sunscreen nor sunglasses. Looking now into living winters in E.S. or maybe Yucatan or CR. To be decided soon.
My issue started in 21 after ppl started getting the jab. I get symptoms being around them. I had to close my store of 11 years and stay home mostly. I discovered CDS, MMS and Ivermectin which got me through 2021. The Iverm stopped working and I discovered Fenbendazole which got me through 2022. Then I discovered Jack and that's made my Fenben uptake go waaay down but still 1 or 2x week.
I'm gluten intolerant and have mega senses, In every way. I've had perfect vision until 2 years ago. I can hear, feel, sense, smell and taste abnormally well but I am highly intuitive so it may be related bc I can download knowledge.

At around 10 my dog died and my parents had me get 15 rabies injections in 15 days at the urging of the doctor. Back then jabs were not as lethal as today but well, not good. They were Lilly brand I remember. I had many silver fillings removed in 2008 and I think my proneness to colds got worse and worse.

6 years ago I went hyper thyroid, went to a functional med doc and after 6 weeks of Tapazole I returned to normal. Got corrected with vitamins/minerals and herbs and never had to take drugs again.
I've been naturally allergic to toxins. I used to be a vegetarian but when I came back to the States in 03 GMOs had been in the american table for years and so I became allergic to soy, tempeh, gluten, oats so I started eating meat as remaining a veggie was impossible then.
I have not used fluoride toothpaste nor alu deodorants or alu foil or pots and pans since my Maui days at 21. Never used creams on my skin (only face). Never drank alcohol nor coffee. Eaten organic since 21 so my body has been very clean for a while now.

I love staying up late. My mind is so clear at night, I'm like a kid who doesn't want to go to bed. That all ended upon finding Jack. Before that I was going to bed at 5am because I was working with factories in China on prototypes so I was blue light toxic and even when I was done w China I couldn't turn my schedule. Once I bought blue blockers and minded the blue light I was finally able to turn around my sleep. Still, it's hard bc I'm used to eating well at night and eating at 6 pm feels wrong. I'm often distracted and don't eat til 8 or 9pm. I digest well so it never keeps me awake but going to bed at midnight and waking up at 645 since I started 3 months ago.
Waking up early and heading to the beach daily has changed my life. I have more energy and my body looks young now. Trudging def helps. It's the perfect workout, I haven't stepped in a gym since 2020. Can't be in a space with the jabd and handle it well so not an option. No need really. I can't eat that much protein. I'm an A blood type and I need to eat a good amount of food. I burn calories.

Because I can feel energy I started my own EMF protection company once I closed my holistic pet boutique. [www.emffortress.com](http://www.emffortress.com) It's allowed me to work from home as I can't be in daily contact with the 60% who went for it. I will have a headache within 3 hours of being next to one jabd person.

Life is hard from the perspective that my immune system appears to be on strike, but through my potions I have not had full on cvid at all.

Thank you for taking the time to read and any suggestions or help would be highly appreciated!

It's allowed me to work from home as I can't be in daily contact with the 60% who went for it. I will have a headache within 3 hours of being next to one jabd person.

Been unable to swallow solids for 18 months, 30 lbs weight loss, initially diagnosed as GERD but multiple PPI's had no effect, recent gastroscopy showed no evidence of acid damage, biopsies negative for EoE and H. Pylori. I am now having trouble swallowing liquids as well. Ultrasound, Neck CT no signs of tumours/cancer.

Gastro suspects Achalasia which is not very encouraging. Will be getting an esophageal manometry this month to confirm.

43rd latitude, previously heavy tech user. Went on a tech diet, got morning sun and + 1~ hour of afternoon sun for the last three months. No ALAN + blue blockers 2 hours before bed.

D level 50 ng/ml, decent tan.

Diet is blended fish, chicken + seasonal veggies. I am eating lots of ice cream recently as I need to keep weight on and I don't choke on it but now my LDL and Cholesterol levels are rising. Total CHOL: 242 mg/dL HDL: 75 mg/dL LDL 153 mg/dl Triglycerides: 79 mg/dL

Any idea if this is related to melanin?

# Thread: Sunhunter's journal to happiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SunHunter ☀️ — 2023-08-12T15:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-324980>

Dear black swans,

Let me tell you my story. I am female, 50 years and live in Germany.

### **How I found Dr. Kruse**​

After eight years of being a fairly happy carnivore, I became increasingly dissatisfied with my health. My weight kept creeping up and then was higher than it had ever been. The other illnesses also began to come back. Then hot flashes came along. So I started looking for explanations on the Internet again. I came across Sarah Kleiner and her leptin reset course. There she also mentioned Jack Kruse.

### **Mother /Grandmother History**​

My grandmother died at 95. She was in pretty good health. Her problem issues were heart, pancreas and rheumatism. Too low blood pressure and heart weakness. She always complained of a dry mouth. She was active and had a bright and curious mind until high age and she was full of thankfulness.

My great-grandmother died of parotid glands cancer.

My mother is 83 and also in pretty good shape. She has osteoporosis, rheumatism, high blood pressure. Maybe also heart weakness, but undiagnosed cause she stays away from the doctors. She ist still interested in life and lives on her own, doing gardening.
In her twenties/thirties she worked in a pharma chemical lab with lots of toxic substances. She stopped shortly before I was born. That’s maybe where my autism comes from?

### **Prior to discovering Dr. Kruse**​

I got my first mobile phone in 2000 when I was 28 and since then I have been in blue light and EMFs 24/7. I put aside all warnings because they seemed esoteric bullsh\*t.

Throughout my early years I never ate crap or fast food. My parents were really invested into vegetarian real food. We ate heaps of self made goat cheese, butter, eggs and vegetables from our garden. With 32 years I discovered low carb and sticked with that until I was 42.

#### Carnivore​

Then I tried carnivore and had immense health wins with that (Lost 17kg, more energy, improved focus and mood, no acne, no dandruff any more, less wrinkles), so I stayed 90% carnivore until this spring.

After 2-3 years into carnivore, my weight slowly kept creeping up and also some of my former health issues returned. In 2020 I got Thyreoditis de Quervain which is thought to be a rare autoimmune disease of the thyroid. I thought to be quite healthy, never catching a cold, not taking any meds, low blood pressure, low blood sugar, but I had to admit that it was not optimal. I don’t have a lot of lab data because I almost never went to the doc. When I got hot flashes last summer I started researching the internet to find explanations and a cure.

I first started with a very high fat and very strict carnivore diet, eating 120-150g fat/day. That got me rid of the hot flashes, but I gained 7kg during 4 months in autumn and this extreme diet also led to heart palpitations and leg cramps. I added magnesium and potassium which helped a bit.

#### Iodine protocol​

Then I tried the Iodine protocol with 2-8 drops of Lugol’s daily. I think that was a huge mistake. I did this 5 months during winter and spring, until I came across Dr. Kruse mentioning that Lugol’s is not good. I had a lot of bromoderma (kind of acne caused by bromine detox) no matter how slow I went with increasing the iodine. I also gained another 7 kg during that time. I stopped Lugol’s in May but I still have this bromoderma with new breakouts almost daily.

### **Working the Protocols starting Nov 2022**​

Parallel to the high fat and the iodine protocol, I tried to follow the leptin reset protocol from Sarah Kleiner (I think she got most of it from Dr. Kruse) with eliminating nnEMFs, reducing blue light, seeing the sunrise, being more outside, eating a big breakfast and early dinner.

Plus CT in the lake with temperatures between 3 and 12 degrees C (37 - 55 F) twice a week for 2-7 minutes.

I also started to read the blogs and listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts. It was hard to hear about the benefits of sunlight being locked in a German winter! In my despair I bought a red light panel and a UV lamp.

Since spring I am hunting the sun. Whenever there is a 15min sunny slot between the clouds I jump outside and throw off my clothes. That’s why I choose my name, Sunhunter .

I am pretty tanned and outside most of the time. I also still swim in the lake, but of course the water is warmer now in the summer.

So while doing the iodine protocol, I also started the leptin rx during winter. And while my energy and mood improved A LOT, I still kept gaining weight. Why is that? Is it because of the Lugol’s?

My weight has stayed stable at 80kgs since spring. At least I am not gaining any more. But how can I start losing? No matter what I do, the weight is stubborn.

I have an EMF meter and measured and checked my environment thoroughly. We still have the WIFI on during day, but I use ethernet cables 98% of the time and I stay far away from the WIFI router. I live on the edge of a small town with a population of 20k. We also shut down the circuits in the bedrooms during night since a couple of weeks. My sleep has gotten better, and also my HRV has increased.

I added some veggies and carbs back into my diet, because it’s summer time. Most of my food is regional and seasonal, with some exceptions like lemons, coffee, occasional almonds.

I threw out the microwave and things like xylitol.

### **Labs** May 2023​

CRP 0.1
TSH 2.92 (maybe still from the Thyroiditis de Quervain)
Glucose 107 (It’s always high in the morning and then drops below 85 during the whole day, even after my keto meals)
Triglycerides 59.7
HDL 50
LDL 243 (Maybe from vitamin d supplements? I stopped them in March, 2 months before the labs)
Vitamin D 44.8
hbA1c 29.7
haemoglobin-A1C 4.87%

### **Books read so far**​

Power, Sex, Suicide - Nick Lane
Fourth phase of water - Dr. Gerald Pollack
Cells, gels and the engines of life - Dr. Gerald Pollackcomp
Health & Light - John N.Ott
Epi Paleo Rx - Dr Jack Kruse
The Body Electric - Robert O Becker MD

### **Health Wins**​

I have always been a night owl, but after some days of seeing the sunrise I was a completely different person. Jumping out of the bed with energy and great mood! Never need any breaks any more.

My memory improved. Still not at the same level as Dr. Kruse

My focus, concentration, ability to get in the flow and just do the work improved a lot.

I am a much happier person, simply enjoying life most of the time.

My myopia gets better whenever I am in the sun for a couple of days, but is back again after some rainy days where I am mostly inside.

I am less cold (compared to before and compared to other people), but still cold adaptation is a long way to go for me I think.

My hot flashes came back in spring but they have mostly vanished again.

I am menopausal but don’t suffer from all the typical things.

### Biggest Questions​

1.  What is causing the bromoderma /acne and how can I stop it?
2.  Why can’t I lose weight? It seems I am missing a huge thing.

Thanks for reading!

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-12T18:29:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-324985>

Hi Sunhunter

In terms of the acne/bromoderma link, I'd first be looking at body water ... Where do you think the bromine is coming from or came from? How was it diagnosed as bromoderma acne? Its a halogen, as is fluoride, (fluoroderma?) which also displaces iodine, and iodine is needed for the hydrogen bonding networks to make exclusion zone water in the body, affecting redox, and its redox before detox. So auditing of your current intake of the halogens bromine, chlorine and fluoride could be a first step. So tap water, fluoride toothpastes and mouthwashes etc. Aluminium is another one - on its own its bad enough, but as a tag team with fluoride its worse, particularly if the blood brain barrier is weakened by nnEMF. Fluoride is implicated in thyroid problems and (your Mum link) osteoporosis. Aluminium reflects light, slowing down reactions and possibly interfering with the ability to make vit D in the skin. Aluminium and fluoride are both implicated in symptoms associated with autism.

The skin is also an elimination route for aluminium, along with the urine and the gut. It is better to be in the skin (which sloughs off, or can be removed via sweat) than the brain. Silica is the way that the body eliminates aluminium, and that's through silica rich plant foods or silica rich water, which if you were full on carnivore for several years, might have slowly led to accumulation in the body's tissues of even modest amounts. Even normal tissue or bone turnover can release it back into the circulation. In terms of the lugols, if the iodine is causing a dumping of halogens into the system, I believe in theory it could come out of one place and finish up somewhere else (a secondary pathway) if the primary detox pathway for a toxin isn't working well, which is a good thing if it protects the brain or vital organs.

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-12T21:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-324989>

> [SunHunter ☀️ said:](/goto/post?id=324988)
>
> Hi [@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/), thanks for your thoughts. The bromoderma came with the Lugol's, so I guess it is bromoderma as this is a well-known side effect of Lugol's. It is thought to be a detox sign for bromine or other toxins. Yes, I also thought that maybe I'm full of toxins since birth.
>
> Aluminium accumulation because of no plants in my diet: Good hint, never heard of that.
>
> I'm just wondering why the bromoderma detoxing thing still happens although I stopped iodine intake 5 months ago.

This is an overview of silica - there's a small section on acne. The thing with aluminium is that silica in the diet or water takes out aluminium, so people think of it as silica takes out aluminium, but then I look at it as in the absence of silica from food or water, exposure to aluminium will take out silica, affecting collagen, including that of the skin, added to the reduction of silica thought to be part of aging. In terms of removing systemic aluminium, (for autism and AD for example) supplements of things like horsetail are much less effective than silica rich mineral water. The UK has no sources of natural silica rich water, so I use imported water for regular detoxing because of my risk of AD (mother and maternal grandmother). In Germany there are several waters that are good sources. I can give you the list if you like.

### [Health Benefits of Silica](https://www.healthaid.co.uk/blogs/news/health-benefits-silica#:~:text=Acne,and%20rebuilding%20the%20connective%20tissues.)

Health Benefits of Silica, bamboo extract, silica for hair loss, silica for skin.

 www.healthaid.co.uk

_“What we have found in clinical trials, involving both healthy individuals and individuals with disease, is that drinking around a litre of silicon-rich mineral water every day can speed up the removal of toxic aluminium from the body via the kidneys and ultimately urine. In fact, our studies showed that individuals experienced significant reductions in their body burden of aluminium, including falls of up to 70% in one case, over a 12-week period.”_

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-13T17:59:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-325004>

> [SunHunter ☀️ said:](/goto/post?id=324993)
>
> Yes, please give me the list

Sorry this is a bit long, but having mentioned it, and to avoid unintentionally sending you down what might be the wrong rabbit hole for your context, I’ve tried to explain it in terms of mine.

[https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlepdf/2013/em/c3em00374d](https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlepdf/2013/em/c3em00374d)

Experiments done by the author of the paper to reduce body load of Al used a silicon rich water with 32 mg/L and he went on to use one at 55 mg/L. I’ve also used one with 93 mg/L. They are all imported as we have no natural sources, but Germany has several brands. The silica content on bottles is shown as X mg/L but for systemic use the key number is the amount of orthosilicic acid, (OSA), which isn’t usually shown on the bottle. But the 32mg/L I use has 50, so in terms of cost it's working out if it's cheaper to drink half a litre of a water with an OSA of 100, or drink double the amount of the 50, and a choice might also include what else is in a particular brand, and what daily minimum I’m aiming for. How much water someone uses might also depend on their Al body burden, whether they can increase dietary intake of silicon, (and some foods supply more OSA than others per 100g), what reduction in exposure they can do, and what exposure they aren’t able to avoid at this moment in time. (For example not a good idea to drop prescribed medications without medical supervision, then there’s air pollution etc). Anyway, here's the list of German waters (source Silica Water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminium age, by Dennis N Crouse, PhD).
The first figure is the silica in mg/L, the OSA figure is in brackets.

Vulkani Heilwasser 83.5 (133.6)
St Linus Heilvasser 58.9 (92.2)
Dunaris 54.9 (87.8)
Hirschquelle 53.9 (86.2)
Konig Otto Sprudel 50 (80)
Wildsber Quelle 40.9 (65.4)
Gerolsteiner 40.2 (64.3)
Teinacher 40 (64)
Tonisteiner 35.4 (56.6)
Wittenseer Quelle 33.4 (53.4)
Eifel Stil 33.3 (53.3).

Although the paper above gives an overview of some of the potential exposures to aluminium, and elimination routes, in terms of the elimination via faeces, what it misses that I think is connected to Jack’s work is that even if it goes down through the gut without going into circulation, it has the potential to interfere with the light signalling of the microbiome and in the skin its reflectance properties can interfere with absorbing light, particularly UV. My concern in terms of being a carnivore who might not be pooping every day is that I will have a microbiome/gut effect for longer than if I did, but either way any ingested or excreted in the bile could be absorbed or reabsorbed on the way down.

A carnivore such as a lion in the wild will eat the internal organs and intestines of the herbivore it eats, so will be getting a source of fermenting plant fibre and silicon, as well as the bacteria from the gut of their prey, whilst also getting more microbial diversity from their environment. The modern carnivore diet is more like a lion in a zoo and in the wild lions are not exposed to anything like the levels of aluminium that humans expose themselves to, often unknowingly.

The so called blue zone areas found in specific areas of Costa Rica, Japan, Greece, Sardinia, and in Loma Linda California, despite different diets, all share having natural silicon rich water drinking water, used to water their crops, and their traditional diets included latitude based plant foods rich in silicon. The UK has no natural sources of silicon rich mineral water, which is possibly why oats became a year round staple, along with barley, wholegrain wheat/rye bread, potatoes and/or other roots (with skins) and beer … I see it as we became the human equivalent of the larder hoarding squirrel, adding in what could be dug up or scavenged or hunted in winter as we went along, together with stored fermented foods and drinks. More microbiome diversity, less aluminium exposure and more sources of silicon.

With less historical exposure to Al, and by keeping aluminium out, I think in the past countries with definite seasons were better able to make use of the sun when it was available, and survive seasonal cold far more naturally than we do now. Although in earlier times we would have had periods with no food, and burned body fat for days, the same thing now might dump fat stored toxins into the circulation, going from a relatively safe storage site to the brain or other major organ, so water fasting is not a risk I’d take at the moment. Another reason the carnivore, high fat diets or going beyond IF to extended fasting isn’t for me is that although chronic high levels of blood sugar and insulin are bad, chronically low blood sugar and insulin would be a risk for my n=, because I’ve read that insulin degrading enzymes also degrade amyloid plaque, and my mother and maternal grandmother had dementia. Whatever benefits are seen initially to insulin sensitivity from these diets, once insulin sensitivity is restored, chronically if nothing stimulates insulin, there would be no signal from lowering blood sugar to stimulate insulin degrading hormone. So the epi paleo with seasonal carbs suits me better in terms of year round DHA, moderate IF, adding seasonal carbs in summer, but in winter I adjust winter epi paleo to suit my n=1 context.

The other reason I have for regularly using the water is because of what nnemf potentially does to a cell or system contaminated with aluminium, in terms of heat damage as well as interfering with light signalling. If the aluminium is ingested and the blood brain barrier is intact, the body may dump it in the skin, collagen cartilage or bone etc (diseases of aging like osteoporosis, vascular system problems etc), so the brain may have relative protection if anything that affects the BBB is avoided, such as a head injury. But nnemf weakens the BBB ….

### ChristineH — 2023-08-26T20:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunhunters-journal-to-happiness.28072/post-325602>

> [SunHunter ☀️ said:](/goto/post?id=324980)
>
> Dear black swans,
>
> Let me tell you my story. I am female, 50 years and live in Germany.
>
>
>
> ### **How I found Dr. Kruse**​
>
> After eight years of being a fairly happy carnivore, I became increasingly dissatisfied with my health. My weight kept creeping up and then was higher than it had ever been. The other illnesses also began to come back. Then hot flashes came along. So I started looking for explanations on the Internet again. I came across Sarah Kleiner and her leptin reset course. There she also mentioned Jack Kruse.
>
>
>
> ### **Mother /Grandmother History**​
>
> My grandmother died at 95. She was in pretty good health. Her problem issues were heart, pancreas and rheumatism. Too low blood pressure and heart weakness. She always complained of a dry mouth. She was active and had a bright and curious mind until high age and she was full of thankfulness.
>
> My great-grandmother died of parotid glands cancer.
>
> My mother is 83 and also in pretty good shape. She has osteoporosis, rheumatism, high blood pressure. Maybe also heart weakness, but undiagnosed cause she stays away from the doctors. She ist still interested in life and lives on her own, doing gardening.
> In her twenties/thirties she worked in a pharma chemical lab with lots of toxic substances. She stopped shortly before I was born. That’s maybe where my autism comes from?
>
>
> ### **Prior to discovering Dr. Kruse**​
>
> I got my first mobile phone in 2000 when I was 28 and since then I have been in blue light and EMFs 24/7. I put aside all warnings because they seemed esoteric bullsh\*t.
>
> Throughout my early years I never ate crap or fast food. My parents were really invested into vegetarian real food. We ate heaps of self made goat cheese, butter, eggs and vegetables from our garden. With 32 years I discovered low carb and sticked with that until I was 42.
>
>
> #### Carnivore​
>
> Then I tried carnivore and had immense health wins with that (Lost 17kg, more energy, improved focus and mood, no acne, no dandruff any more, less wrinkles), so I stayed 90% carnivore until this spring.
>
> After 2-3 years into carnivore, my weight slowly kept creeping up and also some of my former health issues returned. In 2020 I got Thyreoditis de Quervain which is thought to be a rare autoimmune disease of the thyroid. I thought to be quite healthy, never catching a cold, not taking any meds, low blood pressure, low blood sugar, but I had to admit that it was not optimal. I don’t have a lot of lab data because I almost never went to the doc. When I got hot flashes last summer I started researching the internet to find explanations and a cure.
>
> I first started with a very high fat and very strict carnivore diet, eating 120-150g fat/day. That got me rid of the hot flashes, but I gained 7kg during 4 months in autumn and this extreme diet also led to heart palpitations and leg cramps. I added magnesium and potassium which helped a bit.
>
>
> #### Iodine protocol​
>
> Then I tried the Iodine protocol with 2-8 drops of Lugol’s daily. I think that was a huge mistake. I did this 5 months during winter and spring, until I came across Dr. Kruse mentioning that Lugol’s is not good. I had a lot of bromoderma (kind of acne caused by bromine detox) no matter how slow I went with increasing the iodine. I also gained another 7 kg during that time. I stopped Lugol’s in May but I still have this bromoderma with new breakouts almost daily.
>
>
>
> ### **Working the Protocols starting Nov 2022**​
>
> Parallel to the high fat and the iodine protocol, I tried to follow the leptin reset protocol from Sarah Kleiner (I think she got most of it from Dr. Kruse) with eliminating nnEMFs, reducing blue light, seeing the sunrise, being more outside, eating a big breakfast and early dinner.
>
> Plus CT in the lake with temperatures between 3 and 12 degrees C (37 - 55 F) twice a week for 2-7 minutes.
>
> I also started to read the blogs and listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts. It was hard to hear about the benefits of sunlight being locked in a German winter! In my despair I bought a red light panel and a UV lamp.
>
> Since spring I am hunting the sun. Whenever there is a 15min sunny slot between the clouds I jump outside and throw off my clothes. That’s why I choose my name, Sunhunter .
>
> I am pretty tanned and outside most of the time. I also still swim in the lake, but of course the water is warmer now in the summer.
>
> So while doing the iodine protocol, I also started the leptin rx during winter. And while my energy and mood improved A LOT, I still kept gaining weight. Why is that? Is it because of the Lugol’s?
>
> My weight has stayed stable at 80kgs since spring. At least I am not gaining any more. But how can I start losing? No matter what I do, the weight is stubborn.
>
> I have an EMF meter and measured and checked my environment thoroughly. We still have the WIFI on during day, but I use ethernet cables 98% of the time and I stay far away from the WIFI router. I live on the edge of a small town with a population of 20k. We also shut down the circuits in the bedrooms during night since a couple of weeks. My sleep has gotten better, and also my HRV has increased.
>
> I added some veggies and carbs back into my diet, because it’s summer time. Most of my food is regional and seasonal, with some exceptions like lemons, coffee, occasional almonds.
>
> I threw out the microwave and things like xylitol.
>
>
>
> ### **Labs** May 2023​
>
> CRP 0.1
> TSH 2.92 (maybe still from the Thyroiditis de Quervain)
> Glucose 107 (It’s always high in the morning and then drops below 85 during the whole day, even after my keto meals)
> Triglycerides 59.7
> HDL 50
> LDL 243 (Maybe from vitamin d supplements? I stopped them in March, 2 months before the labs)
> Vitamin D 44.8
> hbA1c 29.7
> haemoglobin-A1C 4.87%
>
>
>
> ### **Books read so far**​
>
> Power, Sex, Suicide - Nick Lane
> Fourth phase of water - Dr. Gerald Pollack
> Cells, gels and the engines of life - Dr. Gerald Pollackcomp
> Health & Light - John N.Ott
> Epi Paleo Rx - Dr Jack Kruse
> The Body Electric - Robert O Becker MD
>
>
>
> ### **Health Wins**​
>
> I have always been a night owl, but after some days of seeing the sunrise I was a completely different person. Jumping out of the bed with energy and great mood! Never need any breaks any more.
>
> My memory improved. Still not at the same level as Dr. Kruse
>
> My focus, concentration, ability to get in the flow and just do the work improved a lot.
>
> I am a much happier person, simply enjoying life most of the time.
>
> My myopia gets better whenever I am in the sun for a couple of days, but is back again after some rainy days where I am mostly inside.
>
> I am less cold (compared to before and compared to other people), but still cold adaptation is a long way to go for me I think.
>
> My hot flashes came back in spring but they have mostly vanished again.
>
> I am menopausal but don’t suffer from all the typical things.
>
>
>
> ### Biggest Questions​
>
> 1.  What is causing the bromoderma /acne and how can I stop it?
> 2.  Why can’t I lose weight? It seems I am missing a huge thing.
>
>
>
> Thanks for reading!

Verstehe. Wie ich verstehe, bekommt man auch die Strahlungen wenn es bewölkt ist. Also möglichst viel Zeit draußen und in der Natur verbringen. Und viel Fisch essen, was auch Jod vom Natur her hat. Kaffee nur wenn man da ist wo die Bohnen wachsen, am Equator.

# Thread: My son's journey to health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/>

**Posts:** 9

### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T19:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325329>

Hello everyone,

I'm a new member and I'm here to help and support my son in his healing journey.

My son is 9 years old, suffered a metabolic stroke (hypoglycemia) at birth and went on to develop infantile spasms at 7 months and 14 months of age. He was successfully treated with ACTH shots both times. Along with these, came nocturnal episodes of partial seizures and nystagmus. The seizures stopped for a few years.

Right before the pandemic in 2019 he was diagnosed with cyclical neutropenia, and ever since then he's been on a cycle of illness. At first every month, and ever since he got Covid last year, every 2 weeks (and now he also gets grand mal seizures, which is new to him). We live in Queens, NY and after spending some time back home in the DR last year and noticing an improvement in his health, we have decided to move (hopefully before this fall starts).

I'm wondering if anyone here has had success treating either neutropenia or seizures. Feel free to point me to a blog post if so. I'm trying to read as much as possible.

Thank you in advance!

### Saichi — 2023-08-19T19:51:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325331>

Have you mitigated nnEMF in your home?

### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T20:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325333>

No, we're currently packing to move out to a new city within a few weeks. But I know it must be really bad (Queens, NY there must be thousands of antennas nearby)
I'm reading about how to mitigate nnEMF from my laptop etc once we're in our new space.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-08-19T20:52:09+0300

### clunaluna — 2023-08-19T22:14:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325344>

### Sue-UK — 2023-08-20T11:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sons-journey-to-health.28099/post-325353>

Apart from cleaning up the nnemf environment it might be worth checking out aluminum exposure. I have a book (Silica water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminum age by Dennis D Crouse) and there’s a section on seizures, giving quite a few references to research carried out that shows seizures can be induced by aluminum, as well as several anecdotal reports from parents seeing reductions in seizures from using silica rich mineral water. If you think of what happens if you put aluminum foil in a microwave, it may be worth considering if aluminum is a factor in the improvements seen when you spent time in a lower nnemf environment and where exposure from things like air pollution were probably reduced too. Although it's in childhood vaccines as an adjuvant, there are also many other exposure routes, from meds, foods, cooking methods (cooking food in aluminum foil being one of the worst). Some exposure routes are worse than others, for example IIRC there’s no blood brain barrier at the olfactory bulb, and in any event nnEMF weakens the BBB.

Chris Exley is the UK scientist who has researched aluminum for years (I have his book, and read a lot of his research) but I’ve picked this report of a case study which notes him in the references, because the case involved seizures.
[https://www.researchgate.net/public...\_with\_silica-rich\_mineral\_water\_A\_case\_report](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/334676388_Childhood_neuro-motor_disorders_treated_with_silica-rich_mineral_water_A_case_report)

Although they did an audit of potential exposures, I would suspect this to be way underestimated, and doesn’t account for the child’s environment for exposure or in terms of nnEMF.

Some exposure routes are worse than others, for example IIRC there’s no blood brain barrier at the olfactory bulb, and in any event nnEMF weakens the BBB.
>

Thank you so much, I'm definitely looking into this study as well as the book you recommended. Last year I started clearing out cookware that was deemed toxic as well as the aluminum foil in cooking which I did use for a while. Also stopped using the microwave a few years ago. Thanks again for sharing, I definitely have tons to learn. Happy Sunday!

The Pediatric Feeding Disorders Program treats the full spectrum of feeding disorders and works with children with behavior-related feeding issues. Services include an outpatient program, a day program and an inpatient program.

# Thread: Moose Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### enyaw — 2023-10-08T19:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-327106>

I'm a 43 yo male that lives just shy of 43N near Grand Rapids, MI with a H5 maternal haplotype. I've been a forum lurker since I started reading the blogs in 2012. I've dabbled in some of the protocols, but ultimately did not need them at the time (thought I still saw benefits). I had a consult with Jack in 2017 and the key takeaway was that my timing was off. Wouldn't you know it, Jack was right. After a few months of fixing my timing, I was back to where I needed to be in regard to weight and energy levels. I suppose I'll begin my story there.

In 2017 I fixed my timing issues and I was as healthy and felt as great as I have in my adult life. I was 36. I'm 5'8" and weighed around 160 lbs; a weight I had no problems maintaining. Fast forward to October 2020. In those three years, without much change to diet and exercise, I gained 30 lbs. It worked out to be almost exactly 10 lbs per year for three years. I then gained 5 lbs per year for two years, putting me at my current weight at 200. My energy levels are a lot lower, my cognitive ability can best be described as brain fog. In 2021 I passed a kidney stone. I got thrush. I went to a functional medicine doctor in Sept 2021 because my PCP was uninterested in determining why I gained so much weight and lost so much energy in such a short amount of time. They took labs, provided a consult from a dietician, then "prescribed" a bunch of supplements. Some of the lab work was interesting. More on that later.

In 2023 I went to a determatologist to treat athlete's foot. I think I had it for a few years (I thought it was dry feet because OTC meds didn't change anything). It looks like it's healed but the soles of my feet still have an easy ability to peel (dermatologist said a sort of dermatitis and prescribed a steroid cream). I had a yeast growing on my scalp and on my face (t-zone) that I just recently cleared up with a cream from the same dermatologist (for years I thought it was just dry skin; same with feet). In April I had an inguinal hernia repaired. I'm not sure if this relates to everything else.

Labs: I've had high cholesterol (200-300+) since my 20s. HDL has always been in the normal range, but below 60. Vitamin D has always been on the low end of normal (30-40s ng/mL), but I think I've always tested in Autumn. (I think D levels can fall relatively quickly, so I could have had better levels in the middle of summer, but I haven't tested that yet.) My ALT was high last year, but has slooooooooowly been falling. Still high though. My BUN/Creatinine ratio is 11.7 (as of two days ago), but has bounced between 10 and 17 over the last ten or so years. In 2021, the functional doctor labs did show h pylori and some elevated numbers (more than just the usually high cholesterol). hsCRP was good (0.7 mg/L), but my Secretory IgA was low and my Anti-gliadin IgA was at the very low end of the range (43 on a 0-157 scale). The doctor thought this may be indicative of a chronic stress response. I have more numbers and pretty charts if anyone is interested, but my inability to lose weight combined with the ease of putting it on and my lessened cognitive ability are more concerning to me.

I currently eat an epi-paleo diet maybe 70% of the time. I should eat more seafood, especially with winter coming. I have been good about only eating when the sun is up, finishing before 7p (usually closer to 6p), and no snacking. I get morning sunlight in my eyes, though I cannot see the sun as it crests over the horizon. I sit on my front porch and look into the sky, even on rainy days. I may install a radiant heater to make it more bearable when it's 18 degrees and windy outside.

I do spend quite a few hours a day looking at a computer screen, though not all 8 hours of the work day. I use a thermabulb (IR) and two duracell black CFL bulbs (UV-A) while working. I also have daytime blue blockers (TruDark) but don't wear them as often as I should. I also turn on two UV-B reptile bulbs midday. I have a Sperti red and I'm thinking about using it this winter, especially on my gut. I may also use it on my children. (Maybe more about them in subsequent posts.)

I'm unsure if my current health issues have an underlying cause that is concerning or if I'm merely over weight and slower thinking because my timing is still off. I have really struggled in the last couple of years with maintaining a regular bedtime, but I've been better about it this last month now that the kids are in school and I made a semi-firm resolution to do so.

### enyaw — 2023-10-08T19:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-327107>

Going into winter in Michigan, what should be my goal for timing my day? I am unable to obtain optimal while my kids are in school. We rise between 0600 - 0630 but the sun doesn't rise until anywhere between 0730 (currently) and 0830 (Dec 21). The sun sets early, but that's not a huge issue for us. We don't watch much tv (maybe a football game on Sunday afternoon) and the kids have zero devices. I can turn mine off by 9p at the latest, but I try to sooner. Would it be best to wake up at the same time everyday (~0600) even though it is an hour or two before sunrise? Fasting until the sun comes up seems like it'd be a good idea in that case.

Answer, carbs that contain potassium.

There is another reason why we need carbs. Randle Cycle.

\----------
Now, speaking of cortisol.
There is a Randle Cycle.
In the end of its discussion the conclusion is that:

• The Randle cycle refers to an antagonism between glucose and fats as fuel, which explains why you can become insulin resistant when you avoid carbohydrates

• If you’re in chronic ketosis, your tissues decrease their insulin sensitivity, which can lead to or contribute to insulin resistance

• A low-carb diet is best implemented as a temporary strategy to restore metabolic flexibility. Once metabolic flexibility is regained, adding more carbs back in will help to lower cortisol, which is highly inflammatory
\-----------------------------------------

After eating all those carbs

### doctorwu85 — 2024-03-14T01:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moose-journal.28230/post-332523>

I think it's easy to go down a paralysis-by-analysis rabbit hole with labs, especially if you're seeing a functional medicine doc. They can be useful, but it's also easy to over-analyze specific results, and the doc will invariably recommend a bunch of supplements that are unlikely to address the root cause of your problems. They're not pushing you into the same Big Pharma sinkhole as allopathic doctors, but remember they have to make a buck too.

You seem to have a number of simultaneous issues, which suggests some sort of systemic problem with redox, hormone levels, circadian rhythms, etc. Have you tried doing the strict Leptin RX for 6 weeks? How careful are you about blocking blue light at night? Are you using artificial light at night in general? Uncle Jack has mentioned on many podcasts that biology is non-linear, and you could be doing 9 things pretty well but it's the 10th that jumpstarts your redox and associated issues in the right direction.

Another thing -- Anthony mentioned this earlier in the thread but I didn't see a reply. What is your EMF environment like? EMFs have been shown to produce a chronic stress response and mess with cortisol (see the last 100 pages or so of Robert O Becker's book), as well as significantly raise triglyceride levels. I would bet that this is part of your problem.

# Thread: Cold and hour a day, living in the sun, skin improving
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-hour-a-day-living-in-the-sun-skin-improving.28288/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2023-10-31T22:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-hour-a-day-living-in-the-sun-skin-improving.28288/post-327735>

It’s my last month in California. Soon I’ll return back to my home in Costa Rica. The hardest part of leaving is that my husband chooses to stay in California.

I’m maximizing my cold exposure (and sun) with long exposures of floating and swimming in the ocean and sleeping with just a flannel sheet covering me. It feels like my blood is made of coolant. I can surf without a wetsuit for an hour too without getting cold. But I’m the only one I know that’s doing this. Most everyone around me except my husband is pale. But this is also a similar experience for me in Costa Rica. I hope to find my peeps.

Yesterday I had a dexa scan. My fat levels increased from 19% to 26% over last year and my bone density dropped from 1.3 to 1. I wonder if this test is a centralized scam because perhaps my WAT changed to BAT and it’s all good?

I don’t understand the bone mineral loss. Perhaps this is just a variation in testing and insignificant? My skin has become better at healing the “precancerous “ swollen and tender solar keratosis and atrophic skin. So I’m seeing overall progress at age 58.

Grateful for Jack and our tribe.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-hour-a-day-living-in-the-sun-skin-improving.28288/post-327736>

My fat levels increased from 19% to 26% over last year
\====================================

Something is not right.
Unless your avatar picture is of someone else.
Even if that picture is one year old.
You look much less than 19% fat.
Check your BMI compare with the desirable.
But you look like you are well within where you are supposed to be.
\===============
Since you are 59yo, so more than 35 or 40 yo,
You may want to try NO2U.com lozenges.
If that makes a difference that you could easily recognize (after about month of their use), then you would likely keep using them and additionally use about 10mg/day of tadalafil.
Overall, that should significantly improve your blood circulation.
But from looking at you and the way you describe yourself, just enjoy it.

### Phil — 2023-11-13T05:35:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-328219>

Accidentally just deleted my entire journal on here. Thought I was just deleting one post that I thought was a little irrelevant.

I just got done upgrading the plastic on my greenhouse to UV open greenhouse plastic. Most greenhouse plastic has additives to block UV so it’s possible the old plastic was blocking more UVA than even my windows. I also used this plastic to build window inserts for a window in every room of my house.

In 2017 I had just made a lot of money trading shit coins and quite my shit corporate desk job on the 25th floor downtown that was killing me. ### Phil — 2023-11-29T19:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-328779>

370nm UV A spotlight. Got a waveform 365 spotlight, the peak light output is 368 so pretty much 370. There is no flicker on these lights. You can hardly even tell this light is on during the day because most of the light isn’t visible. I also have tried out the 395 waveform spotlight but it kicks out a lot more visible light and is kind of annoying on the eyes. I know the intensity is pretty strong with these based off how bright the 395 spotlight was even during the day. So if you get one of these be careful. I’ll use these by shining up or at a wall to get the light reflecting off the wall or I’ll place this a few feet above me where I won’t get the light directly in the eye.

We replaced the window to the left with UV open greenhouse plastic so we are likely getting most the UV and IR light passing through it.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-12-17T18:28:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329325>

> [Phil said:](/goto/post?id=328779)
>
> We replaced the window to the left with UV open greenhouse plastic so we are likely getting most the UV and IR light passing through it

I was thinking some more about this UV-open plastic earlier this week. My understanding is that many visibly transparent and translucent plastics out there can be very opaque to NIR wavelengths.

Have you tested this particular plastic to see if it is transparent to NIR wavelengths specifically?

If it isn't, this plastic may be doing more harm than good...

If you are getting all the UV, but none of the NIR...

### coho3131 — 2023-12-18T17:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329335>

Agree. Filtered sunlight = artificial light (nnEMF)

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-18T18:58:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329342>

We all agree that outside, full spectrum sun light is best . . . But when inside (on a hot or cold day) and open windows are not an option, is filtered window light (maybe balanced with some artificial UV/NIR better than no light?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-12-18T21:51:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329349>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329342)
>
> But when inside (on a hot or cold day) and open windows are not an option, is filtered window light (maybe balanced with some artificial UV/NIR better than no light?

I think any light during the day is better than no light. Sitting in a cave all day is a quick way to derail your circadian rhythm.

A primary concern with indoor environments though, is that even with windows open and lights turned on, it can still be akin to sitting in a cave. Indoor light is simply no match to the intensity of light outdoors. Our bodies NEED intense light during the day (except when near winter solstice)

But yes, I would say some is superior to none at all.

Now to optimize the light further:
It is important that the light your are getting has IR to balance the oxidative effects from higher energy photons.

The tricky part is ensuring that you are actually getting enough IR. The sun's rays are approximately 42% IR. I haven't tried this or tested it out personally, but I feel like that level of IR would be very difficult to replicate in an indoor environment.

I would absolutely augment with as much IR as possible if you have the lamps to do it though.

On the opposite end of the spectrum, we of course, need UV as well.

UVB is not important this time of year (outside of the tropics), but definitely need UVA year round.

So any UVA lamps will help as well.

I would be careful using any UV lamps in the absence of sufficient IR though.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-19T00:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329351>

When I can’t be outside I am surrounded by windows and I have the equivalent of 1,000 watts of NIR light about 2 - 3 feet from me, (behind my iPad screen) + a UVA light + a full spectrum grow light.

### petrichor — 2023-12-23T02:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329433>

Why not use an air curtain instead of all those lights? Open the window all the way, regardless of weather.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-23T04:11:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329435>

> [petercowan said:](/goto/post?id=329433)
>
> Why not use an air curtain instead of all those lights? Open the window all the way, regardless of weather.

The lights are grounded, no electric or magnetic fields - Air curtain, let me see . . . $700 - $2100 (each) . . . a constant 60Hz cycle noise . . . Electric field extends 6 - 8 feet into the room . . . Magnetic field from the motor . . . And I would need several as the sun moves throughout the day . . . that’s why.

### petrichor — 2023-12-23T21:49:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329442>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329435)
>
> The lights are grounded, no electric or magnetic fields - Air curtain, let me see . . . $700 - $2100 (each) . . . a constant 60Hz cycle noise . . . Electric field extends 6 - 8 feet into the room . . . Magnetic field from the motor . . . And I would need several as the sun moves throughout the day . . . that’s why.

Thank you. That was informative and those are very good reasons.

Putting cost aside for a moment, and please bear with me, I am quite new to this stuff. Could we:

Ground the air curtain as well.
Eliminate the 60hz cycle noise with an isolated power supply.
Use magnetic sheidling to redirect the magnetic field and effictively eliminate it in the indoor environment.

### Anthony Smith — 2023-12-23T22:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329443>

> [petercowan said:](/goto/post?id=329442)
>
> Thank you. That was informative and those are very good reasons.
>
> Putting cost aside for a moment, and please bear with me, I am quite new to this stuff. Could we:
>
> Ground the air curtain as well.
> Eliminate the 60hz cycle noise with an isolated power supply.
> Use magnetic sheidling to redirect the magnetic field and effictively eliminate it in the indoor environment.

All good questions.
Yes the unit could possibly be grounded - but only by measuring can one know if or how well that worked. It depends on the internal wiring of the unit.
The noise from the unit I’m talking about is the sound it makes when running and you can’t cover the unit to suppress the sound because it has to be open top and bottom to “breathe”. The frequency/vibration of the fan/motor would be very draining. Think of the relief you feel when you turn off any motorized device. Any “DE” (noise) could be filtered, I expect they all run on 120V AC.
Magnetic shielding is very hard to do and the materials expensive IF it is even possible. The type of motor would determine how far the magnetic field extended.
In very hot, cold, wet weather the best solution would be to use non UV/NIR blocking/reducing plastic/glass.
Do you have meters to measure?

### Phil — 2023-12-26T15:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329474>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=329325)
>
> I was thinking some more about this UV-open plastic earlier this week. My understanding is that many visibly transparent and translucent plastics out there can be very opaque to NIR wavelengths.
>
> Have you tested this particular plastic to see if it is transparent to NIR wavelengths specifically?
>
> If it isn't, this plastic may be doing more harm than good...
>
> If you are getting all the UV, but none of the NIR...

The plastic I used is UV open polyethylene, polyethylene is has a massive window for letting Infrared light through. polyesters, polycarbonates, and acrylics are some plastics thag are opeque to NIR. I prefer the plastic to even an open window because it diffuses the light. This means the sun is coming down at an angle at my window and the diffused light reflects that light all over my room instead of just a bright intense spot on my floor. This means the plastic is lighting up my house with brighter light than If I just had the window open.

I even have troubles believing the idea that most windows block NIR and UV A light. Based off what I’ve researched only newer windows with specific films block near infrared and UV A light. Standard glass blocks around 10-15% UV A and maybe the smallest amount of NIR light. My house is older so I have older windows, there is a chance me replacing my windows with plastic inserts has no added benefit. NIR light is the deepest penetrating frequency of light and easily passes through things if the other visible spectrum are passing through. Mid and far infrared light are the frequencies that generate a lot of heat on our skin because they don’t penetrate deeply and are absorbed at the surface of our skin, these frequencies are more likely to be blocked by a window and this is why a window gets warm/hot. Mid and far infrared light penetrate the greenhouse plastic far better than glass this is why I can sit in my greenhouse when it’s -10 degrees F and get hot and sweaty.

On top of having all my windows replaced with this greenhouse plastic I have the 370nm spot light blanketing my kitchen and living room along with a NIR spotlight and a BFP 9000 panel mounted to my ceiling blanketing my house it NIR light. [https://gembared.com/collections/full-body-lights/products/gembared-bfp-9000-body-light-led-panel](https://gembared.com/collections/full-body-lights/products/gembared-bfp-9000-body-light-led-panel)

### Phil — 2023-12-26T15:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329475>

Using clear marbles as lenses on this low intensity panel is pretty awesome. The marbles really direct and focus the light. The marbles press into the skin pressing the skin pushes the blood that would normally block/absorb some of the light to get the deepest penetration possible. You have to be careful if you do this with another panel the marbles or glass orb intensifies the light more than 4x

### petrichor — 2023-12-30T04:42:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phil%E2%80%99s-journal.28326/post-329616>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329443)

> The noise from the unit I’m talking about is the sound it makes when running and you can’t cover the unit to suppress the sound because it has to be open top and bottom to “breathe”. > In very hot, cold, wet weather the best solution would be to use non UV/NIR blocking/reducing plastic/glass.

Thanks for explaining, Anthony.

I don't actually own an air curtain. I read about them in John Ott's Light and Health and thought it might be a novel way to get more sun inside on a cold day. Based on your response, it sounds like more trouble than it would be worth.

This means the sun is coming down at an angle at my window and the diffused light reflects that light all over my room instead of just a bright intense spot on my floor. This means the plastic is lighting up my house with brighter light than If I just had the window open.

> I even have troubles believing the idea that most windows block NIR and UV A light. Based off what I’ve researched only newer windows with specific films block near infrared and UV A light. My house is older so I have older windows, there is a chance me replacing my windows with plastic inserts has no added benefit. NIR light is the deepest penetrating frequency of light and easily passes through things if the other visible spectrum are passing through. Good information! Thanks

I have older glass windows as well. When I stand in front of them with sun rays coming through, it feels like I'm standing in direct sun outdoors. So they are definitely letting FIR thru.

I think I've read before that glass, in general, lets more IR frequencies thru, when compared to plastics and plastics allow more UV than glass. That is a very big generality, but I think it holds true for most cases.

# Thread: How to maximize cenote time for healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-maximize-cenote-time-for-healing.28432/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Oosting — 2023-12-20T18:44:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-maximize-cenote-time-for-healing.28432/post-329370>

I am going to the yucatan for 3 months (jan, feb, mar 2024) to heal my chronic fatigue/lyme and I have a few questions.

I will watch the sunrise and sunset in swimshorts on the beach and will stay as long in the sun as possible, which most likely will be around 2 hours in the morning and 2 in the evening. When the sun becomes too hot, I plan to stay in cenotes the rest of the day every day. My questions are:

\- Is it ok to spend as much time in the cenote, or would it be better to only spend an hour or two and spend the rest of the day outside in the shade (to better increase vit D to increase my immune system and kill the lyme)? Or is more time spend in cenote better for healing even if it means less time in the shade (which would be a filter between the sun but still increasing vit D a little bit)?

\- Can I drink the water in the cenote? If so I plan to fill up 2 liters of water per day.

\- are the differences between cenotes in terms of healing?

Thanks

ps: does someone perhaps know an airbnb place (up to $1400 per month) that has a private cenote, or is directly next to one?

# Thread: Apteryx - a journal from two-thousand and twenty-four. Let's go
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apteryx-a-journal-from-two-thousand-and-twenty-four-lets-go.28464/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Apteryx — 2024-01-01T11:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apteryx-a-journal-from-two-thousand-and-twenty-four-lets-go.28464/post-329693>

The first day of the year, and where I'm located (on the hemisphere containing Antarctica) the days arrive earlier than almost anywhere else.
So what did I do today:
Grounding in the morning. Tall grass - hands and feet right in it, the sun's rising and shining.
I climb up a lemonwood tree and then an oak tree - the largest oak here is not all that large, fifteen to twenty metres tall. Still quite young, really, under thirty years old.
Lots of red ... far red ... near-infrared ...
I eat a few things from the garden and ponder the last few months that have passed since I commenced my first regular outdoors employment - taking care of a forest-garden. Reflecting with gratitude. Tummy is feeling settled.

Many things to do. One step at a time, because I lack the wings. If you don't count those of a soaring mind of course. Melanin in the feathers? The light is delightful.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Kendall's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-10T20:35:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/post-330062>

I wanted to post my experience so far to at least help other people who may have similar symptoms and issues to me.

**Background**
\-I'm 30 years old, Finnish and German descent, 6' and 160 lbs
\-Am living and have lived most of my life around 40th degree latitude
\-I've abused tech for many years and spent much of my teens reverse engineering my Xbox's software and hardware. My entire life has been poor circadian cycles staying up until 12-2 am and sleeping until 9-11 am
\-I've eaten mostly vegetarian my entire life until 2020 when I switched to mostly whole foods and lots of meat
\-I've been seed oil free since 2020 as well, alongside getting lots of mid day sun (not ideal but better than the prior years of my life)

**I owned a Tesla for 4 years** which is what I believe led to my issues, combined with buying and **living in a** **house in Seattle WA which ended up being high in mold** and more importantly **had a very strong magnetic field throughout the entire home**. As a tech abuser, I had the entire home also hooked up with smart lights, smart blinds, and other connected devices. I also was dealing with a stressful relationship on top of these stressors. I would often drive the Tesla hours on road trips around Washington State several times a week. Around 6+ hours a day, 2-3 days a week

**Symptoms**
\-My symptoms peaked suddenly after a single day of driving 11 hours in the Tesla in a loop around the majority of the Cascade mountain range
\-This coincided interestingly enough within a week after the fall equinox
\-I began feeling a sense of doom whenever inside the house I bought and moved into, even after mold eradication
\-Slowly got worsening pinpoint internal stabbing pain in my lower left abdomen at lower abdominal V
\-I then started developing a very annoying feeling of fullness along my entire left half of my abdomen that would come and go
\-Alongside the fullness, I felt a sharp pain at times inside my abdomen under the bottom of my left ribcage

**Attempts to solve**
\-Like everyone I've tried many supplements, homeopathic doctors, traditional doctors, with none having a clue about what's going on. I've received a hilarious number of incorrect diagnoses from so many "professionals"
\-I tried eating every diet under the sun. Lots of experiments. Lower PRAL, lower oxalates, lower lectins, lower fiber, higher polyphenols, etc
\-Animal based diet with fruit and absolutely no lectins was very helpful but not optimal
\-I spent lots of time outside and getting sun, which helped, but my circadian rhythm was still far from ideal until I learned more about it's importance
\-Found the true issue behind my symptoms through traveling twice for work. My symptoms greatly reduced within 2 days of leaving home and would come back a day or two after arrival.

**Findings**
\-Although animal based was helpful, I didn't see real results until going full carnivore
\-Luckily around the same time I began listening to and reading more of Jack's work
\-Dry skin and low iodine symptoms led me to fully believe that seafood must be included in a proper species appropriate human diet, which resolved both quickly
\-Unable to shake coffee this entire time, I tried going off of it and eventually learned that tapering is important to not shock gut motility too quickly, causing my symptoms to slightly come back
\-Dairy is the biggest contributor to recurrent symptoms, especially milk (although A2 and raw react less)
\-Methylene blue supplementation of 2-10 mg/day initially had massive effects on my mood and mental focus. It seems to have reversed the feeling of dread that has hung over me since living in my toxic environment. Stopped doing this daily after 2 months and don't notice much effect from it when taken now

**Current day**
\-I'm still dealing with a very minor version of my abdominal symptoms occasionally when I am stuck for too long in a low UV environment or eat the wrong foods
\-These physical symptoms also come back stronger in a dose dependent manner when eating products with lectins, but especially cakes/breads
\-Drinking water before or without a meal starts to trigger the upper abdominal pain, which is what I'm trying to figure out. I don't believe it to be related to GERD based on my testing and since I have no other symptoms
\-The lower abdominal pain is always accompanied by harder stool when it happens, but I'm unsure what the issue is internally as I have extremely low markers of gut inflammation and zero blood, both from high accuracy testing
\-I'm trying to get everything to fully heal up currently with as much grounding, sunlight, and connection to nature as possible
\-UV light has the strongest healing effect as I've abruptly changed environments via travel and also a Sperti UV lamp which both instantly hide symptoms
\-I eat fully carnivore, primarily high fat red meat, salmon, shrimp, and occasionally seaweed
\-Have had optimal circadian rhythm and minimal tech usage for about 2 years now
\-I don't burn from the sun even laying in one position for an hour mid day in the summer

My symptoms are currently 99% managed as I know the triggers and healing components

I'm now trying to get what I assume to be internal wounds to fully heal, so they don't get too much more recurring damage for the rest of my life. Hoping UV light will do this for me over time. Would love any input on this.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-11T20:37:58+0200

WiFi and Bluetooth are 2.4 GHz as is a microwave oven, AT&T (+Verizon?) “5G” is “C” band between 3 & 4 GHz. T-Mobile ”5G” is 600 MHz. It is my understanding that 90% of 5G is sub 6 GHz. For shielding think microwave over door, same idea as “smart meter” covers. If you are on the ground floor you could put metal screen on the outside of the building or on the fence between you and your neighbors. Not 100% but reduces RF significantly. Think of the RF source (cell tower, neighbors WiFi) as a light and the metal mesh as a shade, your bed should be in the shade. This is from HomeDepot, if used inside needs to be grounded.

RF shielding acts aa a mirror to RF so IF you have one strong RF source coming from one direction, then shielding one side can be advantageous (measure to be sure). If you have RF coming from all directions then shielding one side could increase the RF level coming from the other side, think light and mirrors. Be sure to measure electric fields as well.
