### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T19:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/post-330331>

T-Mobile:

| Technology | Frequencies |
| --- | --- |
| 5GUC (Ultra Capacity 5G) |
-   Band n41 (2.5 GHz)
-   Band n258 (24 GHz)
-   Band n260 (39 GHz)
-   Band n261 (28 GHz)

 |
| 5G (Extended Range 5G) |

-   Band n71 (600 MHz)

 |

T-Mobile have a lot of double speed on their web site. Their main 5G (5th Generation) service is 600MHz. I don’t know where their “5GUC” service is even available, a few major city down town areas I expect. Their 5G in home internet service appears to be the same as everyone else’s with 2.4 GHz and/or 5GHz connection option. Everyone around you with WiFi is the same.

A general rule of thumb in home networking says that Wi-Fi routers operating on the 2.4 GHz band can reach up to 150 feet indoors and 300 feet outdoors. Older 802.11a routers that ran on 5 GHz bands reached approximately one-third of these distances. Newer 802.11n and 802.11ac routers that operate on both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands reach greater distances.

Because it uses narrower wavelengths, a 5 GHz Wi-Fi connection is more susceptible to obstructions than 2.4 GHz connections, and so will usually have a slightly shorter effective range, typically, 10 to 15 feet shorter.

## Factors Influencing Range​

There are three main factors that influence your Wi-Fi range: the access point or router itself, the structure you're in, and the wireless standard you're using.

### Access Point or Router​

The Wi-Fi signal range of any given access point varies significantly from device to device. Factors that determine the range of an access point include the specific [802.11 protocol](https://www.lifewire.com/what-is-802-11-wireless-protocol-2378244) it runs, the strength of its device transmitter, and the nature of physical obstructions and radio interference in the surrounding area.

The distance at which someone can connect to an access point varies depending on antenna orientation. Smartphone users, in particular, may see their connection strength increase or decrease simply by turning the device at different angles. Furthermore, some access points use directional antennas that enable longer reach in areas the antenna is pointing but shorter reach in other areas.

Change the antenna that came with your router if you're not getting the signal strength you need.

### Type of Structure or Building​

Physical obstructions in homes, such as brick walls and metal frames or siding, can reduce the range of a Wi-Fi network by 25 percent or more.

A Wi-Fi signal weakens every time it encounters an obstruction, which happens a lot indoors, thanks to walls, floors, and even the electronic interference caused by appliances.

### Wireless Standard​

The wireless standard you're using has a direct effect on your wireless signal range and strength. The 802.11g protocol has an indoor range of 125 feet, while 802.11n has a range of 235 feet.

All this applies to everyone with neighbors, so . . . measure and shield accordingly . . . then measure again. AND remember your mobile phone is also sending out a signal that can reach a cell tower up to 1 or even 2 miles away . . . And so is everyone else’s phone. If you live around other people this is just a fact of modern life . . . Have fun.

# Thread: Healing PCOS, making homocysteine connections with blood clotting issues…
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/>

### Niki Stanton — 2024-02-18T16:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/post-331586>

When learning from Dr Jack and from Dr Masgutova (primary reflex integration- I believe there’s a link here- story for another day)- there are so many layers and connections that point to absolute truth in their teachings.

Dr Jack talked about pcos sufferers having a blue light in the retina issue- ding ding. As a child of divorced parents I would fall asleep to the tv every night in my room at my dads house. I’m sure the gardasil vaccine didn’t help matters at age 13.

I was fortunate to live in south Florida and be a very so my fitness level and sunlight counterbalanced my blue light abuse. I still struggle with tech addiction and have to constantly put myself on tech fasts and such.

Our firstborn was a surprise and has subclinical CP- just low enough muscle tone for me to notice with my subpar Occupational therapy degree- but not low enough for Docs to admit. Dr masgutovas interventions at a year allowed him to start walking by 15-16 months and has helped tremendously. All of her (additional, outside of reflex) advice has aligned 100% with Jacks teachings. She always tells clients to avoid blue light, be outside at least 2 hours a day, etc.

I posted about him in the kids section. He’s doing great and jacks specific interventions are helping a ton- mostly the emf avoidance, grounding, and seafood for him. We also use the nanovi device which was a huge healing boost for him- energy, endurance, cognition.

Our second born was cooking during the Zika virus scare. So since I wasn’t fully awakened he didn’t get sunlight in utero. I also took iron with him which I thought caused my nearly catastrophic 50% placental abruption during delivery. But now I’m thinking about homocysteine.

I had labs recently that showed levels out of range for it and have recently had horrible menstrual clotting. Then I had very strange blood clotting type issues though not exactly clotting. I had red splotches on my hands after gentle weight bearing and has red purple spots on my sit bones from sitting on a hard surface.

I started taking B12 and B6 and this last week of my cycle is feeling much better. I had to take a class in Orlando under blue light with minimal breaks and stayed in a condo building (won’t make that mistake again). Came home and broke out in a shingles rash which lit up when I drove under high powered transmission line. Sunlight and iodine paint completely cured it.

Anyway. Interesting connections. Blessed to live close to the shore but feeling a little panicky about the 5G poles that are sprouting up like weeds all around me. We live in a single family home on a quarter acre-ish and have our sights set on an acre and a quarter when finances line up. Not sure where else to go that won’t have the poles in-town.

Goes without saying but I’ll say it anyway- thank you for sharing this with us, Dr. Jack.

# Thread: J's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/unread>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-19T16:53:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331641>

New member here, excited to participate in the knowledge exchange.

I found out about Uncle Jack through the Mitochondriac Manifesto book and the Ben Greenfield podcast. My wife and I are both naturally very skeptical of "the system" in any iteration, (e.g. I was doing the paleo diet in 2010 when it was still not a mainstream idea), so the general anti-centralization message really resonated with us. One of the recent pre-Jack decentralized medicine ideas that really helped us was Klenner's Vitamin C protocol for pregnancy, which we believe facilitated a smooth pregnancy and home birth with zero issues.

My wife started doing the circadian rhythm stuff last year after seeing Sara Kleiner's posts on Instagram, which we now realize was basically repackaged Uncle Jack material. Just by blocking blue light at night and seeing the sun in the morning, she was able to lose 15 lbs over the course of a few months and get back to her pre-pregnancy weight with minimal change in her diet and exercise levels. We also started using a vortexer to structure all of our water last year, and she noticed that her recurring pain from an ACL reconstruction 10 years prior has totally disappeared. I also saw some slight health benefits from it.

My job requires me to be around EXTREMELY bright LED light after sundown for about half of the year, and I could never figure out why I slowly but steadily gained weight during this time, despite exercising almost every day and eating largely keto-carnivore. Once this period ended this year and my schedule became more normal, I started eating a regular breakfast, seeing the morning sun, and blocking blue light at night. I lost several pounds effortlessly and became a believer in the importance of light, water, and magnetism. Can't wait to implement it during the busy season this year.

Over the winter we replaced all of the lights in our house with incandescent bulbs, and we're very conscious of having minimal household lights on after sundown. We both wear VivaRays glasses every night to block blue light, get outside in the morning sunlight (not always exactly at sunrise, but within the hour after) and for a couple minutes at sundown, and try to time-restrict eating to 10 hours. We've ditched all sunscreen and sunglasses, and we take no prescription medications. We also have not taken any experimental medical interventions that have come out in the last couple years. I've dabbled with cold plunges, and I plan to make this a regular practice over the next few weeks. We're not perfect by any means, but I think we have the big items checked off to improve our redox.

Our most recent project has been to move about an hour outside the city to a more rural area. We've noticed the grounding is better and there's a much lower nnEMF load overall. In our previous home we'd pick up 10-12 wifi networks, whereas at the new home it's ours and one neighbor's, and the strength is much weaker/slower than in the city. We're at 34 latitude - not amazing for sun in the winter, but enough to get some benefits, and still works well in the summer. Nearly all of our food is local, organic, grass-fed meat. The baby eats what we eat, just as a paste instead of full chunks

We also had a building biology firm to come and measure our house for nnEMF. Based on their dirty electricity readings we bought a Satic PowerPerfect box that has helped reduce the house's dirty electricity fields. They said that we had very little RFs in our area, though I would like to get a better sense of wifi-type nnEMFs within the house before we go full hard-wired. Judging by phone reception just outside the neighborhood, there is not a lot of 5G in the area... yet.

I'm still trying to learn as much as possible. I'm in the middle of reading The Body Electric, with 4th Phase Of Water next on my list.

Here are some of the questions and issues we're having:

\- Our 1-year-old has eczema on parts of her legs. We are getting her out into the midday sun more and will soon be spending 6 weeks at a lower latitude (27), so we are confident the stronger sun will help. We had her in the direct midday sun for 30-40 minutes per day last summer and she did not develop this issue until the sun weakened in the winter. People used to joke about our baby's great tan, so we'll get back to that point and report back. I suspect it will clear itself up with stronger sun exposure.

\- Baby also has FPIES (type of food allergy) to a couple of foods, so we suspect some sort of gut imbalance. Otherwise she has been extremely healthy -- two fevers in her life, never an ear infection, etc. We are supplementing with liposomal Vitamin C and a gut health supplement our functional medicine doc recommended, and we're hoping she'll grow out of it. My wife makes all of her food using grass fed ruminants and organic/local vegetable ingredients, so I'm hoping continued nutritious food will strengthen her gut.

\- I am extremely allergic to most fish and oysters (FPIES-type vomiting reaction). I know Dr. Jack has said that this is a redox issue. I try to get as much DHA as possible from lamb, which we eat 2-3 times a week, but recognize this is probably not ideal. Not sure if there is a solution here besides improving overall redox and hoping for the allergy to lessen in severity. I also have a mild allergy to eggs, which I still eat occasionally but should probably eliminate due to the redox/inflammation effects. I attribute both of these to getting the full childhood schedule in the mid-80s.

\- We each regained a few pounds after stressful travel and some non-leptin friendly circadian and diet stuff over the holiday week, and we've plateaued off since then. Probably need to do the full leptin reset again to get going, which is already in progress.

\- Considering getting a Magnetico pad for the bed, as long as it's OK for the baby as well. I have read through some of the experiences on the forum but open to any advice about whether they make a noticeable difference.

\- Miron glass - Found out about this on the forum and have one on the way. Can't hurt to further energize water once in a while.

Short term goals:

\- Get 1-2 hours of direct sun every day. Due to the nature of my job over the next few months, this should not be difficult

\- Get 3-4 cold plunges in per week. I'm planning to buy one of the plunge buckets while we're at our temporary home. Is there any back of the envelope way to estimate heat loss given mass, the temperature of the water, and the time spent? Would be curious whether 3 minutes in 55 degree water or 10 minutes in 65 degree water is more effective, for example. I don't want to overthink it so I'll just do what feels correct, but always enjoy quantifying things when possible.

\- Tighten up diet. Have been enjoying an occasional baked good with breakfast lately. We don't drink caffeine, but we've also been having a few decaf coffees per week. Coffee beans are far from native to our area, so I recognize this is a sub-optimal seasonal/light mismatch. I am very sensitive to carbs so my default is to maintain ketosis-level carb intake (<40g) on most days. Will be giving up almost all coffee for 6 weeks and report back.

\- Work in more HIIT sprints. My cardio endurance has dropped as I've favored more outdoor garden work and landscape construction type projects to get into the sun.

\- Figure out how to rewire the house to hard-wire internet, since only one jack is active and it's far from my office. We have wifi on during the day but unplug at night.

\- Reduce computer screen exposure at night. I'm wearing blue blocking glasses and have a deep red / flicker reduction setting on Iris, but still not ideal. I've scaled back the latest computer-using time by about half an hour in the last week.

\- Buy a couple of EMF meters capable of assessing hazards other than dirty electricity. Still researching these on past forum posts.

Open to any feedback or suggestions that anyone has. We've been telling everyone who will listen about blue light, circadian practices, and light-water-magnetism, and have even gotten a couple friends to try them out. It takes a certain personality type to be interested in counter-culture medical and biological knowledge, but we'll keep trying to help our friends and family as much as possible.

Thanks for reading.

All animals, birds, fish farmed are fed by extreme amounts of seed oils (Linoleic Acid LA Omega-6)
All organic anything including so called wild but raised by humans are fed by remains of seeds after (most) seed oil were taken out of it.
Farmed animals, birds, fish, when killed are really on their last legs, very sick. Usually just few days when they would die on their own if not slaughtered.
See discussions on why steak meat is tender.

To get rid of currently excessive LA, about 5-6 years of diligent effort is need, due to very long half life.

So, any carnivore diet is highly suspect, if one worries about health.
Jack talking about food, especially when lately, mentions mostly oysters.
I think that other than oysters, all kind wild seafood is the last place we have to go to.
If you have a gun and dear in your backyard, that maybe one way to get some meat that has normal LA levels.

//
Drink plain water.
//

LA/ALA

In USA everything industrial happened 100 years earlier.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T08:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331848>

Your WiFi (if you have) & your neighbors WiFi and your personal phones, due to proximity, are probably a greater source of RF than the cell tower. The 60Hz AC field from the wires in the walls and everything plugged into them is likely a more significant source of nnEMF than the RF. Check your body voltage in your sleep areas, at night turn off the circuits for a week and see how you feel: [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-23T15:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331853>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=331848)
>
> Your WiFi (if you have) & your neighbors WiFi and your personal phones, due to proximity, are probably a greater source of RF than the cell tower. The 60Hz AC field from the wires in the walls and everything plugged into them is likely a more significant source of nnEMF than the RF. Check your body voltage in your sleep areas, at night turn off the circuits for a week and see how you feel: [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Thank you Anthony, all great reminders. These are the sleep precautions we normally take, regardless of whether we're at home or traveling:

\- Unplug our unit's wifi at night (and anytime we're not actively using it during the day)
\- One phone is kept on airplane mode outside the room, the other is on airplane on the bedside table (2-3 feet away from body)
\- Unplug the salt lamp that's by the bed
\- No other electric devices in the room
\- We have two portable plugin dirty electricity filters, one in the bedroom and the other in the kitchen

I used a Greenwave meter in several of the outlets in the condo, including the bedroom, when we arrived. They were "high" but still not terrible (150-180), and none of them are close to our bodies while sleeping. Compare this with the time I got a 1500 (!) in a hotel room outlet next to the head of the bed.

I just got a body voltmeter in the mail, and did the sleep area measurement like you suggested, and it read 0.155V relative to the ground pin in the wall outlet. I took a reading in the kitchen and it was higher (0.50). I checked around the forum a bit and I couldn't find a scale of how bad different voltage levels are, if anyone has a guideline like that please feel free to share.

I also tested my body voltage outside while standing on the grass with the black pin in the ground and it read 0.00, which I think is what should happen. Though I'm not sure if there's a way to measure relative voltage if there's a current already in the ground (e.g. there are underground power lines but I'm standing on the same ground), but I might have that wrong. I might upgrade to one of your products if necessary once we get back home.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T19:07:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331873>

The Building Biology Institute BV reading at night
<10 milli volts (mV) - no concern
10-100 mV - slight concern
109-1000 mV - moderate concern
\>1000 mV - extreme concern

If you are double grounded anywhere you will get a low or zero reading. For an accurate BV reading of the electric field being induced into your body, your only connection to ground must be the ground cord from the BV meter. Measuring ground currents is different and requires more expensive equipment.

A shield between you and the phone, even in airplane mode, is an added precaution.

# Thread: One meal a day and sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jelle — 2024-02-21T10:09:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331727>

Hi all,

I'm experimenting with one meal a day. I try to eat all my food for dinner. I noticed a few things:
\- Energylevels are great and recovery from exercise also is fantastic.
\- Sleep is a bit worse. I get awake earlier (instead of 8 hours of sleep I have 6,5 hours know). I think my cortisol is higher what causes worsening of my sleep.

Anybody has experience with this and has comments on my findings? It would be great if I have both: high energylevels troughout the day and perfect sleep.

Thanks!

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T14:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331728>

I did OMAD for a little while and had decent success, but had better success when I ate breakfast upon waking as recommended in the Leptin RX.

For me, the one thing was that I couldn’t eat the meal too late. If I eat within 4 hours of bedtime my sleep is noticeably worse. It might be worth trying to push the feeding window up to mid-afternoon and see if you still have the sleep issues.

### jules3791 — 2024-02-21T16:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331736>

I agree with [@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/), eating late does not bode well for sleep. I eat upon waking and depending on my lunch amount and timing then tells me if/when I eat dinner, preferably before 6pm. The earlier dinner is for me, the better.

### Alper — 2024-02-21T18:32:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331740>

[@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/) Correct me if i am wrong but Big ass breakfast in the leptin rx is meant for those who are leptin resistent, so if you arent leptin-resistent, BAB is not the best choice i gues.

[@jelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29277/) eating time depends on your sunset, according to your local situation. So if the sun sets at 7pm, you gotta eat before the sun sets.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T18:53:04+0200

> [@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/) Correct me if i am wrong but Big ass breakfast in the leptin rx is meant for those who are leptin resistent, so if you arent leptin-resistent, BAB is not the best choice i gues.

I'm saying that, subjectively, I felt better when I ate breakfast while doing the leptin reset, so I have continued to do it. Therefore, it is the best choice for me.

I don't eat a BAB, more just getting 40-50g of protein and some healthy fats.

# Thread: My Journey to Waking up with the Sun CONSISTENTLY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Solarmagbro — 2024-03-23T00:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332772>

Hi All,

I used to live in London, England at 51.5072° N, 0.1276° W.

I now live in Tetouan, Morocco at 35.5889° N, 5.3626° W.

In 2023, I spent two months reading the entire Jack Kruse Patreon archive and began experimenting with sunrise waking, Epi Paleo Rx, better blue light management, and EMF mitigation. Although I was already well versed in these things, diving deep into Kruse's work helped me up my level.

To hold myself accountable, I started tracking how often I wake with sunrise on the 1st January 2024.

**So far**

In **January**, I woke up with the sunrise on **17 days (61%).**
In **February**, I woke up with the sunrise **14 days (50%)**

My goal in 2024 is to have an average of 80-90% percent wake with sunrise.

I find it difficult to be consistent with this practice. This journal is a way for me to be held publically accountable. I will share a journal every week detailing how often I woke with the sun, and how I'm getting more consistent, and where I've failed.

I understand that this simple habit is incredibly important. It's the foundation.

And yet, it's been so difficult for me to maintain due to a lifetime of bad circadian management and insomnia.

I appreciate your support.

### Alexandra — 2024-03-23T02:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332773>

Hello! Way to go on your progress so far. I, too, am making changes and understand certain things can be difficult and take time alto adjust. But you can do it. I try to remember how a little…then a little more…becomes a lot.

### Sandra — 2024-03-23T07:43:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332776>

I've also started to see every sunrise. I had to set my alarm for 5:30am for the first 10 days, but then I started to wake up on time without it. Sometimes an hour before which was not ideal!! But now as the sunrise is getting a little later for me, it's getting easier too. I've seen every one since 23 Feb, so just one month in, but I've made it a priority for me to see if it makes a difference. Any sunrise is better than none so just keep gong in the right direction.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-23T13:07:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332778>

I was doing Jack's stuff in my little apartment in Battersea SW LDN back in the day.

Made a lot of it work, and still kinda miss LDN.

May return one day. I was just happier there.

Lets see some progress.

\-Ravi

### Solarmagbro — 2024-03-26T00:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332842>

Managed to wake up once with sunrise this past week. However, I then went back to sleep at 9AM. Since then, my sleep cycle has been screwed. This start of the week, today, I woke up at 10:30 AM and at the very least, stayed up the whole day. I've set up an automated block on both of my computers which will prevent me from ever going on the computer post 12AM for the rest of the year. Of course, I also use f.lux in the evening hours. Typically, I switch off the PC at 10 PM. But sometimes, I need to stay online past that particularly when I'm struggling to fall asleep, or I haven't done enough work in the day.

Tomorrow, my plan is to wake up even earlier than 10:30 AM, and get back in the rhthym.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-26T07:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332848>

Maximize the good, minimize the bad. There are several types of nnEMF: RF from WiFi, Bluetooth, cell phones, smart meters etc, Magnetic fields from motors, bad wiring etc and Electric fields from wires in the walls, floor & ceiling and everything plugged into them. It is not just screen time & blue light at night. Create a EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary to help with optimal circadian re-balance. Visit [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse) and look around, watch the videos - info that might help.

### Solarmagbro — 2024-04-08T17:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-333403>

Last week was crap. Woke up for sunrise just once.

This past week I woke up 5x for the sunrise.

It's been incredibly spiritually illuminating to see it rise each time.

My hearts shakes with reverberations of ectasy and love the rest of the day when I manage to glimpse the majesty of that deep orange glow. This sunrise protocol is more than just a circadian rhthym hack designed to optimise health; it's a deep visceral dive into living from my heart and accessing my higher power.

I feel this in my bones. Each day I wake up with the sun and see it rise the rest of my day is enveloped in a higher state of concscious awareness that's inexplicably and tantamountally different to when I don't.

Can you relate?

### Solarmagbro — 2024-05-19T18:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-334900>

This past week I woke up for sunrise 6/7 days.... so magical to get the sunrise for 20 mins... This sunday I didn't wake up for sunrise... I woke up at 9AM.... and the difference in how I feel is palpable... I believe eating too much fish yesterday made me more sloggy and it was consequently harder to get up.

### Solarmagbro — 2024-05-27T19:53:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-335194>

Only woke up for sunrise 4/7 times last week. Also went back to sleep for a few hours during those days. So that wasn't great. This week will be better. I woke up for sunrise today this Monday but also went back to sleep due to needing extra recovery after 10 days of antibiotics. Tomorrow I will feel enough energy to stay up.

# Thread: Anne and Blake's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Annemcnulty — 2024-04-03T13:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/post-333157>

I am a 42 year old woman in Ottawa, Canada and have been diagnosed with chronic fatigue, mold illness/CIRS, POTS, mild PCOS, EBV, a variety of chronic infections (including strep and Lyme) plus complex PTSD from childhood. We've always been "health conscious" and worked with functional medicine practitioners, but I only started learning about quantum biology and Dr. Kruse's work about six months ago. Since then we've significantly reduced nnEMF in our home, changed all our lighting, become much more careful about wearing orange glasses after sunset, and see the sunrise almost every day. I seem to feel best on a mostly carnivore diet. I do travel for work a fair amount though which is a nightmare for light, water and magnetism.

In spite of my health issues, I am still functional (i.e. can work and parent). I am currently much more focused on helping my 8 year old son, who has been diagnosed with PANS/PANDAS, ADHD and Pathological Demand Avoidance (an autism spectrum disorder characterized by nervous system activation from any demands). The practitioners we work with feel this is mostly driven by mold exposure (which likely started in utero) and Bartonella. His symptoms have been mood instability, impulsivity, vision issues (needs reading glasses plus has a stigmatism), hyperactivity, chronic fight/flight, many sensory issues, picky eating, occasional depression (he won't leave the house sometimes), severe leg pains at night (about once every two weeks), and keratosis pilaris all over his arms and legs. His symptoms have improved with mold remediation, a mold detox protocol (nystatin, itraconazole, LDN, welchol, ketotifen, H1/H2 blockers and many supplements) plus doing all the nnEMF and light work above. We also use homeopathy and frequency specific microcurrent. I am guilty of using a lot of interventions at once so it's hard to know what has helped the most.

I would say he is 70% improved, but still struggles with nervous system dysregulation, impulsivity (which has social consequences), the leg pains, and reading at school (which is worse when he is in fight/flight). I'm struggling with whether I should stay the course with the treatment modalities above, or try to find a practitioner who is mold/lyme informed but also quantum biology informed. He also has issues with retained primitive reflexes (which seems very common with spectrum kids and we haven't really dealt with that). In general, there are SO MANY suggested causes and interventions for these kids (and many profiteers in the non-neurotypical kids market).

My overarching question is - for conditions driven at least in part by mold/Lyme/etc. - is there still a role for the functional medicine protocols (nutritional supports, anti fungals/anti microbials, mast cell stabilizers, etc.) in healing?

### jules3791 — 2024-04-03T16:11:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/post-333160>

Welcome, Anne!
I believe you came to the best community for answers. As I am new as well, I won't have much to offer except you will need to take the plunge and become a member so you can read Dr Kruse's Patreon blogs. I've printed many of his offerings so that I am not on the computer as much. The "search" option is your BFF. My mom heart goes out to you with your son. Keep digging and advocating for him.
Julia

A couple of links that may help:

### [Hey Lyme Disease, Meet Leptin!](https://jackkruse.com/hey-lyme-disease-meet-leptin/)

Readers Summary Why is lyme misunderstood Is lyme connected to fibromyaliga, mold, and chronic fatigue syndrome? How do we test for it and how might we treat it? How does leptin tie into this story? I do not believe there is a more misunderstood disease by conventional medicine than Lyme...

 jackkruse.com

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-45-83724567](https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-45-83724567)

### Inger — 2025-01-25T17:28:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/post-345744>

I would try give your son (and you) soluble fiber in the form of psyllium husk+water, or legumes - in many small portions through the day - the soluble fiber will act like cholestyramine, but without the side effect.
Mold is detoxed through the bile, and by mopping up the bile through soluble fiber(which attach like a magnet to the bile and takes it out in the body instead of recirculating it), you will get rid of the mold too.

I can tell that doing this almost for 2 years now has calmed down my own nervous system in a big way.

# Thread: Aaron's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### aaronwebb — 2024-04-08T20:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-333414>

**About Me**
I'm Aaron Webb. I'm 43. I live in a rural area north-northwest of Nashville. I have 10 children; four are grown and out. The rest are ages 7 to 17 about 2 years apart. I've worked in IT for 25 years. I love hiking and music.

**Past health**
The past 10 years I have had several "unexplained" health issues. I have visited doctors very minimally so there hasn't been any diagnosis, mostly because I've been self-pay most of my life for routine visits and never prioritized my own health. I have tried many herbalists, chiropractors, kinesiologists, food gurus, and other forms of therapies. While they have helped in one way or another, several symptoms persist and just seem to get worse over time.

I haven't been able to get rid of my gut. It looks bloated and nothing I do seems to touch it. I've felt bloated, had indigestion, and poor digestion for a long time. I got on the keto train a couple of years ago. It was the one diet I tried that made me feel amazing. I leaned out and dropped 20 pounds but my gut wasn't affected. I always plateau at 175. I'll attach a pic.

My heart gives me trouble. I've had occasional palpitations for a long time. It's hard to explain the way it feels sometimes. Over the past few years, I would go through spells where I would have random dizziness, exhaustion, light headed. About 4 years ago it got bad enough I went to the ER. They ran a bunch of tests, took xrays, did heart CT. They said there was nothing wrong with me and sent me home with a beautiful bill for the privilege. My heart doesn't race. I have good resting heart rate. My heart rate variability isn't great. I have pounding heartbeat, it's noticeable most of the time. When it starts feeling weird it feels like a constant adrenalin rush like a stress reaction.

I have these fatty tissues bumps on my body in various places, just under the skin. The first one appeared maybe 20 years ago and then every so often I'd notice another one. The few times I've asked about them there were various opinions but no one was very concerned. They're mostly around my gut and a few on my forearms. I haven't noticed any new ones in quite a while but they aren't going away either.

My ears ring. It's mostly noticeable at night, but it's gotten a little worse over the past couple of years. Related to that, I can only describe it as a ringing in my head that comes and goes. My job requires long periods of deep concentration. I wouldn't say it has been affected. I don't feel like I experience brain fog. It just feels a bit like I'm going crazy.

**Recent health**
The past year I've become increasingly frustrated with a lot of things and decided I was going to stop making excuses and start prioritizing my health in all areas. I also just had an intuition, though I didn't know exactly how to prove it, that my career was literally killing me. I set a goal to be out of IT by the end of next year. I think I'll hit that goal by the end of this year.

I had started seeing a therapist for other reasons but it led to me really becoming focused and starting to simplify my life in many ways. I was wildly over committed and not doing anything very well. I renewed my commitment to keto and working out but continued to be frustrated with hitting a plateau and no results. When I would relax my diet even a little, I would start having the previous described reactions. I would have good periods working out but then go into a period of huge exhaustion and my heart would feel weird. My head seems to be "buzzing" more.

I'm a massive podcast listener. Since I would spend many hours a day in isolation or driving, I could get through an incredible amount of content. I picked up on the functional medicine and biohacking space. I'd been listening to Huberman, Hyman, Saladino, Attia, Asprey, WAPF, and when I decided to prioritize my health and find some answers, I started tracking everything in a daily journal - everything I ate, supplements, heart stats, blood pressure, bowel movements, observations, etc. I felt like I was only getting worse and my sleep was deteriorating quickly. So, I signed up at a functional medicine clinic, Maxwell Clinic in Nashville. In early February, I had my first appointment and testing. In March, we reviewed my results.

I'll attach the testing but here are the results specifically for the ones Dr Kruse has mentioned in blogs, emails, and book.
HS-CRP - 1.10
Vitamin D - 100
DHEA - 518
Glucose - 84
A1C - 5.2
LDL-P - 1308
LCL-C - 103
Triglycerides - 209
HDL-C - 42
HDL-P - 29.9
CO2 - 20
SHBG - 16.5
Estrodial - 22.7
Free T3 - 3.2
Free T4 - 1.39
Free T - 13.7
TSH - 1.08
BUN/Creatinine - 13:1
Ferritin - 511
Homocystine - 12
VO2 Max - 40.6
Cortisol - abnormal spike in evening

DHT, IGF-1, Pregnenalone, Progesterone weren't in the test but I'll get them included next round.

At the end of the appointment, we had chatted quite a bit about my background and research I was doing. He said you're pretty open minded so I'm going to give you a resource and let you take it from there, and he gave me the link jackkruse.com and told me to look up the Leptin Rx. On the way home, I started naturally looking for a podcast and saw the Huberman episodes. I listened to those and the RFK one. It connected so many dots and I signed up as a silver member March 9.

**Current health**
About a month in, here's where we stand. I'm very pleased with what I'm learning and progress. I'm back to a baseline of at least sleeping great and feeling good with a small caveat so far. Here's what I've done:
**Functional clinician requests to start...**
\- A diet high protein and fat and low carb
\- Take s-Acetyl Glutathione (indicated low in test)
\- Take Alpha-Lipoic Acid (indicated low in test)
\- Take a probiotic (general gut support)
\- Take Biocidin (related to leaky gut)
\- Take gene methylation support (methylation issues indicated in test)

**What I've implemented from Dr Kruse and others:**
\- Eating the Epi Paleo diet and more seafood than I have ever eaten. I'm actually starting to like it
\- Seen the sunrise every day except 2 in the very first week with as few clothes on as I could stand
\- Bought a patio table and chair and put it out in the backyard so I can sit there and work as much as possible.
\- Been getting the midday sun at least an hour in only shorts (I bought the cooltan) when it's not really cold and/or raining.
\- On the nicer days, if I don't have meetings or other obligations, I'm outside the entire day. Yesterday I spent 90% of the day outside in my cooltan shorts using shade to control exposure.
\- Haven't been as good about sunsets but I've seen about 60% of them.
\- Ditched my contacts and have been wearing blue blockers.
\- Cut back light usage in the house and started using NIRA lighting bulbs.
\- Bought a body voltage kit from EMFSafe and have been working my way through the house - meeting with a building biologist this week.
\- Ran cat6a to my office, living room, and bedroom and set wifi to be off all night and cycle during the day.
\- Listened to about a dozen podcasts of Dr Kruse
\- Working my way through Decentralized Radio podcasts
\- Read Epi-paleo Rx book
\- Working through the blogs from the beginning
\- Currently reading Liberman's book Light

Last week I did a DEXA scan and VO2 Max test. I didn't have any issues powering through that. A palpitation or two in the recovery phase.

**How I'm feeling:**
\- I'm sleeping great. At first I used the circadian life app to wake me up at sunrise. Now I wake up at daybreak with no alarm. By 9:30 pm if I'm not out or doing something, I'm nodding off. Ears ringing and head bother me at night but lately it doesn't keep me awake.
\- My gut health has improved back to that baseline. I'm digesting well. No indigestion. I'm feeling hungry. I don't feel bloated.
\- My energy levels have improved. I had cut down my workouts to just walking a few miles every other day. Now I've started running again and doing some bodyweight exercises.

**Working plans:**
\- Learning about and continuing to optimize nnEMF exposure.
\- Been looking at Magnetico sleep pad and planning to get one.
\- Interested in improving my near sightedness.

I think this message has resonated so well as it connects a lot of dots for me. I feel I am a decentralized thinker in many ways. My first act was about 20 years ago when I opted out of a centralized religious cult. Soon after that, centralized education, centralized food, centralized medicine, self-employment. I've been buying and holding small amounts of bitcoin for a number of years but hadn't really embraced the community and network. This community inspired me to go to my first meetup at Bitcoin Park in Nashville.
I have a long way to go and so many things to improve but I'm excited to have new ideas to consider and integrate!

Welcome! Reading your journal, especially the part about the heart palpitations and dizziness with no clinical answers, had me thinking what does your blood pressure look like? What is your LDL and are your triglycerides really 209? I have the random heart palpitations and dizziness too, but have low BP, lower triglycerides 34, higher LDL 200 and normal HDL 80. Interesting to see what others say. Good luck!

### aaronwebb — 2024-04-09T00:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-333431>

Morning blood pressure is around 118/81, afternoon blood pressure around 132/87. Here's the full lipid.

### aaronwebb — 2024-05-19T21:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-334905>

A little over a month later, here's an updated report and some challenges.

A couple of weeks after the last report, I was feeling really great. I was sleeping well, rising before sunrise eagerly, and continuing my daily routing mostly outside all day. Attached is a pic of the outside work area I have set up. My body voltage sitting here with my laptop is .09. I'm working on mounting an Ethernet outlet out here so I can plug in a long cable instead of the WiFi. My heart palpitations, tinnitus, and dizziness have greatly improved. This lasted a couple of weeks.

About 3 weeks ago I started feeling bad. It was a light sore throat along with very active sinuses and felt like I was fighting something. I handled it ok the first couple of days then the exhaustion started. I laid outside all day and slept. Side note: for most of my life, whenever I get sick with almost anything, it seems to take me a longer to recover than most people I know, a week minimum. After the customary week, I started to feel better like I wasn't actively sick anymore and was much more functional, however, the exhaustion and sinus activity still hasn't quit. It's hard to put words to. The exhaustion is nearly debilitating, my muscles feel weird and like they're being drained, there's kind of an anxious feeling, my appetite has gone way down, and brain fog has been terrible. There's been a few days I nearly slept the whole day.

I've tried to maintain my routine. I've missed maybe a quarter of the sunrises since then. I try to get up for sunrise, get some food in me, then sometimes I'll go back to bed. I've been spending time outside as usual but it's been quite stormy the past week. I've been eating Epi Paleo but it's a real challenge to get as much volume. This morning I'm optimistic. I woke at sunrise, got outside, ate a little, then slept 3 more hours. I'm outside writing this and it's sunny day the the exhaustion isn't as bad today. I'm still sleeping well at night.

I've found through experimentation that charcoal, zeolite, magnesium seems to help a lot with the muscle, heart, and anxious feelings. It's been suggested that my body is healing and needs a lot of extra support right now. I don't really know where else to go with it other than just trying to stick to the routine, experimenting, and hope it resolves.

### aaronwebb — 2024-06-28T17:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-336239>

As I've been increasing my time in the sun outside the shade, work has been increasingly impacted as I can't see my screen. Box to the rescue  Looking forward to a Daylight Computer laptop someday. Body voltage floats around 7mV here. Black Cooltan shorts.

# Thread: Justin's Optimal Journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justin Goldberg — 2024-05-02T12:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justins-optimal-journal.28839/post-334210>

Fifth day on the diet.

Messed up and had corn chips on day three and pistachios on day 4.

Is wild caught salmon from China better than farmed salmon from elsewhere?

I'm considering crowd cow or butcher box. Also at my church I met a farmer who raises cows for meat and I told him I would purchase 20 lb of beef. Next time he butchers a cow I'll go in on a quarter. He said that the butcher dry ages the beef, but I can request him to not dry age it. Anyone else from North Georgia wants to split the half cow into a quarter? DM me here or on the nostr primal link in my signature.

On day four I seem to have started to get keto flu. First symptom was a scratch in my throat, like when you first get a cold. Took some airborne. Today's the 5th day and has progressed to a headache. I will be getting some activated charcoal to help. And perhaps some prebiotic powder, (seen it in Fast This Way) but I'm wary of anything Dave Ass-spray says from what Jack says about him.

Bought some grass fed butter, will simmer & filter it to ghee since like most Jewish people I have problems with dairy protein. Butter coffee!

Also on day 4, I woke up at sunrise without an alarm clock, and I wasn't groggy and cranky like usual. First time I watched the sunrise in a very long time (I don't think I've ever watched it really) and stayed out for 45 minutes.

Will confront girlfriend about wifi at some point (they are on timers right now). It's her house and her money. She doesn't understand that I can run a cable in her bed room and kitchen, the common wifi usage areas, and set the signal power level to as low as possible. I'll get a couple of metal mesh covers, but do those dampen or kill the signal?

I've seen coax to Ethernet adapters but DirecTV uses our coax. Afaik coax doesn't work like Ethernet, so it's not just a matter of putting coax splitters between them. I've also seen hpna power line network adapters, although I need to find out if our house has a smart meter before using those. Won't those just radiate the smart meters signal even more? It was built in 2016 so it probably does.

# Thread: My attempt at "full-spectrum" lighting for my home office?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeffgibson2024 — 2024-05-29T19:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335259>

I'd like your thoughts on my office setup, is this janky or genius (or both)? I got a 5-arm lamp and have been playing w different bulbs trying to replicate natural light as much as possible. I know there's no substitute for natural sunlight, but when I'm stuck at my desk why not try to maximize my bright circadian stimulation during the day (after I get my sunrise time outside...and i still get outside for UVA and UVB rise)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Getting close to natural light...

I also got this for other side of my desk for extra IR balance, thanks for the reccomendation [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)! Measurment taken at surface level = 17mW/cm^2, this is nothing compared to RLT devices output, but this way I'm not getting over-exposed to Red/IR like I would if I was sitting 2ft from RLT device all day.

### petrichor — 2024-05-29T20:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335263>

Interesting setup. How does it feel having all that light shining directly at you from close range? Do you not have any windows you can open up?

Curious, I don't see any UV in the reading on the right of your office setup. I see the black flood light in the photo, but it is labeled as 495 nm, which is actually cyan. That supports the reading, but doesn't make sense for a black light. Is it actually 395 nm? In which case, back to the first question, why is it not showing up on the meter?

### jeffgibson2024 — 2024-05-29T21:34:33+0300

That supports the reading, but doesn't make sense for a black light. Is it actually 395 nm? In which case, back to the first question, why is it not showing up on the meter?

It feels quite stimulating honestly, I feel more energy throughout day and better mood. I never stare directly at it obvs, I just let my iPRGCs download it from my peripheral vision when I'm on this cpu.

And good catch [@petrichor](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/30014/)... I accidentally shared pic from before the most recent black light addition, my bad. It does have a small spike at 395nm now, although this black flood light is intense AF when that one's directly pointing in my peripheral so I point that against back wall so that this one's not directly shining in my eyes, so I just get more reflection off wall from it. I won't mess w sharing another pic, just image small peak up to 0.3 on y axis from UV-A peak at 395nm

Yes I always have window open as well, but we only get so much extra light in from that.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-05-30T23:09:40+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335431>

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-06-04T20:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335432>

Why not focus your efforts on creating a home office space that can function outdoors?

Instead of trying to recreate the outdoors indoors. I think this would be a more fruitful and successful endeavor in the long run.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-06T00:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335484>

### Pratima — 2024-06-06T00:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-attempt-at-full-spectrum-lighting-for-my-home-office.28965/post-335486>

>
> Why not focus your efforts on creating a home office space that can function outdoors?
>
> Instead of trying to recreate the outdoors indoors. I think this would be a more fruitful and successful endeavor in the long run.

I sometimes take my laptop outside on the deck, sit it in a box on its side so I can stop it getting sun damaged and see the screen. Its good for small periods of time, and certain cases like having to review or draft documents. Other things, like spreadsheeting or doc writing, its distracting when you have to really concentrate. Also when you get calls, particularly on teams, its not a good look.

If you have a multi-meter handy you can check this via a very basic body voltage measurement which is ultimately measuring the pressure (volts) differential between two points (you and the earth). Note: this method does not measure current flow result of any contact currents that may be present. If it is not grounded there are ways to shield this, however the shield must be grounded otherwise it will make the issue worst. I'm sure Anthony could assist you with this.

# Thread: ajohnson90 Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2024-06-19T01:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/post-335887>

Hello fellow Mitochondriacs,

I wanted to start a new journal to be a little more precise and comprehensive.
10 years ago at the age of 25 I started experiencing debilitating chronic health issues. Initially my sleep quality went way downhill, I started having night sweats, sleeping 12 hours a day, and waking up feeling like I pounded 40 beers and wrestled a bear - I was exhasuted, groggy, my eyes and mouth felt like they were burning and I was dehydrated, with yellow foamy urine.
A year passed and I woke up one day with intense brain fog that hasn't relented.
Over the next few years I started to develop anxiety, depression, panic attack, paranoia, mood swings, weight gain, cold hands/feet, TMJ issues, scalloped tongue, bruxism, vision issues, heart palpitations, POTS, orthostatic hypotension, dyspnea, gut issues like bloating, discomfort, and irregular stool quality, fatigue, exhaustion, weakness, and a "burning" sensation in my muscles akin to working out, but when doing basic tasks like changing clothes or carrying groceries.

I was subsequently diagnosed with mild obstructive sleep apnea, chronic fatigue syndrome and fibromyalgia.

The sleep apnea diagnosis is interesting because the first symptom to appear was sleep issues, but it's also very mild. I have an AHI of 5 and now that I've finally adjusted to my CPAP I experience less than 1 event per hour with no real dip in oxygenation.

I tried so much stuff to address my symptoms. Everything that is popular now, I was doing over a decade ago, and nothing helped at all.

I spent alot of time outside as a kid and into my 20s, I remember feeling an intuitive call to eat breakfast outside every morning in high school- no one I knew was talking about the benefits of the sun back in the late 2000s, but it felt like the right thing to do. I started taking cold showers around the same time.

Fast forward to the illnesses, I continued these practices and discovered Jack in 2017, making his protocols a priority in my life. Still I found no discernible benefit.

There have been two times in my life since the health issues started that they actually went away.

The first time was in 2019 when I had my wisdom teeth removed, I speculated that there was a structural issue causing the sleep issues and a few days after getting my wisdom teeth removed, I woke up feeling great and sprang out of bed like a happy, healthy human being. And my urine was clear! This only lasted for a month though and my issues came back.

In 2020 I moved from 35N (OKC, OK) to 40N (SLC, UT) and my issues worsened- the CFS and Fibro really came into the picture around then.

in April 2022 I participated in Erwan Le Corre's BreathHoldWork Meditation course. Ive spent alot of time studying respiration and practicing breathwork, so this was just an interest of mine but I also knew if I could learn to hold my breath for a long time I could quell the mental healkth issues. Later in the day, on the last day of the 4 week course, I felt a release in my body, as if a boa constrictor had been wrapped around me the last decade and it just let go. That night I slept like a baby and all of my issues went away. Every single one of them, No more brain fog, no more fatigue, pain, mental issues, gut issues etc. I felt 100% healthy and energized. I even lost 40lbs over the next few months

For some reason this only lasted for about 4 months and my sleep issues slowly came back, followed by the rest of my issues. It's worse than ever but I have to figure out what's going on or I won't make it.

That's my story so far, and I need to figure out how to get back to that place of health and clarity, or I'm not going to make it.

I'll post my relevant labs and health info below and answer any questions and responding to any advice you guys have.

### ajohnson90 — 2024-06-19T01:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/post-335888>

Age - 33
Height - 5'9
Weight - 235lbs
Body fat - unkown but probably 30% (i've gained alot of fat and lost alot of muscle over the last few years)

Cholesterol - 186mg/dL
Triglycerides - 82mg/dl
LDL - 112mg/dl
Lipoprotein A - <6mg/dl
Apolipoprotein B - 82mg/dl

Vit D 25 OH - 33ng/ml

Creatine Kinase - 486U/L

TSH - 1.25 mU/L

Fasting Insulin - 5 uIU/ml
Glucose - 87mg/dl
HGB A1C - 4.9%

Testosterone by Immunoassay - 543ng/dl

### ajohnson90 — 2025-03-22T02:53:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ajohnson90-optimal-journal.29043/post-349095>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I just had a CT scan that shows “Age advanced atherosclerotic calcifications of the intracranial arterial vasculature”. I’m only 35, so that’s worrying to hear. Of course I need to follow up with CTA or MRA to assess severity, but I want to get a handle on this.

For transparency, I did get two jabs. And have had Covid twice. I got Bells palsy after my first bout with Covid in 2022 so they did an MRI to rule out a stroke and they didn’t find anything worrisome so this must be new.

Which blogs do I need to read?

My understanding is α-MSH helps with reabsorption of plaque. I have a good tan (but not on my gut, which I’m changing this year) and am outside most of the day, but I do live at latitude 40. I wonder if I need to move.

### Revelation — 2025-04-12T17:16:25+0300

You know Uncle Jack would say MOVE south. 100%! Eliminate nnEMF as much as possible. Never miss a sunrise, walk on the beach, drink lots of water (I order Icelandic Glacial from Amazon). Block the blue light. It takes time, but I had a close friend who knows me well, mention to me I look not only younger but my stance is improved I guess he means my presence? Been adjusting back to the way we were designed to live for now about 6 weeks. I had a life of being an airline pilot, flying international and upside down (circadian wise not the plane). Getting my life back slowly.

# Thread: Steve's optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steve Z — 2024-07-06T22:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-336764>

History on maternal side:

Grandma: No major health issues most of her life. Died from lung cancer at 78, after around 60 years of smoking. Wore glasses. Mostly an indoor dweller. Was overweight but not obese. Had a brain issue with surgery around age 60. Lived in California until mid 1960’s, then moved to Oregon.

Mother: Also a smoker. Said she was allergic to seafood, got sick on it once I recall as a young child. Mostly an indoor dweller but enjoyed working in the yard. Wore glasses. Was overweight but not obese. She developed rheumatoid arthritis in her late 30’s. Mom passed away at 49 due to iatrogenesis (she got aplastic anemia years after taking methotrexate which built to a toxic level in her system). Her RA was never under control, it just ate away at her slowly, and was hard for me to witness. My mom grew up in California, then left in the early 1970’s to move to Oregon.

My history: born with blue eyes and blonde hair. Still have blue eyes but hair got darker (brown) by 5th grade. I grew up saying I was ‘light sensitive’, and would have a hard time looking at say a white car on a sunny summer day. I burned fairly easy. My vision was good as a kid but by middle school I was very near sighted. Wore contacts and occasionally glasses from roughly 8th grade until my 30’s. I did lasik at 32, not knowing any better. I have lived at the 44th latitude essentially all of my life. I’m 54 now and since age 25 have worked in a dispatch center – shiftwork, overtime, working indoors, in front of a lot of computer screens. By age 49, after promoting and spending the prior 6 years on night shift I felt broken. I was broken. However, my labs told my standard doctor things were fine. I had the energy of an 80 year old man, never felt rested even after a long sleep, had long term depression, brain fog, and never felt good.

Starting around 2018, I started listening to a lot of podcasts looking for answers and came across Dr. Kruse via Ben Greenfield. There was something about what Dr. Kruse was saying. He sounded crazy but I just felt he knew what I needed – call it entanglement - so I kept listening. And reading a lot. I was in the $5/month club from 2020-2022, then became a gold club member in late 2022. I have easily spent 1000 hours listening to Dr. Kruse podcasts, reading his blogs, and listening to the private recordings.

Since 2018-ish I have made major progress but am still not optimal – likely tied to redox. I have exited a very toxic marriage, have no signs of depression for 4+ years, bought and religiously use blue blockers at night, spend regular time outside in the sun, don’t miss a sunrise (even if just on a break at work), have embraced the cold, bought a magnetico, turn the breaker to my bedroom off at night. I started taking sun trips during winter last year (first year it was financially possible) and plan to increase that going forward. I've been on dayshift since 2018, but often come in to work at 3am or work until 10pm in the winter.

In 2023 I bought a junker house trying to re-establish myself financially. It was more of a junker than I realized, so I spent a lot less time outside, more blue light stress, and had more stress in general than planned this last year. It is in the finishing phase now, however my wellness declined during this period, plus I gained some weight. The experience was a learning opportunity for where my boundaries are health wise to at least not loose ground.

I know I still have major blue light toxicity from my job. There is a lot of nnEMF near my work per a tri-meter. Population density isn’t too bad where I live but not great either (around 250K people in the metro area).

My epi-retinal membrane detached 2 years ago. My eye doctor hypothesized it was from weight lifting. I did not have the surgery to remove it, because they remove your vitreous fluid and you later get cataracts. Seemed like a no-brainer to say hell no to that. I no longer feel anxiety (was quite bad initially) and while vision in that eye has improved there are blurry areas in my visual field still. I believe my eye doctor did OCT scans as part of his evaluation.

I am not on any medications and don't have any diagnosis, and want to improve. I know I need more sun, am getting that as I can, plus am planning sun trips this winter. I need a long vacation in the tropics or playa del carmen area. Would love to find mito minded people near by (Oregon).

Labs in late 2022 showed elevated LDL. The doctor talked about statins (which is a hell no from me). I haven't seen them since and they are no longer with the practice. Haven't done labs since.

### Steve Z — 2024-12-09T17:40:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343583>

For some reason my right knee has gone from feeling slightly off, to occasionally painful to fairly painful over the last 6 months. I have done a good amount of CT during this time, plus get regular sun and red light exposure, and yet the pain/problem has just slowly increased. My knee was not obviously swollen.

There was no injury or event to start this process, and no history of problems with the knee. The knee did not get worse if I walked numerous miles on it. Walking was not painful in general but getting up and starting to move took a moment. My walking stride was a bit off as I tried to compensate.

I hadn't thought much about it until it became fairly painful. That's when it dawned on me how odd it seemed - no trigger event and no history of issues. Doing regular CT it seemed inflammation would be reasonably in chest, and yet this problem persisted and got worse. The worry in the back of my head tied back to my mom having RA.

I spent a week in the Florida panhandle last month and hoped the relaxation would help. It did but not very much. Then I tried DMSO and RevGenetics TetraCircumin plus spent about a weeks' worth of nights massaging the area. And now the pain is still there but only 20% of what it was.

My life stress has been way too high for about 12-18 months, so maybe that's is a root cause. That issue will be resolved in another month or two.

Has anyone else had something similar happen?

### Bodycoach — 2024-12-11T13:36:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343692>

How did you apply the DMSO?

### Steve Z — 2024-12-15T06:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-343917>

> [Bodycoach said:](/goto/post?id=343692)
>
> How did you apply the DMSO?

Topically around the knee. The container says its 90% DMSO in an aloe vera gel.

### Steve Z — 2024-12-25T19:48:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-344344>

Update on the knee issue. After paying a lot more attention to this, and going to a chiropractor I know but haven't seen since 2017, it turns out my hips were rotated and locked into place. I began to realize the pain wasn't really in my knee joint, it was the connective tissue running on either side of my knee cap that was problematic.

The adjustment helped a lot. Right after I was able to walk normally. As the day progressed my body kept trying to go back to where it had been. Lots of stretching. Today is was back where it had been. More stretching, massage and foam roller work. Looks like this will take some work to resolve. But at least I seem to have a clear picture of the cause.

### Steve Z — 2025-03-29T20:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-349593>

My hip/back/leg issue continues to wax and wane the last 3-4 months. The cycle seems to be: after a long stressful work week I'm tight again (too much sitting, long hours, stress), and after a long weekend of stretching and getting walks I'm better. Back and forth. Forth and back. I hope with spring here and my house rehab project winding down I can get back to basics. More time in morning sun, more afternoon & evening in sun, more walking/moderate exercise, etc.

In several recent podcasts Dr Kruse has talked about stress having only 1 pathway in the brain. When tight, my body creaks and pops a lot when stretching - way more than ever before. I wonder if I over-stressed myself this last year or so. Does stress dehydrate the body?

<edit> I no longer think DMSO had any benefit for this issue, outside of the placebo effect.

### Steve Z — 2025-10-27T10:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-361669>

A new chapter on the journey to optimal -

Something changed recently.

In my original post in this thread, I said I started making significant changes around 2018/2019, following Dr. Kruse's principals. I probably should have moved to the tropics for 6 months to a year, but it wasn't in the cards for me then (EDIT: I was not willing to leave my tween & teen kids). I stayed in my 45-degree north latitude and changed as much of my life as I could - seeing the sunrise, dark nights when I could, blocking blue at night when I couldn't, grounding, eating seafood, cold exposure, stress reduction, etc.. When I started, if I did everything right, I might get to the point at the end of the weekend that I would not feel horrible. I kept working the program and slowly improved but never got close to optimal.

For years, if I saw blue light at night without blockers on, I would feel a little ill. This was after 25 years in a job requiring long hours staring at a computer screen. I did cold exposure but didn't acclimate well to it. Dr. Kruse has said that smokers have a hard time cold adapting. I grew up in a cloud of secondhand smoke and theorize I had negative transgenerational epigenetic factors from my grandma and mom. Plus I imbibed some myself.

In late spring this year, I finished a house renovation and was able to slow down. I ran across something on the Forum about salt intake so boosted my salt intake, even putting it in my occasional morning coffee and noticed I didn't excessively urinate for the next several hours. I felt more energy. I spent many mornings sitting on my front porch getting direct morning sun for an hour or more. Lots of afternoons walks in the sun. Doing cold plunging outside in a grounded tub. Other than salt, I was doing the same things, just more of them than I was able to for the prior 2 years.

In the last month or so, I can suddenly tolerate unfiltered blue light exposure in the evening without feeling ill or queasy. Maybe my blue light toxicity has diminished enough to pass over some threshold? I didn't leave my blockers off as a result but there has been a definite shift for the better in this area. In that same time frame my cold adaptation has notably improved and it seems clear my hormone panel is improving (testing is upcoming). In the last 2 months, my forward strides are substantial, after years of being static. Those years of being stagnant were due in part to the house renovation. That took a lot of time, stress and energy, pulled me indoors more and seemed to be a long slow slideways walk, health-wise, but I have my own place again.

Is this a normal progression some people go through? Get somewhat better - stagnate - then suddenly jump ahead substantially. Or do you think it's just a mirror of my life and how much I was able to do the right things?

### Steve Z — 2026-01-05T03:19:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-364765>

Sharing some recent thinking -

Using first principal thinking, it appears I entered life with higher heteroplasmy. My mom and grandmother both smoked (indoors, the norm of that time). My mother had some sort of psychological break or blowup as a teen, developed an autoimmune condition (RA) in her late 30's and passed away a little shy of 50. My mom and dad tried conceiving for years, adopted my sister and about a year later they were surprised to learn my mom was pregnant. Taken together, those factors paint a picture.

Adding to that, I had early circadian disruption from jobs in middle and high school. Post college I worked indoors staring at screens for long hours doing shiftwork. With light blue eyes I am more susceptible to blue light, and always felt my eyes burn from screens and would turn the brightness way down.

### Steve Z — 2026-01-05T03:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steves-optimal-journal.29115/post-364768>

Update on me -

I have been doing CT at colder temps than ever before - water temps in the high 30's or low 40's.
I spent many mornings last summer reading in the morning sun for 1+ hours and was outside a lot.

Additionally, some lab results came back -

Vitamin D was 40. Not where Dr. Jack recommends but better than prior tests. And that's from blood taken in December.
BUN/Creatinine ratio is still high (around 19).
Testosterone was decent, higher than any prior test but not where I want it.

These numbers provide some clarity on where I am, and on what I need to work on.

# Thread: My new persona - middle Tennessee mom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-persona-middle-tennessee-mom.29142/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-07-13T04:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-persona-middle-tennessee-mom.29142/post-337035>

Hola fellow mitochondriacs

I’m a mom of an 18 year old Sebastian

1\. Is this a safe place to dox myself?

2\. Yesterday I consciously adopted a new persona - middle Tennessee mito mom

I tell people the true wellbeing starts with a sunrise and sunset daily - they say “when I fix my \_\_\_\_\_ issue I will have time for sunrise and sunset, right now. I’m in survival mode.”

People are open and almost all are excited to learn, one possible solution to their survival issues is.. daily sunrises and sunsets…

Yesterday, I attended my son Sebastian’s college orientation day at middle Tennessee state university MTSU.

I got the t-shirt and some sunset.

I love how the colors match the flag El Salvador.

# Thread: What 2 months of listening to Uncle Jack did to me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-2-months-of-listening-to-uncle-jack-did-to-me.29283/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-11T03:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-2-months-of-listening-to-uncle-jack-did-to-me.29283/post-338272>

Today Apple (and my karma) gave me a memory video and I was shocked to see myself and the contrast in just two months.

I was scared to move my teen and I to El Salvador. I was living in a yoga ashram in Tennessee. Peaceful. Safe (ish) But not allowed to show much skin. I would hide in the woods and find patches of sun to shine on belly skin I would show.

Now I’m blessed to live in El Salvador - in a house with no neighbors. Investing in many long hours of “junk maintenance” mode as Uncle Jack calls it. Making sure I’m turning as many white areas as tan as possible.

After lightening storms I walk to the beach which is a 5 minute walk and cover myself with wet sand. I meditate with hope that eyes closed meditation is still allowing the red light into my retina.

My work and zoom meetings are outside as often as possible with my bare feet on the ground.

Eating fresh seafood from the La Libertad market at least 3-4 times a week

I’ve enjoyed fresh sourdough bread from Bitcoin farmers market. And am not attempting to be keto. Although soon I may do that again.

The picture of me in white is mid may 2024 exploring El Salvador to decide if I wanted to live here. It was an easy yes.

The picture of me in blue is about 2 months later.

My favorite upgrade is how much thicker my hair is getting.

I’m thinking Uncle Jack might be on to something with his “light, water and magnetism” theory

I’m living a dream and I’ve been through enough tough times to be certain, that no matter what will happen in the future, I know everything is all good if I’m alive for another day.

This day… I’m overwhelmed with gratitude and perspective about how quickly things can get better when choosing to live life as a black swan mitochondriac

# Thread: A deeply spiritual song / chant I love to listen to on repeat many times
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-deeply-spiritual-song-chant-i-love-to-listen-to-on-repeat-many-times.29284/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-11T05:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-deeply-spiritual-song-chant-i-love-to-listen-to-on-repeat-many-times.29284/post-338274>

I find this song so beautiful - then I learned what the lyrics meant and fell even deeper in love

God is One, I am One with all beings

Truth is God’s Name… The vibration of God’s Name is what created all beings…. that vibration is within me…. therefore in my core I am true.

God is the Doer…. God does all things… God works through me…. God works through everyone in my life.

God is fearless… I am fearless.

God has no revenge…. I exist in my heart… I stay present… I don’t hold on to the past…. I live in this moment in full compassion and acceptance of what is.

God exists in the undying form… God is in the purity of our efforts to live in a spiritual way, and these efforts never die… these efforts live on forever.

God is self illumined… I am self illumined. Within me I have the power to find light, to find love, to find joy. I don’t need anyone or anything for this.

it is the Grace of the Divine One…. that give us Grace.

Meditate!

True in the Beginning

True throughout the ages

True here and now

forever and always true. Spirit, Truth, Love, and Light…. these are the things that are true, that have always been true.
