# Thread: Adrenal Fatigue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-15T17:32:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338450>

Hi All.

Since 8 years both me and my twin brother have been experiencing extreme fatigue, which suddenly appeared during a dry-fast. Now 8 years later we are still not better at all. We suspect we have adrenal fatigue because we have low metabolised cortisol (see 17 hydroxysteroids on laboratory work). We have been following circadian lifestyle for a year (also went to the yucatan for 2 months) and have build up a very nice tan. No change in energy though.

Symtoms:
\- chronic fatigue (unable to go for walks, mostly chair/couch bound, unable to sit up for extended periods of time due to fatigue/exhaustion. Not being able to work at all, no social live, no exercise. Lie down or sit down all day. We receive sickness benefit from our ex company/government).

\- besides the constant fatigue we also have mid-morning and mid-afternoon crashes when we need to lie down. Too exhausted to even lift a finger.

\- often too tired to talk

\- large parts of the day too weak to read or concentrate on anything

\- horrible overall feeling of wellbeing. Just hanging on to make it to the end of the day

\- sometimes we have reactive hypoglycaemia

\- Reactive hypoglycaemia after drinking coffee. We don’t drink coffee but we tried 4 cups in 8 years.

\- Brain fog

\- Nausea (which seems to do with hypolgycemia)

\- Blurry vision on days of little sleep

\- Extreme hay fever during May-June. Rest of year no issue

\- Premature ejaculation

\- Muscle soreness

\- muscle weakness

\- late onset fatigue

\- Gut issues all life

\- Cold hands & feet

We have also noticed the following:

\- we seem to feel even worse when we go low carb/carnivore/ketogenic

\- No more allergic rhinitis (probably due to eating strictly carnivore). It now only arises when around pets, perfumes, smoke , dust and hay fever

\- In the past, whenever we allowed a cheat meal (a lot of ice-cream and cookies) we have experienced a temporary bump in energy.

\- We tried activated niacin a few times and we get a very intense niacin flush (only when we take activated niacin).

\- If we eat beef liver we have hypoglycaemic symptoms all day. Even eating carbs does not seem to help. Does beef liver contain insulin? (Could it be that we are deficient in cortisol and that cortisol is needed to counteract insulin?)

Thanks

### rtd — 2024-08-15T19:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338455>

I suggest you delete the attachment, scrub your personal information from it, and repost. Not a good idea to have personal information, especially HIPA stuff, unsecured online.

### Thijs Oosting — 2024-08-24T12:14:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue.29309/post-338853>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Hey Jack. Your consult with us was great and we have decided to move to the equator for a year! We have found a great place on the vietnamese island Phu Quoc (10 degrees north latitude). It is a resort called: Cay Sao Beach Resort. The adres is: [63CF+VCC, Cây Sao, Phú Quốc, Kiên Giang, Vietnam](https://www.google.nl/maps/place//data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x31a7891044c22b89:0x6a81df65514d339b?sa=X&ved=1t:8290&ictx=111). The resort is located on the east side of the island in a low tourist, quiet area. Lots of affordable seafood available.

We will purchase a cold plunge bath that reaches a maximum low of 40 degrees fahrenheit in 86+ degrees environments.

### [Polar Pro | Powerful Cold Plunge For Daily Recovery](https://polarplungemy.com/products/sub-zero-v2)

Polar Pro is a premium cold plunge ice bath built for daily recovery. Drops to 3°C with a powerful chiller, filtration system, and durable indoor outdoor design

 polarplungemy.com

We prefer to set up our cold bath outside our bungalow so that we can be outside while taking baths. However, since the cooling machine needs to run for 6 hours a day and because we dont know how loud it is, we might have to install it inside our bungalow.

We are planning to book a bungalow with ocean view. However, all bungalows have acces to wifi. We contacted them and the owner is ok with removing the wifi station/booster in our bungalow so we can get cabled internet instead. However, we wonder if the wifi from the neighbouring bungalows would still be a problem?

Here are some photos in which you can see the distance between the bungalows. Would than be fine?

Thanks
Daan & Thijs

# Thread: From Blood Disorder to Optimal - w/Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### alleerielle — 2024-08-16T03:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/post-338476>

I wanted to log this journey for myself and for anyone else it may help with a similar diagnosis. I would love any feedback because my head is spinning and I am seeking more clarity. There is so much to learn and I want to know it all. Thanks in advance to everyone here for any insight. I am so grateful to Dr. Kruse for all the work he has done and for creating this forum. It is such a relief to finally feel like I am getting the truth.

I am Female, 54 years old and live in a suburb of Dallas, TX. Haplogroup H1. My Mom is 87 and still active and healthy. I work from home and I do spend a lot of time on my computer. And a couple of years ago, they put a 5G "tower" on my street. They make it look like a street light. It's not fooling anybody. I am working on moving out of here.

A little about my health issue...around 2011, I had surgery to remove a cervical polyp. It took 2 years for doctors to find the polyp and during that time I was bleeding a lot. Because of this, I became borderline anemic and my platelet counts went high. After the surgery, the bleeding stopped. I assumed the platelets would return to normal, however, even after an iron infusion, they did not. I saw a hematologist who suspected Essential Thrombocythemia. He monitored it for a few years but my counts, although high, were pretty stable. Since I had no symptoms, other than occasional fatigue, I really wasn't too concerned when my doctor moved to another city. I never really believed that it wouldn't go back to normal. My theory was that it was something like an epigenetic switch that got turned on. I obviously haven't figured out how to turn if off.

The past couple of years, I have been feeling more fatigue, headaches, brain fog and at times having difficulty concentrating. Also, my hair has been steadily falling out for about a year. Fast forward to a month ago, I started getting a weird symptom that felt like a pin was pricking me after I got out of the shower. When I looked it up, I saw that it was a symptom of Polycythemia Vera, so I went and got some labs done. And wow, they looked really concerning. I am trying to find a specialist just to find out exactly what I am dealing with. I'm also concerned about it being Myleofibrosis.

Currently I am watching the sunrise every morning. I go outside at lunchtime for my Vitamin D. I am increasing my sushi consumption. Drinking 1.5-2 liters 125ppm DDW for the past month and just started 105 ppm DDW per day. I wear blue blocking glasses every night. I try to get to bed by dark so that I don't even have to use them. I eat a meat based diet. I walk 3 miles a few times a week. I am researching more about what to do for my environment regarding NN-Emf. I'm highly concerned about it.

I have considered taking baby aspirin, however, my Dad had a slow brain bleed after taking it for years, and it concerns me. Any other ideas for safely thinning the blood? I've got to train myself to eat raw oysters. I have also considered Cold Thermogenesis, however, I am not sure if it would be a good idea for me since my blood is probably so thick. I am taking cool showers and baths, but mostly because I feel hot.

I am trying to get an appointment with a hematologist. Just sent in my labs today. I hate going to these centralized Cancer Centers. Something has always felt so "off" to me about them. It creeps me out. I'm going because I just feel like I need more information. So we'll see.

I attached the labs just taken a couple of days ago. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### alleerielle — 2024-09-28T20:58:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-blood-disorder-to-optimal-w-labs.29313/post-340126>

I wanted to update here. My lab results are pretty much unchanged. I received a call from the hematologist informing me that I tested positive for the Jak2 mutation. This confirmed the diagnosis of Polycythemia Vera. He indicated that I needed to do a phlebotomy asap. I am hesitant because my iron is already so low that my hair is falling out and I feel like the fatigue is affecting my work. I don't want to do something that will make me feel worse. Plus I am small 5'1", around 105 lbs. They want to take 500ml of blood. He said he would give me fluids if I felt dizzy.

He also said to take baby aspirin once a day. I am hesitant to do that also. When I conveyed this to him, he just said that is how we treat this. I told him I started taking the supplement lumbrokinase which is supposed to affect fibrin in the blood, break up clots and lower the blood viscosity. He had never heard of it. I don't believe I have any clots, however, it seems like a good preventative measure.

I have yet to meet this doctor in person. My first appointment is in a couple of days. My instincts are telling me to not follow his protocol. He is saying that he wants to keep my iron low indefinitely so that my body does not create more red blood cells. I am trying to understand this better. I cannot reconcile this in my mind though.

On a positive side, I am continuing with morning sun and sunset. And getting my entire body exposed to UVB mid day. Also I have continued the DDW. My BUN/Creatinine ratio has dropped from 19 to 12.5 since I started that.

I am also learning more about nnEMF side of this. I purchased the Body Volt Meter and am now shutting off the electricity to one room and sleeping in there. I feel like I am sleeping better. It has just been one week though.

Does anyone have any thoughts or experience regarding this?

# Thread: I'm living a dream - don't pinch me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-living-a-dream-dont-pinch-me.29334/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-19T18:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-living-a-dream-dont-pinch-me.29334/post-338645>

Yesterday's live Q&A with Jack was awesome as always.

I asked for insights on how to my frozen shoulder heal

I was not surprised - that a big part of the answer was be at the beach, grounding, putting my hands on the sand to get electrons into my shoulder.

I moved to El Salvador a month ago, a huge reason was because of the health benefits and Jack's consistent message about the health benefits of equatorial sunlight and grounding at volcanic sand beaches.

So my sunrise this morning was at the beach. As I sat there, with rarely another human in sight, looking at the beautiful ocean and rising sun... the message I kept hearing in my head was

"I am living a dream"

In what world did we ever imagine that the prescription for getting healthy is "spend more time at the beach"

I also knew I'd be returning home, to a place where I can sit outside for some more grounding and junk maintenance while starting my work day.

Eventually putting on some clothes because a wonderful local lady will be arriving to cook fresh seafood and take care of house chores.

My dream morning got a little better because when i returned home, my mom was awake and we had a nice chat that started with a lot of laughter. She got out to the beach yesterday and had a wonderful time. It had been many months since she had been to the beach. Although every day she's been getting her sunshine. She's been listening to Dr. Jack too

it's so important to slow down and appreciate dreams manifested. To savor the simple things and to find reasons to be grateful no matter what.

I've had horrible days and traumatic times and learned how to find the silver lining and be grateful there too. The silver lining for me is that in this vast cosmos, the opportunity to be human for a minuscule moment of time is a blessing. Cherish it. Be grateful I woke up today.

Days like this make it easy to be grateful.

thanks Jack, I am grateful for you and all the tribe members that help your wisdom reach more people

# Thread: Optimal Journal, profanities abound.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachael Bowen — 2024-08-19T20:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/post-338650>

Hello all - trying to keep it concise here in the first post of my optimal journal. Grateful to be here and to have already learned so much devouring threads on this forum, Jack’s Patreon, podcasts and in my third read through of EpiPaleo RX. I’m pretty fired up these days and drop a few f-bombs below.

Maternal grandmother was born and raised in North Carolina (35th latitude) to a farming family - tobacco, cotton and hogs. She married my grandfather and moved to Iron Mountain, MI which is at 45 latitude. She was healthy, active and outside for much of her life though she did have diagnosed osteoporosis later in life (mid 60’s) and congestive heart failure towards the end (she lived to 89, had a stroke alone in her home and passed away a day later in hospital). She was one of her only siblings (there were 11) to not have type 2 diabetes.

My Mama: Born in Iron Mountain, MI 1948 at the 45th lat, natural birth, breast fed. **Breakdown basics: osteopenia, pre-diabetic, spine surgery, neuropathy, diverticulitis, elevated cholesterol = statins.**

-   Healthy, normal childhood spent outside in summers. Lots of lake time and skiing in the winter. No health concerns as a child.
-   Motorcycle accident in her early 20’s, compound fracture and hospital stay where she went septic and almost died.
-   Moved to Nederland, CO at the 39thn latitude in the 60’s. where she spent a ton of time outside and in nature living a half way hippie lifestyle.
-   My older brother was born naturally in 1981 - he was a tough baby, colic and some developmental issues. Later diagnosed with a seizure disorder and learning disabilities. He was reliant on my parents and would never have lived alone - he passed away ‘suddenly’ (though now I am sure there were plenty of signs his neurologists and doctors didn’t see or couldn’t understand) in 2008 after not feeling well, going to the hospital, having a ‘dangerously low” white blood cell count, being put into a medical coma, discovering a small hole in his heart and then his organs started failing. He was on life support for about 5 days and my dad didn’t want to do an autopsy. Cause of death was ruled organ failure but they didn’t know why.
-   I was born naturally in 1984 in Montrose, CO. I was breast fed for \_\_\_
-   In 1995, she had a back injury and ruptured a disc in her low back. After surgery to repair, she recovered but has always had issues with this. Mostly neuropathy, back pain etc.
-   High fasting blood glucose around 105-115 over the last 20-25 years. Bouts of diverticulitis.
-   Osteopenia for the last 25 years or so, I don’t believe she’s ever taken medication for this but they’ve tried.
-   High cholesterol and a statin script that she barely takes though her PCP are insisting now. She’s reading EpiPaleo RX and even gave it to her naturopath which warms my little heart.

Me: Born at 38th latitude currently living in Denver, CO (39th latitude). **Breakdown basics: history of disordered eating/yo-yo-ing, two moderna jabs, live in a big city, work in a high nnEMF environment, have a good amount of tattoos (99% black ink, no areas fully covered), osteopenic, likely leptin resistant, beginnings of insulin resistance, shitty environment.**

-   I was raised on the western slope (38th lat) on a farm/ranch in my baby days.
-   Breast fed for 18 months
-   Moved to Boulder area and grew up there - lots of outside time in the summer and I was a tan kid.
-   Had a bout of ear infections when I was a baby and going into kindergarten.
-   Had skin issues (only ever in my scalp and behind my ears) that presented as a flaky, gooey scab. A friend diagnosed it as eczema. It was bad for years on and off. I went to a derm in college and was told it was sebborrhic dermatits - prescribed clobetsol. I haven’t needed it since I got myself back in the sun starting June of this year and at this point it is pretty much gone.
-   I’ll never forget when we got AOL dial up though my parents were very strict with me being online. I loved computer games and video games but they closely monitored my screen time until high school.
-   Started menstruating at age 11 - I’ll never forget the pain of that first period and have had mostly awful cramping and pain with my cycle since then up until very recently.
-   High school - we had computers with internet in the library. We typed up papers to hand in. I cruised chat rooms. I got my first pager and then a Nokia brick cell phone my junior year. I still spent a lot of time hanging outside with friends, smoking weed and drinking cheap vodka.
    -   I had a fasciotomy surgery in high school to relieve pressure in my lower left leg. Shin splint caused aches and pains in the lower legs. Never did the right leg. This has been my only ever surgery.
    -   I also started hormonal birth control pills in high school - I was told it would help with my cramps - it did a bit. I got off of hormonal birth control in 2008 and have never touched it again.
-   With the advent of screen time, me finally getting my own Playstation and TV and more and more communication with friends in chat rooms, i became aware for the first time of weight gain (having been a ‘normal healthy’ weight for all of my life).
-   College - marked mostly by disordered eating, “Freshmen fifteen”, lots of time inside on the computer, recreational drug use, a year long period of extremely restricted eating and rapid weight loss and many trips to the OB/GYN for ovarian cysts and an interstitial cystitis diagnosis. I was prescribed flagyl a lot though i remember feeling very suspicious as none of the things the OB was doing helped very much.
-   After college - lots of diet fads, bouts of exercising a lot and then not at all. More time inside, less time outside. Frustration with diets and exercising never ‘working’ the way it was supposed to even though I was a strict rule follower.
-   First jobs after college were in tech - spent 9 years in front of a computer screen in buildings filled with servers and screens and nerds and phones and literally every gadget you can think of. Weight continued to do ‘weird things’ despite best efforts.
-   No “major health issues” during this time - I didn’t have a PCP. I had my teeth cleaned regularly and had a blood draw every couple of years - all “normal”.
-   Quit tech in 2016 and became a nail technician. Started my own business which exploded after 6 months. I felt better - I wasn’t starting at a screen all day. My weight normalized easily with basic efforts. I started eating Paleo and then Keto. I felt like superwoman. I was still inside a lot though, working 6 days a week to build my clientele.
-   As business got busier, I threw myself into it. Few days off, if I wasn’t working I was thinking about work. I started training apprentices, the business became my biggest focus and time suck. It also became quite the little fiat farming operation which is how I’m framing it these days.
-   2020 shut all of it down. Didn’t work for two months, came back and followed all the rules because “they" were shutting businesses like mine down for non-compliance and “fellow community members”were ratting out these businesses for the same.
-   March and April 2021, got two moderna jabs. May, June, July, Aug, Sept, Oct 2021 - had the most insane periods of my life. Clotted blood so dark it seemed black and had full blown covid symptoms for the first two days of my period. No one believed me that it was jab related. No doc would even talk to me about it .
-   November 2021, got covid….BAD. I was furious. Wasn’t I supposed to be ‘protected’
-   December 2021 - FINALLY got my head out of my own ass, started listening to my ball and chain who RAGED against me getting vaccinated and began the long journey of re-educating myself towards what I now know to be a decentralized life. Spoiler alert - I fuck up a few more times.
-   July 2022 - Nov 2023 - began a lifestyle challenge and start counting macros and exercising in a gym 3-4x/week first thing in the morning. Something doesn’t feel right after months and still no ‘results’. My coaches solution is to cut more calories and eat things like yogurt and dates right before bed. At this suggestion, I opt out. I know something is wrong but I don’t know what yet.
-   December 2022 - I get a dexa scan and it shows I am ‘almost’ osteopenic and I have a high body fat % (38%). Which shouldn’t be the case with all the macro counting and exercise… HA!
-   February 2023 - I find a functional medicine NP and she’s the first person who will listen to me about the side effects of the jab. We start doing tests (duh). And I start taking supplements (also duh).
    -   I’ll post labs down below but I had a high fasting blood glucose and A1C (5.7) as well as thyroid hormone issues and a high CRP. She \*did not test vitamin D levels\*.
-   Feb 2023 - December 2023: Prescribed liothyronine for thyroid from functional NP - 25mg. Started feeling better. Was still counting macros and working out and not seeing any benefit to that either in my body composition or on the scale. Energy sucked, brain sucked, eating was towing the line disordered, I was very concerned about my elevated A1C and fasting blood glucose and was able to bring those numbers down over the course of a year.
    -   I had a SARS COV2 Spike IgG antibody test run in an effort to see what the jab was doing in my system - the first value was 15828 and the second, done in july of 2024 was higher at 20003. Would love to know if this means anything while also knowing that redox is the best medicine for jabs and also tropic living.
-   January 2024 - After reading more and educating myself, I ask functional med lady for a vitamin D test, feeling incredulous at this point that she didn’t order one for me initially.
-   February 2024 - Vitamin d is in the absolute toilet at 25. She starts me on supplements. Never suggests I go outside.
-   April 2024 - In the process of listening to as many RFK Jr podcasts I can, I’m introduced to the Uncle Jack, RFK, Rick episode by a dear friend and my mind is blown into smithereens. An obsessive, almost manic devouring of podcasts, blogs, posts, threads, books and conversations follow and continue.
-   **April 2024 - present:**
    -   Started eating from the farmers market only in June.
    -   Eat within 45 min of waking, BAB
    -   Switched from filtered water to spring water from local spring.
    -   Watch the sunrise every morning, kiniki or similar, eyeballs staring for as long as I can (easily can do 5 min now and up to 10)
    -   2-5 hrs of early morning and afternoon sun 7 days a week
    -   Grounding 30-60 min a day
    -   blue light blockers at work and at night after sunset
    -   no lights on in the house in the evening, circuits cut to most of the house after dark
    -   in bed 30-45 min after the sun sets in complete darkness
    -   Started CT up in the mountains at the river 3-4 times per month, have a CT set up on its way for the fall and winter months. For now doing cold showers and some face submersion.
    -   mitigated my bedroom for sleep sanctuary thanks to Anthony’s help so my nnEMF is about 20. (haven’t done the RF readings yet though I know they will be insane living in Denver).
    -   Found out that my nnEMF at work is very high - 5-6,000 on the BVK. Working on mitigating that as much as possible while I fiat farm
    -   Reordered Vitamin d test - i’m up to 85 though I know the reading is inaccurate as I was supplementing as well. I stopped taking the supplement after this last test and will test again after 8 weeks or more to see the difference.
    -   Stopped taking liothyronine and all supplements from functional NP - very happy to have that $400 every other month back!!!! Also I feel better than I did when I was taking all that stuff now just doing what I've listed here.
    -   HS CRP - 2.0 as of 7/2024
    -   A1C 5.5 (up from 5.3 in february)
    -   Fasting Blood glucose (7/16/24) 90 (up from 86 in February)
    -   LDL 104 in feb 2024
    -   Fired functional NP.
    -   Planned southern latitude trips for Sept, November (See ya in El Salvador?!) December and February so far.
    -   Increasing seafood - I have always gotten pretty violently ill from raw oysters so working on cooked for now. Anyone have any thoughts on this? Haven’t tried a raw oyster in 6+ years as it is a wretched experiment but I am game and appreciate any tips!

Not short but hopefully more on the concise side. I have really enjoyed reading the optimal journals so a big thank you to all who have shared there. I find it fascinating, encouraging and often times more helpful than anything else I can find online.

Some things I’ll be working on over the next year:

Redox
nnEMF mitigation @ work
I’d like to become a CT savage by end of winter this year
Meditation (quantum breathing blog)
MovNat
Buy more BTC
Researching Business opportunities for long term plan in ES
Making connections with more decentralized humans, upgrading the close circle of friends and community

I’d also like to get better at distilling what I’m learning into ‘friends and family’ morsels. I talk to a lot of people all day and they really “want to know” what I’m up to. This is just part of my job and always has been. I am not holding out a lot of hope here of changing minds but I have to say, I really like, as Jack might say, “punching people in the face”. I also get fired up thinking about how this learning has already completely changed my life and I do have some altruistic tendencies.

Before Covid, I was slightly more okay not standing out or having a different view. That whole mess really changed the way I think and I guess I can be grateful for it now that I’m out the other side. Especially now that my brain is working well for the first time in what feels like decades. I’ll never be the same person as I was before 2020 and I truly feel that way now, too.

Thanks for reading if you got this far! Below are a few labs - salivary cortisol, Thyroid and a leptin test (when I mentioned to my functional med that I thought I was leptin resistant, she ordered this test for me).

### Rachael Bowen — 2024-09-03T18:03:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-profanities-abound.29336/post-339200>

A few things that I've noticed improving these days:

\- Skin texture and moisture improvements: living in Colorado, I have always relied heavily on creams, lotions and oils after a shower or being in water of any kind. I no longer need any of these products at all and my skin stays soft and does not feel dry. I do use a beef tallow moisturizer on my face (no longer using any products my esthetician recommends, she thinks I'm nuts but can't deny my skin looks happy).

\- Thirst: hardly ever have a regular feeling of thirst (especially since switching to spring water) and notice myself drinking less water (down from a gallon or more a day to about 80-90oz) and peeing way less.

\- Digestion: bubble guts and "oh shit" situations greatly reduced over the last few months, even if I'm traveling or away from my regular food sources.

\- Sebborhic Dermatitis: nearly gone, have stopped using all topical steroids and haven't needed them at all. Scalp stays moisturized though still a bit flaky, nowhere near what it was.

\- Light sensitivity: I consider this an improvement in that my eyes are getting more sensitive at night time which greatly incentivizes me getting my ass in the dark and going to sleep. It's nearly impossible for me to be driving a car in the dark now without blue blockers as the headlights give me an instant headache.

\- Body composition: slow but steadily improving for the first time in many years. I believe mitigating my bedroom and adding 5 min cold showers daily has been the corner turned in this along with everything else. Excited to see where I'm at a year from now.

\- Oysters!!!! Tried my first raw oyster in over 7 years last weekend with no negative effect. Very happy about this one!

### Rachael Bowen — 2024-10-05T19:28:23+0300

>
> I think the problem with grounding mats around electricity is that you are grounding yourself to the 60 Hz AC electricity EMF that is coming at you instead of naturally grounding like you would out in nature on the ground.

My previous statement might be wrong. Due to the "umbrella effect" EMF's are pushed away from the body when grounding. This seems to indicate that you are repelling the EMF created by 60hz AC current in your house NOT creating a current in your body from being grounded around 60hz AC electricty EMF.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Viv's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Viv — 2024-09-20T07:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-339752>

The Beginning.
The time is now. Time for accountability. Time for action.

I came across Jack around 10 yrs ago on YouTube. Loved what he said, it made complete sense, intuitively knew he was speaking truth. Truth that I hadn't heard before. Incredible!
Life got in the way.
How did I end up back to Jack? 2024 started menopause, things with me weren't "right". Started going to gym to retain and possibly gain some muscle as I was positive this was not going well due to sedentary lifestyle. Weight had increased too and needs to come down. Also started keto type diet. So basically trying to regain my health. Came across, Gabrielle Lyon and Hubberman, liked what they said but wasn't convinced on everything, however it fueled my desire to look after my health again. Then Lyon interviewed Dr Alexis Cowan, she mentioned Jack and the Rick Ruben podcast. Wow Jack Kruse, I remember him, surely this stuff isn't gaining traction. I listened to both parts of the tetragrammaton podcast. Wow!. And I'm back to Jack!
Thank you Dr Alexis Cowan for being courageous in your talking with your mainstream friend and her mainstream audience. Thank you for leading me back to Jack.
I bit the bullet and joined the tribe. Life is amazing, everything happens exactly when and how it is supposed to, and if you loose your way, it figures out a way to bring you back. You just have to listen and take action.

Location
\- Born 35 S (Murray Bridge SA)
\- Grew up 36 S (Albury, NSW)
\- Late teens and adulthood lived in:
\- 37 S (Melbourne, VIC)
\- 31 S (Perth, WA)
\- 20 S (Port Hedland, WA)
\- 31 S and 33 S (Central, NSW)
\- 21 S (Sarina Beach, QLD)
\- 12 S (Darwin, NT) 2 months only
\- 34 S (Adelaide, SA) Currently live in Adelaide
Where have I been happiest - Perth, Sarina Beach & Darwin. Port Hedland - not too bad. Weird energy tho. Edge of desert. High iron ore content.
The sun makes me happy. Hate the cold (low tolerance) and winter.
Yes we have moved a lot, my husband is in the mining industry.

Parents (divorced when I was 6yrs)
\- Father born in Albury, NSW. Died when I was 11yrs. Not much known about ancestry. Unverified ancestry NZ Maori.
Died of cirrhosis, was alcoholic. Vietnam Vet.
\- Mother born Amsterdam. Came to Australia at 4yrs. Developed alopecia totalis. Scarred her for life, suspected PTSD from family trauma from the condition. Now in her late 70's. Has some type of memory problem they think is due to oxygen to brain, I think it's PTSD coupled with loneliness and blue light toxicity.

Me
49yrs
1 x husband
3 x kids (20, 18 & 16)
Skin - tan very easily, olive, summer very dark if in the sun. Tan fades in winter but still considered olive complexion.
Have never worn sunscreen as I never burned and then when it was a criminal act in Aust not to use it, I didn't want all those cancer causing chemicals on me.
Never wear make up.
Haven't worn sunglasses in many years, when I did wear them, wasn't all the time.
I do wear prescription glasses for astigmatism, reading is worse than long distance.
Last 5 years since living in Adelaide, very sedentary, inside and lots of time in front of computer and/or phone.
Stay at home mum for 20 yrs. Grateful for the opportunity to be able to raise my kids. Homeschooled them for a couple of years when living in QLD.
University degree in Business.
5 years ago started dabbling in crypto. More into precious metals.

Health
Developing anxiety (undiagnosed). Especially while driving and stopped at lights. Weird, I know.
Get funny light headedness/dizzy at times. I put it down to ears (suspect menieres), hormones, could it be bp.
3 x cesarean sections
2 x bone spurs removed ankles (teens)
1 x screw inserted left ankle after fracture (late teens). Screw removed (early 20's)
Born with twisted legs due to bad breach birth.
Twisted pelvis.
Pain and limited mobility in left ankle.
Meniscus tear R knee. Part removal (?). (early 20's)
Dodgy right hip.
Don't go to doctors (last 15 years). Prefer to treat with food, vitamins, minerals, herbs etc. (and now sun)

A week and a half after joining as a member here, I went for an eye test. Hadn't had one done in 5 yrs and thought I should as glasses were very scratched and need new ones.
Optometrist finds a piece of suspected plaque in left eye blood vessel. Says to visit GP (family doctor) for vascular work up as could mean there is a problem. I have not gone to a doctor yet. I guess I probably should. Am I a ticking time bomb?
I put this issue in the Ask Jack forum. He answered! So grateful.
Jack says they are thinking it is PAD but he said I am blue light toxic (which I know is correct) and gave me a link to blog with video about NO and PAD.
Can healing blue light toxicity heal PAD or whatever I have? Can it dissolve the eye plaque? Just changing from blue light toxic to not being blue light toxic ?? I wonder.

Currently working on: (a long way to go until optimal!)
\- Developing courage to see doctor.
\- Increasing NO production. Trying to learn if it's possible to retore broken pathways. How to know if pathways are broken?
\- Eliminating Blue light at night - Have standard blue block glasses and waiting for delivery of prescription ones. Using once sun goes down. Made bedroom as dark as possible. Changed most house light blubs to bio lights. No blue light portable lights in bathroom for night showers and going to toilet at night.
\- Eliminating blue light in day - trying to wear blue blockers while at computer, will be easier when prescription ones arrive. Installed Iris on computer. Phone on most warm night shift setting always.
\- Trying to be outdoors as much as possible. Ranging from 1 to 4 hours a day currently. Easier to do when it's sunny. However finding it difficult to be outside when I have things that need doing inside. I will work it out.
\- AM sunlight. In 3 weeks since starting have missed only 3 days of sunrise. I stay out for approx 30-45mins (full clothing to stay warm atm. Summer will be easier!). Alarm goes off and I hear Jacks voice "embrace the suck. Get off your lazy arse" etc I'm not a morning person, especially in winter, so am so proud of this effort, something I never thought I would be able to do. I feel so much better for it. I am sleeping so much better. I'm actually enjoying it! It's going to be great when it gets warmer and sunnier.
\- Grounding when outside.
\-nnEMF, trying to break my addiction to carrying my phone on me all the time. Trying to break my addiction to looking at phone all the time, easier to do with more sunlight. Makes me think it's a dopamine issue. Much more to do with the nnEMF side of things.
\- grounding at night. I've had a grounding sheet on my bed for over 10 yrs. However now worried when I hear Jack talk about dirty electricity. Need to look into this further.
\- learning about testing for nnEMF. Finding it difficult to understand all this. Used husbands multimeter (he used to be an electrician). Tested body voltage at bed time. Runs at about 1.95mV. I read somewhere on here about staying under 2mV at night is the aim? Need to get better informed. As well as understanding if I am measuring correctly.
\- Gaining an understanding of the water that is made in the mito and the effects of nnEMF on that, I think this is also a problem. However sunlight creates it, so I need to learn more and sort this out.
\- Diet. I've always been very up and down with healthy diet. Haven't eaten much meat in many years. Have started eating grass fed beef and it helps with making me feel better. Eliminated grains and sugar, however slip sometimes. Need to be vigilant as need to ensure these are not destroying arteries and I need to loose weight. I need to read Jack's Epi Paleo Rx, will order next week. Not sure if I can do shellfish though.....I struggle to stomach fish! However I'm starting to think I am deficient in DHA.

That's a lot in one go! I wonder if anyone will actually read it, I'm surprised I had the courage to write all this about myself on a forum, I'm usually a very private person.
If you have read it, hello and thank you.

This is my start. I will continue reading, listening, learning. Is it possible to learn all the science? Yes I can!
I have so many questions now and I will have so many as I continue. Jack's material is so complicated yet so simplistic.
I wish I joined 10 yrs ago, but if I did would it have worked out for me? no one knows. All I know is nature/the universe/the source has decided now is the time and nature makes no mistakes.

Sunshine is life - I've known it forever, now I know it's ok to know it.
I am so grateful to Dr Jack Kruse and to be here with you all.

### Rusty — 2024-09-21T12:05:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-339789>

> [Viv said:](/goto/post?id=339752)
>
> \- grounding at night. I've had a grounding sheet on my bed for over 10 yrs. However now worried when I hear Jack talk about dirty electricity. Need to look into this further.

Welcome Viv!

Grounding is something a lot of people misunderstand because we tend to lump everything together. Grounding via grounding mats plugged into the wall isn't as the same as therapeutic Grounding.

The first one is **Grounding** as **Shielding**. It’s important to know the difference.

When we talk about **grounding for electric fields**, we’re actually talking about grounding as shielding. If you’re around AC electricity, grounding lets that electricity flow through your body to the ground. That’s not a natural process because AC electricity isn’t something we evolved with.

On the other hand, **grounding for therapy** is completely different. It’s about connecting to the Earth’s natural DC current, which reduces inflammation and has real health benefits. The problem is, a lot of studies don’t measure this properly, they focus on AC electricity, which misses the point. The whole idea behind therapeutic grounding is to receive the Earth’s ions and DC voltage, not the AC current from man-made sources.

So, when you ground yourself around electricity, two things happen: your body becomes a conduit for the AC electricity to go to the ground, and at the same time, you’re still getting some benefit from the Earth’s DC energy. But let’s be real, it’s not natural to have AC electricity running through us.

You can however still use an earthing mat/sheet if it is grounded directly using a copper rod and a wire. Just make sure there's no power lines beneath the ground.

But let’s be real, it’s not natural to have AC electricity running through us.

Thank you Rusty. This all makes sense. Thank you for explaining.
I wonder how much damage I have done to myself over the last 10 years!!!
Ok now to rig up a different system, our power lines are above ground so I should be ok to ground it to the earth in the back yard.
Thanks again

### Viv — 2024-09-30T14:19:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-340195>

The Journey 1 (approx 1 month since joining)

Devastated I can't use my castor oil on my face at night after listening to the PowWow. Maybe the sunrise light will work better not having used it?
Hating the dry feeling of my skin after my shower. Suck it up! Let's see how it goes!

Def feel better getting the morning light. Didn't get up in time this morning, made me realise how much I've learnt to enjoy it. Sun is up before 6am now so looking forward to daylight saving starting next week, even though I'm sure it's a mismatch to what I'm trying to do now.

Started reading The 4th Phase of Water and The Body Electric. Still waiting for Epi-Paleo Rx to be delivered.
Need to read more blogs, printing them is easier (must find cheaper printer ink!).

Giving myself permission to be in the sun is magical. The chemical produced at the skin (forget the name) that makes us addicted to it, that's real! I don't want to be inside anymore, I want to be outside in the sun getting my tan, just being and thinking. This sun has wisdom. Struggling to balance what I need to do inside with being outside. I need to set up something under the verandah so I can use my laptop so at least I'm outside when needing to be using it.
I'm a little concerned however about something I read (or heard) Jack say about Australians shouldn't be in the sun as much or at certain times as others because of our OZone issue? I need to try find out more but can't find where I found that and the forum is so overwhelming trying to find what your looking for, it's like going down a 100 rabbit holes at once!

I have a very long way to go in all the understanding and the implementation, it's much more than just hanging out in the sun!
I am loving that I have finally taken this next step in really getting into Jack's work, still kicking myself I didn't go ahead with it 10yrs ago.

The diet is not going so well, so I really need to concentrate on that.
nnEMF this is a tough one, I really need to get onto this. More life changes.

Still need to go see the doctor.... keep putting it off.

### drsongihan — 2024-10-01T04:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-340210>

> [Viv said:](/goto/post?id=340207)
>
> Exactly, how much residue is in left in the layers of the skin by the morning? No way to tell (that I know of).

Welcome Viv!

I joined the tribe for a different reason but there are many “collateral” benefits I found out when following the protocol. I never liked using products for my skin, but did sometimes use them because I was hammered that I have to blah blah. I completely stopped using any product once I started to follow the protocol and found a few things about my skin:

\- Infrared light is a natural moisturizer. Morning and evening sunlight. You can biohack with in infrared/far infrared panel if you have to. My skin is naturally moisturized by doing nothing except to go out to the sun as much as I can, especially morning light. Infrared evens out uneven coloration too but it takes a long time. 2 hours every morning in sun would do, but any amount of time helps.
\- Blue light, indoors and especially in the evening, dries out your skin. For that blue blocking glass is not enough since it’s your skin that’s exposed to blue light. In my case there is no more LED in my house, but you can also just cover up from indoor light.
\- UV heals broken and scarred skin and heals wounds. My legs had perpetual scars. Turns out I never exposed them to UV. So UV heals them and red light moisturizes. Same principle as for face. My leg scars are completely gone. What were once scars are dried and slightly white. I have to wait for my melanocytes to migrate around to establish even coloring. The first part took 3 solid months, and the latter probably will take the same amount, except it’s now Fall in the 40th latitude. So we will see how long it takes.

PS. I think mild CT also moisturizes the skin or makes it more elastic but I am not sure.

Hope it helps.

### Viv — 2024-10-02T02:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-340268>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=340210)
>
> Welcome Viv!
>
> I joined the tribe for a different reason but there are many “collateral” benefits I found out when following the protocol. I never liked using products for my skin, but did sometimes use them because I was hammered that I have to blah blah. I completely stopped using any product once I started to follow the protocol and found a few things about my skin:
>
> \- Infrared light is a natural moisturizer. Morning and evening sunlight. You can biohack with in infrared/far infrared panel if you have to. My skin is naturally moisturized by doing nothing except to go out to the sun as much as I can, especially morning light. Infrared evens out uneven coloration too but it takes a long time. 2 hours every morning in sun would do, but any amount of time helps.
> \- Blue light, indoors and especially in the evening, dries out your skin. For that blue blocking glass is not enough since it’s your skin that’s exposed to blue light. In my case there is no more LED in my house, but you can also just cover up from indoor light.
> \- UV heals broken and scarred skin and heals wounds. My legs had perpetual scars. Turns out I never exposed them to UV. So UV heals them and red light moisturizes. Same principle as for face. My leg scars are completely gone. What were once scars are dried and slightly white. I have to wait for my melanocytes to migrate around to establish even coloring. The first part took 3 solid months, and the latter probably will take the same amount, except it’s now Fall in the 40th latitude. So we will see how long it takes.
>
> PS. I think mild CT also moisturizes the skin or makes it more elastic but I am not sure.
>
> Hope it helps.

Thank you so much for this, yes it is very helpful. The mositurising effects I suspected, thank you for validating. I have noticed since getting more sun and a great tan that my legs have become so very dry (kind of scaly), more than any other part of the body. I'm wondering if it means I have an issue in my legs that I am unaware of?
I will continue reading and researching I'm sure all this incredible information will kick in at some point! Starting to wish I studied science instead of humanities/business!!!
I am loving the kindness and camaraderie of this tribe

I don’t know enough to really answer - maybe Jack will. But I can give you a collection of my observations.

My face is not dry and well regulated. I have stil slightly uneven coloring but I am pretty sure if I could stay 4-5 hours solidly out, morning and evening, in a good climate, all of these will go away.

Being in the suburbs near Chicago the natural sunlight is not always there on demand. So I admit I biohack by having a high intensity infrared/far infrared panel next to my face every early evening for 1-2 hours while I read or cook etc.

My leg is a different story. I just managed to get the scars to heal completely and disappear. My legs are also very dry. But I think I had a lot more problems there to begin with and my exposure to sun on face vs leg is like 10:1.

So my guess is that indeed you might have had more problems on your leg to begin with and it’s a lot more work to expose your legs to the sun. I am quite certain that consistent exposure of sunlight and complete elimination of narrow band blue light will probably solve the skin issues.

I am just speculating based on what I read and some personal trial and error.

So, CT should promote collagen production, counteract stress factors and maintain skin firmness. However, I remember Jack saying, and also several other experts, that too cold CT temperature can generate stress factors in your body and hence is counteractive. The threshold temperature is different for men and women. Generally, I heard 50-55 degF, but for women above 45 and older, apparently the ideal CT temperature is above 55, even 60. Maybe your CT temperature is too cold?

Another aspect is that CT is supposed to induce the adipose pathway and generate melanin from inside you, and likely uses UV photons inside us as a mechanism. If we think of CT having an effect like UV, then I assume there is a counter-balance needed from infrared / far-infrared light for regeneration and moisturizing. My speculation is that milder CT, and then counterbalance with infrared and near-infrared light with maximum sunlight exposure even during winter and also add infrared panel, that might get you the "summer skin" back?

### Viv — 2025-07-17T05:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-356470>

Been a while so thought i should update to keep myself accountable.
I've been struggling to adapt to winter at 34 S lat. I am now understanding the notion of light stability/instability Jack talks about. Adjusting to summer is much easier. It's a learning curve adjusting to winter knowing what I now know. However, Still getting out rain hail or shine for sunrise.
ALAN still haunts me, yes I am addicted to my phone......This must stop and I have the power to stop it. Just do it!
Nail polish is now a distant memory.
With the latest ATH, I checked .....nice surprise, had more than I thought I did (hadn't checked for a couple of years). Not a lot in the grand scheme of things, happy with it being more than I thought tho and happy with the double in value since 2021 when it went in.
Concerned with everything going on at the moment being in Australia.....shit, where's it all going to go for us. I need to find a way to create a citadel.
Need to be outside, have struggled with this in winter. Again, get off your arse and just do it!
I feel my vision is getting worse......(get he f outside!).
Have started taking NO lozenge from Dr Nathan Bryan to help with PAD that Jack thinks I have (as well as blue light tox). One of the pow wow's Jack said he was ok with it when another lady asked about it. Hope this is still the case. I am finding it is helping with my energy levels, I'll take that. Also reading Dr Bryans book.
Very concerned about brain health after moving my mother to Adelaide and her living here for 2 months.....77yrs her memory is very bad, her mental health is out of this world and her nervous system seems shot (heightened, like the opposite of a brown out) she's in big trouble. So from what I have learned (mitro from maternal side) myself (and my kids) need to take brain health VERY seriously now....or face the same. Get the f into the sun now!
Hope everyone who reads this is well and thriving.

### MayaB — 2025-08-03T14:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vivs-journal.29466/post-357431>

> [Viv said:](/goto/post?id=339752)
>
> The Beginning.
> The time is now. Time for accountability. Time for action.
>
> I came across Jack around 10 yrs ago on YouTube. Loved what he said, it made complete sense, intuitively knew he was speaking truth. Truth that I hadn't heard before. Incredible!
> Life got in the way.
> How did I end up back to Jack? 2024 started menopause, things with me weren't "right". Started going to gym to retain and possibly gain some muscle as I was positive this was not going well due to sedentary lifestyle. Weight had increased too and needs to come down. Also started keto type diet. So basically trying to regain my health. Came across, Gabrielle Lyon and Hubberman, liked what they said but wasn't convinced on everything, however it fueled my desire to look after my health again. Then Lyon interviewed Dr Alexis Cowan, she mentioned Jack and the Rick Ruben podcast. Wow Jack Kruse, I remember him, surely this stuff isn't gaining traction. I listened to both parts of the tetragrammaton podcast. Wow!. And I'm back to Jack!
> Thank you Dr Alexis Cowan for being courageous in your talking with your mainstream friend and her mainstream audience. Thank you for leading me back to Jack.
> I bit the bullet and joined the tribe. Life is amazing, everything happens exactly when and how it is supposed to, and if you loose your way, it figures out a way to bring you back. You just have to listen and take action.
>
> Location
> \- Born 35 S (Murray Bridge SA)
> \- Grew up 36 S (Albury, NSW)
> \- Late teens and adulthood lived in:
> \- 37 S (Melbourne, VIC)
> \- 31 S (Perth, WA)
> \- 20 S (Port Hedland, WA)
> \- 31 S and 33 S (Central, NSW)
> \- 21 S (Sarina Beach, QLD)
> \- 12 S (Darwin, NT) 2 months only
> \- 34 S (Adelaide, SA) Currently live in Adelaide
> Where have I been happiest - Perth, Sarina Beach & Darwin. Port Hedland - not too bad. Weird energy tho. Edge of desert. High iron ore content.
> The sun makes me happy. Hate the cold (low tolerance) and winter.
> Yes we have moved a lot, my husband is in the mining industry.
>
> Parents (divorced when I was 6yrs)
> \- Father born in Albury, NSW. Died when I was 11yrs. Not much known about ancestry. Unverified ancestry NZ Maori.
> Died of cirrhosis, was alcoholic. Vietnam Vet.
> \- Mother born Amsterdam. Came to Australia at 4yrs. Developed alopecia totalis. Scarred her for life, suspected PTSD from family trauma from the condition. Now in her late 70's. Has some type of memory problem they think is due to oxygen to brain, I think it's PTSD coupled with loneliness and blue light toxicity.
>
> Me
> 49yrs
> 1 x husband
> 3 x kids (20, 18 & 16)
> Skin - tan very easily, olive, summer very dark if in the sun. Tan fades in winter but still considered olive complexion.
> Have never worn sunscreen as I never burned and then when it was a criminal act in Aust not to use it, I didn't want all those cancer causing chemicals on me.
> Never wear make up.
> Haven't worn sunglasses in many years, when I did wear them, wasn't all the time.
> I do wear prescription glasses for astigmatism, reading is worse than long distance.
> Last 5 years since living in Adelaide, very sedentary, inside and lots of time in front of computer and/or phone.
> Stay at home mum for 20 yrs. Grateful for the opportunity to be able to raise my kids. Homeschooled them for a couple of years when living in QLD.
> University degree in Business.
> 5 years ago started dabbling in crypto. More into precious metals.
>
> Health
> Developing anxiety (undiagnosed). Especially while driving and stopped at lights. Weird, I know.
> Get funny light headedness/dizzy at times. I put it down to ears (suspect menieres), hormones, could it be bp.
> 3 x cesarean sections
> 2 x bone spurs removed ankles (teens)
> 1 x screw inserted left ankle after fracture (late teens). Screw removed (early 20's)
> Born with twisted legs due to bad breach birth.
> Twisted pelvis.
> Pain and limited mobility in left ankle.
> Meniscus tear R knee. Part removal (?). (early 20's)
> Dodgy right hip.
> Don't go to doctors (last 15 years). Prefer to treat with food, vitamins, minerals, herbs etc. (and now sun)
>
> A week and a half after joining as a member here, I went for an eye test. Hadn't had one done in 5 yrs and thought I should as glasses were very scratched and need new ones.
> Optometrist finds a piece of suspected plaque in left eye blood vessel. Says to visit GP (family doctor) for vascular work up as could mean there is a problem. I have not gone to a doctor yet. I guess I probably should. Am I a ticking time bomb?
> I put this issue in the Ask Jack forum. He answered! So grateful.
> Jack says they are thinking it is PAD but he said I am blue light toxic (which I know is correct) and gave me a link to blog with video about NO and PAD.
> Can healing blue light toxicity heal PAD or whatever I have? Can it dissolve the eye plaque? Just changing from blue light toxic to not being blue light toxic ?? I wonder.
>
> Currently working on: (a long way to go until optimal!)
> \- Developing courage to see doctor.
> \- Increasing NO production. Trying to learn if it's possible to retore broken pathways. How to know if pathways are broken?
> \- Eliminating Blue light at night - Have standard blue block glasses and waiting for delivery of prescription ones. Using once sun goes down. Made bedroom as dark as possible. Changed most house light blubs to bio lights. No blue light portable lights in bathroom for night showers and going to toilet at night.
> \- Eliminating blue light in day - trying to wear blue blockers while at computer, will be easier when prescription ones arrive. Installed Iris on computer. Phone on most warm night shift setting always.
> \- Trying to be outdoors as much as possible. Ranging from 1 to 4 hours a day currently. Easier to do when it's sunny. However finding it difficult to be outside when I have things that need doing inside. I will work it out.
> \- AM sunlight. In 3 weeks since starting have missed only 3 days of sunrise. I stay out for approx 30-45mins (full clothing to stay warm atm. Summer will be easier!). Alarm goes off and I hear Jacks voice "embrace the suck. Get off your lazy arse" etc I'm not a morning person, especially in winter, so am so proud of this effort, something I never thought I would be able to do. I feel so much better for it. I am sleeping so much better. I'm actually enjoying it! It's going to be great when it gets warmer and sunnier.
> \- Grounding when outside.
> \-nnEMF, trying to break my addiction to carrying my phone on me all the time. Trying to break my addiction to looking at phone all the time, easier to do with more sunlight. Makes me think it's a dopamine issue. Much more to do with the nnEMF side of things.
> \- grounding at night. I've had a grounding sheet on my bed for over 10 yrs. However now worried when I hear Jack talk about dirty electricity. Need to look into this further.
> \- learning about testing for nnEMF. Finding it difficult to understand all this. Used husbands multimeter (he used to be an electrician). Tested body voltage at bed time. Runs at about 1.95mV. I read somewhere on here about staying under 2mV at night is the aim? Need to get better informed. As well as understanding if I am measuring correctly.
> \- Gaining an understanding of the water that is made in the mito and the effects of nnEMF on that, I think this is also a problem. However sunlight creates it, so I need to learn more and sort this out.
> \- Diet. I've always been very up and down with healthy diet. Haven't eaten much meat in many years. Have started eating grass fed beef and it helps with making me feel better. Eliminated grains and sugar, however slip sometimes. Need to be vigilant as need to ensure these are not destroying arteries and I need to loose weight. I need to read Jack's Epi Paleo Rx, will order next week. Not sure if I can do shellfish though.....I struggle to stomach fish! However I'm starting to think I am deficient in DHA.
>
> That's a lot in one go! I wonder if anyone will actually read it, I'm surprised I had the courage to write all this about myself on a forum, I'm usually a very private person.
> If you have read it, hello and thank you.
>
> This is my start. I will continue reading, listening, learning. Is it possible to learn all the science? Yes I can!
> I have so many questions now and I will have so many as I continue. Jack's material is so complicated yet so simplistic.
> I wish I joined 10 yrs ago, but if I did would it have worked out for me? no one knows. All I know is nature/the universe/the source has decided now is the time and nature makes no mistakes.
>
> Sunshine is life - I've known it forever, now I know it's ok to know it.
> I am so grateful to Dr Jack Kruse and to be here with you all.

Just wanted to say hi - I also grew up in Albury/Wodonga - probably why I prefer heat as we were always used to it!

### Alain Dwight — 2024-10-04T04:44:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-340392>

I’ve been looking into Uncle Jack’s work since early June this year, here’s a rough summary

The main thing I’m trying to accomplish for myself is improved energy and clarity of thought, also want to support my wife and infant daughter in increasing their health as much as possible

The results are definitely good so far, but I want more

The main barrier for me personally is probably my career as a programmer, this makes me location independent, but ties me to my screens

\# STEPS TAKEN
\-> Watch every sunrise and sunset
\-> In El Salvador, working on residency (was in middle of selling house so tried life down here and really liking it)
\-> Leptin Reset diet
\-> Iris on screens, turn brightness and blue as low as possible without straining to see
\-> Run red + infrared light in room when using computer
\-> Boox Palma instead of smart phone (zero blue)
\-> Magnetico sleep pad (in storage while working on El Salvador)
\-> Beach every weekend
\-> All exercise performed out doors
\-> Removed all LED bulbs (candles and incandescent only)
\-> Removed all blue tooth
\-> Black out bedroom for sleep
\-> Shift to speakers instead of headphones, if using headphones only use air tube head phones
\-> Ice baths (in storage while working on El Salvador)

\# NEXT STEPS
\-> Settle into living situation
\-> Replace all blue lit screens
\-> Move office outside
\-> Ethernet instead of wifi

\# RESULTS SO FAR
\-> Increased energy
\-> Increase mood
\-> Increased motivation
\-> Dropped the 30 or 40 extra pounds I was carrying, currently in hgih 150s

### Alain Dwight — 2024-10-30T04:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-341734>

\# CURRENT STEPS
\- Dasung color e ink monitor on the way to cut down blue light while coding for work
\- Need to figure out good outside play area for daughter so whole family can get more sun, this might have to wait a few months until we coordinate better living arrangements, but still giving it thought
\- Stopped using red LED during work day, instead just keep window open to let sun in, seems this might be better
\- Vacation soon, as usual will use this to cycle caffeine out, I think Kruse might be right about this one too, I crave it, it's an addiction, probably a symptom and or cause of energy bottle neck, need to double down efforts to get myself to a point where I don't feel it is needed
\- Generally prioritizing sun exposure with free time, even more than before, the big payload should be shifting my work office outside

### Alain Dwight — 2024-11-23T03:20:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-342820>

\# STACKING SMALL WINS
\- new air bnb has great outdoor play areas for the baby, so more sun for whole family
\- home office has door to outside to let in giant sun beam for the morning, more sun for me
\- daughter grows a bit holder, enjoys the beach more, easier access to more beach time for whole family
\- remove lower permanent retainer wire (thanks to prodding from wife)
\- cut out xylitol mints (thanks to prodding from wife)
\- set up dasung color monitor, big reduction in blue dose

So overall, energy subjectively seems to improve. Removing that lower wire really seems to alleviate some of the difficulty I was having caring for those lower teeth and gum recession. This seems a lot better than the xylitol strategy for oral health, Kruse blogs/comments note xylitol undermines brain gut axis and it seems true, digestive distress is reducing

Vacation reset didn't go as well as planned due to some food poisoning, but still cycled out caffeine to reset tolerance. Notice now there is a bit of a ceiling effect on the caffeine, doesn't seem to do much after a certain point, and the more I take, the longer I end up sleeping, as if my body is forcing compensatory recovery. No idea how to verify this, just as anecdotal hypothesis and taking it as a good sign

Really need to work on eliminating this caffeine addiction

### JD01 — 2024-11-25T15:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-342885>

Hi everyone, I'm Julia, Alain's wife, popping in here to add a little to his journal.

**About me**
\- I’m loving the sun in El Salvador—my mitochondrial DNA is from Burma, so it feels like the perfect environment for me.
\- I’m 9 months postpartum and had a great pregnancy and vaginal delivery without complications, though I was induced at about 42 weeks after experiencing prodromal labor.

_Some personal challenges I’ve faced:_
\- Moderate to severe bowleggedness since childhood, likely from a lack of sun and Vitamin D.
\- Crowded and large teeth, which I suspect may also tie back to childhood deficiencies.
\- Knee pain after prolonged running, though swimming and calisthenics have been my go-to fitness routines for years.

**Adding to Alain’s journal:**
About ten years ago, I started getting bouts of tinea versicolor. A few weeks ago, I had another outbreak, and since then, I’ve noticed similar patches on both my husband and baby. I’ve always been told it’s not contagious, but now I’m starting to question that, especially given the timing. Also found out food poisoning can be contagious...(sorry husband and baby)

### Alain Dwight — 2024-11-27T03:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-342961>

\# MONITORS
Low blue, Dasung color is great improvement, Ezeye monitors on the way, consider alternative light sources for monitors that allow custom light sources, maybe emulate Daylight Computer of Spectrum View (spectrumview.com)

\# NOTE FROM BLOGS RE BLOOD TESTS
"This is why I advocate checking a patients omega 6/omega 3 level ratio on the blood test often. I check mine quarterly. Ideally we want this number 2/1 to 6/1 for optimal human health. I personally shoot for a 1/1 ratio. Moreover, as we saw with cholesterol transport and the LDL receptor, blood glucose from dietary carbs also compromises the production of the good prostaglandins for lipid synthesis in the brain. So when you order your O6/O3 ratio you should also want your fasting insulin level as close to zero as possible. Below 3 is acceptable for most but if you have a neurologic disease you really need very tight control of your insulin to restock the brain with lipids."

> Oysters will supply you with a lot of the iodine , have you been to the fish market in La Libertad?
> I would want to go there every day or every other day . Despite the traffic. So much variety and so " relatively cheap" to expat eyes.

I live the US near many oyster farms, a high tourist area with the NYC $. Going rate is $3-$3.50 shucked/ea. $1.50 each not shucked. Specials can be found, like buck-a-shuck or 50% off.

What are the prices you saw in Libertad?

### Alain Dwight — 2024-11-28T03:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-343007>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=342965)
>
> Oysters will supply you with a lot of the iodine , have you been to the fish market in La Libertad?
> I would want to go there every day or every other day . Despite the traffic. So much variety and so " relatively cheap" to expat eyes.

Interesting, not yet! We will have to work our courage up on that one, we suspect some raw cockles may have given us food poisoning a few weeks ago.

For us once we find a good source for something we want, we would likely set up a delivery schedule to get it brought to us, although I could see the appeal of some daily rounds as well

### Alain Dwight — 2025-02-01T04:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-346062>

Decided to have a little more patience and hold off on the blood tests. Not entirely sure what the blood tests could have even done for me, the end suggestion for mitochondriacs seems to more or less end up with get outside in the sun, eliminate EMFs, and if you concern yourself with food then choose an Epi Paleo diet. So the prescription remains the same, unless maybe there is some unqiue edge case with partcular nuances in how to go about all that.

I also noticed Jack often suggests 12 - 18 months as an adaption period for the body (eg. to the leptin reset, to equitorial sun).

### Alain Dwight — 2025-02-01T04:22:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-346063>

I'm now 6 weeks without caffeine, which is a huge win for me. I was drinking way too much for years, and had been fighting to abstain on weekends and vacations for a couple years, just to keep it somewhat under control, but down here it almost feels like it dropped away with barely any effort.

I just stopped for Christmas vacation and never needed it again, felt slight desires at moments, but always definitely felt it was not needed, and mostly felt disgusted at the thought of taking any due to how much the addiction has cost me. The desire is absolutely impotent, and gets weaker with more distance betweens occurances, just interesting to observe my internal dialogue and watch a vice fade away.

Needing caffeine to focus well and needing to cycle off when not at my job was a hell of an incentive too, it feels like I have my mind back because while I enjoy my job, my deepest intellectual interests are pursued in my "free time".

This has made a huge improvement in my energy levels, focus, and emotional states.

### urko — 2025-12-04T21:52:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-363593>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=342961)
>
> \# MONITORS
> Low blue, Dasung color is great improvement, Ezeye monitors on the way, consider alternative light sources for monitors that allow custom light sources, maybe emulate Daylight Computer of Spectrum View (spectrumview.com)
>
> \# NOTE FROM BLOGS RE BLOOD TESTS
> "This is why I advocate checking a patients omega 6/omega 3 level ratio on the blood test often. I check mine quarterly. Ideally we want this number 2/1 to 6/1 for optimal human health. I personally shoot for a 1/1 ratio. Moreover, as we saw with cholesterol transport and the LDL receptor, blood glucose from dietary carbs also compromises the production of the good prostaglandins for lipid synthesis in the brain. So when you order your O6/O3 ratio you should also want your fasting insulin level as close to zero as possible. Below 3 is acceptable for most but if you have a neurologic disease you really need very tight control of your insulin to restock the brain with lipids."

Hello Alain, could you please share feedback about the dasung? I'm thinking about purchasing one to replace my laptop's default screen. Did you also purchase the Spectrum View? I've watched the website and seems to be pretty interesting

### Alain Dwight — 2025-12-23T05:37:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-364159>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=363593)
>
> Hello Alain, could you please share feedback about the dasung? I'm thinking about purchasing one to replace my laptop's default screen. Did you also purchase the Spectrum View? I've watched the website and seems to be pretty interesting

Hi Urko,

The Dasung is definitely an improvement but pricey. I used it for coding and had to adjust settings quite a bit to make it usable. For normal browsing and coding backends it works, but it would never cut it for front-end or graphic heavy work.

I ended up purchasing some Eazeye V1 monitors, which are seem to be discontinued. Technologically they seem to be the same as Spectrm View. Basically a conventional LCD screen with the LED removed so that you can use incadescent or sun as a light source.

In the end, I wound up using Eazeye a lot more and retired the Dasung, just because the Eazeye picture looks better.

My wife was talking to [@AlbertW](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/39294/) about a potential concern with LCD tech polarizing light, wheras e-ink does not. Although I'm not sure light from the LCD is still polarized by the time it hits the eye. It's possible that e-ink are technically healthier. I'm not sure if we have that answer yet.

### urko — 2025-12-23T10:01:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-364161>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=364159)
>
> Hi Urko,
>
> The Dasung is definitely an improvement but pricey. I used it for coding and had to adjust settings quite a bit to make it usable. For normal browsing and coding backends it works, but it would never cut it for front-end or graphic heavy work.
>
> I ended up purchasing some Eazeye V1 monitors, which are seem to be discontinued. Technologically they seem to be the same as Spectrm View. Basically a conventional LCD screen with the LED removed so that you can use incadescent or sun as a light source.
>
> In the end, I wound up using Eazeye a lot more and retired the Dasung, just because the Eazeye picture looks better.
>
> My wife was talking to [@AlbertW](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/39294/) about a potential concern with LCD tech polarizing light, wheras e-ink does not. Although I'm not sure light from the LCD is still polarized by the time it hits the eye. It's possible that e-ink are technically healthier. I'm not sure if we have that answer yet.

Thanks a lot Alain I really appreciate your time to give such a nice response. I will go definitely for the dasung, would you recommend the 13 inches for traveling or just wait to be settled for the 25 inches?
In regards to the EAZEYE V1and the Spectro View I still have to research about how do them work because I read one of the SpectroView models needed some light source but the other doesn't but anyways. To me I don't need light screen to do my daily work so is pretty fine but my partner will need something better for design so I will take a look to Eazeye V1 too.

Anything that you can share we are really grateful to read and receive feedback.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-12-23T16:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-364167>

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=364161)
>
> Thanks a lot Alain I really appreciate your time to give such a nice response. I will go definitely for the dasung, would you recommend the 13 inches for traveling or just wait to be settled for the 25 inches?
> In regards to the EAZEYE V1and the Spectro View I still have to research about how do them work because I read one of the SpectroView models needed some light source but the other doesn't but anyways. To me I don't need light screen to do my daily work so is pretty fine but my partner will need something better for design so I will take a look to Eazeye V1 too.
>
> Anything that you can share we are really grateful to read and receive feedback.

Glad to help! I'd also add that EAZEYE V1 and similar models seem better if you're intending to use them after sun down. They require less light than the color e-ink to be usable. I use an 11w red incandescent bulb after sundown. The set up is simple, there is no LED so you have to light them with natural lighting or your own source like a simple desk lamp. You can also make your own by stripping down any regular monitor and removing the back + LED.

As for the size, I'd decide based on how much you use screens and if you are fully outdoors in bright sun while using them. If you use screens occasionally or are using them under strong sun all the time then maybe it's not as urgent.

I use screens often for work, and I have big windows and doors open but still not fully outside. In my case, I considered a regular blue lit screen to be a serious problem, even with Iris. In cases like mine, I'd replace ASAP as long you can work with the smaller screen.

### AlbertW — 2025-12-23T16:59:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-364170>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=364159)
>
> Hi Urko,
>
> The Dasung is definitely an improvement but pricey. I used it for coding and had to adjust settings quite a bit to make it usable. For normal browsing and coding backends it works, but it would never cut it for front-end or graphic heavy work.
>
> I ended up purchasing some Eazeye V1 monitors, which are seem to be discontinued. Technologically they seem to be the same as Spectrm View. Basically a conventional LCD screen with the LED removed so that you can use incadescent or sun as a light source.
>
> In the end, I wound up using Eazeye a lot more and retired the Dasung, just because the Eazeye picture looks better.
>
> My wife was talking to [@AlbertW](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/39294/) about a potential concern with LCD tech polarizing light, wheras e-ink does not. Although I'm not sure light from the LCD is still polarized by the time it hits the eye. It's possible that e-ink are technically healthier. I'm not sure if we have that answer yet.

I suppose you can test by holding up a polarizing filter. Should buy one and test.

But I don’t think you’d be able to fix the polarization of the light coming from the monitor. You’d just be hoping that light from non-monitor sources are great enough to “wash out” the monitor light source.

### urko — 2025-12-24T20:53:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alains-journal.29536/post-364202>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=364167)

You can also make your own by stripping down any regular monitor and removing the back + LED.
