>
> I use screens often for work, and I have big windows and doors open but still not fully outside. In my case, I considered a regular blue lit screen to be a serious problem, even with Iris. In cases like mine, I'd replace ASAP as long you can work with the smaller screen.

I use screens every day... but now we are on relocation process again. We came to the Gambia in late July 2025 but we are not very comfortable to spend the rest of our lives here so we decided to go to Guatemala by April 2026. So now is a bit hard to have a fixed place where I would have a marvelous big screen but maybe I skip purchase for now until we reach Guatemala.

Did you already order the Spectroview screen? I'm curious about it.

On the other hand, I don't use any light bulbs since 2023 and I use to cover myself when there is little light at the evening and always wear blue light blocking glasses when I'm in front of the screen. I would like to experience how these screens works, but to me I would prefer to work with computer just at daytime and use darktime to rest, but sometimes I have to use computer after sunset for a while but not till too late.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-12-25T02:59:30+0200

I would like to experience how these screens works, but to me I would prefer to work with computer just at daytime and use darktime to rest, but sometimes I have to use computer after sunset for a while but not till too late.

I doubt I'll orde a spectrumview anytime soon. Between the E-Ink and the Eazeye V1s I have enough screens to cover the whole family for quite a while.

Spectrumview seems the same as the Eazeye V1, just a regular LCD with the LED removed. In Spectrumview's case they also include a panel with incandescent bulbs. You can make one yourself by just removing the LED from any regular LCD monitor.

# Thread: Weird White Bumps on Neck Area (Solved) - When In Doubt Search the Forum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-white-bumps-on-neck-area-solved-when-in-doubt-search-the-forum.29567/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-10-08T00:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-white-bumps-on-neck-area-solved-when-in-doubt-search-the-forum.29567/post-340562>

I moved to El Salvador in mid July 2024

I got tons of sun - at all times of the day

To my surprise - after awhile these weird white bumps started to appear on the top of my chest area, and under my neck.

I wondered - how can I be spending so much time, in the sun and grounding on the beach... and end up with a weird skin thing

I appreciate how much energy Jack pours into humanity with his time. I see him over and over answer the same questions. I think its awesome in the last Live Q&A he referenced which blogs to read to get the answers. It made me curious about starting a "study the basics of jack kruse" study group where we can review the blogs he mentions each live Q&A. If you would be up for joining something like that - let me know....

back to the weird skin issue

Instead of asking what it could be... I searched the forum and found a dad talking about his daughter with a similar thing. Jack's answer (as I recall it)... a skin thing is always an environment thing.

As I started to review my environment - I realized, one thing I had not done yet, was hard wire the internet to my laptop. But really? Could turning off wifi and using an Ethernet cable really make a difference?

I'll admit - I don't always listen to Jack. I've made a lot of changes but I'm not 100% doing everything on the list.

So it was a facepalm moment when maybe less than a week many bumps disappeared. Within two weeks, 95% were gone. But a couple on my actual neck stayed.

It's great to have mito friends because in sharing the story I just shared, with a mito friend, she reminded me to use my measuring tools I bought from Live EMF Safe - only to my HORROR to realize a device sitting on my desk that is a backup battery device - was giving off a reading of over 14 (when under .5 is considered safe and over 5 is considered extremely bad)

another facepalm

with a huge wave of gratitude for Jack's wisdom, Anthony at Live EMF Safe mentorship and awesome self testing tools and for my mito friend, who understands this wild black swan mitochondriac journey we are on.

Once I moved it away from my desk a few days ago - voila... the last remaining white bumps on my neck disappeared. Within days... freaky how fast my body responded.

It's kind of shocking how simple it was... I still have a hard time believing it is that simple.

I feel incredible blessed to find this tribe.

May your day be filled with love

jess

### Mat.I — 2024-10-08T01:53:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-white-bumps-on-neck-area-solved-when-in-doubt-search-the-forum.29567/post-340567>

The circadian cycle of the skin is affected by non-visible light (not from the sun) in a negative way.

There is so much information to find. And the more I understand and the more it sinks in, I can read the exact same blog and get more out of it. I just watched the Kruse for Dummies which inspired me to re-read a book I read years ago. # Thread: Ali's optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alis-optimal-journal.29662/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### alimarie — 2024-10-22T01:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alis-optimal-journal.29662/post-341351>

Working towards better overall health and energy.

Had bloodwork done...the root cause protocol recomended list. Will get HS-crp and dhea soon. But Vit D was low at 35. We are going to start eating seafood daily. I am new to jack kruse and finally hit a podcast where he said if you are in northern latitude you cant get enough vit d from the sun you have to get dha in high quanitity. So we are going to be on it and retest in a couple months.

I am hoping this will help to clear some visual snow I have had for about four years. I will also be getting a red light therapy device to see if this can improve the situation. I am following the full epi paleo rx and we have block blue light light bulbs, blu blocker glasses, and we get morning light everyday and evening and spend as much time outside as possible.

I used to wear contacts and think that may have caused this situation or been one of the items to lead to it. I also used to figure skate first thing every morning also learned not good for my body. And then additional stress of marriage child and divorce. Improved situation by following root cause protocol by morley robbins after my child and remarried and less stress and another baby and my vision is better than it used to be and I have more energy but I know not idea.

I take a minimal dose of DHEA and pregnenalone right now and it seems to help with my energy all day and no more acne on my forehead(wasnt bad but was there)

Eating two meals a day. Early breakfast and a 2pm lunch. I go to bed around 7:30/8 and wake up from 4-6 am would love to sleep in a llittle more but my baby wakes up around that time and I tend to have trouble going back to sleep because I hit 8hrs already. I also dream a lot and have been a good dreamer most of my life. I know that was an item that was listed a may have good redox...

Looking to add magnetico bed insert, cold therapy, and vacations in this winter.

# Thread: n=1 at 40°N
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-at-40%C2%B0n.29694/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michele K — 2024-10-27T18:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-at-40%C2%B0n.29694/post-341592>

Hello from PA on this beautiful fall day. I am taking a quick break from sunbathing topless in a loved one's semiprivate yard. I am reading Becker's The Body Electric, and am about halfway through. Wow. I have always been very sensitive to lots of things. I started doing Kundalini Yoga in 2007 or 8, my crown chakra popped while meditating one day, and my sensitivity to light increased dramatically. I became even more sensitive to ALAN- I could see not only through my eyelids but the skin in my forehead. I wish I knew of Dr. Jack back then, lol.
I have been addicted to year-round sunbathing since 2003. My major struggles are and always have been with sleep and digestion. Stress and bad environments seem to be a major factor I cannot get away from. I am foreshadowing here and will resume later. Going back out for more titty sun!

### Michele K — 2024-10-27T19:55:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-at-40%C2%B0n.29694/post-341596>

A bitch nurse berating me into letting her give me the meningitis vaccine to live in a dorm at college in 2003 was a turning point in my health. My guts have never been the same. Quit eating gluten in 2010 I think. Helped somewhat.

### Michele K — 2024-10-27T20:12:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-at-40%C2%B0n.29694/post-341598>

I have always struggled with living environments. From 2015 to this past April I lived 100 yards from a busy (60-80 trains a day) railroad, infrasound hell! I have been high-level homeless since then (stuff in a storage unit and staying with loved ones who don't have room for me), two months after my father passed away and left life insurance for me to finally get my own house. I don't make much, so this was the only way.
After months of looking at cheap shitholes in bad neighborhoods, which I could pay cash for, I decided to aim higher and get a loan (after repairing my credit which was ruined years ago by me loving and trusting a sociopath). I did it and found the perfect place for me and my ways. The down payment was 40%, as much as the shitholes.
But it's on over half an acre! And set back from the other houses, too, with a creek behind, and a 15 ft wide pit where I can sunbathe naked, and hide from the wind doing it in the winter! No outdoor ALAN!!! At least 100 ft from the nearest router or charging device! Perfect!!
I was supposed to close and move in Oct 16. The Friday before that, I got a text while I was at work, from the closing agent lady my realtor told me I'd be hearing from. She said she'd emailed me some things. I hurried to the computer, signed a Docusign document, and ran around the corner to the bank to wire the down payment and closing costs like she said.
That Sunday on the final walk-through my realtor said again that the lady would be contacting me. I said she did, and explained. She said, uh-oh. Of course it was a holiday weekend and banks were closed Monday. Well. Long story slightly shorter, it went into the account of a Chinese national and they can't get it back, so I'm f\*cked without a miracle.
Thanks for reading and feeling sorry for me if you did. My sister is made a gofundme for me, if anyone cares that much. You can't make this shit up. Even the bank teller who wired the funds was fooled. A lady from the mortgage department said she'd never seen anything like this (or so sad) in 19 years. Am I low-agency? Stupid? I got a 1200/1600 on the SAT, and my IQ is in the 130s, but I feel like a complete idiot. Now I can't afford to finish buying my little Buffer Zone from the Electromagnetic Pandemic. I need help ASAP. I am sorry for whining and begging, but I am desperate and devastated.
I don't want to get in trouble for posting the link, but it is on my twitter page: [https://x.com/ArtistSlightly](https://x.com/ArtistSlightly)

# Thread: EZ's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ezs-optimal-journal.29746/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheOnlyEZ — 2024-11-04T08:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ezs-optimal-journal.29746/post-341917>

"A man can change his stars. I won't spend the rest of my life as nothing. I've waited my whole life for this moment." -Sir Ulrich Von Liechtenstein

This will be my story past, present, and future. For it is within one's story that you can find true connection to the meaning of their life. Good, bad, and ugly. It is the story that inspiration arises from. Uncle Jack had a story. That's why we're all here. You have a story and together we have a community that wants to push the limits of achieving our best self. There is no testimony without the test. And so it begins...

I was born and raised in Hobbs NM. Desert climate with the constant smell of oil in the air. Perhaps it played a large role in why I was a sickly kid in the winters. Thin framed "hard gainer" always a respiratory illness. Got tubes in my ears at 2 and tonsils out at 4. Classic allergy shots every 2 weeks. Bronchitis couple times a year. I still vividly remember having to sleep in a recliner, given Robitussin, and sucking on cough drops to sleep at night from age 8-18. Not every night but when cold/flu season came around. I lived outside though. Maybe the sunlight was helping to balance out that shitshow. My parents were health conscience about home cooked dinners most nights. Never sodas. Almost no screen time until 15.

I played every sport competively. Traveled the US for different sporting events. My parents almost moved us to Albuquerque as I was getting noticed for soccer scholarships, but I realized I could get more money and D1 offers in swimming. It was the right choice as I earned one to swim for LSU. I love Baton Rouge, New Orleans, and Louisana. Ironically, my wife, who I met when I was 12, also earned a swimming scholarship to LSU (before me). She was better than me. No shame. Still one of the greatest swimmers in New Mexico history. We swam 2007-11 and I graduated Construction Engineering. I used to build houses in high school, loved the technical details of things, and thought I should follow that path.

Right out of college great first job. Urenco USA the first uranium enrichment facility built in the US in over 30 years. I was in peak health condition and accomplishing life goals like Ironman racing. I was doing Paleo and crossfit. Found Robb Wolf and Abel James. I didn't listen to enough Abel or Paleo conferences because I never found Uncle Jack until this year. Finished the Urenco project and moved to my local city engineering department for almost a year...then my wife and I moved to Iowa. We wanted out of Hobbs. Start our own life.

Des Moines Iowa was perfect. Or so we thought (more on that later). I ran projects in commercial construction. My wife had a Biology degree from LSU and was a public school teacher. Then my work life became more toxic. 60hrs a week and bringing work home. My health and sleep deteriorated and eventually I walked away from construction. There was no side of that industry I saw myself enjoying anymore. Instead God gave me an exit ramp. The best club swimming team in Iowa wanted to pay my wife and I to help run their swim team. Full time salary just enough to live on.

We were good. Record number of kids joining the team. Team and state records broken all season long. Helping kids make it to national meets and earning D1 scholarships. Then our world flipped upside down. Extreme jealousy and internal coaching politics made us realize it wasn't going to work out. We made it 2 years. On top of that, we had our first boy. That was the beginning of some dark times mentally, emotionally, spiritually, and physically.

I don't know about you, but my parents and family did not prepare, or warn, me what it is really like having kids. It was always sunshine and rainbows. Babies and kids are amazing. Yes, my children now are a joy of my life, but back then it was darkness. My wife was borderline post partum psychosis. I never slept, lived on caffeine, and ended up in the ER with a kidney stone. This ER encounter also should have left a bigger scorn on the appeal of centralized healthcare (more on that later). I needed a new career and a way to provide for my family. I wanted balance. Make enough, be a present/active dad in my kid's life, not work 60hrs a week. Believe it or not I landed on becoming a nurse. Hah!

Nursing school only took me 1 year. I landed the unit I wanted. Cardiothoracic and vascular surgery stepdown (non-ICU). Story is getting good. Here's the kicker. This was August 2019. My health was better. My wife was better. I had two kids now, both boys. Then that little Wuhan bioweapon shut down the world 3 weeks after we had the 2nd baby. Now, no offense to anyone (or your family) or the choices you made that were affected by Covid or the jab. But Covid was the best thing that ever happened at the time. I'd been skeptical of the government since 2003 and the Iraq WMD lies. I knew I was on the right track when PhDs were the highest category of people not getting the vaccine. Thanks to Chris Martenson, and Kevin McKernan, and Jikky the Mouse, and Lynn Fynn. I found them early. I read the papers. I didn't know the SV40 link that Uncle Jack did, but I knew there was no way I was getting that jab. No one in my family is jabbed, except my dad (facepalm). He didn't listen.

I worked the front lines of Covid. You people outside of centralized healthcare have no freaking clue. What Jack says is right. It is algorithmic Zombie medicine. I asked questions. Suggested to doctors to try something different. But I'm just a nurse. The "standard of care" did nothing. I was calling the labs on positive tests asking for Cycle Thresholds on the PCR tests. They didn't know. FYI all testing done anywhere in the city was sent to the "state lab" for result. I got patients with no Covid symptoms on room air admitted for a NSTEMI and they want to diagnose Covid as admitting diagnosis. Even better. Who knew Covid cured the flu epidemic! And everyone just nodded and followed along. **When Uncle Jack says he doesn't trust the 55% on this election, he's wrong. It is way higher.** I worked the bedside. I talk to these people during 12 hour shifts. Boomers are stuck on CNN/FoxNews. The others are ordering Big Macs delivered to their hospital room after getting 2 stents put in their coronary arteries. So now I have found myself in a new little predicament.

I found Bitcoin in 2020. I didn't full understand it until end of 2021 but it matched my ethos. Orange pill. Maxi. Whatever you want to call it. I will never forget January 2022 sitting in bed talking to my wife. We had achieved the white middle class "American Dream". Go to college, get married, have kids, own a big enough house. But the world was controlled by centralized BS. We had early dreams 10 years before of owning land with a nice house. Bitcoin and Permaculture (I could talk for hours about Permaculture) were now my favorite passions. Born from the ashes for 2020. How do I decentralize my life after being on my 3rd career? I pulled my wife out of teaching to homeschool our kids. She saw firsthand how broken the centralized education was destroying our community's life. Then i decided to do what all parents tell their kids when their young. Go travel and see the world. So I became a travel nurse.

I've been to Missouri, South Dakota, Texas, Virginia, Nebraska, and Florida. It's all the same. Centralized doctors and Epic/Cerner charting galore. Once again you non-healthcare people have no idea. I literally don't trust a single doctor now. The last 4 years I took mine and my family's health into our own hands. Read books. Listen to podcasts. N=1. We aren't perfect Paleo but we are pretty close. But now that I found Jack it's a whole new ballgame.

I am always curious. I love learning. I'm a vacuum for new information. Polymath. I have 2 bachelor degrees and in 2023 I finished from a software tech school. Mastering this information Jack is sharing (who stands on the shoulders of giants) I am pursuing a new path now. I want to be a builder. Build the infrastructure that is required to fix the money to fix the world. I am trying to get a job with a Bitcoin company. Or one that believes in implementing the BItcoin ethos. It's been a challenge as most want software developers with multiple years experience. So I am metaphorically standing on the train of a broken centralized fiat healthcare system with one leg out trying to jump on the passing train to a decentralized future. Maybe in a way I'm Julian Mantle seeking the sages of Sivana. Either way onward and upward.

I will fill you in with more. This post is long enough for now. It is not so much a Savage beginning, I realize, but more a Savage awakening. Kaizen

I guess I should go get IgG and IgM tested to see where I'm at.

# Thread: Three TBI's - Red Light Laser Therapy Helmet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/three-tbis-red-light-laser-therapy-helmet.29820/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### La-Shawn Hill — 2024-11-18T02:00:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/three-tbis-red-light-laser-therapy-helmet.29820/post-342524>

Over the past four years, I've made the following declaration hundreds of times--"I've figured out what's wrong with me!" As I'd reached the milestone age of 40, I just knew that all my newfound physical and mental health issues were indicators of my entering perimenopause. So, it wasn't long before I joined a growing community of other women who believed that all our new mental health struggles were related to untreated ADHD and a need for bio-identical hormone therapy--except ADHD meds and hormone replacement therapy didn't help me. Though I could be just as wrong as I've been so many times before in the past, I believe I've finally figured out what's wrong with me and what I need in order to get my mental and physical health back. At last count, I've got three TBI's under my belt. The first one was a TBI that occurred at age 5, for which I took Phenobarbitol up until my early twenties. My second TBI was a result of developing Long COVID. What I 'thought' was the flu back in February 2020 was actually SARS COVID. I was in El Paso for work when I caught a bug I couldn't get rid of with OTC meds--I finally visited an urgent care facility. When I returned home, I noticed I had a newfound anxiety, even from being around loved ones. I was also having weird heart palpitations. Again, at my age, I was thinking 'it must be perimenopause.' After watching enough of Dr. Paul Anderson's YouTube videos, I now know that due to my already-comprised blood-brain barrier, SARS COVID entered my body and its spike proteins did a real number on my brain. My third and final TBI is a result of the MK Ultra mind control-damage caused by today's blue-lit tech screens. Dr. Kruse has recommended time spent at a location with better sunlight quality to improve one's redox potential. Well, I recently returned from a weeklong cruise to Mexico, and though I sunned myself as much as possible, it wasn't enough to repair my POMC and remove inflammation from my brain. I'm desperate, y'all. I'm renting a neurofeedback device for a month to see if that gets my brain back on track. I also did my first Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HOT) earlier this week at a place called Anchor Restorative Medicine (ARM). I rarely visit Instagram, but today I was led there and found upon an Oct. 31st post Dr. Kruse had shared about "decentralized TBI treatment." I've been working outside and grounding myself every day since discovering Dr. Kruse. The sun provides full-spectrum light, 1/7th of which is red light. After reading Dr. Kruse's latest LinkedIn article about decentralized TBI treatment, I'm certain I need concentrated transcranial red light therapy to get my brain back on track. Dr. Kruse, I really hope you respond, but I'm open to responses from anyone with knowledge/familiarity with laser red light therapy helmets. There are several companies that make TBI helmets, and I'd like a recommendation for the best one. I haven't had a single traumatic brain injury, I've had THREE (3), which explains why my executive function is offline. I'll finally have my neurolgist's appointment next month, then I plan to have a consult with Dr. Kruse. I want an MRI so that I'll have proof of my brain injury. I kind of already have proof of my second one, as Environmental Health Center-Dallas collected a panel of my Immunoglobulin (IgG) and Lymphocytes, which returned a lot of "Abnormal" results, which is an indicator of something attacking my immune system. I'm currently treating myself with FLCCC protocols for long haulers. I was healthy as a horse before the pandemic came along.

### Gomes1983 — 2024-11-18T14:05:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey.29828/post-342566>

Hi everyone, this is my first post here.
I started 8 weeks ago, my leptin reset... I was not fat or overweight, but I still decided to do it anyway. I practice sports endurance running for marathons.
I was already doing some of the stuff Dr. Jack talks about. I see the sun rising every morning barefoot. (I'm on the 38ºN.)
What I noted differently was my cravings had gone away, I was more peaceful and not explosive, and my sleep improved.
I haven't solved yet the light at night, but I'm getting better, I'm using less tech and improving the light quality of my bulbs.
But I'm concerned about my blood work values...

Total cholesterol went up 321
Try went up also to 160
HDL 77
Ferritin 287
CRP-US 0.05
Insulin - 9.2
Vit D went a little up 33 (no supplement)

But my glucose level went up also even though I was in a leptin reset with less than 50g of carbs and not doing any sport.
Should I be worried about it? Did anyone experience that?
I'm starting now to return to sport again and training for long-distance running but follow Dr. Jack's principles. (eat fat for fuel, using cold thermogenesis) besides sharing what I'm doing, I would like to learn from someone who's tried this before and or any thoughts about my blood markers.

# Thread: Deviated Septum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annav — 2024-12-26T18:04:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-344355>

Hello All,

I saw Uncle Jack's post about a deviated septum from 2019. I am looking for some feedback about surgery from Uncle Jack and the forum.

Do you have a deviated septum? Did you have surgery? Why or why not? Has anything helped (besides redox, sun, etc)? I have read the blogs relating to this and learned a lot but still contemplating the best approach to all the congestion and night wake ups.
I don't know if this is something that requires a consult, but I thought I would start by putting my question here. A little bit about my situation. An ENT recently confirmed a "moderate" deviated septum and swollen/inflamed nasal turbinates. I knew this but wanted to get a specialist's opinion. Of course, he recommends surgery.

What I have tried besides Jack's protocol:
\-Salt, saltwater rinses
\-All nasal rinses
\-No dairy
\-Elevated bed
\-Nasal strips
\-Mouth taping at night (still do this as it helps the mouth breathing)
\-Chiropractic help
\-Allergy medications

I am weighing the pros and cons of surgery. My biggest concern is I have to go under the anesthesia which is a concern for someone withs Alzheimer’s with my mom and all her sisters. It is very strong in my family history. Would I be better off with the surgery? I have read the blogs related to this issue (hypoxia blogs, sleep blogs) and some threads. My conventional ENT does not think I will get better without surgery. Jack's description of a deviated septum is me except for the nosebleeds and I don’t think I have sleep apnea. I don't snore, I don’t wake up gasping for air and feel 80-85% rested when I wake up. I have noticed slightly elevated BPM (60’s). The 2-3 wake ups to urinate are probably preventing deeper sleep and longer REM. I temporarily wore an Oura Ring and my sleep scores were 82-88 for what it is worth. Deep sleep is around an hour as well REM. I have persistent congestion or a runny nose, sinus headaches (improving!). It is hard to breath through my nose when I exercise but can when I clear my nose, and I am not exercising.

I have been following Jack's protocol for approximately 3.5 years and seen improvement in many areas. My Mom came from Italy (37 latitude) and I live in Ohio (41). I never miss sunrise/sunset, frequent walks outside, a great tan in the spring/summer/fall (need to get a Cool Tan suit), darkness at night, blue blockers, reduction of nnEMF (little tv time, less time on my computer, Wi-Fi off at night, cold showers, face dunks, grounding, eating within an hour of sunrise, in the process of trying DDW etc.) in the hopes that I could solve the congestion and night wake ups.
Other than the congestion and night wake ups, I feel my health is better than most 59-year-olds with no autoimmune conditions or other diseases and no meds accept BHRT. Potential setbacks include lots of dental work, good size scar on my neck from a surgery when 5 years old, 20+ years of excessive blue light exposure and currently live within ½ mile of high-tension wires. In the past I had low Free T3 and high Reverse T3. Free T3 improved to normal with BHRT and light protocol. I am very tan 8 months out of the year, tan holds and semi-tan 4 months of the year.

In conclusion, the last thing I want is surgery, but I am concerned about hypoxia at night and wish the congestion during the day is a challenge. My Mom had Alzheimer's as well as her three sisters. The deviated septum is from my maternal side.
Any input, success, experience with a deviated septum is greatly appreciated!

Thanks! Anna

### rtd — 2024-12-26T19:41:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-344362>

> [annavargo said:](/goto/post?id=344355)
>
> Do you have a deviated septum? Did you have surgery? Why or why not? Has anything helped (besides redox, sun, etc)? I have read the blogs relating to this and learned a lot but still contemplating the best approach to all the congestion and night wake ups.
> I don't know if this is something that requires a consult, but I thought I would start by putting my question here. A little bit about my situation. An ENT recently confirmed a "moderate" deviated septum and swollen/inflamed nasal turbinates. I knew this but wanted to get a specialist's opinion. Of course, he recommends surgery.

I have a deviated septum (right nostril, almost fully closed off) from a car air bag. Pre jab, I had a nasal scan that showed the problem is structural and I did not have inflammation. I did not get the surgery, which I now regret. Post jab I have had respiratory issues. 2025 I plan to get a turbinate surgery so I can breath through my nose. BTW, my resting BPM are 80-95. Too high! I hope full nasal breathing will bring down my BPM. I do low, slow cardio like walking and cycling, zone 2. I used to do yoga with different nasal breathing, as well a breath of fire. I cannot do those breathing techniques effectively any more.

### Richard.B — 2025-02-10T19:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346546>

I have a significant left deviated septum, resulting in near-complete obstruction of one of my nostril since childhood. This primarily manifests as intermittent breathing difficulties (characterized by one nostril being partially open while the other is largely obstructed). Apart from that, I don't have any other symptoms.
Not jabbed

My ENT told me that the only solution for this deviated septum would be surgery (septoplasty and turbinoplasty) and that it would substantially improve my quality of life with minimal risk of adverse effects. But I don't trust centralized medecine as they are incentives by the surgery.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) what would you do if you had a deviated septum that impeded your breathing ?

My apprehension arises from the possibility of physiological adaptation to the chronic airflow restriction or that resulted into this deviated septum. I am concerned that the resultant increase in nasal breathing following surgical intervention could lead to supraphysiological oxygen concentrations, potentially predisposing to an increase in free radical generation and oxidative stress.

### Adamo — 2025-02-11T04:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346589>

Chiming in and would love to know about this as well, as I have several family members facing the same issue.

### meredith24 — 2025-02-12T19:11:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346635>

I also have a very deviated nasal septum. I catch myself mouth breathing often. I likely have UARS. My sleep scores are always terrible with Oura ring. I am considering functional septorhinoplasty but have not been convinced 100% yet.

I am currently reading Breathe through your nose don't pay for it and it emphasizes the use of rhinomanometry to measure flow to even determine if surgery would help. I am also going to read Dr. Kruse's viewpoints on the matter.

Curious to hear others thought processes and experiences.

# Thread: Shedding skin I've been picking My scabs again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/unread>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Carly18 — 2025-01-08T03:28:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-344876>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I’ll start from the beginning… picking out a pair of glasses at the age of 7 because my teacher mentioned I was squinting in class. Myopia and astigmatism. After years of chronic ear infections, school was intimidating but I enjoyed it and now I could see the board too. When they showed up with fluoride mouthwash every morning, I did my part and swished a spit to “keep the cavities away”. But I did have a healthy fear of injections and managed to avoid a few of the immunizations which they did (and still do) in the schools.
A few years later, my smile diminished with crooked teeth. Of course my parents felt the best way to fix this was to get a mean, old orthodontist to straighten them. He decided I needed 5 teeth pulled (1 baby and 4 adult) so there would enough space to straighten them along with breaking my jaw and wiring it shut for a few months. They pulled my teeth but thankfully I pushed back on the jaw reconstruction.
At 13, I started feeling better about myself when my family moved from my small rural town to the big city. This year I ended up with chronic strep infections and subsequently massive depression after all of the antibiotics I had to take. My periods were also incredibly painful, meaning I missed a lot of school every month. The “solution” to this was the pill. It didn’t help much and it caused other problems.
5 years later I finally got all the metal in my mouth removed and graduate high school with an addiction to alcohol. Wisdom teeth get removed and I begin working in the retail grind rather than going to post secondary, ending up in a dark, fluorescent lit warehouse working shift work at any time of the day or night for the next 2 decades.
At 21 I’m diagnosed with an ovarian cyst and gaslit into surgery as the only option. They cut me open from hip to hip like a c section to remove a mandarin orange sized dermoid cyst on my L ovary and a follicular cyst on my R. I had to stay in the hospital for 3 days until I could poop again and while I was packing to leave, I pitched out the prescription birth control I’d been on since the beginning of my teens.
This was the time I finally started getting very disillusioned with the healthcare industry.

### Carly18 — 2025-05-11T02:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-352296>

My 20’s were all about drinking and finding boyfriends. All failed. I did stop drinking, I did get engaged and became a really bad ass martial artist to eventually succumb to more depression and anxiety. I left my fiancé to end up with a complete clown who I married in my mid thirties (after losing 30lbs and started drinking again). 6 months later I get pregnant in an abusive marriage. I’m forced to have a IV of antibiotics because I tested positive for strep while delivering my child. PPD/GAD creeps back in after I deliver and end up with a child with as messed up of a circadian rhythm as mine. We barely sleep properly, I become psychotic and incredibly unstable. I end up going back to work 18 months later and discover my husband is cheating. 6 more weeks off work to start my divorce and try to be a good single mama while working 60+ hours a week with a nursing toddler. Eventually, another 18 months later I end up totally breaking down, living in a new apartment with a toddler trying to navigate an abusive dad freaking everyone out but having to share parenting time. I couldn’t make rational decisions at this point and allowed this a-hole to gain shared custody when I could have eliminated most of his access. 5 years go by and I cannot return to the high paying middle management job I had, still dealing with panic attacks and dropping weight one more time. At this time I think it’s a good idea to start dating again and completely make more and more stupid decisions without being able to support myself. I move onto a new career, begin to run a new business and start making a bit more money to be able to keep my house hopefully. Then COVID hits and I get shut down, with a kid in elementary school, struggling with an ADHD/sensory disorder “diagnosis”. Thankfully, I get a glimpse into the schoolday while my child tried to learn online and immediately decide to homeschool. Unfortunately I can’t run a business from home, homeschool half time and keep myself afloat. 5 years later here I am, my kid living in a rural community, finishing school while living with dad, becoming more healthy and staying away from video games, listening to mama talk about how to eat and be in the sun. Mama also just sold her house, is sitting on a TINY bit of savings which will soon be invested like a savage and am planning to leave Arctic Mexico (Canada) for hopefully some reclamation of time. I’m in menopause, 50 lbs overweight, have a nasty case of eczema in my left ear (especially now in my temp living situation), osteoarthritis symptoms, a tech addiction and just became a Catholic. Welcome to my jungle folks.
(I do have a fabulous tan already though!)

### Carly18 — 2025-06-07T03:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354159>

> [Jojoplachta said:](/goto/post?id=354149)
>
> Good for you! How are you doing now? ❤️

Still struggling. I had a REALLY good set up in my home for nude sunbathing and keeping wifi to a minimum. I don’t any longer but I am always on the lookout for opportunities! I’m contemplating just leaving and heading to ES right away but I want to get my child started on his confirmation journey in Sept so I’ll try to stick it out here a few more months and get down to ES in the fall. Hopefully that gives me enough time to make some more money, store/sell all of my stuff, find decent accommodations in ES and maybe make some mito friends before I leave.

### Carly18 — 2025-06-07T03:49:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354160>

The best part about the past few days is all the comments I keep getting about my tan! It takes me all of 2 hours to be nice and caramel after some cooler temps this month. People think I’m part Native American ~ far from it. My ancestory is Dutch (father), Norwegian and German (mother). Then I start teaching them about how to build a solar callus!

### Carly18 — 2025-06-10T16:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354415>

Firstly, I want to say thank you to Dr Kruse, Chantal and Christy for hosting a wonderful zoom with all of us this past Sunday. The reason why I signed up for the Gold membership was because of hearing Noah's story on Dr Kruse's patreon and I am so happy little Emily has this tribe rooting for her. It also helped me realize that there is no alternative for my health but to head to the equator. Summers in Canada are lovely but the mornings are always around 6-11C (40-50F) so I don't get the full effects of the healing light of sunrise during these months either.

I've been thinking about my life since the chaos of 2020 started. I knew in that year I would have to leave my country. I have been quietly preparing myself for that inevitability but it still punches me in the gut thinking about leaving my family and starting over by myself. The only way I can see being able to make any kind of a legacy for my child though is to start over in a country where our health and investments can flourish. Embracing the suck.

Dr Kruse suggested when answering my question about pain that I read Dr Becker's books. That was such a lovely entanglement moment for me because I had just signed up for Dr Cowan's book club a few days before. We are reading Cross Currents at the moment. My goal for the minimum 6 months in Central America this fall is to discover a country in which I would like to work towards a PR/citizenship, build a citadel, grow a business, improve my health and STUDY. It buoys my spirit to think about all that time in the sun, healing, learning, exploring and making new friends. I need a community that won't roll their eyes at me when I tell them I can't live near a cel tower or eat after sunset or wear sunglasses.

If you'd like to be friends, please reach out. I would adore meeting up with fellow mitos in ES or anywhere in C America later this year.

### Carly18 — 2025-06-10T18:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354433>

ok, back to my journal....

Maternal grandmother passed away at 81 after multiple heart attacks, having to spend a few months in the hospital trying to recover. She hated doctors and hospitals. She had osteoarthritis and took medication for it to which she attributed her stomach problems. She had 7 children, oldest and youngest were male and the rest female. My mother was a twin and that was my grandmother's 4th pregnancy. The oldest son never married, lived with and cared for my grandmother his whole life, an alcoholic, had diabetes early and passed before my grandmother of a heart attack. He was also suicidal near the end of his life, going blind and with high blood pressure. The youngest male was 14 years younger then his closest sibling, my grandmother's 6th pregnancy. My grandmother lived on a farm most of her life, did not have running water for the entirety of it and drank spring water. She did have electricity in her later years. She spent most of her time tending the children, her garden, the animals and cooking/baking. As a child, I rarely saw her leave the house except to take care of the animals before I woke or to go to the bathroom (no running water = outhouse year round). She was very pale for the time I knew her and overweight. They grew crops conventionally so likely was exposed to many chemicals all her life. No big electrical towers on their property but there were smaller ones bringing power and telephone access along the highway opposite the large forest between her homestead and the highway. Her marriage was abusive and my grandfather an alcoholic. He passed at 96. His ancestry was Norwegian and they emigrated to the USA before my grandfather came to Canada after the war. Her ancestry is German but we don't have much information about that lineage unfortunately.

My mother had 2 children, starting at age 30. I am the oldest and my brother 18 months later. She's had anxiety all her life (I finally figured this out this year while living with her). Went into menopause around the same age as myself and had a hernia and pelvic floor reconstruction in her 60's. Her deliveries were also quick but she thankfully gave birth without interventions. She had elevated LDL in her 60's and took statins for 12 years before choosing to come off of them. She is now almost 82 and on no medications. She had basal cells removed from her forehead and the side of her nose a few years ago and is now terrified of the sun, wears clothing outdoors and sunglasses, is pale and grey, bitter, resentful and struggles with emotional stability. Her anxiety is off the charts and I am suspecting dementia will likely take her. She is very upset with me wanting to leave the country. She worked in offices, under fluorescent lights most of her life and into my school years and retired when my grandmother passed. She now has an engrained routine she refuses to change, an addiction to technology watching tv most of the day indoors, rarely spends time outdoors and complains constantly. I DO NOT want to end up like my mother. She used to be able to tan so incredibly well and when I was very young I remember her sunbathing often. Her trip to Hawaii in the 80's has some of my favourite pictures of her in a bikini, almost milk chocolate brown and a beautiful smile. My father is Dutch and emigrated here when he was 5. He had a heart attack last summer and is also set in his ways, living indoors but took my advice and did some red light therapy this winter and got a bit of colour back. My Oma passed at 98 without many health issues. She contracted pneumonia a few times near the end and was unable to eat.

Interestingly, all of my family has had core dysfunction in some capacity... father has a hernia (surgery to happen this summer), mother also and pelvic floor prolapse, I have a rectocele and my brother has had 2 hernia surgeries. Collagen failing.... my butt needs some sun!!!!

### Carly18 — 2025-06-12T14:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354550>

Since moving into my temp digs, the eczema in my ear is unbearable. I’m able to turn off wifi overnight without causing WWIII with my family now but can I try some topical Benadryl to help with the itch or is that too close to the brain to risk?
It seems to flare more with carbs so I’m eliminating those as well.

I’ll be housesitting all of July so I’ll have much more control over my nnEMF exposure and can tan privately most of the day.

### Carly18 — 2025-06-18T14:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354812>

It’s truly all about the light. Summer for me is by the lake. It’s focused on cold, quiet morning sunrises, paddleboarding, sunning all day long, campfires, reading books and slow, peaceful sunsets.
Nothing else matters. I don’t need much. Only enough for some local food and a roof over my head. That’s it. That’s all that matters.

### Carly18 — 2025-08-17T16:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-358369>

I did stop drinking, I did get engaged and became a really bad ass martial artist to eventually succumb to more depression and anxiety. I left my fiancé to end up with a complete clown who I married in my mid thirties (after losing 30lbs and started drinking again). 6 months later I get pregnant in an abusive marriage. I’m forced to have a IV of antibiotics because I tested positive for strep while delivering my child. PPD/GAD creeps back in after I deliver and end up with a child with as messed up of a circadian rhythm as mine. We barely sleep properly, I become psychotic and incredibly unstable. I end up going back to work 18 months later and discover my husband is cheating. 6 more weeks off work to start my divorce and try to be a good single mama while working 60+ hours a week with a nursing toddler. Eventually, another 18 months later I end up totally breaking down, living in a new apartment with a toddler trying to navigate an abusive dad freaking everyone out but having to share parenting time. I couldn’t make rational decisions at this point and allowed this a-hole to gain shared custody when I could have eliminated most of his access. 5 years go by and I cannot return to the high paying middle management job I had, still dealing with panic attacks and dropping weight one more time. At this time I think it’s a good idea to start dating again and completely make more and more stupid decisions without being able to support myself. I move onto a new career, begin to run a new business and start making a bit more money to be able to keep my house hopefully. Then COVID hits and I get shut down, with a kid in elementary school, struggling with an ADHD/sensory disorder “diagnosis”. Thankfully, I get a glimpse into the schoolday while my child tried to learn online and immediately decide to homeschool. Unfortunately I can’t run a business from home, homeschool half time and keep myself afloat. 5 years later here I am, my kid living in a rural community, finishing school while living with dad, becoming more healthy and staying away from video games, listening to mama talk about how to eat and be in the sun. Mama also just sold her house, is sitting on a TINY bit of savings which will soon be invested like a savage and am planning to leave Arctic Mexico (Canada) for hopefully some reclamation of time. I’m in menopause, 50 lbs overweight, have a nasty case of eczema in my left ear (especially now in my temp living situation), osteoarthritis symptoms, a tech addiction and just became a Catholic. Welcome to my jungle folks.
> (I do have a fabulous tan already though!)

I’m going to dive deeper into how I put the pieces together with my dog’s health and mine. In 2022, I noticed the municipalities were digging up the roads and around utility boxes to install new telecommunications. I wasn’t too concerned because, at the time I lived in a new subdivision and figured we wouldn’t have too many upgrades or be as impacted. Boy was I wrong. That must have been the year they installed all the 5G towers because for a solid week, my son and I suffered the worst “flu” symptoms simultaneously. For him they lasted a week, for me a month and I’ve never been the same since. It felt as if I was being held down by some kind of heavy, oppressive force. I couldn’t move easily, I felt drained and exhausted all the time. I only had energy enough to walk to the park where I would then sit on the grass, exhausted. My dog didn’t get much walking in that month (July 2022). I’ve never had a “summer cold” hit me that hard before. After a few weeks I was able to function again and that’s when I noticed all the 5G towers in my area. My dog had to be put down 6 months later due to what the emergency vet suspected was a brain tumour. That fall was when my hair began falling out and I turned to red light therapy. Thankfully, I was consistent with it and then found Dr Kruse from his Tetragrammaton pod with Huberman. I’ve only slightly been able to recover since that incident in July 2022 and it’s why I’ve sold everything and am leaving for Central America in the next month or so. I experienced that same oppressive, heavy feeling driving into a different city this week, close to where I live now. All of these places make me feel the same way, cause anxiety and migraines and everyone I speak to about this thinks I'm nuts. I HAVE to leave. I can’t keep trading in my time for money now. I’ll end up with same fate as my dog or worse. My menopausal symptoms have INCREASED since selling my home and moving to a temporary living arrangement this year and I’m only able to sleep when I’m in rural areas now.
I’ll be packing away the rest of my life into a cargo trailer and storing it along with my vehicle at a friends home near my current location and flying south within 6 weeks. I’d love to leave sooner but expenses here have stalled that timeline unfortunately.
Anyone else in ES in October and want to meet up and explore? I doubt I’ll be able to do much of anything except recover and renovate for the first few months.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-17T16:31:38+0300

### Mr.AJ — 2025-08-17T16:34:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-358372>

> [Carly18 said:](/goto/post?id=354433)
>
> ok, back to my journal....
>
> Maternal grandmother passed away at 81 after multiple heart attacks, having to spend a few months in the hospital trying to recover. She hated doctors and hospitals. She had osteoarthritis and took medication for it to which she attributed her stomach problems. She had 7 children, oldest and youngest were male and the rest female. My mother was a twin and that was my grandmother's 4th pregnancy. The oldest son never married, lived with and cared for my grandmother his whole life, an alcoholic, had diabetes early and passed before my grandmother of a heart attack. He was also suicidal near the end of his life, going blind and with high blood pressure. The youngest male was 14 years younger then his closest sibling, my grandmother's 6th pregnancy. My grandmother lived on a farm most of her life, did not have running water for the entirety of it and drank spring water. She did have electricity in her later years. She spent most of her time tending the children, her garden, the animals and cooking/baking. As a child, I rarely saw her leave the house except to take care of the animals before I woke or to go to the bathroom (no running water = outhouse year round). She was very pale for the time I knew her and overweight. They grew crops conventionally so likely was exposed to many chemicals all her life. No big electrical towers on their property but there were smaller ones bringing power and telephone access along the highway opposite the large forest between her homestead and the highway. Her marriage was abusive and my grandfather an alcoholic. He passed at 96. His ancestry was Norwegian and they emigrated to the USA before my grandfather came to Canada after the war. Her ancestry is German but we don't have much information about that lineage unfortunately.
>
> My mother had 2 children, starting at age 30. I am the oldest and my brother 18 months later. She's had anxiety all her life (I finally figured this out this year while living with her). Went into menopause around the same age as myself and had a hernia and pelvic floor reconstruction in her 60's. Her deliveries were also quick but she thankfully gave birth without interventions. She had elevated LDL in her 60's and took statins for 12 years before choosing to come off of them. She is now almost 82 and on no medications. She had basal cells removed from her forehead and the side of her nose a few years ago and is now terrified of the sun, wears clothing outdoors and sunglasses, is pale and grey, bitter, resentful and struggles with emotional stability. Her anxiety is off the charts and I am suspecting dementia will likely take her. She is very upset with me wanting to leave the country. She worked in offices, under fluorescent lights most of her life and into my school years and retired when my grandmother passed. She now has an engrained routine she refuses to change, an addiction to technology watching tv most of the day indoors, rarely spends time outdoors and complains constantly. I DO NOT want to end up like my mother. She used to be able to tan so incredibly well and when I was very young I remember her sunbathing often. Her trip to Hawaii in the 80's has some of my favourite pictures of her in a bikini, almost milk chocolate brown and a beautiful smile. My father is Dutch and emigrated here when he was 5. He had a heart attack last summer and is also set in his ways, living indoors but took my advice and did some red light therapy this winter and got a bit of colour back. My Oma passed at 98 without many health issues. She contracted pneumonia a few times near the end and was unable to eat.
>
> Interestingly, all of my family has had core dysfunction in some capacity... father has a hernia (surgery to happen this summer), mother also and pelvic floor prolapse, I have a rectocele and my brother has had 2 hernia surgeries. Collagen failing.... my butt needs some sun!!!!

I just liked the title of your blog.

### Carly18 — 2025-10-22T17:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-361412>

I’m a little embarrassed about my tears in the last member Q&A but Dr Kruse always manages to hit me right in the feels.

What I’ve learned over the past 8 months where I have had no control over my environment, trying to farm fiat by selling my time and talking to people about my plans for my future (and ultimately the future of my child):

Building a citadel gives you sovereignty over your environment and is a crucial key to your ability to claw back your time.

Talking to people that have a vested interest in your time with them, will cause a drain on your own time.Choose wisely. Be a good steward of both.

Being able to give to others means you must first secure your sovereignty and the only true source of wealth here is God’s grace. Get right with Him and move forward.

Pray often, with humility and gratitude.

### Carly18 — 2026-03-09T18:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-368444>

All the noise has disappeared.

Things I overcame in 2 months while in El Salvador:
\* within the first week, I felt my skin texture improve and the psoriasis in my ear calmed somewhat
\* after 3 weeks, I noticed I had lost some weight, gained some energy and was able to handle the sun much better
\* after 4 weeks, I challenged my panic disorder trigger and found it had disappeared
\* after 5 weeks, all the noise was gone and I could tell if I was cheating or not
\* after 6 weeks, I could handle the noise of the ocean and the feel of the sand (both of which overstimulated me before)
\* after 7 weeks, the chocolate arrived and I was finally able to attain the goal of 4-6 hrs on the beach daily

I chose to leave after 8 weeks to help a friend but managed to maintain my progress while cruising for a month.
I am now back in the environment which got me sick and within 5 weeks, ALL of my issues have resurfaced in some capacity. The only calm left is the focus I have to return (I will be back to ES for 2.5 wks in April), set up some businesses while there and figure out how I am going to make it my home now.

STOP MAKING EXCUSES AND TRYING TO HEAL IN THE ENVIRONMENT THAT MADE YOU SICK. JUMP WITHOUT WINGS.

### Carly18 — 2026-03-24T22:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-369561>

I'm loving the fact that even though I am back in the D- environment, my tan has disappeared and some of the health issues have resurfaced, they have not taken hold as badly as I first imagined they would.
First things to disappear when I landed in ES were my skin issues. My ears, nose and legs were the worst. Psoriasis in my L ear; dry, cracked nose which would not heal even over the warmer summer months in Canada; scaly, dry skin on my legs and 3 lesions which would not heal started to improve within the first week in ES. The more time I spent in the sun (either at the beach, by the pool or in the shade), the more the skin issues improved and by the time 3 weeks rolled around, my nose was completely healed, my legs were beginning to soften and the lesions disappear and my ear was slowly improving. Within 5 weeks, my legs were glowing and iridescent with my darker tan (the lesions still have alot of melanin around them so I assume more sun is still necessary), my hearing had finally returned in my L ear and most of the psoriasis plaque - which had grown onto my cheek near the tragus, up to the choncha symba and down to the lobe - had disappeared except for a small amount internally on the tragus and canal. The most substantial changes occurred when I was finally able to handle the loud surf, the sand on my skin and float in the ocean with my head under the water. All of which was so incredibly overstimulating for me when I first arrived. Christmas week was when MY chocolate princess tan popped off! I can't tell you how incredibly healing ES is, you just have to experience it yourself and go all in.

### Carly18 — 2026-04-29T00:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-372369>

Back in Chernobyl that is Canada after 2.5 weeks in ES again. So many amazing things happened while I was there. Dr Kruse's PowWow reenforced a hunch I had during my time in ES last year, that the magnetism on the beach is what pushed me over the hump to make a good amount of melanin. It took me almost 2 months to get to that ability but knowing just being there was healing things. My callous is still in a good place as I was able to make a decent amount of melanin quite quickly BUT I was already losing hair before I left Canada and that only took 2 months of crap environment to happen again. My skin INSTANTLY becomes ashy and dry (Mars) the moment I get into a shit environment and rehydrates in ES. When I first tried tanning in ES, I got a big heat rash on my chest. My maternal history is inclined to heart attacks and high blood pressure. I burn on my nose and did this time as well and that is one of the first places I have skin issues. I discovered I don't like Atami (except for the incredible community there) but there are some AMAZING places in ES to explore next time. Mitos are unbelievably inspiring people. willing to support one another and have amazing body awareness. Temporary residency in country requirements for a PR in ES are now 90 days vs 8 months which makes it much more doable for my family, friends and clients.
Things I wonder about now:
is the well water in my area so full of iron because of the magnetic declination? Yes. Just like us.
are the dumb people I live near currently suffering from the pole shift? YES. And I pray for them but I have to leave.
if I make my skills available to people in need, no matter where I am in the world, can I help people? Yes. And they get results more quickly!
is the lake I love to summer at drying up because of magnetic declination? yes, and that makes me so sad.
are the farmers in my area not able to grow their crops successfully without chemicals or is it because of the pole shift?

My intuition lead me to look for a new country to live in, somewhere that supports life, with people loving to live in and with nature, who want to protect their culture, their population and their health. Salut El Salvador. I look forward to making your country a large part of the new chapter of my life!

# Thread: Dan C's Optimal Journal - Creating Art Outside, In the Sun, & Grounded
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-cs-optimal-journal-creating-art-outside-in-the-sun-grounded.30119/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dcetlin — 2025-01-16T04:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-cs-optimal-journal-creating-art-outside-in-the-sun-grounded.30119/post-345267>

Formal hello to this community

#### ​

#### **The past 6 months, I have been studiously lurking in the world of Dr. Kruse.**​

I have made a few posts here and there on the forum, been on a few Q&A's, but have largely kept to myself, an observer.

When I first heard Jack speak of the irreplaceable value of sunrise, I doubted my ability to change my sleep-wake rhythm. Yet now I find myself unable to miss a single sunrise. The transformation has been both subtle, and radical. I have always intuitively enjoyed spending my daylight outside. But now TINA and ALAN are the most fucking obvious axioms.

(Initially, I was writing this journal inside by the open window, but realized that made no sense. I'm now shirtless and feet in the grass at 3pm)

#### **My biggest takeaway these days: Embrace my time as my most important & precious resource!**​

Each moment presents a choice—conscious or unconscious: Whatever I find myself doing, wherever I find myself, I can persist, pivot, fully embrace the current path, or completely opt-out. When I act with aligned, inspired intention, I become the masterful architect of my finite time. Choice is inherent to my self-sovereign nature. Though I respect the past commitments I've made, and the potential future consequences of choices, I refuse to be bound by them, let alone the centralizing forces that seek to colonize my body, mind and reality. I want to make the most out of my time in this life. I want to feel that I am spending my time wisely, never wasted.

#### **Jack's quote on _ideation without execution_ was the distilled slap in the face I needed to hear.** ​

The decentralized wisdom is ultimately quite simple. Nature is god, so listen to her whispers. I will feel into what I want most in life, and take the steps to make it real. I will do so while cultivating an impeccable mind, recognizing that mind develops adaptively (or mal-adaptively) within its environment. I will also do so while building decentralized wealth.

I will build **financial wealth** through strategic reserves of cold-storage BTC, supported by income streams that respect the interconnected nature of health and wealth.

I will build **social wealth** via a tribe of high-trust brother and sister savages.

I will build **spiritual wealth** through net-maximizing my REDOX, increasingly living attuned to the cycles of nature, embracing myself as a solar powered being connecting to source, and minimizing bullshit energy & information from permeating my adaptive membrane.

I will build **emotional wealth** through embracing the suck, never lying to myself, rejecting the pull of lower vibrations, and striving to always be maximally compassionate towards myself and others (even if that means sharing or withholding hard truths from those I love, or cutting people out of my life).

Finally, I will build **intellectual wealth** through my incessant curiosity, desire to learn, seeking of the illuminated truth, and understanding of history.

#### **I appreciate Uncle Jack's rejection of _"balance"_ as an aspirational virtue.** ​

Having studied Ilya Prigogine, I see dynamic unfolding transformation through dissipative structures as a more compelling mental-model for how I construct my life. Instead of seeking a "balanced" life, I continually calibrate my clocks, fine-tuning the timing of my cycles, and lean hard into my edges. I am ready for life to test me. I will embrace leaps of faith, and learn to trust nature's wisdom buried deep within me. I will embrace the free-fall, the experience of reality as extemporaneous unfolding, and reject living in a pre-scripted simulation. Strategic ideation has its place, but at this point in my life, I need to orient more towards aligned action.

1\. ⬆doing - the investment of my time and energy into executing/acting-on my purest intentions & goals
2\. ⬆thinking - ideation / contemplation on past—via reflection—, and future—via visioning, planning, imagining, strategizing, dreaming, and praying.
3\. ⬆being - living into / embracing the spontaneous aliveness of life's unfolding

#### Lifestyle​

On one of the previous Q&As, I asked about nocturia. Jack quickly recommended I read [QE #23](https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-23-clock-76681761). Somehow, I always knew my condition of waking to pee in the night was circadian mismatch in nature, I just didn't have all the information to put it together. Even before I read the blog, I had a feeling the solution to my condition was to refine my clocks with a more impeccable relationship to sunlight, darkness, and technology. Now, I have a more intimate understandings of the mechanisms of vasopressin/ADH, the timing of water creation and retention, and cellular hydration, let alone nnEMF and Kruse 101. I was always curious why my bladder was strong during the day (infrequent urination), but hypersensitive at night. Now it's so fucking obvious. The crazy thing is that some aspect of this was always intuitive to me. Now it's a non-issue.

I am grateful for all the lessons I have been able to learn as a result of my tech abuse growing up. I am 26 today. My relationship has dramatically shifted. I am well aware of the environmental challenges as result of choosing to live in San Fransisco. But despite working a tech job on the computer, I spend 80% of daylight outside feet grounded in the park grass (no electric pollution via underground power-lines). I get as much skin in the game as I can. I cut out fluorinated water, currently Green Mountain Valley. My bedroom is dark. My wifi is off (though I live in an apartment and city where RF is unavoidable). I get my feet in the ocean regularly. I orient my vacations/travel around high UV and clean EMF.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My current portable office. I'll connect via wired hotspot with long 12ft cabel, airtubes if I need them.

I am currently exploring the next 2-3 steps with regards to where to live, especially considering implications around starting a family in the next 2 years. Even at this point in my journey, given all of my environmental pollutants, I am living orders-of-magnitude more circadian in nature than I was in the past. It has been sufficient to dramaticly improve my sleep quality and efficiency. My mind thinks better, I don’t feel afternoon fatigue. I make better bottom-up choices more aligned with my wanting. I am much more intune with the messages from my body. Here's to living with greator and greator agency and proaction!

I’m also grateful how much more refined my bullshit-detector is. It helps me avoid wasting my time with clownshows (or Mockingbird assets).

#### Music​

I have been playing violin since the age of 3. I have played a lot of music outside, but always amplified with pedals plugged into the A/C power grid. Over the years, I have intuitively become increasingly attracted to accoustic violin. However, the wooden string instruments require indoor spaces to truly sound. Playing outside creates a dry sound given the lack of reverations. Nevertheless, I am finding ways to create music outside during the day, grounded and in the sun.

Even without my own backyard, the park works just fine as a portable recording studio with nice bird chirp ambiance. Feels amazing and sounds great! I just have to get lucky with the farting motorcycles!

At my heart I am an improvisor. This piece is a fully extemporaneous continuous take. I later produced the track, adding all sorts of layers including my voice, violin, synths, beats, bass, and the debut of my cello playing, much of which was also done outside!

I have developed myself as a multi-disciplinary artist (and human). I have studied many artists artists and genres over the years, incorperating all kinds of vocabulary into my personal style.

#### Gratitude To This Community​

Special shoutouts to Rusty, Anthony Smith. I've learned a lot from your posts! And of course Jack!
