# Thread: Nicole Ashley Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nashley — 2025-01-23T00:27:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345595>

I’ve been experiencing migraines since I was six years old, and I’m now 39. I’ve noticed a clear hormonal pattern, as they occur monthly, and other triggers seem to include stress and lack of sleep.

My migraines are excruciating and often lead to vomiting. The pain is so overwhelming that I sometimes feel like I want to kill myself, though I push through because I know the pain will eventually end. I’m actively searching for solutions to overcome my migraines because I’d like to start a family and don’t want to wait any longer. However, I worry that pregnancy might make my migraines worse or that this condition could somehow be passed to my children, which I want to prevent. A few other issues that might be connected are cold hands and feet, difficulty staying warm, and having no libido.

I’m incredibly grateful to have come across Jack Kruse’s work and feel optimistic about finding answers.

### Nashley — 2025-01-24T03:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345668>

### I listened to “Getting Started with Cold Thermogenesis” webinar today. If was so helpful and inspiring. One of my questions I had for Jack in the up coming powpow is using cold for the pain when I have a migraine attack. In the webinar he said one of the best ways is to put ice packs on your carotid artery and femur artery, going to give this a try next time I have an attack! On the webinar Jack talk about a lot of the problems ct helps, I’m going to make it a part of my life and become cold adapted. I’m still not sure what the cause is of the migraines but I’m certain Ct will in improve this and many other things. I heard Jack say in a Q and A, “treat your condition as if you already have cancer”, that’s how I am going to eradicate the migraines. I’m writing this entry as a sit in cold tub of water, legs only today , tomorrow ice woman!​

### Nashley — 2025-01-26T21:17:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345778>

Had a migraine this morning, tried ice on both sides of carotid artery, reduce the pain a little but still ended up throwing up and resorting to aspirin, so disappointed. I really was hoping it was going to work and wouldn’t need other pain relieve.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-26T22:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345780>

I had a client that got bad migraines and NIR light on the temples helped them.

### Nashley — 2025-01-30T17:36:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345992>

While I agree that EMF and blue light aren’t good for anyone, I believe there’s more to my migraines than just that. I started experiencing them at six years old, which could have been influenced by classroom lighting and EMF exposure from devices. However, if that were the main cause, it would stand to reason that most of the girls I grew up with—who lived the same lifestyle—would have had migraines too, yet I don’t know of anyone else who did. Additionally, I’ve spent summers deep in the wilderness, far from cell phones and technology, sleeping on the ground, yet I still experienced migraines during those times.

This also doesn’t explain why my migraines are primarily tied to my cycle. If blue light and EMF were the main causes, I would expect to experience them constantly rather than in a distinct pattern. While I do occasionally get migraines from lack of sleep or stress, those triggers are rare for me. Given this, I truly believe something isn’t right with my hormones.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-10T06:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-346524>

> [Nashley said:](/goto/post?id=345595)
>
> I’ve been experiencing migraines since I was six years old, and I’m now 39. I’ve noticed a clear hormonal pattern, as they occur monthly, and other triggers seem to include stress and lack of sleep.
>
>
> My migraines are excruciating and often lead to vomiting. The pain is so overwhelming that I sometimes feel like I want to kill myself, though I push through because I know the pain will eventually end. I’m actively searching for solutions to overcome my migraines because I’d like to start a family and don’t want to wait any longer. However, I worry that pregnancy might make my migraines worse or that this condition could somehow be passed to my children, which I want to prevent. A few other issues that might be connected are cold hands and feet, difficulty staying warm, and having no libido.
>
>
> I’m incredibly grateful to have come across Jack Kruse’s work and feel optimistic about finding answers.

Just my thoughts that may help you look into a few different areas -

Have you looked into the role of magnesium on the NMDA receptor? it plays a role in stabilizing this receptor involved in neural signalling (movement of ions through these receptors can influence electrical signals within the brain). A deficiency in Mg can lead to the over stimulation of the NMDA receptor (potentially contributing to pathophysiology of migraines). This link has been studied and published in papers (plug it into a search engine).

Are you around lots of nn-EMF ? have you had your environment checked by a building biologist? As nn-EMF will interact with the VGCCs (Voltage Gated Calcium Channels) leading excess inward leaking of calcium within the cell & excess Ca can displace Mg ions leading to Mg deficiency within the cell etc..

The link to your cycle could be -
(Ovulation) ~ day 14 (out of 28) - progesterone can increase Mg excretion through urine. This can carry through luteal phase up until menstruation.

If you drink caffeine - this will be further exacerbated.

If not linked to Mg deficiency, there could be many other possibilities, however this could be a place for you to look into.
Hope this helps.

### eve001 — 2025-03-04T11:07:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-347853>

> [Nashley said:](/goto/post?id=345992)
>
> While I agree that EMF and blue light aren’t good for anyone, I believe there’s more to my migraines than just that. I started experiencing them at six years old, which could have been influenced by classroom lighting and EMF exposure from devices. However, if that were the main cause, it would stand to reason that most of the girls I grew up with—who lived the same lifestyle—would have had migraines too, yet I don’t know of anyone else who did. Additionally, I’ve spent summers deep in the wilderness, far from cell phones and technology, sleeping on the ground, yet I still experienced migraines during those times.
>
>
> This also doesn’t explain why my migraines are primarily tied to my cycle. If blue light and EMF were the main causes, I would expect to experience them constantly rather than in a distinct pattern. While I do occasionally get migraines from lack of sleep or stress, those triggers are rare for me. Given this, I truly believe something isn’t right with my hormones.

Hi there,

How are you doing now?

It took me maybe 20 years to link nnEMFs + external stressors to on and off serious fatigue and sleep problems.

Some ideas I gathered on this site and elsewhere which may be helpful/true or not.
\- estrogen (first half of cycle) is protective against nnEMFs. Are your symptoms worse second part of your cycle?
\- menses are a way a women detox and gets rid of deuterium too. That to me indicates that in women the felt impact of any external toxins will fluctuate with their cycle.
\- any stressor, environmental or mental, will generate calcum efflux and nnEMF will do so 24/7 unless mitigated/reduced
\- in Dr. Sircus book on transdermal magnesium I read that magnesium deficiency has very similar effects as nnEMFs. He also refers to Mg in chlorophyl and to the sun and it reminded me of Dr. Kruse mentioning hemoglobin and iron as the human counterpart for chlorophyl.

I know of a man with strong migranes who is a vegetarian working and relaxing with computers and screens in the city for about 30years now.
He saw many MDs and seems to have never found help in centralized medicine.

Regarding non-environmlental factors, I wonder about individual differences in magnetite crystals in the brain for instance.

Hope you found/find relief.

# Thread: Robert's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/roberts-optimal-journal.30366/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AcuHealth — 2025-02-27T01:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/roberts-optimal-journal.30366/post-347446>

I am happy to be a part of this tribe and grateful for the varied experts on this forum. I am here to learn. I recently joined the Optimal Klub practitioners forum. Dr. Kruse's work has helped me both personally and professionally and I am very grateful. I own and operate an acupuncture clinic. My wife and I are no longer confident that our environment is compatible with optimal and are trying to navigate the transition to a more user friendly environment to live in. We do not know where that might be or how to make it happen but we are open to suggestions from fellow members? Maybe Dr. Kruse wants a skilled acupuncturist to work at his clinic in El Salvador who is familiar with his work?

Recently our son returned from university in Sweden and has had an explosion of painful cystic acne that has inflamed his entire face, neck, back, chest and shoulders with painful soars. His stool test showed elevated candida and strep. His WBC count was through the roof. He has been convalescing at our home now going on 45 days and he is still exhausted and not sleeping well from the pain. We are terrified of handing him over to centralized medicine which would be massive antibiotics, steroids, and probably accutane. He is a beautiful, smart and sensitive young man and this has ripped our hearts apart and we are terrified. Upon his return from Sweden I sent him and my wife to Tulum for 10 days which raised his Vitamin D to 50. We did several herbal protocols of anti bacterial/viral herbs and give him a a gummy to sleep at night. He spends most of his days outside in the sun connected to the earth on our lawn. His energy still fluctuates and he is grumpy as hell. He is eating basically seafood, meat and vegetables and broth. He eats low glycemic berries and cantaloupe. I can tell as I write this that I am exhausted and worried. I haven't slept well these past few months.

We spend our evenings outside by the fire which is one of the few moments I gather a few moments of peace. My prayer life has been challenged yet I know from my bible:
"Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you".

I believe that God is love and we are here on this earth to learn how to love in preparation for heaven. If you have health suggestions we are open to listening. If you are a prayerful person please leave a prayer for Ellis. Thank you.

### michellejorge — 2025-03-17T19:43:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dystonia-genetic-tests-completed.30495/post-348775>

Dear Dr. Kruse,

I attach my results from my genetic testing that you recomended on our consult. Please help me interpret them and what drugs I may obtain benefit from. If you recall, I take Carbidopa Levadopa three times a day and clonazepan as needed. You mentioned I might benefit from BH4. Is that simply methylfolate that I can buy as a supplement as I already consume it with no noticable effect? Please tell me specifically what you recommend because you had little faith in my movement disorder specialist that hadn't pursued this work up of genetic testing. I am getting sun for the breast cancer and hormones as I remain dealing with the perimenopausal symptoms which aggravate the dystonia that I shared with you surfaced dramatically after I gave birth almost 12 years ago. I am 46 now. I anxiously await your response.

Thank you,
Michelle who - No longer wants to be "low dopamine" due to DRD

# Thread: Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30509/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JGrace — 2025-03-19T04:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30509/post-348881>

Vitiligo
Jeanie Grace, German, Scottish, Age 66, 5'6", 118lbs.
Blood Type A
Born in PA
FL at age 4 - Vitiligo started
FL to S. CA - from 1986 until 2014
Flt Attendant from 1987 to 2004
FL again 2014
Vitiligo improved (no longer of face) in CA. I was determined to reverse it.
I've done everything imaginable and unimaginable, holistically, lol.
Now, covers front and back torso, 3/4 of arms, 1/2 legs, feet and hands

I have had impeccable physical health my whole life, active, athletic until the plandemic.

March 2021 - started in gut/solar plex pressure/missing BMs

No Jab! Never ever wore a mask! Never closed my office/practice down, saw many patients
who were jabbed. Possible CV19 shedding?

Symptoms are worse at my home than anywhere else. Leads me to EMF/5G concern.
Will test since finding your recommendation EMFSafe.com

In April I will be living 1/2 the weeks in Jacksonville Beach. Opening a 2nd office.
Will that be any better than Melbourne/ Space X / Patrick Air Force base?

Key symptoms: -gripping tightness around solar plexus after food or even without
\- bloating almost immediately after any kind of food. Charting worse offenders.
\- pain comes and goes, unilateral, bilateral changes but always mid section.
\- no longer have hair on arms, armpits or legs (head is good)
\- dizzy, now daily
\- calf and feet cramps(better when consistent with electrolytes)
\- fatigue
\- foggy brain (comes and goes)
\- memory loss!

My sleep is and always been excellent. and always feel better with rest, even if for 20 mins.
Bought your book Epi-Paleo RX and will focus on eating seaweeds again and making
survival soup and other recommendations.

My grandmother lived until 104. She simply stopped eating.
My mother died of breast cancer age 55, 1986.
(2 tumors to brain ultimately turned off her breathing. I took care of her, and watched her
slow painful death, she was my best friend).

Mother's death redirected my life to find a way to help end pain and suffering on this planet so
I moved to CA became Acupuncture Physician and earned Doctorate in Oriental Medicine.
Graduated in 1995. Been in private practice ever since! Doctoral dissertation-
Aging Without Illness: A comparative analysis of Eastern and Western medicine's
approach to treating chronic disease - 2009.

With your help Dr. Jack, I will help many more with what I learn through my recovery. Amen!!

With appreciation and gratitude!

# Thread: My Journal: Publishing a Book on Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 78

### JHackett — 2025-03-21T19:25:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349050>

Hi everyone,

Jack suggested that I write an optimal journal of sorts on my work from the last year, which has been writing a book on **leptin’s overarching effects on optimal health.**

The book is part of a series of approximately seven, that I’m calling the _Decentralized Health Rebellion_.

This first book on leptin, called _The Metabolic Scam: Leptin Unlocks the Truth_, is basically my way of paying homage to Jack’s earlier blogs, **the Leptin series**. I hope to shine my own little version of a flashlight on his work and with that, spread the word to other people.

I just wanted to introduce the work today but over time I’ll be using some excerpts within this journal that I hope will spark some conversation.

Please let me know if you’d be interested in reading the book, it’s going to be ready via PDF soon. I cannot say at this time when paperback will be available. Thanks!

### JHackett — 2025-03-23T15:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349167>

Excerpt: “_Leptin crosses the blood-brain barrier and enters the hypothalamus to bind leptin receptors in the arcuate nucleus, where it can relay system wide energy status. This is not just a passive signal—leptin evaluates how much energy was used throughout the day and determines whether fat should be burned or stored.”_

That’s not something taught in any textbook I ever read in med school. These few sentences dismantle the entire calories-in, calories-out model, yet that is a thriving multi-billion dollar industry and growing.

### JHackett — 2025-03-25T00:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349262>

_Excerpt: “If sunlight is the most critical metabolic regular, artificial light is the greatest metabolic disrupter. In fact, poor light environment is a greater driver of metabolic disease than diet.”_

Doing my best to also "light" matches and toss them onto current nutritional dogma.

### JHackett — 2025-03-27T15:32:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349434>

Excerpt:

“Quantum tunneling, in essence, is a phenomenon where tiny particles, like electrons, can pass through energy barriers that they normally wouldn’t be able to cross—almost like a ghost walking through a wall. In your mitochondria, this allows electrons to move more efficiently, speeding up energy production and reducing waste.”

On this forum, we already know: mitochondria aren’t just ATP factories—they’re quantum processors. But articulating tunneling as the mechanism for metabolic elegance feels like pulling classical biology into the quantum firelight and saying, “You’ve served your time—now step aside.” I hope my future readers understand and appreciate the significance.

### JHackett — 2025-03-31T17:30:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349743>

Excerpt:

“Obesity isn’t a calorie problem. It’s not even just about insulin. It’s a leptin hijacking—an attack on your brain’s ability to regulate energy. Big Food, artificial light, and 24/7 access to calories keep you in a constant state of fat storage.”

⸻

For years I have watched people blame themselves, obsess over food, and fail—because no one ever told them the war was being fought in the brain, not the mouth.

### JHackett — 2025-04-05T03:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-350126>

**Excerpt:**
_“Circadian biology doesn’t just affect sleep—it governs hormone release, mitochondrial efficiency, immune signaling, and fat metabolism. Break the light-dark cycle, and you break everything downstream.”_

This one came from my own place of clarity—and exhaustion. Clarity because once you understand circadian biology, the rest of medicine feels like noise. Exhaustion because I realized how far off-course we've gone—in a field that I dedicated my life to. Writing this felt like distilling a thousand papers and a decade of clinical frustration into one scalpel-sharp truth.

### JHackett — 2025-04-17T19:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-350843>

**"Leptin is made by your fat cells, but don’t mistake it for a passive byproduct. It's your fuel gauge, sending continuous updates to the hypothalamus—the command center of your brain's energy regulation. When leptin signaling is intact, your brain gets a clear message: energy is sufficient, burn calories freely, suppress appetite and stay lean. But when leptin resistance sets in, the brain goes blind. It reads scarcity where abundance exists. You have access to thousands of calories a day, yet hunger increases, metabolism slows, fat is hoarded and energy crashes."**
— _The Metabolic Scam_

### JHackett — 2025-04-19T17:17:45+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-20T19:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-351007>

> [AngieM said:](/goto/post?id=350978)
>
> What an amazing experience! I’m curious, how did your wife change her mind? Did she simply make a decision? Did she do meditations/visualizations?

Who we are, what we think and feel comes from a life time of experiences, decisions made, emotional responses, programing (from parents, school, media, MKUltra etc). For most people, most of the time all these are subconscious “programs” running in the background, dictating our reactions, thoughts and feelings to what we experience today.

I believe the key to becoming who you want to be, not who you have been programed to be, is to become conscious and remove these old programs. Meditation and visualizations can help but just looking and becoming conscious of the programs there empowers you to cancel them or rewrite and replace them with new ones of your choosing.

The sub conscious mind is very powerful, take the time to do the work to have it work for you, not against you. I am healthy, wealthy and wise. I am successful. I am in the right place at the right time. When I need help, help will be there etc.

Yes, she “just” changed her mind one day but it was a core decision, she decided not to be heavy any more and the weight left. Watching the sun rise and being in the sun IS good for everyone but why do some get faster results than others? Multiple reasons, severity of underlying conditions etc BUT - I expect people who BELIEVE it works get better, faster results than those just giving it a try or who are skeptical.

Today is resurrection Sunday, I believe in resurrection, the “impossible”, life from death. Choose life.

> Hi everyone,
>
> Jack suggested that I write an optimal journal of sorts on my work from the last year, which has been writing a book on **leptin’s overarching effects on optimal health.**
>
> The book is part of a series of approximately seven, that I’m calling the _Decentralized Health Rebellion_.
>
> This first book on leptin, called _The Metabolic Scam: Leptin Unlocks the Truth_, is basically my way of paying homage to Jack’s earlier blogs, **the Leptin series**. I hope to shine my own little version of a flashlight on his work and with that, spread the word to other people.

> I just wanted to introduce the work today but over time I’ll be using some excerpts within this journal that I hope will spark some conversation.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-07T16:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-355916>

> [moon77 said:](/goto/post?id=355909)
>
> if this is true then why do animal get cancer? my dog died of cancer, ar you telling me its form his light environment?
>
> im smelling somthing in the air

Dogs get cancer because they're kept inside fed crap and generally given too much food and they get vaccinated yearly.

sound familiar? Pampered dogs generally get cancer. I'll tell you a story of two dogs
When I was in London and I had a dog. We would go to the park and eventually about two years in we met an Airedale terrier. now this dog was kept in a house and given wheat biscuits with sugar. And was a complete nightmare as far as good was concerned. They used to have to keep him behind a barrier in the utility room that they had in their nice big Victorian end Semi detached House

But I don't think apart from the park that he got much sunlight and he was indulged.

Mine. I would let her go out as much as possible. I would even leave the door open so she could come and go at will, the back door to the garden

She did not steal food off the counters.

I had another dog walking friend deliver a food box to me who also had an Airedale terrier

She looked scandaliséd by the fact that the food bag was on the floor and open ish and she said. You can leave your food bag like that with your dog ? And I said yeah. What's the difficulty? She said her dog would have got into it and eaten the lot

So back to Freddie the Airedale terrier

And to my Doberman
I learnt later that Freddie had died of cancer at age 5

And then recently his mistress she also died. But that was 2 years ago and she was only 71

So , there was a huge plasma TV flat screen in that house which I'm sure the dog was watching

John his master was at that point wondering whether to get an Led TV

What did I have. An old Mitsubishi cathode ray TV which I barely watched

In fact so old that when I got burgled they didn't take the TV. They left that behind

So light environment was a total factor

There you go.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-07-07T17:52:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-355925>

> [moon77 said:](/goto/post?id=355920)
>
> top of the line response and info.
>
> that i believe. that makes sense the food the environment and light and jbs
> but not just light
>
> thanks for sharing this.
> if i ever have a pet again i will remember

I agree with everything Dr. Robert Kapur said, and having dogs, I would post again that they are meant to eat their ancestral food, just like the people living in the "blue zones" who are removed from the Western diet, highly processed and convenient in abundance, yet never satisfying a person's true hunger.

I takes no effort to research what dogs eat in the wild, as they are predators or scavengers, eating everything except the sound the animals make. Internal organs, skin, muscle, etc.

They are pure examples of instinct, something humans abandoned when they became "civilized". They live outside, find shade when necessary, and unless they hunt at night, are asleep at sunset, or before, and arise at sunrise.

One would learn a great deal to learn how humans are meant to live if they would just observe animals/pets in their natural environment.

"The Dog Whisperer" show makes revenue from showing the owners are the source of the problem, if there is one, as Caesar does best when he takes a dog who has behavioral problems back around other healthy dogs who are given the opportunity to just be dogs.

Nature will heal humans if the person hasn't succumbed to the Western lifestyle for too long. Every sunrise I thank God for his Son, and our Sun. Manna turned to worms if it wasn't gathered early, when the people of Israel where forced to wander for 40 years. Morning light is the same way. The benefit is gone if not taken early.

### ​

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-08T12:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-355993>

I have my dog as much outside time as she wanted. And I fed her decent food and id give her Christmas dinner. Turkey, gravy (gluten free of course because wheat for dogs is damaging to their guts) potatoes and sprouts. Ate it all up.

Used to give scraps on top of the dry food

If I couldn't finish it. She'd get it. Once bought a beef joint that had been marked down in Tesco and gave her that too
Would give her beef bones, Pig ears, and cow ears and no more than 3 treats a day.

I once left her at a friend's for a weekend with a week's worth of treats. He finished them all and I asked him. What the hell. That was a week's worth of treats ! He said oh but she was hungry. But then the thing is they have no impulse control around food. None whatsoever. Dogs are generally on a see food diet

He is wanting to come to the USA to work as a surgeon but is being ignored by every place he has contacted. Such a shame because people come to him from all over the world including me.

I see. Well that would be fantastic to have him read it!

I am not surprised by that story. The cross qualification of physicians to and from other countries is lined with red tape and quite difficult.

I would love to read more about Leptin for those who do not have weight issues. I am not trained in biology and whenever the story of Leptin is presented as a weight issue, I start to tune out, because I don't have a weight issue and so it sounds not relevant to my situation. I also don't take stimulants (other than a cup of coffee maybe once every two weeks), I am not sleep deprived and I don't do a starvation diet or excessive cardio. I have had endometriosis and fibroids. So how does the Leptin story interact with conditions that result from a Warburg shifted metabolism? Sorry if that's obvious. Thank you in advance from a biology-retard.

### JHackett — 2025-07-30T20:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-357222>

> [BiohackersTO said:](/goto/post?id=357157)
>
> I would love to read more about Leptin for those who do not have weight issues. I am not trained in biology and whenever the story of Leptin is presented as a weight issue, I start to tune out, because I don't have a weight issue and so it sounds not relevant to my situation. I also don't take stimulants (other than a cup of coffee maybe once every two weeks), I am not sleep deprived and I don't do a starvation diet or excessive cardio. I have had endometriosis and fibroids. So how does the Leptin story interact with conditions that result from a Warburg shifted metabolism? Sorry if that's obvious. Thank you in advance from a biology-retard.

Great question and extremely relevant to my book. Leptin is the energy accountant of the body. It deals with every tissue, connecting said tissue to the brain and assessing energetics. Do we need to down regulate production because this tissue is stressed? Can we continue with optimal production of a hormone because we have the energy inputs needed? Is the 'signal' throughout the body being presented more as 'noise', requiring protection/survival mode (very common with nnEMF exposure)? Etc.

Leptin is tightly regulated to the weight loss story as it is produced from adipose tissue and deals intimately with system-wide energy status. But it has its hands everywhere.

# Thread: My journey back to health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:32:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349063>

### Myself​

-   Age: 49 years old, male.
-   Background: Grew up in the Bay Area, California.

#### Current Health Issues​

-   Insomnia: I go to bed early and fall asleep easily but wake up ~5 hours later to use the bathroom and struggle to fall back asleep for 45 minutes to 1.5 hours. Worse in winter. I'm not very alert or energetic when I wake, it's just a ruminating mind. It's not every night, seems related to stress, and when I was was in a relationship I always slept through the night when my girlfriend shared the bed with me.
-   Minor Fatigue: Directly tied to sleep quality. Generally, my energy levels are very high.
-   Tinea Versicolor: Minor, in areas that are prone to staying damp.
-   Tinnitus: Minor and not bothersome. Additional context: Played loud music in a band for years and drums prior to that.
-   Muscle Stiffness: No pain, but limited mobility, especially in back, neck, and hamstrings. I sometimes throw my back out. Usually stress related.
-   Mental Health: Overall amazing, though I'm currently processing significant grief accumulated over 30 years from a tumultuous life.

Otherwise, life feels very good. I have mental clarity, sense of well being, and have acquired some sort of magnetic affect that is drawing people towards me.

### My Mother​

-   Childhood: Diagnosed with a benign heart murmur.
-   Ages 5–8: Frequent ear infections.
-   Ages 16–18: Cystic acne.
-   Age 25: Developed lactose intolerance following a Giardia infection.
-   Age 47: Diagnosed with mitral valve prolapse.
-   Ages 54–60: Diagnosed with basal cell skin cancer (5 occurrences).
-   Undated: Diagnosed with ocular and facial rosacea.
-   Age 59: Diagnosed with interstitial cystitis.
-   Age 62: Posterior vitreous detachment in the right eye.
-   Age 65: Diagnosed with glaucoma.
-   Age 67: Underwent mitral valve repair surgery.
-   Age 74: Diagnosed with gluten intolerance.

### My Maternal Grandmother​

-   No known health issues until age 39.
-   Early Spring, Age 39: Appendectomy due to a nearly ruptured appendix. Doctor noted significant inflammation in her small bowel.
-   Summer, Age 39: Intermittent diarrhea or constipation, developed hemorrhoids.
-   Later in 39th Year: Began vomiting; diagnosed with bowel obstruction caused by cancer.
-   October, Age 39: Underwent surgery, but cancer had already spread.
-   December 31, Age 40: Passed away on New Year’s Eve, one week after her 40th birthday (born December 25).

My grandmother grew up on a farm in the Central Valley CA, born in 1920. My mom was raised on that farm, but left at age 18.

### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:34:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349064>

### Health History - Significant Events​

-   Birth:
    -   Born 7 weeks premature; spent first week in an incubator.
-   Ages 5 & ~7–8:
    -   Experienced acute, non-violent physical/mental trauma (DM for details if needed).
    -   I struggled to go to sleep for years afterward. (Not fall asleep. Scared to go to bed)
    -   Bed wetting. Headaches.
-   Childhood in general: In general though, I was very robust and rarely got sick or injured. Never broke a bone despite many bad falls.
-   Age 16:
    -   Prescribed tetracycline for acne (3 months).
    -   Advised to avoid sun exposure.
-   Age 17:
    -   Became depressed, dropped out of high school.
    -   Inverted sleep schedule for computer science self-study.
-   Age 19:
    -   Traded computers for excessive alcohol use.
    -   Returned to outdoor activities.
-   Age 22:
    -   Struggles with sinus infections began.
    -   Back injury (L4-L5) with positive ANA (lupus test).
    -   Contracted mononucleosis.
    -   Developed sciatica and pain in feet from standing.
    -   Workers’ comp denied, returned to computer work.
-   Age 23:
    -   Mono relapsed (2–3 months).
    -   Taking Aleve for back pain, triggered severe GERD.
    -   Something triggered full-body rash with peeling skin.
-   Age 24:
    -   Moved to New Orleans (1999), most symptoms improved except GERD, triggered by certain foods and alcohol.
    -   Moved back to CA, started remote tech work.
    -   RSI in wrists (disabled 1.5 years), treated with cortisone.
    -   Brief period panic attacks due to high stress.
-   Age 29:
    -   Moved to Oregon.
    -   Sinus infections returned with full-body muscle pain and inflammation.
-   Age 33:
    -   Insomnia began (middle-of-night waking).
    -   Developed panic disorder.
    -   At ER, given Xanax.
    -   The primary doctor prescribed Ativan to bridge to Zoloft.
        -   Didn’t tolerate Zoloft (inability to sleep, nausea leading to vomiting).
        -   Switched to Celexa, sleep worsened, experienced brain zaps.
        -   Refused SSRIs after three days.
    -   ND diagnosed adrenal fatigue; treatment resolved panic.
    -   Unable to quit Ativan for 9 months, weaned off by switching to Klonopin.
-   Age 34:
    -   Diagnosed with candida.
    -   Adopted Weston A. Price diet, cut alcohol/coffee, health improved significantly.
    -   Diagnosed as functional hypothyroid.
        -   Given Armour Thyroid, caused panic attacks and severe hunger.
-   Age 37:
    -   RSI in wrists returned.
    -   Resolved with prolotherapy.
-   Age 38:
    -   Diagnosed with “ADHD”
    -   Skeptical, refused treatment. I do have time blindness though.
-   Age 41:
    -   Divorce with severely stressful aftermath (DM for details).
    -   Panic attacks stopped with separation though.
    -   Resumed Weston A. Price diet and weightlifting, health improved.
    -   Started melatonin dependency for jet lag from work travel.
-   Age 42:
    -   1 year of unrelenting stress + prescription-grade Advil and Mucinex for persistent sinus issues.
    -   Relationship with a woman diagnosed with Borderline personality disorder.
    -   Lead to physical breakdown (GERD, inflamed esophagous fatigue).
        -   Given antacids, which made me nauseous.
        -   Inability to breathe through nose at night (waking 10+ times).
-   Age 43:
    -   Worked in office for a company that developed WiFi/microwave devices. Dozens or more were always on.
    -   Underwent septoplasty/turbinate reduction.
    -   Anesthesia sensitivity led to 3–4 months of brain fog.
    -   Candida diagnosed again, treated with nystatin/diet.
    -   Quit job, spent time in sun and nature, started to heal.
    -   Diagnosed with C-PTSD.
        -   Suspicious of the diagnosis, but will provide details below.
-   Age 44:
    -   Returned to remote tech work.
    -   Methylcobalamin shots for MTHFR (homozygous C677T), but hypermethylation worsened symptoms.
    -   NAC resolved remaining panic/mood issues.
-   Age 45:
    -   Began full-body tanning regularly.
    -   Tan very easily but noticed a reddish tan tone compared to childhood.
-   Age 46:
    -   Received glutathione injections and liposomal glutathione.
-   Age 47:
    -   Discovered quantum biology.
    -   Stopped seeing doctors, weaned off supplements except magnesium.
-   Age 48:
    -   Discontinued magnesium.
    -   Began waking for sunrise consistently.
    -   Life got better

### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:37:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349065>

**C-PTSD Notes**

These are symptoms that I wrote down at the time of the diagnoses. I never sought treatment and they are all completely resolved except for:

-   Exaggerated startle response.
-   Trouble maintaining (romantic) relationships
-   Suppressing emotions unconsciously (but it's getting better).

I did not include all the details of things considered "trauma" in my history. Too personal. But there is a not-insignificant amount, and there were both acute and chronic events. Perhaps my skepticism of the diagnosis is a symptom to the of the diagnosis, but it just doesn't feel like a big issue to me anymore.

**Mental Symptoms:**

-   Emotional detachment/disassociation. Pervasive sense of being defective or “different”, self-loathing.
-   Trouble maintaining relationships, and feeling disconnected or isolated, even when around others.
-   Emotional Flashbacks
-   Nightmares related to events.
-   Triggers causing sudden emotional reactions.
-   Constantly feeling on edge, or unable to relax. Coping by always “doing something”
-   Exaggerated startle response.
-   Sleep disturbances.
-   Avoiding thoughts, places, or people tied to the trauma.
-   Suppressing emotions to cope, often unconsciously.
-   Anhedonia, spiritual/existential disconnection.

**Physical Manifestations:**

-   Chronic fatigue/pain.
-   Gastrointestinal issues.
-   Headaches.
-   Shallow breathing

### petrichor — 2025-03-24T00:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349210>

**Insomnia notes**

Experiments and their effect on the middle of the night wake ups. None of these were interventions I stuck to for the long term. I just wanted to experiment and learn.

1\. Protein + carb snack before bed - No effect
2\. Benadryl - Effective at 25mg, but side effects (grogginess, low mood) are intolerable.
3\. Psylocibin - 100 mg in the morning. Effective at first. One dose got me several days of sleeping through the night. The next dose got me a week. Eventually it stopped working
4\. Procain therapy - 10 mg injected into lower back. Three days of sleeping through the night.
5\. Glycine - 3 grams. Negative effect. Had trouble falling asleep
6\. CBD - 10 mg. Negative effect. Unable to fall asleep for hours
7\. CBD + THC 1:1 ratio - 5 mg. Slept through the night, but didn't like it otherwise. Didn't wake up rested.
8\. Phenibut - 800mg. I was taking this inadvertently. It was one ingredient in a supplement I was taking. It was very effective, but when the FDA cracked down on it and the supplement was no longer available I had two weeks of terrible withdrawal effects.
9\. Melatonin - .5 mg. This helped shorten the duration of the wake up, but often lead to grogginess in the AM

# Thread: Is the vernal equinox causing puffy eyes and infection/ immune system crash?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-vernal-equinox-causing-puffy-eyes-and-infection-immune-system-crash.30584/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2025-03-29T23:10:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-vernal-equinox-causing-puffy-eyes-and-infection-immune-system-crash.30584/post-349601>

Jack our consult raised a lot of questions and I am still searching for useful solutions, although it was interesting to hear so much of the girl in the Dallas airport on the spring equinox. I am wondering what you think:

My gums above my canine on the right side has been a little inflamed the past few weeks alongside my conjunctivitis symptoms and skin eruptions. Is this another symptom of the same thing?

Last year I also had conjunctivitis for several months on and off starting in January and subsiding in November. (This is my second year, and so it's 3 months. ) The eyes create mucus with the initial onset but continue to be inflamed, puffy and watery without mucus. Still the optometrist (from my May visit last year ) said it tests convincingly at conjunctivitis. Yes the situation returns after even a month of antibiotics for the eyes. You suggested saline solution. I am wondering if you might suggest Ivermectin (how many treatments?) or something else?

I eat all local, mostly fresh vacuum packed organic ribeyes and seafood raw with some fruit. I cook the shellfish. Could these raw meats be causing the problem? (To optimize the biophotons)

If I am in the northern hemisphere then I experience the sun's light the most intensely and coming from the highest angle in the sky at the summer solstice, yes? Not at the spring equinox. Yes? Or am I confused?

Or could my immune system struggle with this weird infection that created a few pussy cysts be a bacterial related challenge unrelated to the equinox?

Is my immune system really "old"" or is it just currently taxed with bacteria\\microbes here in Costa Rica?

If I am turning 60 and still have my natural blonde hair color without graying, is it possible that I haven't run out of stem cells but rather my chronic dry skin is a result of my environment and reversible if I just crack the code?

My daughter appreciated your feedback. We are currently reading Jason Lowry's book and Jaws. But both books seem to be misguiding as they point to diet as both the change agents. Jason is missing the DHA (light) component and instead pointing to cooked food and our access to more calories as pivotal to our brain development. Jaws points to sleeping with our mouths open and chewing methods instead of light environment as the cause of malocclusions. Yet my daughter is wondering if you--as a neurosurgeon and dentist-- see any validity to these authors' arguments?

# Thread: Richards optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Richard Sköld — 2025-04-01T01:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/post-349801>

I was born 4 days early at a hospital, in Gotenburg during the late 80s. When I arrived I had Jaundice but this healed itself without any treatment after just one day.

I bet my mum had big time Blue light toxic, both my mum and my grandmother both had big eye problems. Grandmother had glucoma and my mum now in here 70s also have glucoma on one eye and have had glasses since early childhood.

I did not get a lot of vaccines, just 3 different ones (tetanus (3 shots, first year), polio (3 shots first year) and MMR ( 2 shoots at 2 years and 12 years). Later in life I have taken one shoot for the swine flu in 2009 in the US and 2 shoots against TBE ( between 2010-2012)

In my 2nd year of life I developed an ear infection that did not go away by antibiotics and it took me almost 6 months being away from daycare to get rid of the infection.

First 7 years in my life I grew pretty fast and was already 5 feet and 3 inches tall. Then it slowed down and today Im at 6,6 feet tall.

I have as long as I can remember struggled with my memory. Feeling that I need to read texts over and over again to make sense of it. When I read a book I hardly remember anything from it. I struggle with oral communication in front of crowds where I loose my line of thoughts. In conversations with other people I seldom recall a lot of what was told. Having troubles holding eye contact with a person while talking.
I have also never felt comfortable with my voice. My smell is not great either, I feel Im not picking up on smells easily and I really need to focus to be able to smell stuff.

Also during my childhood I got Raynauds , which my Mum also have so dont know if this is from her mitochondria or the environment causing this.

My nails on my feet and my fingers have a wavy or ridges shape. Never been certain to what this means and what I can do about this? Had this since like 10 years back. Asked Jack about this and what I got from this is might be related to Vitamin B12 deficiency, that could be that my mitochondria is not making enough water (most likely due to nnEMF) and therefore cannot retain this soluble vitamin.

After graduating in Economics I went into the finance sector in Stockholm, Sweden and spent almost 10 years between 2010-2020 in this sector. All the time in front of computer screens, indoors during heavy phone usage and wifi. During the same period of time I was a heavy coffee user and added to this in my spare time working out indoors under blue lights and added protein shakes and PWO with high amount of caffeine.
This slowly laid the foundation for a lot of different health issues, in 2013 when I had a period of 6 months with throat issues (starting in autumn) and was treated with antibiotics 2 times and finally when we entered spring it went away, probably due to more UV light.

Then during 2015 I had big troubles with keeping myself awake during work. Many times during 10am-4pm I yawned a lot and struggle to stay awake. Sometimes in meetings I feel asleep.

I think I felt some kind of brain fog and the doctors could find anything when testing me. I had troubles falling asleep , always felt too hot and uneven tempature when going to bed. Drank a lot of caffeine and used PWO drinks when working out in a blue lit gym. I was very often sick and felt low in energy.

This was when I found the cold and started following Wim Hof. Without me knowing it many of my health issues became better.

I felt more energized, moving away from indoor training to outdoors training and more natural movement (ido portal).

Still struggle with my memory, have hard time remembering words and oral communication. Started thinking it might be related to hearing and not enough mitochondria energy in my brain area.

Since 2015 or 2016 I have noticed some red spots on my body. (Think its Campell de Morgan, cherry angioma)
First one and now I think I have 5 spots. Two on my left inside elbow. 2 on my right chest. And one behind my left ear (on the skull bone).

I also have what I think is some kind of fungus on my hands and feet which has never been treated properly. I always though that sun would heal it but its apparent to me that this is not the case for me at least maybe I need to add something?

Find Dr jack in the beginning of 2017. Since then it has been a long journey which has made me more energized , open minded and I feel that my thinking is more clear.

I also had one of my wisdom teeth removed around 2019-2020. Curious if there are any negative side effects of this?

During Covid I got infected but my only symptoms was a mild headache for 4 days and no other issue. I never got vaccinated.

Since birth I also have one cafe au lait spot on my upper thigh. What I could read from one of your blogs just one wiuld be of no issue. In curious to why we get these spots and is it a sign of some mismatch?

I also have bunions on both my feet since childhood.

Today I dont work in the finance industry anymore. Working with people, natural movement and Manual Therapy (Feldenkrais).

This year im turning 37 years old. I have been reducing blue light from screens, indoor lightning etc and felt a small difference. I have come to realize that I wont be able to be optimal living here and decided to try out a move to El Salvador in December this year.

### Richard Sköld — 2025-04-01T01:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/post-349803>

Today

Been in el salvador now for almost 5 months, spending my days watching the sunrise been out almost the whole day during sun is out. Just a small break between 2-4 and then back at the sunset.

Blocking all lights at night. Bed time at 8pm and getting up around 4-5am depending on my body.

Done measurement of RF in the areas that I spend time sunbathing and also in my sleeping area to maximize my benefits here and thats been good measurements.

Had some wins while Im here.

Think my raynauds went away (been trying out ice bath without any sign of it) and seems like I have got some new or added pigment on my hands that I did not have before.

Fungus on my hands went away.
Right foot still some fungus issues under the foot.

Bunions is starting to heal and go away

My voice sounds different and Im not so tired after speaking as I was before coming here.

Coming here got me also thinking that I might have had some gut issues connected with my autoimmune but this seems to have improved, feel less tight in my abdomen so something has shifted.

Still dont feel a big difference regarding memory so wonder if I need to do things differently, staying longer or added things?

Did some labs this weekend and would love some kind of feedback.

Vitamin D 100,8 ng/mL

HS crp 0,17 mg/L

Periferal Blood smear:

Red Blood cells: Notormocytic with mild hypocromy.

\* This means that red blood cells are normal (normocytic) in size, but have a slight deficiency of haemoglobin (hypochromy). Haemoglobin is the protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells, so hypocromy may indicate possible anaemia.

\* White blood cell: It is observed: Mild leucopenia with normal cell maturation, without any other particularity.

\* This indicates that there is a slight decrease in the number of white blood cells (leukopenia). White blood cells are part of the immune system and help fight infections. Normal cell maturation means that the white cells that are present are normally developed.

\*platelet: It is observed: Normal in number, size and shape.

Dont really understand what this tells me?

Liver Panel
See attached file

Kidney Panel

See attached file

What I could tell is my Vitamin D is very good.

Low inflammation from the HS crp.

the Bun/Cre ratio is high approx 16 , is telling me Im not making mitochondria water good enough? Have struggled with hydration here seeing as Im outdoors all the day. Have added some days salt to my water and trying to eat more oysters and bone broth.

The other ones I dont know how to interpret

# Thread: Sam's Optimal Journal: Phoenix Rising
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### samantha_jordan — 2025-04-16T21:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-350798>

I'm so grateful to Dr. Kruse and to be here in this moment. I felt close to leaving this plane and found the needed trailhead in the nick of time.

Me: 36 years old, live in Rural Oregon, mother to a 3 year old beautiful soul, artist, lover of life

I found Dr. Kruse's work through Danny Jones. Hope expanded for me after this. My intuitive belief that the Sun was essential and would not cause harm was affirmed. My belief that we are creature's of light had a physical map of understanding laid out.

Before I start my history of struggles, I want to tell myself I am good. I am enough. I am a phoenix rising. I am beautiful. I am strong. I've always pushed and worked so hard. I have survived so much, to make it to this moment. There is hope for me to improve, to grow to FLOURISH. I've spent countless days/hours/months camping, hiking and soaking in hotsprings, feeling love and light all around me.

Physical battles to have overcome:

2003 (age 15) weird "viral" response. Tested positive for Epstein Barr. Symptoms: extremely painful migraine lasting 10+ hours, at some point temperature read 104. Took to hospital.
Fever broke, but was unable to respond/slept almost non stop for 4 days.
Frequent streptococcus and tonsillitis through middle school/highscool/young 20's
2009 sadly had an abortion that led to symptoms of depression/anxiety/derealization. Started fluoxetine and was on and off (average 3 years, on 3 years off) since my final quit after finding Dr Kruse's work 2024.
Hairstylist from 2008-2022 (chemical exposure/heavy metals)
Became severely responsive to any common virus and would indefinitely turn into ear infections/tonsillitis/sinus infection/bronchitis and two boughts of pneumonia. Bronchitis/sinus infections would repeatedly prolong for up to 3 months. Would become so ill would be unable to work and desperately sought medical help. Was given countless series of antibiotics, prednisone and diagnosed with mild asthma.
Found out was living in heavy black mold (floors caved in bathroom).
HSV II diagnosis 2014
Tonsillectomy 2016ish after 8 month long tonsillitis that ended up in fatigue so heavy I could hardly stay awake.
Had some relief after tonsillectomy and was chasing the sun for two years with the best tan of my life.
Carpal tunnel syndrome diagnosis
Developed severe pain in base of spine, hips, glutes, sciatica that wouldn't quit for months.
Had always had extremely tight muscles, neck pain.
Frequent stomach pain, nausea and diarrhea.
Started breaking out in hives.
No solid bowel movements for long periods up to 2 years.
Felt extremely tired again.
Fibromyalgia diagnosis 2017
Stomach pain and nausea became increasingly worse. Horrible stomach pain in the morning.
Saw gastroentonoligist 2018. Was told likely an ulcer and they wanted to scope and put me on protonpump inhibitors, I declined.
Went keto for 3 months, closely monitored my diet. This did not resolve stomach pain and nausea.
Blood testing for allergies. Found mild Ig allergy to eggs. Had definite relief when removing eggs as they were a primary food source. Still struggled with some pain and nausea.
Bilateral osteoarthritis knee diagnosis 2019
2022 pregnancy. Vomiting and nausea to the extreme for the entire gestation. Lived in the toilet bowl. Couldn't handle eating anything but carbs. Meat smelled like it was rotting. Had relief of chronic pain during this time. Naturally went into labor after being pregnant for exactly 43 weeks, refusing fear mongering to induce. Was committed to natural birth, but after 44+ hours of labor she wouldn't descend and ended up having a c section.

Was able to eat again after pregnancy. Had good digestive for awhile, but life events led to a place of extreme stress. The chronic nausea returned and eventually led to daily vomiting. I felt extremely unwell. Began having strong dizziness spells where I felt like I was going to the sky. Developed tremors that went on for months and knew something was very wrong.

Then I found Dr Kruse's work and everything has begun to change ☀️

Now:

I've had complete relief of stomach pain, nausea, vomiting. I intuitively believe I had an ulcer that healed. Not living with daily nausea after so many years is INCREDIBLE.

Tremors are gone. I no longer feel nearness of death/neurological symptoms

Unspeakably grateful for the time and relief gifted to me by this incredible knowledge.

Habits:

Wake up with the sunrise. Barefoot/least clothes possible outside every morning, accomplished through the winter. Sun salutations in the mud and now the spring sun finally warms me. Minimum 30 minutes morning sun.

Asleep by 10pm. Committed to this, but still in training

Reducing screen use and artificial light significantly. I acknowledge I still have room for improvement here, but I'm far from the place I was.

Grounding sheet on bed, walking barefoot everywhere possible.

Methylene blue: have consumed over 800mg since October (troscriptions). Incredible feeling of life after using.

Maximum time spent outside possible.

Hormone testing done in December after feelings of so much improvement (Note testing was done at optimal time in cycle, just forgot to write date down):

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Improvement Needed: Though I have had miraculous physical relief, I feel my hormones are still trashed. Intellectually I believe in my resiliency and power to heal. Mentally, I'm struggling. I'm blessed to say my life circumstances are wonderful. I've manifested my dreams into reality. Yet I feel an emotionally battle that must be primarily physical. Though I fall asleep easily, I wake up too early often (530AM), with so much anxiety (high cortisol probably). I feel a constant mental struggle to encourage myself and know that I'm successful. I do not feel I've reached my highest potential of optimal health. I'm willing to make bigger changes, but not sure the best direction. I've always been a workhorse and consistent. My goal is to maintain consistency in these life improvements and see what may come. To be patient and know that I am enough.

Quitting vaping nicotine. I am going to do this and had 5 years with no nicotine previously. It is a crutch for relieving physical anxiety. I'm two months off cannabis which I'm very proud of. I was an extremely heavy concentrate smoker for almost 20 years and now I'm done.

Looking forward to improvement of my hormone function. So encouraged after reviewing my progress here. I can see my journey a bit clearer after writing it out.

I want to know I'm at optimal health levels. When I review this I know I will see more progress. I'm open to growing and learning and that journey is not always smooth. I'm opening myself up to hear what I need to change and how I can create the feelings of wellness that I crave.

Love & Light

### samantha_jordan — 2025-04-25T20:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-351277>

Last night was my 4th day cutting out all artificial light after sundown/powering off wifi. I'm so proud of breaking these addictions. The silence at night is so relieving, soothing to me. I become tired as the sun goes down.
I'm deeply aware of the escapism from reality I've relied on for so long. I feel things are much clearer. When I lay onto the earth at sunrise, my vision snaps clear.
I've always been a perceptive individual, intuitively knowing truth. I've known many actions by the general public are folly. I've protected myself from disgusting vaccinations and trash food and lies, by sheer will and belief in my truth. Yet the transformation I'm experiencing now is radical and humbling. All of the escapism, the pacifiers that have misaligned me, are falling away.
Yet it is painful. I woke last night at 2, unable to fall back asleep as irrational concerns chatted incessantly in my mind. I can feel the front part of my brain, where my third eye is, pulsing me awake when I wish to sleep. Maybe this is my time for contemplation and not for rest yet. Maybe it is hormones out of balance and begging for the right care.
I'm going to rise up. I'm going to differentiate the duality within myself.
I incessantly dream of the beach and moving somewhere better. I worry that I can't reach my healthy goals here, despite incredible gains over the last 6 months of morning sun gazing and dirt hugging. There is hair regrowing all over my head I didn't even know I'd lost. There is light rebuilding inside me, creating a new glow more radiant than ever. There is passion to change resonating from me. There is courage growing that has allowed me to throw the crutches away. Without the crutches I'm limping, dragging, but I believe I will run. I believe I will be able to give so much to a community and to feel the warm hug of communion with other beautiful individuals. The happiness will grow inside of me and create beauty all around me.
I will cast aside the doubt, the shame. Shining, I will hold my daughter in the light and triumph.

### mfawski — 2025-04-25T21:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-351280>

Amazing Samantha! Thank you for sharing

# Thread: Kelly in Mexico
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kel — 2025-04-22T01:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351066>

Hello everyone. I just joined a couple weeks ago but have been following Uncle Jack for a few years. I was first told about him by a friend going through brain cancer. I was searching for info to try and help my dad who was dealing with stage 4 pancreatic cancer. We lost him in January of 2024. It was a highly stressful experience and a definite punch in the mouth as uncle Jack likes to say..
I am here picking up the pieces. I had a strange sort of spiritual awakening the end of 2019 and basically got the message the sun cures all from a singer who had passed over. curiously enough he has a song where he says “hemaglobin and chlorophyll same thing.” So when I found Uncle Jack the picture started to make more sense and I knew I was in the right place.
Background:
I grew up in the Bay area. No real health issues as a kid. I did receive a vaccine around 3 years old that cause me to drag one leg around for a few months. Only surgery I have even had was my wisdom teeth out around 10 years old. I took accutane in my early 20’s. Me and two of my three brothers all had bad acne as teens. I did work out that my mom had alot of dental procedures around that time, so guess that could have something to do with it.
My mom had stage 4 ovarian cancer in her early 40’s. It was removed and she is healthy and 76 now. She also had a benign tumor removed from her parada gland about 20 years ago. Says she remembers that spot in her neck bothering her since she was a kid. she grew up in upstate New York.
My dad had nephritis as a kid before penicillin was discovered. He spend a year around 9 years old in the hospital, watched all the kids die around him and then somehow survived. He was also born with a collapsed lung. violent childhood. became a life long pot addict. So alot of unresolved trauma and emotional stress for all of us growing up around him. He mostly grew up in Westport, Connecticut. He had stomach aches for last ten years or so which we now believe were the cancer and then got 2 covid vaccines… I think thats when the cancer went wild.

I traveled to India in 2008 and got 3 vaccines in one day. One was for hepatitis, then a tetunus shot and not sure what the 3rd was. Left for India a few days later and then started to feel like hell. Thought I was going to die on hotel room floor one night. Stomach and digestion hasn't been great since. I was hospitalized 5 weeks later when we got home after coming down with dengue fever. Started loosing consciousness and was rushed to emergency room where I was given Cipro and then had an allergic reaction to that.
No vaccines since!
I was a vegetarian for 20 years.
Had a healthy daughter in 2016. Home birth! All went well!

Fast forward to today. Main issues and reason I joined forum are
poor sleep, waking up tired
bags under eyes
lots of gas, burping and indigestion
pee at least a couple times thru night
no sex drive
stinky armpits
also my ears feel extra sensitive. especially during solar eclipses. the one last year had my ears ringing for a week straight. then there was one last month and ears rang again for 2 days..

I live near Puerto Vallarta Mexico. Have been here 20 plus years. Get a ton of sun. Definitly did over due the nnEMF and computer time during covid.Jumped down all the rabbit holes and then spent way too much time trying to figure it all out as I stared at the screen.
I am now grounding daily and have been watching sunrise for 2 months or so. Also trying to watch sunsets. We are outside tho most of our day here. Eating more local. getting used to oysters! breathe work and massage and walks in nature. I have a loving partner and alot of support. good community and strong family connections.
I am super sensitive to shitty light bulbs. wifi, perfume, soaps, cleaning products etc etc…
I feel extremely grateful for Uncle Jack. I told my partner they need to add another love language to the 5 love languages book. Tough love!! I so resonate with Uncle Jack’s straight talk and see why it is so necessary living in the world we are today to snap everyone out of their stupor!
I will stop there for now..

### Kel — 2025-04-27T18:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351384>

One week in of morning sun, while grounded. Feeling so connected to my garden and finding all the different little sit spots! My hair feels thicker, I am sleeping, my digestion is better, and I think my wrinkles look softer! Also got two dear friends doing the same. Renewed attitude towards life. So magical and bewildering and also infuriating that this has been there all along. Contemplating where else in life simplicity has been overlooked!

# Thread: Nick's Optimal Journal: My Journey to Health and Decentralized Psychotherapy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal-my-journey-to-health-and-decentralized-psychotherapy.30846/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nick-Rolser — 2025-05-07T17:12:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal-my-journey-to-health-and-decentralized-psychotherapy.30846/post-352098>

New member here. 27-year-old male, living in New Hampshire on the Seacoast. My reasons for being here are two-fold:

First, I’m a songwriter and psychology student.

My interest in psychology started with Carl Jung, whose work I began reading when I first dropped out of school at 19 to pursue music. I attribute reading Jung with learning how to think properly, based off of empirical observation. He also gave me a map to better understand the unconscious mind which I found helped channel my creativity. As satisfying as it is to make and perform music, I found that it wasn't something I could make a living doing. So my passion for psychology led me back to school. However, the psychology taught in academia is highly centralized and ineffective. They've completely lost touch with the history of dynamic psychiatry.

Later I found Rupert Sheldrake, whose morphic resonance theory gave a potential basis for Jung’s archetypal hypothesis. Then when I read Becker the whole thing clicked: The “morphogenetic field” from Sheldrake's hypothesis, which seemed so abstract, had a potential basis in the electromagnetic field, acting as the mechanism behind information transfer and species-level inheritance of instincts (Jung's "collective unconscious"). I'm far from having it all worked out yet, but it feels like the right direction to take. I haven't looked into Levin's research yet, but I'm glad I found this community first to understand where his limitations will be.

Psychodynamic theory at its best, despite it’s criticism as being “subjective”, goes much deeper than the cognitive behavioral theory which currently monopolizes the field, in the same way molecular biology has monopolized medicine. Poppers rejection of Freud's theories as "psuedoscience" were premature, since neuroscience had yet to catch up to provide any objective basis for the subjective experience. By updating it with modern neuroscience and quantum physics, I believe it could become a truly decentralized approach to psychotherapy. So I’ve gone back to school in the hopes of contributing to that. That’s one reason for my quantum entanglement within this community.

I start with the above because that describes who I am and what I'm passionate about far more than my second reason for being here, which is merely a symptom and not something I intend to define myself by.

That reason is a health issue that's persisted for about three years now. Dermatitis/rosacea of some kind. Exclusively on my face. Last year I went down the diet guru route—primarily carnivore/animal based. I followed a protocol of customizing the diet, “gentle cleansing”, and exercising from an online guru who claimed to heal skin diseases. After a year of dedicating myself to that, I wound up somewhat jacked with better redox in my gut, although my skin didn’t budge.

Fast-forward to January of this year when a friend of mine introduced me to Dr. Kruse. I started with the Huberman podcast and it clicked immediately. I didn’t understand most of it, but my intuition told me everything I needed to know. Since then, I’ve been deep diving—I read Becker’s _The Body Electric_ and am halfway through _Cross Currents_ right now.

I’ve also read the Solar Callus blog and have started making shrimp broth. I’ve been getting at least an hour of AM sunlight most mornings, as well as tanning frequently in the afternoon. Pretty much every area of my body can tan with the exception of my face which burns up pretty quickly.

I’ve been desperately trying to put an end to my caffeine addiction, which I know is a consequence of my low-redox environment. I usually drink one or two cups of coffee a day so my brain can function optimally. But it simultaneously produces anxiety and is too much of a stressor on top of all the light pollution of modern living.

I’m planning on moving to a better location in December once I finish my bachelors. Obviously I’m aware that my light environment sucks here, and even with the arrival of spring they’ve been going berserk with the chemtrails. I’ve been thinking about Florida, but leaving the country is not out of the question some time down the road. My mitochondrial haplotype is V. I’m not aware of any relevant SNPS. Perhaps someone can recommend where I might send my data to find these out?

As far as family history goes, my mother has had kidney stones in the past. My maternal grandmother has lost her hearing and is showing signs of memory impairment (which is no surprise to me given that my family recently got her on Bluetooth hearing aids). If I'm not mistaken my great-grandmother also had both of these issues. I'm aware of the skin-brain neuroectoderm connection so I'm assuming that's how that ties in to my own situation. Curious to hear if anyone has additional insights to offer here.

Thanks for reading. Excited to be here and to learn from this community.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Optimal Journal - From Nassau Bahamas to Piha NZ, addictions distractions and lifelong sun obsession
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-from-nassau-bahamas-to-piha-nz-addictions-distractions-and-lifelong-sun-obsession.30849/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-05-08T02:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-from-nassau-bahamas-to-piha-nz-addictions-distractions-and-lifelong-sun-obsession.30849/post-352122>

**Warning LONG and not expecting anyone to read, this is more for me to keep track and see patterns etc.**

41/f no health concerns except, lifelong um sometime problematic enthusiasm (ahaem addiction) with anything buzzy!

**History**:

Born and grew up in the Bahamas from Bahamian dad and German mum
Dad is a doc, and after my one and only vax at 3 months (and life threatening reaction) went quantum/holistic and never looked back
The bad: because of this and shunning of conventional medicine that almost killed his daughter, dad has too much focus on botanical medicine/sups/intravenous vitamins/ozone functional BS MUM opened a chain of health shops that funded my education and life for 30 years.
The good: Dad always had us up in the morning barefoot, shuffling our feet on the damp grass or sand, mum was obsessed with sun when pregnant, never wore sunscreen, grew up on the beach, not allowed cell phone etc until I left Bahamas for university. Ate a macrobiotic type diet rich in fresh seafood.
Went to university in Florida, high EMF but great sun... BUT then to Canada to become a naturopathic doctor at 21- problems started. Started smoking weed to sleep, lived in 11floor downtown condo, continued for almost 15 years. Became quite dependant on cannabis to sleep
Moved back to Nassau to practice with my dad in 2013, began drinking a fuck lot. Worked in blue lit office.
2014 Visisted AUckland NZ- fell in love, left my husband and clinic behind for new relationship also addict, toxic relationship serious, class A, cannabis, alcohol, psycadelics, u name it, I probably did it weekly at least.
Discovered JK in 2016. Quit drinking to be able to wake up for sunrise, red lights in house, blue blockers, more seafood daily. Started keto diet.
Still weed dependant to sleep, then discovered phenibut and used this for another 5 years 3x per week
2020 Dumped ex- who was the driver and supplier of all of the substances. Moved rurally to black sand titanomagnetic beach in west Auckland

Started CT, began to change my life, gave up weed, and phenibut. Started sleeping properly and DREAMING for the first time in 15+ years!!

**Where I'm at now:**

I live in an open plan tiny house/studio with no internal walls and no artificial light. Switched from red lights to only a few candles in winter (dark at 6:30)
I work outside on online biz naked in the sun (though for new clients I do wear a shirt haha until they get to know me
I'm outside ALL day. I do Kundalini yoga from 5:30-6:30am then go barefoot walking in the hills (or rucking with 18kg bag) from 7:30-8:30
Work on my daylight (when the fucker will accept my ethenet connecter!!) outside. But do use a BT keyboard as its on a stand. Use phone hardwired for video calls.
Swim in the ocean or waterfall daily at lunch break
Sunset walk barefoot every evening rain or shine.
Barefoot HITT workouts in the sun 2-3x per week

EMF testing/mitigation last year - kill switch on circuits near bed, hardwired internet, removed smart meter, fridge on timer at night, aluminum shielding on deck where I work.
EMF testing 3 months ago dt new tower in area- working on new EMF aluminum screen for above our deck, and already bought the shielding paint - but have yet to get the electrician to put the grounding plate in for the painted wall.
Eat local seasonal raw meat/fish diet (body craves the rae meat ha) . I do poach my eggs still. I grow microgreens so most of my veggies are in the form of brassica etc micros homegrown in the sun.

**Current over enthusiasms - trying to let go of.:**

Walnut, pistachio and macadamia over indulgence- there is NO off switch if these are in the house!
NICOTINE!!! After stressful period with my parents here about 3 months ago, started vaping. Always outside, all day in the sun, but this sucker is pretty sticky!!!!
I fucking LOVE my sauna, been doing infrared sauna since 17, my parents had one, and when I got my first place I bought one. I have one now (Building biologist verified very low EMF) but man if I miss a few days I get quite antsy!!

Labs good, but doc always freaked out about my total cholesterol is over 9mmol
Fasting insulin 6
HBAic bit high at 34
I'm skinny but strong as fuck. 48kg 165cm fitter than I've ever been.
Never injured anymore after years of gym workouts herniated disks, issues with knees etc from 17-35

**Questions/Opinions**?

Our water is rain water, collected from roof, carbon block filtered, then refiltered in Berkey. Then "structured/vortexted in Mayu jug in natural light-
Due to high supplement use, (just sent away salivary samples for testing) wondering if its wort doing DDW to help with addictions?

As a lifelong sun worshipper and melanin-obsessive (was teased so much for being a white kid in the Bahamas so get a bit freaked out when I'm not super brown ha ha ), wondering about spending half the year June-Oct in either Pacific islands (shitty water, DE/Shitty light pollution, awesome sun and seafood) or NE AUstralia (shitty water, shitty magnetism, awesome winter sun). OR would it be more ideal to just stay put and do more CT as I'm pretty healthy/young etc, do some indoor tanning (shitty nnEMF!) but beds here have red light/UVA UVB bulbs?

Given everything else is pretty good/optimal, I mean, can I just relax and enjoy a few more months of vaping

I know JK thinks Ir sauna are underpowered/not worth it but I love the relaxation/sweat, is there a downside if I have time and enjoy it (confirmed low EMF, after we removed the stupid BT thing it had in the roof)? Because I'm pretty skinny in the winter after swims it takes me a while to warm up if I DONT have a short sauna after...
