# Thread: Mood Swings & Mitochondria—Why Cloud Cover Wrecks Me
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-06-19T19:57:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/post-354888>

Can anyone explain—through a quantum biology lens—why my mood shifts so dramatically just from a cloud passing over the sun? It’s like my body downshifts the moment light dims.

This morning, I was outside barefoot trying to get some sunrise through the clouds and trees—but instead of feeling uplifted, I felt flat and down. Late morning, as the sun finally broke through, I felt a noticeable lift. Sitting outside in the strong sunlight, barefoot, eating lunch, everything felt good... until a cloud passed. I felt my mood and energy dip instantly again.

I’ve tried mantras like "My mood is not determined by the weather"—but they don’t touch this. It's not psychological. It feels mitochondrial.

Does anyone else experience this? Any hacks or “tribe tricks” for when the sky is gray?

### Mat.I — 2025-06-20T14:13:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mood-swings-mitochondria%E2%80%94why-cloud-cover-wrecks-me.31144/post-354913>

Your brain is a quantum-coherent light processor. The sun is its oscillator. Clouds scramble the feed. Effect would be redox dependent e.g. low redox = mood instability from energy loss and high redox = mood attunment from coherence loss.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Paige's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaigeK — 2025-06-21T02:28:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-354931>

Hello,
I'm a married 59yo female, Northern European (mostly German) ancestry.
Blood Type B-
mtDNA Haplogroup U, Subclass U2e1
Paternal German family lived in Volga River Valley in Russia for almost 150 years 1765-1900.

Birth Mom was generally healthy except a smoker and died at 67 with lung issues with untreated Lyme disease. She was born in Baltimore and lived near DC in Germantown, MD until age 7 then moved to Evergreen, CO.
Maternal grandmother is from MD, VA, WV, was healthy until the 6+ jabs, now 93 with blood clots, heart issues but lives independently and drives herself to have margaritas with her friends once a week.
Maternal great-grandmother died of bladder cancer in her 90's, lived in MD, VA, WV.

I have brown hair (now half silver), very light blue eyes and skin that tans and freckles, have developed white, brown and red spots over the years.
Born in Denver, CO. Raised in western burbs of Denver uncomfortably close to Rocky Flats where they made nuclear triggers for bombs and became a superfund site and is supposedly clean now. Currently live in Evergreen, CO at 8,000ft elevation. EMF specialist came a few days ago and found 5G blasting us, strong AC magnetic field from overhead power lines at 100' from our back door, I was sleeping with my head where power comes in to the house and a smart meter was just installed and my neighbors have solar that is affecting us. I was schooled in the devastating effects of wifi and our many Sonos speakers and devices. All are off now as is wifi. I need get out of here, I just don't know where to.

I was adopted as a newborn. I had severe allergies as a small child to this day. Allergy shots- ages 5-25.
As a child I had untreated ear infections and multiple treated cases of strep throat.
Tonsils/adenoids removed- age 12
Joined the Air Force - Age 18, (got all the jabs required in the 1980's & 90's) worked as an aircraft mechanic on KC-135, A-7, F-16 aircraft. (Many, many bad chemical/EMF exposures daily in those 9 years, 2 more years in admin, got out at age 29)(worked at Buckley SFB around the corner from the giant satellite dishes for 9 years)
Pelvic Inflammatory infection- age 19
Birth control pills- age 20-30
Migraines started- age 20
Kidney Infection- age 22
Vaginal birth of daughter, she had mild jaundice otherwise healthy- age 23
Cesarean birth of son, major birth defect (gastro-schesis, hypertonic), a lot of emotional trauma for me and a major infection followed
Concussion/closed head injury auto accident- age 28
Got out of the military - age 29
Breast implants inserted- age 29
Tubal ligation- age 30
Complete hysterectomy due to endometriosis - age 31
Started taking Premarin - age 31-38, then bio-identical bi-est to present.
Hypo-thyroid (healed this once but reoccurred and is current), Adrenal Fatigue age 31 (mostly healed now), started taking levothyroxine, DHEA, pregnenelone, natural cortisol supplements
Somewhere around here we got Wifi.
Breast Implants removed - age 43
Celiac diagnosed, Melasma, bunion/bunionette surgery right foot - age 43
Subtotal Colectomy w/ ileo-rectal anastomoses, sigmoidocele, cyctocele, rectocele repairs (due to colonic inertia, serious infection followed as well as severe anemia)- age 44
Upper GI bleed (esophageal tear) while climbing a strenuous 14k foot mountain- age 45
Rt shoulder rotator cuff repair- age 47
Abdominal scar revision surgery- age 47
Nasal terbinate reduction/septoplasty- age 49
Intraocular Lens Implants to correct vision- age 50 (blocks 90% of UV)
Lyme disease/ numerous co-infections, chronic pain (all original infections cleared) - age 51
Sjogrens/RA diagnosed (ANA still positive but RA now negative)- age 52
Took hydrocortisone for a year.
CVID immunodeficiency w/complement disorder diagnosed (IgG now borderline at 500, IgA low at 9)- age 52
Received crap biologics IVIG infusions plus Ruconest- ages 52-55 (the last year w/chest port, I quit before jabs became widespread in fall of 2020)
Non-length dependent small fiber neuropathy, mast cell activation, severe environmental chemical senstivities - age 52
Hashimoto's - age 53 (now in remission)
Osteoporosis - age 53 (has not progressed)
Aseptic Meningitis/sepis/GI bleed- age 53
Stiff Person Syndrome misdiagnosed- age 53 (it was wrong but they gave me rx of 6mg Xanax and Baclofen and I dumbly took it for 1 year, weaned off at age 54)
Stopped all infusions and prescriptions except for thyroid, bi-est, progesterone, testosterone, singulair- age 54
Numerous and various overlapping Lyme type infections, viruses, bacteria, parasites, one covid infection (no jabs)- age 55-59 (present)
I may have chronic pancreatitis since I need 10 Creon/day to digest food. I don’t drink much alcohol, so most likely autoimmune caused or aftermath of colectomy.
Son committed suicide, daughter became estranged - age 58
I'm finally supposedly free of infections for the first time since my son died 2 years ago. So I've begun my recovery! I want more now.
Recent brain MRI for recurrent migraines showed: Sequela of chronic microvascular ischemia.
My feet and ankles have been painful for 20 years (about the same time we got the first WiFi)
Now I have a neuroma in one foot.
I have a lot of floaters in my vision (maybe dead Lyme spirochetes, the eye dr thought I wouldn’t heal there were so many in my eyes).

Current Rx- Oxycodone, Creon, Levothyroxine, Hydroxycloroquin, Montelucast, Amitriptyline, Sumatriptan. compounded: Bi-estrogen, Progesterone, testosterone.

OTC- Pregnenolone, DHEA, B-Active, Vit C, D3, MK-7, iron, copper, osteo supplement, probiotics, beta plus (gall bladder support), cal/mag butyrate, manganese, xyzal, HistDAO, Quercetin, NAC, L-Lysine, Pharma GABA, Nasocort

I want to get off everything possible without crashing further.

I’m furious I let them break me with the colectomy and ocular lens implants.. and the other things (infusions and drug). Hopefully it’s not too late to heal some of the damage.

I am making plans to go to ES soon by myself for as long as it takes to heal.
Husband plans to sell house and move south. Thinking of TX but need more input. I don't want to jump out of the frying pan and into the fire. I have a feeling that I will want to stay in ES.

The Melasma on my face drives me crazy. I hope one day my mitochondria and system will heal and stop this. What is the melasma telling me? Will learn as much as I can.

### PaigeK — 2025-06-30T17:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-355533>

6/30/25 woke up with a UTI this morning, the second on this year. So much for no infections.

### PaigeK — 2025-07-17T20:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-356492>

Forgot to mention that I received several jabs of RhoGAM in my childbearing years.. The hits keep coming to mind.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-18T12:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-356535>

> [PaigeK said:](/goto/post?id=354931)
>
> What is the melasma telling me?

It is telling you that you have a very bad light environment and there is a deeper issue. The melanin dial gets turned up (no UV required) it is a surface protection mechanism & ends up deposited in the wrong areas/layers. Result is irregular pigmentation..

### PaigeK — 2025-07-18T18:46:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-356555>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=356535)
>
> It is telling you that you have a very bad light environment and there is a deeper issue. The melanin dial gets turned up (no UV required) it is a surface protection mechanism & ends up deposited in the wrong areas/layers. Result is irregular pigmentation..

Thank you Mat! Dr Kruse also said it’s from nn light and wearing sunscreen is counter productive.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-19T06:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-356585>

> [PaigeK said:](/goto/post?id=356555)
>
> Thank you Mat! Dr Kruse also said it’s from nn light and wearing sunscreen is counter productive.

That is your "light" environment. Inclusive of what you put on your skin. It alters the solar spectrum that hits the skin (scrambles the signals)... Light also includes the kind you cannot see in certain frequency bands..We only see less than 1 octave out of 80+ that is 3.4 x 10^-8 % or one part in ~ 2.94 billion based on frequencies...

### PaigeK — 2025-07-20T19:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-356676>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=356585)
>
> That is your "light" environment. Inclusive of what you put on your skin. It alters the solar spectrum that hits the skin (scrambles the signals)... Light also includes the kind you cannot see in certain frequency bands..We only see less than 1 octave out of 80+ that is 3.4 x 10^-8 % or one part in ~ 2.94 billion based on frequencies...

I'be been thinking a lot about this. I really appreciate your input. It makes so much sense. thanks again.

### PaigeK — 2025-10-23T02:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-361451>

I went to El Salvador and spent 6.5 weeks in Shalpa with India (she is a gem!). During that time my body pain went down dramatically and my thinking became clearer again (thank God). I had planned to spend 6 months there but had to go home after I got a parasite and the medication I needed was nowhere to be found. I was miserable with tremendous itchy skin. I must also report that I got 3 total infections (mycoplasma pneumonia, trematodes and gut candida) which is not a surprise with my history. I've been able to actually absorb some of Jack's lessons lately (hooray!) and have been in the sun everyday since I have been home in the Colorado mountains. I'm thankful for the higher UV at 8,000 feet elevation while I'm still here. We have resolved to move (hubs refuses to leave US) and are currently considering far eastern Oklahoma in the Ozarks at 36º N on the Grand Lake O' the Cherokees. The closest military base is 70 miles (thoughts anyone?) I'm looking forward to no magnetic fields and mitigated other nnEMFs.

I see myself returning to ES annually most likely.

I had labs drawn and while I was considering the results, Jack's blog [https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/](https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/) came up and took a stab at reading it since I've had chronic anemia and autoimmunity. Wow, it seemed like he wrote it just for me.

The lab showed that I have slightly low mchc (dehydration, hypoxia and heme issues right?) with high calcium and albumin.
My immunoglobulins actually went up a tic (hooray, maybe from my time in ES?) IgG up from 597 to 602, IgA up from 69 to 72. Ok not fantastic, but they went UP, I'll take that little win.
My SARS CoV spike is 50 (I am not jabbed but got it once) is this good?
Thyroid levels look ok on my current dose and hashimotos is still negative (hashi was positive twice in the past 10 years).
However reverse T3 is within range but high at 21.5 (leptin resistance or ok?)
Vit D 25-Hydroxy is high at 112. I haven't taken oral vit D since August so this is what my skin is making?
Calcitrol (1,25 di-OH VitD) is 66.1. Is this difference showing my liver is not converting well?
ANA is still positive, barely at 1:80 but has changed patterns from speckled to homogenous, it that good or meh?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-10-23T08:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-361463>

> [PaigeK said:](/goto/post?id=361451)

> I see myself returning to ES annually most likely.
>
> I had labs drawn and while I was considering the results, Jack's blog [https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/](https://optimalklubs.com/decentralized-medicine-76-why-are-anemia-and-autoimmunity-bedfellows/) came up and took a stab at reading it since I've had chronic anemia and autoimmunity. Wow, it seemed like he wrote it just for me.
>
> The lab showed that I have slightly low mchc (hypoxia and heme issue right?), high calcium and albumin.
> My immunoglobulins actually went up a tic (hooray, maybe from my time in ES?) IgG up from 597 to 602, IgA up from 69 to 72. Ok not fantastic, but they went UP, I'll take that little win.
> My SARS CoV spike is 50 (I am not jabbed but got it once) is this good?
> Thyroid levels look ok on my current dose and hashimotos is still negative.
> However reverse T3 is within range but high at 21.5 (leptin resistance or ok?)
> Vit D 25-Hydroxy is high at 112. I haven't taken oral vit D since August so this is what my skin is making?
> Calcitrol (1,25 di-OH VitD) is 66.1. Is this difference showing my liver is not converting well?
> ANA is still positive, barely at 1:80 but has changed patterns from speckled to homogenous, it that good or meh?

We all have different medical histories (and systems) so this may not apply to your situation.
I have traveled to MANY third world countries and have always brushed my teeth and bathed in the local water and eaten local food in some VERY off the Main Street locations and several “on the street” locations and only had food poisoning (upset system) once. Ironically it was from a very traditional burger place in Australia.
Maybe I am lucky and have a good system BUT my personal rule when away from home is NEVER eat on an empty stomach . . .
I believe a glass of wine (or any alcohol) before eating out kills many of the things that you may eat that could cause problems. I’m guessing most microbes/parasites do not like alcohol. That’s my excuse and I’m sticking to it . . .

One of the joys of life is experiencing different cultures, customs and FOODS when traveling . . . as is being well. May I suggest an ounce (or several) of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

### PaigeK — 2025-10-23T19:05:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paiges-optimal-journal.31150/post-361491>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=361463)
>
> We all have different medical histories (and systems) so this may not apply to your situation.
> I have traveled to MANY third world countries and have always brushed my teeth and bathed in the local water and eaten local food in some VERY off the Main Street locations and several “on the street” locations and only had food poisoning (upset system) once. Ironically it was from a very traditional burger place in Australia.
> Maybe I am lucky and have a good system BUT my personal rule when away from home is NEVER eat on an empty stomach . . .
> I believe a glass of wine (or any alcohol) before eating out kills many of the things that you may eat that could cause problems. I’m guessing most microbes/parasites do not like alcohol. That’s my excuse and I’m sticking to it . . .
>
> One of the joys of life is experiencing different cultures, customs and FOODS when traveling . . . as is being well. May I suggest an ounce (or several) of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

Love it Anthony! Sadly, my gut is super "special" as is my immune system .. and not in a good way! Gut wise- no colon and chronically low salivary IgA makes for a fragile system. Immune wise- I don't even know where to start. But I'm not sweating it anymore and will keep rolling with the flow, tackling each issue as they come. Thank you so much for your comment, keep them coming!

# Thread: WENDY JANE'S OPTIMAL JOURNAL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wendy-janes-optimal-journal.31180/>

### Wendy Jane — 2025-06-24T21:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wendy-janes-optimal-journal.31180/post-355188>

Gaining momentum; ready to commit to articulating my thoughts. Since being introduced to Circadian Biology principles last summer, I've been outside for sunrise every day except one. This has become a cornerstone in my existence and I'm immensely grateful for having this time outside every day. Since then, I've been learning more and incorporating more and more changes in my life. Understanding the link to Bitcoin was an important step and being strong enough to move forward without support from others in my family was challenging, but I've persevered. I opened personal and retirement accounts with Unchained and will begin funding both today or tomorrow. This is a huge accomplishment for me. I'm trying to move forward with what I can do regarding some terrible choices I made in the past, when I wasn't strong enough to follow my own lead. My grief over having taken the jab multiple times has been the most difficult thing for me. Beginning to see our government and related power structures for what they are has been hard, but is empowering. I'm looking forward to my consultation with Jack.

I feel grateful for some of the unusual aspects of my life. How many people could groom a pregnant mare, and her companion horse, mostly unclothed and barefoot in a field outside of my house? It's kind of incredible. I do that whenever I can before I start working. The horses' lives are also aligned to circadian principles, to the best of my ability.

# Thread: Clotilde's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31233/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clotilde — 2025-07-03T04:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31233/post-355647>

Hello everyone.
It has been over 2 years since I started my journey to optimal health.
Issues I started with...
Inflammation
Teeth/Gum problems
Insulin resistance
Leptin Resistance
Adrenal Fatigue
Hormonal Imbalance
Blue Light toxic
EMF toxic (my discovery)
Trouble Sleeping
Gut issues - digestion, bloating
I am blessed to have been introduced to Jack Kruse by my daughter Emmanuela. She has and is helping me understand and put into practice Jack Kruse's prescriptions/protocols and suggests reading material, podcast, blogs etc. she is brainiac speed reader who remembers and comprehends everything she reads.

My first step was taking charge of my teeth and gum issues, I first read the book "Cure Tooth Decay"and Nutrition and Physical Degeneration by Dr. Weston A. Price. Both the books were outside my comfort zone as at that time I was a vegan!. I knew that my diet choices were contributing to my health problems. I changed my diet to include eggs, raw dairy and seafood and fish. I did experience positive changes with my gums and teeth. As I followed the protocol in the book "Cure Tooth Decay. The drastic changes in my health, however, began when I followed the Leptin Prescription and began taking actions in resetting my circadian rhythm. The power of watching the sunrise every morning has amazing results, like also bringing your glucose level down as well as grounding for charging yourself with electrons which helped me with back pain. I also, started the Epi Paleo RX (I also read the book). Introducing meat has been a slowly increasing change. I made changes in my environment at home, by changing all my light bulbs to incandescent and red and black light. I was a gym rat... I cancelled my gym membership, unplugged my TV, wake-up at sunrise and sleep by 9:00 pm to 9:30 pm. I spend as much time as possible outside. I also had trouble falling asleep at night because of the blue light exposure, at home, at the gym and work! Now when I have no control over the lighting I wear blue-blocking glasses and the WiFi in my home is off I use Ethernet connection.
Now, all these changes were positive steps towards my health journey, however, the monster in my life was nnEMF!
To be continued......

# Thread: Clotilde's Optimal Journal continued...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3

### Clotilde — 2025-07-04T23:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355757>

In the past 2 years, I did make many changes in my home environment. I also spent more time outdoors, try to go to sunrise almost every day (at times more challenging, especially when I work because of the timing). I can see the sunrise by Lake Ontario which is 20/25 minutes away. This year, I started cold thermogenesis, In order for me to fully implement the protocols, this school year 22024/2025 - I am a teacher, in November I took a sick leave. Even with my life-style changes, my work environment was contributing to many reoccurring problems such as my blood sugar being crazy high. I remember one morning it was 6.1, also I had done hormone testing which turned out to be horrible. My cortisol was extremely low. I also experienced UTI's often, I was always feeling drained, no energy so difficult to wake-up and get out of bed in the morning, feeling light headed at work and brain fog at the end of the day. I started seen an hormone doctor to help with this issue and my daughter helped me navigate all the info that Jack Kruse gives in regards to choosing a Doctor and optimal hormonal levels.
I work in a school with over 680 students and hundreds of staff members. There are WiFi receptors in every single classroom, students use there laptops for most of the day, imagine in one room 32 Chromebooks and older students with cell phones in their backpacks. The school is located very close to Toronto hence the nnEMF with all the towers etc, is insane. Of course the lighting! Super bright Fluorescent lights all over the school. After pulling myself away from this toxic environment for 6 months, I now notice how sensitive my body is to nnEMF. For example, if I would visit someone who had there WIFI on, I would get lower abdomen cramps just like when I had UTI and frequent urination. Now, when I walk into a place with WiFi or high nnEMF I can feel it in my right ear - it gets blocked and if I stay long enough I get back pain. Once I leave this environment, example I go outdoors after sometime my ear and back is back to Normal.
After 6 months, I am doing much better, one morning after my sunrise, glucose level 4.9 -the power of watching the sunrise!
One huge mistake I made, I returned to work the last week of May. I wish I never did! After working for a week, on the weekend, I had could not eat as after a few hours of having my meal, I experienced excruciating pain for hours in my intestines. I fasted for 2 days and DGL helped - stayed home for 4 days. To be continued...

### Mat.I — 2025-07-05T12:02:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355788>

You need a new line of work. There is no longevity in that line of work within schools..You are getting blasted with nn-emf and light that has likely already shifted you to a FE3+ state. Remember you do not need food.to raise your blood sugar this is done by blue rich light alone (missing the red/ir component). Your new nnemf sensitivities are due to melanin degradation, and low redox (all down stream effects of the FE3+ state).. If you value your health you need to get out of there and put pen to paper to develop the exit plan..

Centralised schools = EMF soup. That's why we homeschool...

### Clotilde — 2025-07-06T18:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal-continued.31245/post-355864>

Hello Mat. Thank you for your feedback! Yes, I do value my health and your reply confirms what I have started to work on... an exit plan not to return to my job and find other options. I totally feel the negative effects even after so many changes in my life style, When at work,I am in an EMF soup! My plans include a visit to EL Salvador as part of my health journey.
I also homeschooled my 2 girls who are now adults.

# Thread: Dali's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dalis-optimal-journal.31291/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dcintra — 2025-07-12T00:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dalis-optimal-journal.31291/post-356194>

My Optimal Journal

I’m grateful to have found Dr. Kruse through Michael Yon, and Nicole Shanahan’s Council of the Canceled; following is my medical history.

I am a 47 year-old woman born in Lakeland, Florida of Cuban and Spanish ancestry. I lived in Lakeland until college at UF in Gainesville, Florida, and near Atlanta since 2003. I worked in third-party logistics until 2008, married, and am a SAHM and homeschooler of two healthy children, 15 and 11. My main concern is the effects of one Moderna and one Johnson & Johnson Covid vaccine taken in spring 2021. I believe but cannot prove I have had three miscarriages or failures to implant since then, and tested high in Covid spike antibodies recently.

Otherwise a lifelong concern is tiredness and less cognitive performance and emotional regulation than I would like. I have experienced depression and anxiety as long as I can remember, as well as procrastination and indecision. I have difficulty planning and sometimes staying on task. I can write, read, and enjoy complicated text, but it’s challenging to settle my mind to do any of them. I have medicated with television, internet, and podcasts, but have not taken psychotropic meds in years. I love music and have nearly perfect pitch perception. Currently I see a branspotting therapist and use polyvagal stimulation music. I have a happy marriage to a supportive husband, and wonderful children.

I was overweight after puberty, lost some weight in college, and was able to lose weight after my second pregnancy with Pilates and portion control. But after the vaccines and attempts to conceive, I gained 20 pounds. In the past year I have tried to lose weight with a carnivore diet, and later the Leptin Rx, but gained instead. I have been chronically low in vitamin D, which I discovered with my second pregnancy.

I’ve had myopia since 5th grade, wore RGP contacts for years, couldn’t wear them by 2019. Astigmatism. Dx Meibomian gland dysfunction. RGP are no longer uncomfortable but I rarely wear them.

Lifelong upper respiratory congestion, mostly tolerated, unmedicated. Positional nasal congestion, alternates sides. Improves with sun.

In my early 20s, I took tetracycline for months and Accutane briefly for acne. I developed a patch of dermatitis behind my neck in college and another patch on my groin in my late 20s. I have some melasma on my forehead. I am treating these with sun exposure.

Working with a functional diagnostic nutritionist for upper respiratory issues with my son, I discovered in 2019 our whole family had elevated mold mycotoxins as well as parasites and bacterial infections. We followed various protocols. For the past two years we have worked with a naturopathic physician and have done IV and oral glutathione, as well as binders to detox. I have now dropped detox and all supplements except Neprinol and Boluoke. I take ivermectin.

Having found Dr. Kruse in fall of 2024, I started prioritizing sun and raised my vitamin D to 86, and raised my DHA eating salmon and other seafood twice a week. This month I listened to the February 2019 Q&A on 5G and ALS, which helped me finally accept the importance of sunrise. I rebooted the Leptin Rx and for the first time in years, am losing weight and am able to get into ketosis. My blood glucose is testing lower and my ketones, higher.

Following is a family history.

Mother: Born and raised in Guantanamo, Cuba. Gave birth to my brother, had a miscarriage, stillbirth (or irregular adoption) in Madrid, another miscarriage in Florida, then gave birth to me. I was born with jaundice. Hypothyroidism in her 40s, allergies and hypertension, and significant overweight. Obsessive about health, constant doctor visits, experiences much musculo-skeletal pain. Hammer toe surgery. Knee and shoulder surgery. Heart attacks. Quadruple bypass surgery. Aneurism of the pulmonary artery (?). Neuropathy. Arthritis. Chronic circadian mismatch (sleeps 2 a.m. to 10 a.m.). Florid PTSD/histrionic personality/anxiety, is declining cognitively in her early 80s. 2 Moderna vaccines.

Father: Born and raised in central rural Cuba. Carpenter, later steel fitter/welder upon moving to Florida. Snored since childhood. Duodenal ulcer. Tonsillectomy/uvulectomy/soft palate reduction to correct snoring. Type II diabetes. Good eyes. Hearing loss. Has had prostate cancer for 10 years. Now 91. In cognitive decline. 2 Moderna vaccines.

Brother: Born in Cuba. Forceps assisted birth, blow to head in playground accident. Myopia. Many car accidents, one with a broken neck, halo apparatus. Florid grandiosity and insomnia. Chronic circadian mismatch. Irritable. Observed narcolepsy. Morbid obesity. Edema. Heart attacks. Died of congestive heart failure at 53.

Maternal Grandmother: Born and raised in Guantánamo, Cuba. Lived in Madrid, Gitmo, Central Florida. Deformative arthritis. Arteriosclerosis and total dementia in her 70s. Melanoma in the eye; tumor grew outward. Died subsequent to hemorrhage.

Maternal Grandfather: Born and raised in Cuba, worked at Gitmo for 33 years as a heavy machine operator and tool room attendant. Smoker. Reconstruction of the nose and lower mandible due to carcinoma, 40+ operations, all care at Bethesda Naval Hospital. Glaucoma. Pacemaker. Tracheotomy. Stomach feeding tube. Died at 80 subsequent to hemorrhage from a melanoma of the esophagus.

Maternal Aunt: Developed schizophrenia and type II diabetes. Lived at Gitmo. Died in her 70s. Two healthy children and one born with two stomachs and hydrocephalus.

Maternal Uncle: Medical history unknown, currently in cognitive decline. Cuban Revolution and Counterrevolution, political prisoner for 22 years, lived in Venezuela and now Nicaragua.

Paternal Grandfather: Medical history unknown. Died in his 70s(?) by suicide.

Paternal Grandmother: Medical history unknown. Developed dementia.

Paternal Aunts and Uncles: 3 with type II diabetes, 1 blind, one deceased with lupus, one deceased with cancer, one deceased from complications of Parkinson’s. The uncle with Parkinson’s had a healthy son and one profoundly retarded daughter with a large red mark covering half of her face.

Husband: From Paris. IT professional. Childhood appendectomy. Vitiligo. Pleurisy. Kidney stone. Possible colitis. Tiredness. Epididymitis. Tested gluten sensitivity, resolved. 2 Moderna vaccines. Tested low in spike antibodies.

Son: (15) I had ovarian cysts early in his pregnancy; they resolved. Induced labor, Cervadil. Chronic upper respiratory congestion. Tonsillectomy/adenoidectomy/turbinate reduction, ineffective. Parasites, C. difficile, H. pylori, resolved. Stopped vaccines in 2020. No vision correction.

Daughter: (11) Severe constipation at 3, tested severe gluten sensitivity, parasites, resolved. Chronic upper respiratory congestion. Stopped vaccines in 2020. No vision correction.

### dcintra — 2025-07-25T19:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dalis-optimal-journal.31291/post-356965>

Well, I had my consultation with Dr. Kruse this morning. He was surprised at my spike protein number, which I didn't disclose above: 17,822 as of March 2025. I must get it below 10,000 due to high risk of turbocancer. I should check my spike protein levels quarterly, if not monthly. I'll continue ivermectin at 0.6 mg/kg, two weeks on, one week off.

Dr. Kruse believes the environment in Atlanta is driving spike protein production since my immune system is working well, given my vitamin D is good, 86, and I achieved that without supplements. I must assume the mRNA intercalated into my genome unless proven otherwise. He recommended sun, grounding, and highly recommended moving out of Atlanta to one of the black beaches of El Salvador, or one of the calderas, or another place that tests significantly lower in nnEMF than Atlanta, which apparently is as bad or worse than Nashville.

I'm absorbing his recommendations and ordering deuterium-depleted water today. I have sel de Guérande to add to my water per his suggestion.

My neighbor and I had a long chat. Her numbers are similar and she's on the same page healthwise.

Dr. Kruse helped me see where my money would be best spent. I'm supposed to find out about an institute in New Mexico that teaches nnEMF building biology, and/or purchase tools to test my environment on my own.

I forgot to ask him about IV methylene blue and IV vitamin C.

At any rate our session was very much to the point and serious.

### dcintra — 2025-07-25T19:53:47+0300

I also learned that low numbers of spike protein now does not mean mRNA or plasmids won't intercalate into your genome in future. My husband has low numbers but will have to be careful too.

# Thread: Taliesins Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taliesins-optimal-journal.31295/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taliesin — 2025-07-12T22:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taliesins-optimal-journal.31295/post-356219>

Hi! My name is Taliesin. Here are my quick stats:
**1a.** I was born in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
**1b.** I grew up for most of my life in Ellensburg, WA.
**1c.** I have spent the last 4 years in Los Angeles/San Diego, California, for College/Masters.
**2\.** I am 23 years old.
**3a.** Mother is a first-generation American (Maternal Health: Myopia, Midlife Excessive Hair Shedding).
**3b.** Grandmother immigrated from the Netherlands; the family on that side was in the Netherlands for many generations.
**3c.** Grandmother died of ALS at age 46.
**3d.** Great-grandmother lived into her 90s and had glaucoma.
**4\.** I was diagnosed with keratoconus in 2021(?). I saw Dr. Kristina Voss for it again at the University of Southern California Keck School of Medicine in 2023. At that time, she said that I did not need the cross-linkage surgery yet, but would need to monitor it in case I did need it in the future. I have not had an appointment since then, but will soon.
**5\.** For my whole life, I have had convergence insufficiency, which led to frequent headaches, especially when reading, and occasionally double vision when my eyes were exhausted. When I had double vision, I could force my eyes to focus, but it was difficult and uncomfortable. This condition has improved, but has not completely subsided.
**6\.** I had Cubital Tunnel Syndrome since 2021, and had Cubital Tunnel Release Surgery for it at the University of Southern California Keck School of Medicine in 2023. Since the surgery, it has improved, but still acts up when I work out, type for long periods, or play guitar for long periods. It has never been 100% back to normal since the injury occurred. I always have slightly lower dexterity and feeling in my pinky and ring fingers. This is very impactful because I am a musician.
**7a.** I used to be barefoot 24/7, but for the past year, I haven't been barefoot at all.
**7b.** I have glasses, but I only wear them for driving.
**7c.** I have a pretty strict sleeping routine of going to bed at 8:30 PM and waking up at 5:00 AM, and I recently began using a Melatonin supplement for sleep.
**8\.** I have generally high levels of anxiety 24/7, which I have been working on for many years through behavioral and mindfulness techniques.

### laufolan — 2025-07-16T18:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-optimal-journal.31320/post-356422>

Optimal Journal

I joined in May and am grateful to find all this info. I was diagnosed with type LADA Type 1.5 diabetes LADA in February with A1C of 7.0 in january. I also have rosacea and leaky gut. Before fiding you, I went raw vegan for 6 weeks lowered my antibodies and got my c peptide up from 1.08 to 1.44 from feb to march – but htey dropped and increased after travel, lost too much weight and lost my period (for 73 days). I still produce insulin but since going carnivore keto my c petptide has dropped to .6. I got my A1C from 7.1 in February to 5.8 as of last week. My antibodies are going down. ( type 1 and throglobulin)

Had Lasik eye surgery when I was 25 and bunion surgery when I was 23. I have the MTFR mutation and 4 vitamin D mutations – if this is relevant. I live in Massachusetts 42 degrees N on in a coastal town Marshfield and grew up two towns over. I am 35 years old. I had eating disorders in college for 1.5 years , on birth control from 18-31, and had irregular periods. My period has been regular since coming off birth control, except two months where I shifted my diet and lost weight due to going Raw Vegan in february before I found your work. I went from (125-113 now back at 120 with a goal of 128 - my cycle has been back for 2 months) I am new and quickly realized I have been doing major things wrong for the past 35 years. I was vegetarian for the past 8 years 3-4 of those I was vegan- eating whole foods and tons of carbs. I would go on fruit weeks sometimes while living in MA and ate smoothies everyday for 5+ years. I also was a night owl my since I was little up until finding you – I worked in restaurants from age 13-35. I wore contacts from age 13-25. Growing up consistently had strep, mono in college, and have the EBV antibodies currently. Recent testing showed negative though. I haven’t gotten any vaccinations since high school or middle school whatever was required to attend – except one Hepitatis B when I was traveling to India in 2018(didn't finish the sequence). I was a c section, formula, and grew up eating tons of carbs. I am irish/English/dainish/ Scottish heritage – but have been surprisingly tanning well. Body is stuck in sympathetic state - nervous system is fried.

I had a meeting with Dr Jack at the end of May and he told me to get a building biologist and low and behold my partner and I built a microwave that we have been living in for the past 3.5 years. The readings were obnoxiously high, with 10 devices that were off the charts – but fortunately I have removed all of the devices and am able to get the house to be below RF of 10 while sleeping. Still have some work to do with this but progressing. I got a 2025 Mercedes in September that was also a microwave and I have given it up. As well as stepped away from waitressing and the studio I worked at due to the environment and the effects on my body – all changes I started making in may/June. The studio I worked at teaching hot yoga my feet and sometimes hands and tounge would go numb only when I taught there.

I am sitting in the sun for 4-8 hours a day and spending lots of time in the woods currently and putting all my intention and attention to healing and reversing. It was a struggle for me to wake up at sunrise as I spent the last 17 years going to bed between 12-3am and waking up 6-11am. The first 2 weeks I was out a half hour after sunrise, then a few days out at sunrise but falling asleep outside, and now in week 3 up and not falling asleep. My energy has been extremely low. I hope this is an adjustment period. I am eating keto/carnivore with seafood, wild venison/boar, tallow/lard and salt only since the beginning of June.. I think the changes I have already made will show big improvements, and am planning to head to El Salvador & Nicaruaga for the winter months – thinking late November through January/Feb. I am drinking 50 oz deuterium depleted water daily and an additional 20 oz of plain . I have started with your work from the begginning and moving forward - it is easier for me to comprhend this way- but have not been reading anything new yet other than the recents pow wows

I got tested for heart rate variability and bolt score – and they were bad – my nervous system is fried and stuck in a sympathetic state. Im thinking nature and light will ease this

Maternal History

Mom – Bipolar/depression diagnosis but no longer and off all meds, alcoholic started drinking at age 9 – sober for 1.5 years, disordered eating, sexual/physical abuse starting at age 3, has had 12 surgeries ( spine, 6 knees, wrist, 2 bunions, teeth) , osteoporosis

Grandmother – Type 2 diabetes, alcoholic, cancer all over dies in late 70’s

Grandfather – Tyoe 2 diabetes Survied Lung cancer (smoker 50 years) cataracts, alcoholic, died cancer at 92

Paternal History

Dad – Gut issues, alcoholic 1.5 years sober – doctors say hes in great health but definitely has IBS or something

Grandmother – Louie Body Syndrom died

Grandfather – Type 1 diabetes died in 1969 when my dad was 10 from diabetes

**QUESTIONS FOR JACK:**

I would love to know the best suggested place in El Savador to heal for my particular case – I am a person who is very good at being alone and love the jungle and forest and trees – I like the beach too, just love to be in nature – I like people, but have no problem being alone and am very low maitenance- the most important thing to me is being in the most optimal environment to heal. Would the ideal location be on the volcano?

I read on the forum LADA might not want to eat eggs – what are the parameters for this? I eat duck eggs – I have had food sensitivity tests and I am not reactive to them – I will be shifting to more epi paleo over the next few weeks – but still hesitant to introduce more veggies as I was very reactive to them – when does the carnivore become detrimental?

I have been depleted of energy lately – I don’t know if it is the transition of waking up so early, lack of carbs, or something else – My whole body is tiredDo you have any suggestions to add to what I am already doing?

Is the c peptide of .6 due to eating no carbs? It is a drastic drop in my opinion from 1.08 and since I have been able to get it up in march its strange to me

Is there a way to focus redox in a particular area or does it only improve with the whole body practices. My chest gets red but heat rashes – is this a shitty redox area if it heat rashes?

An old video stated to not add salt in water – is this accurate as it seems newer stuff praises salt especially with 5g or is it – more salt on food not in water

I have been doing CT in the ocean and face dunks at the moment as I was under the impression autimmmune and my stressed out nervous system it would not be beneficial . I have a cold plunge but have been avoiding because of this. Is this true – should I not CT ovulation to mesntrul cycle?

Just read the lasik blog -i got lasik 10 years ago is this something that can not be supported? I understand the scar is there but can the sun now help heal the damage already done?

### laufolan — 2025-08-16T01:55:56+0300

I have very low leptin - 1.1 and can see how that is such a root in my situations - sticking to the leptin rx as well eat high fat as I had eating disorders in the past -- I feel I have to refeed myself, I had such strict diet regimen and cleansing protocols the past few years to "heal" I see how it could have just made stressors worse - it's wild - I deprived myself of so much for so long light& proper nutrition, water, sleep it's time to nourish it all back in

# Thread: I NEED to leave the UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-to-leave-the-uk.31324/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Luke Jeal — 2025-07-16T20:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-to-leave-the-uk.31324/post-356432>

Hi everyone,

After listening to June and Julys pow wow webinars, it has been the punch in the mouth I have needed to start executing my exit from the UK.

I am a Chiropractor in the UK and I have a wife and 2 children, who are 1 and 3. I am happy to leave the UK as I can see what is happening whilst my wife is a bit more cautious about leaving. I would like to move to Central America where I can continue practising as a Chiropractor. My wife is a nurse but is currently looking after the children full time.

I am looking for some advice and tips on moving. I have so far looked into Costa Rica, Panama, Nicaragua and El Salvador but it does seem confusing on where would be best to go.

If anyone can help or give me some advice that would be great. I would like to visit some countries first before moving so hoping to go to Central America in the next few months and try and make a decision from there.

# Thread: Sue's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sues-optimal-journal.31342/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SueLeBlanc — 2025-07-20T00:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sues-optimal-journal.31342/post-356623>

Just discovered Dr. Kruse and his work a couple of months ago. Very excited about the journey.
My mother and grandmother both Sicilian. My father Swedish/English. Grandmother had no health issues. Choked on a grapefruit at about age 60. My mother passed at age 83 with lung cancer and never smoked. Dad did smoke and passed at 83 for Alzheimer's.

I'm age 64. Raised in Boston area. Lived there till age 26. Then 6 years in Atlanta area. Back to Boston for 2 year internship. Moved to Arizona for 10 years. 1998 experienced my first emergency c-section. 2nd in 2000. This is where the fun begins. Also diagnosed with vitiligo and Hashimoto's.
2005 moved to Colorado. Majority of the time over 7,000ft. 2015 had to stop working as a chiropractor due to partial paralysis from Lyme and mold exposure. Moved to a new build but was clueless about the Airforce Academy a few miles away. Also very close to I-25. Have been here for 8 years and heavily treated for Lyme and mold. Mold counts went down but still not knowing from day to day if I can drive due to severe lightheadedness. Vitiligo has worsened over the years. I am able to walk 4 miles most days.
Since we found Dr. Kruse, we have taken drastic steps to change our lives and get on his Rx. We have been staying at our rural camp for the last 5 weeks in order to get the sunrise and about 5 hours of sun a day. We are 60 miles away from the nearest military base. We have measured near zero EMF (except when we turn on Starlink). Next step is to ck the low frequency EMF and turn everything off at night. We are at 5800 feet now and I have gotten rid of the CPAP and oxygen.
For the last 6 months I have been dealing with severe back and leg pain that wakes me up at night. Just got an MRI and it looks like severe Deg. disc disease. L5/s1. It has not been reviewed by an MD yet. It's a lot worse at night. I continue to walk most days anyway. I would like to schedule a consult soon. We will be upgrading to family membership this weekend. My husband is already gold.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Looking for a punch in the mouth for my son... (Jess's Optimal journal)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-20T05:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356640>

Alright, I'm new here and finding my way around...
I am a 47 year old solo mum from New Zealand.
I have been all in on Jack's work for the last couple of years. I must've listened to hundreds of hours of podcasts (some of them 10 x before I can feel I have got my head halfway around what he is saying) Learning as much as I can and plan to spend the rest of my life doing so! I believe there is nothing more worthwhile to be learning!

I upped my membership to Gold recently, despite really not being able to afford it as I am starting from scratch financially here, having lost my life's savings learning some hard lessons while tryna get us out of NZ during Convid.

I am here right now due to my sense of urgency around needing to do everything possible to help my son.

My son: Born in 2008. Breech, C-section, jaundiced. Super clingy baby-I had to carry him everywhere as I couldn't put him down or he would cry. Never slept well, would wake up screaming from every nap. Diagnosed with (ALL) Leukaemia at age 1.5. Went through 3+ years of chemo, high-dose steroids, antibiotics, countless anaesthetics/lumbar punctures, x-rays etc etc....

...He is now 17 and living a completely counter-circadian lifestyle-much to my dismay-and despite my best efforts to convey to him the risk he is taking. He just rolls his eyes at me and says his lifestyle is normal and I am off the deep end crazy for making such a big deal about it.
He is not dumb, he is actually a smart kid...but neurodivergent to some degree and has the autistic trait of PDA-the demand avoidance/ oppositional defiance thing...So that, coupled with him being the age he is, I currently can't get him to do anything at all unless he sees the value in it/feels he has chosen it for himself....hence why I am here hoping Uncle Jack can provide him with a come to Jesus moment, a punch in the mouth as he says!....I really need to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health. I need to somehow make more real to him the consequences of his lifestyle choices. Because he sure as hell isn't gonna listen to me.

He does school online so he is on a screen during the day, and he is a gamer so he is on screen at night too. I enforce 3 nights a week off from the gaming and he gets off at 10.30pm on school nights but god knows what time he gets off on the weekends. I am in bed by 8.30 so I really don't know, but I can tell you it is 1.30 Sunday afternoon and he hasn't gotten up yet which is normal for weekends.

There is no way in hell I could get him outside to see the sunrise with me....I try to get him to spend some time outside but he will sit for 5 minutes outside (wearing a freaking hoodie) He is ocd about dirt so he doesn't like his feet touching the ground....He doesn't like the sun. If he gets hot he gets a headache which lasts the rest of the day so he avoids the sun. He doesn't like the beach. He is the whitest half-Samoan you will ever see. He doesn't do any exercise so he is skinny, has no strength or muscle mass and he only wants to eat carbs. I am very focused on eating a local, seasonal diet, so at least while he is still living with me I can have some control over what food I have here for him to eat. I can now get him to eat red meat once a week, eggs once a week, chicken once a week and fish a couple of times a week-and this is massive progress which I am really happy with!...left to his own devices he would only eat bread, chips and pizza.

It is a form of torture for me to see him this way. He just has no enthusiasm for anything apart from gaming. I believe he has burnt out his dopamine system-amongst other things I am sure, and he just has no spark of life, but will say he is fine and it is normal. It breaks my heart.

People suggest things like turning his power off, cutting his internet, or kicking him out etc....but I believe it is better to continue trying to get some buy-in from him in terms of his health....I am very aware he will move out at some point relatively soon anyway and then I will have lost my opportunity to positively impact him in any way.

I am hatching a plan to try to get him to agree to come away with me for a month (Ideally to El Salvador...but probably more realistically to Samoa as it is half the cost from NZ.) ...Samoa is latitude 15ish South...which is similar to El Salvador to the North anyway so I hope it will be good enough. We are 40 something South latitude here so it has got to be better than that.
With the caveat that he will see the sunrise every day for a month, spend more time outside during the day-even in the shade, and have no screen at night....I am going for a melanin renovation, circadian realignment, hard screen detox thing.....IF I can SOMEHOW get him to agree to this plan....I just hope and fucking pray that he will feel better ENOUGH from doing this, that he will see the value in it and will want to continue with at least some of it moving forwards in his life.

I am gonna have to slave for the next 4 months to save to pay for this but I am willing to do whatever it takes at this point. I just need to get him to agree to it.
I cannot just stand by and watch him heading for a slow-motion car crash of a bad health outcome after fighting to keep him alive all this time....Especially not now I am very aware that it was my life/light choices that caused him to be born with such a high rate of heteroplasmy. (A decade of nighttime shift work and an ex-vegan), his Dad was also a nighttime shift worker.

I freaking WISH I could afford a consult so Uncle Jack could speak to him directly for half an hour but I just can't afford it....Maybe could afford 5 minutes. If only there was a 5 minute option!

### Here to learn (Jess) — 2025-07-20T07:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356647>

> [Quintavious the 3rd said:](/goto/post?id=356643)
>
> If a child doesn't listen to his own mother, then he sure as hell won't listen to some random, shirtless guy on the internet more than halfway across the world named Jack Kruse who ain't his daddy, uncle, your husband or boyfriend.
>
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biological-mechanism-of-agitated-anger-and-0-life-zest.29108/)
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/)

He has heard it from me a million times.
Also I am clearly not a Neurosurgeon or anything like that. I am learning as much as I can but I have no professional clinical experience.... I am just a parent here doing everything I can trying to get help for her kid.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-07-20T09:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-356648>

(edit - posted before reading links above!)
I know it's hard when you realise that your actions have harmed your children and you want to fix it, but i can honestly say that your fears about his health will push him away and he will go in the opposite direction just because he can. 17 and a gamer is hard. My son is now 22, was a hardened gamer very late nights, at least his job at the time had him outside during the day, but he struggled because he was going to bed late and having to get up at 8 in the morning, and basically work was boring and couldn't wait to get home and back to the pc.

For a child any attention is good attention in an odd way so your nagging and fussing is feeding some part of him with a conflicting part that shouts in his head, for you to just shut up and leave him alone. I know it's difficult but could I gently suggest you back off and just don't say anything, no rolling of eyes, pointed words, slammed doors, banged cups nothing passive agressive !! hahah really hard coming from someone who was excellent at that for years!!

He may say why aren't you nagging me anymore, and you just say he is old enough now to start being responsible for his body and health, and up to him, (AND STOP THERE) no you will learn, nag nag. And let him realise. It's never too late he has years to work out that all that shit is harming him, but he has to do it on his own terms for himself, not for you.

We had the advantage my son loved to play the guitar and piano so that go him away from tech for a bit.
Our son now gets up early (not quite sunrise) but wthin an hour which is vast improvement, he bought his own blue block glasses, he turns his machine off at 10 now and is in bed and asleep pretty quick. He recently swopped all of his evening shifts for morning ones. He has started asking questions a couple of years ago (because we didn't do any covid nonsense) , and whilst all his friends think we are bat shit crazy, he is realising that we have done soo much research into this and are healthy compared to everyone elses parents and people generally our age, so he can see the connection. I know hear him telling his friends about the dangers of blue light etc, so there is hope.
17 is barely adult and front rational brain is nowhere near developed to really appreciate your new found wisdom, unfortunately. Sending you lots of calming thoughts.
(edit - ok so he is still a minor, still in your house, pull the plug. ) sending you more courage!
Good luck.

He’s still a minor - you set the rules. If he must be on a computer for school, have him do it outdoors on a daylight computer. Pull the plug on the gaming or at the very least put more stringent parameters around it. Don’t expect others (like Jack Kruse) to do what you must do yourself.

I am not expecting anyone to do anything for me. I am here looking for help to get through to my kid.
I did explain if I pull the plug he will leave and be in a worse situation with no support.
I was hoping hearing it from someone like Jack who has a wealth of knowledge and experience in the consequences of bad light choices, could make it more real to him. That's all.

### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-30T17:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357204>

> [Here to learn (Jess) said:](/goto/post?id=357083)
>
> I am not expecting anyone to do anything for me. I am here looking for help to get through to my kid.

You are, though. You’re expecting someone else to convince him of the importance of these choices when you are his parent. Why does he not trust your guidance here? You said he is a gamer and plays well into the night, which is made permissible under your roof. Setting more stringent parameters is important to his health and is your duty. If he leaves, he will learn the hard way - but sometimes that is exactly what people need to change. “Tough love” is difficult, but it’s an ultimate love - it’s harder than just bending to what our children immediately desire.

> > [Jessnet3 said:](/goto/post?id=357204)
> >
> > You are, though. You’re expecting someone else to convince him of the importance of these choices when you are his parent. Why does he not trust your guidance here? You said he is a gamer and plays well into the night, which is made permissible under your roof. Setting more stringent parameters is important to his health and is your duty. If he leaves, he will learn the hard way - but sometimes that is exactly what people need to change. “Tough love” is difficult, but it’s an ultimate love - it’s harder than just bending to what our children immediately desire.
>
> Of course it would mean more coming from someone who is a professional in the field and has extensive clinical experience as well as a long career studying all these things though right? Isn't this why we consult professionals? To help us? I am really struggling with the responses to my post here. I guess I was hoping for a more supportive vibe. Solo parenting is a succession of lonely battles and kids on the spectrum are hard work. If you read my post I did explain he will move out soon anyway and my reasons for taking the approach I am, to try to positively influence him while I still can. Rather than cause a rift between us and leave him alone and unsupported. Potentially very dangerous in such a low dopamine state.

### Jessnet3 — 2025-07-31T06:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357260>

I do understand that you’ve been through a long road with his health, and I have compassion for you doing this alone. That said, I’ve seen firsthand in my own family how softening too much for kids in that state, especially smart, oppositional ones, can backfire long-term. My brother was similar: on the spectrum, resistant to authority, gamer. My parents bent over backwards, sacrificing a lot with limited means to make life easy on him and avoid conflict, fearing that the alternative would be worse. But when he finally had to face the world on his own, he was completely unequipped. No resilience. No structure. He still struggles immensely today at nearly 40 years of age.

For your son, setting boundaries maybe looks like setting screen curfews on the weekends too, and pairing that with a new obligation such as a part-time job, community volunteering, music lessons - anything that demands daylight exposure and a bit of responsibility. Have him listen to a podcast or two with Jack in the car to plant the seed. But ultimately, you are the most important guide in his life. You can emphasize that these changes are part of an ongoing two-way dialogue you wish to have with him on his health.

### MTA321 — 2025-07-31T15:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-punch-in-the-mouth-for-my-son-jesss-optimal-journal.31344/post-357276>

Think of it this way... by doing nothing you are still complicit in making his health outcomes worse. Set boundaries and stick to them. Start with blue blocking glasses at night for gaming. fairly simple rules, if he doesn't wear them he doesn't play. Assuming you take care of all his financial needs, he will soon realize that sticking to your rules is a better alternative than leaving the house and not being able to afford food much less time to play video games; so don't let that fear override your instincts.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: JVs Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Vieceli — 2025-07-20T19:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-356683>

John Vieceli, recently turned 62.

**Grandmother:** Died of a heart attack. I think she was in her early 60s. Overweight but not obese. Active. Polish decent. Loved gardening, composting, making fermented foods.

**Mother:** Died of lung cancer at 50. Smoker since teens. Discovered the tumor late which had metastasized to her brain. Doctors gave her 6 months but she lasted 5 more years.

**JV early years:** According to the baby book my mother kept nothing was unusual at birth other than I was breach. Growing up I was very active. Outside all the time playing sports. I developed asthma in middle school. Attacks would only come around fall every year. We didn't have money so when the attacks came I just laid down at home and didn't move to conserve energy. As I grew older, they attacks were not as bad, and then, when I went away to college they disappeared. In high school. I also start wearing glasses during this period.

**20s-40s:** Get a degree in graphic design. Still active mostly playing baseball and softball. No major injuries or sickness. Had allergies to pollen mostly. Computers become heavily used in graphic design and I become an early adopter. Days are long, sometimes 15 hour days working under blue light in front of a computer. In my 40s I partner with two friends and create a branding agency whose cliental is athletes, sports organizations and musicians. Still working late hours but now stress has doubled.

**Early 50s:** Realizing that I was killing myself, I break off and start my own business where I can pick and choose clients as well as how much I want to work. Up until this point, I haven't had any major injuries or illnesses. May have had the flu but I rarely got sick. No flu shots or vaccines. I avoid taking anything even aspirin. Diet has always been meat, fish, veggies. I get married and move from Chicago suburbs to Park City, Utah. I become super active, mountain biking, snow boarding, hiking. Have two kids and decide to move back to Illinois. First instance of glaucoma shows up. Pressure in one eye is slightly elevated. We would watch this for the next 10 years. Before we move I develop this rash behind my knees and a little rash on the elbow crease in my left arm. I think it's due to moving (dust) and stress of moving back. Rashes disappear when we get back to IL.

**Mid-50s:** I start doing Crossfit. Blood work starts showing some numbers that are out of range. Vitamin D low (taken in Jan though) C-reactive protein is high, LDL and HDL is high, TSH is high, TPO high, Testosterone low. Hypothyroidism runs in the family on my grandfathers side. Going through a difficult divorce during this period. Glaucoma still rising. All during this period I am feeling strong and vibrant.

**60s:** Glaucoma gets to 24. Doc recommends a laser surgery to open duct. We do it but doesn't work. Puts me on eye drops. I read the side affects and reluctantly start taking them. 6 months later new check up shows pressure down to 23. Vieceli stumbles on to Jack's 2017 Vermont Youtube video. I watch as mush jack as I can get. Convinced I can cure this based on Chantel's father's results, I stop all drops and never miss a sunrise rise or sunset, use blue light glasses after sunset and eat more fish. Next check up, doctor raves about efficacy of medication, LOL. Pressure now down to 11. I join Optimal Health.

**8 months ago:** Jack, I had a 30-minute 1-on-1 with you back in early Jan. Seeing the success I had with the glaucoma I wanted to attack this thyroid issue (and talk a little bitcoin ) The protocol you suggested was to get the EMR-tek panel and use it until spring, then get as much sun as I could and test my Vitamin D to get a baseline before anything else. Is this a good time to get Vitamin D tested or wait until mid-August? Is there a specific Vitamin D panel I need? I was hoping to schedule another 1-on-1 with you after the test to discuss next steps.

**2 weeks ago**: Had a rough period of work. Not terribly long hours but stressful. Sitting and standing at desk working. I developed some stiffness in my lower back. Also noticed a small rough patch right side of neck right above hairline. Went to the chiro but tight back has persisted. Last week it was hard to squat down and pick something up. Super tight in my pelvis and goin area. Can't put on my socks or shoes because I can't cross my legs. If I lay on the floor and try to lift my leg I can barely get it 6 inches off the ground. Getting into the car, I need to lift my leg to get in. After a 5-minute ride in the car, when I get out it takes about 2 -3 seconds to stand up straight. Hamstrings are super tight especially right at the glutes. After the couple of seconds everything is normal. Walking no pain. Walking up stairs though isn't painful just no power. Joints are sore. Started in my hips but couple of days ago it's in my shoulders too. Uncomfortable to raise my arms over my head. Feels like I did a heavy workout the day before. Some fatigue. Was concerned so went to a see a doc. All he did was refer me to a back and pelvis specialist and prescribed Naproxen and Cyclobenzaprine. Not taking them.

Jack wasn't sure what my next steps should be? I definitely would like to do another 1-on-1 with you just wasn't sure if I needed to do anything else (labs) before. Thanks for reading my journal!

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-14T18:50:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358235>

On July 31st, I went to see a neurologist at Northwestern. He did a series of tests physical tests, ie pushing against this force, dull and sharp needles on areas to see if I could feel the difference and a tuning fork to determine when the vibrations stopped. Everything was fine except my my power. I arm and leg muscles are weak. He order quite a list of labs. Here is the list of labs which have come back as normal.

Aldolase, CK, Hmg-Coa Reductase Antibody, Serum, Myomarker 3 Plus Profile, Myositis Specific 11 Antibody Panel, TSH, Acetylcholine Recep Binding, Acetylcholine Recep Blocking, Acetylcholine Recep Modulat, Hemoglobin A1c, Vitamin B12/Folate Panel, HIV Antigen/Antibody,Reflex Confirmation, Anca Screen With Mpo/Pr3, Reflex Anca Titer (CDH/DCH/KH/VWH/NWR), Ganglioside Gm1 Antibodies IgG/IgM, Celiac Disease Panel, Axonal Neuropathy, Autoimmune/Paraneoplastic Evaluation, Serum, Rheumatoid Factor, Protein, Total, Albumin

And here is the tests that came back out of range...

**Immunofixation, Serum (Nmh/Lfh)**
IgG = 1,840 Range = 610-1,616 mg/dL
Lambda = 403 Range = 90-210 mg/dL
Kappa/Lambda Ratio = 0.6. Range=1.4-2.7 Ratio

**Protein Electrophoresis, Serum**
Alpha 2 Protein = 1,840 Normal Range = 610-1,616 mg/dL
Lambda = 403 Normal Range = 90-210 mg/dL
Kappa/Lambda Ratio = 0.6 403 Normal Range = 1.4-2.7 Ratio

**CBC With Differential**
Neutrophils = 79 Normal Range: 34.0 - 73.0%
Lymphocytes = 10.2 Normal Range: 15.0 - 50.0%
Absolute Lymphocytes = 0.8 Normal Range: 1.0 - 4.0 10ˆ3/µL

**Ana, Reflex To Autoimmune Disease Panel**
Anti-Nuclear Antibody Value: Positive Normal value: Negative
ANA PATTERN Value: Speckled Normal value: (none)

**Sedimentation Rate = 99**
Normal range: 3 - 10 mm/Hour

**C-Reactive Protein = 49.9**
Normal range: 0.0 - 10.0 mg/L

**Protein Electrophoresis Path Review, Serum**
Value: An accentuated alpha 2 band observed. A restricted band seen in the gamma region.
Immunofixation, Path Review Serum (NMH)

On Tuesday I was referred to a Hematologist at the Cancer center. He ordered more blood work and I am going a 24 hr urine collection. In terms of my condition I would say nothing drastic has changed but each day I feel that I have a diminished range of motion. More to come.

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-25T17:38:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358712>

Update: The urine test and other tests came back and the docs characterized them as unremarkable although they are still detecting abnormal protein in the blood. The Hematologist and Neurologists agree better defining this on an EMG is the critical test to determine if this is related to the IgG Lambda or something else entirely.

Last Friday I had an EMG test. Everything was normal. Docs still can't figure out what's going on. Next up is a MRI Brain with/without contrast and Cervical Spine with/without contrast.

### John Vieceli — 2025-08-28T20:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jvs-optimal-journal.31349/post-358866>

Doctors are now advising a bone marrow and muscle biopsy. Even though the EMG was normal they still believe it amyloidosis. Docs still want the MRIs just the biopsies first.

# Thread: Mike T/s Optimal Health Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-t-s-optimal-health-journal.31352/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michaeltatarjr — 2025-07-21T20:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mike-t-s-optimal-health-journal.31352/post-356749>

Hey all. Here's my optimal journal in outline format...

About me:
47 yrs old
Learned about Jack and his 'tribe' about a year ago.
Recently bumped up to a gold membership. I'm deliberately keeping a somewhat low profile, as I 'read the room' learn, and observe for a bit.
Started with the benefits of sunlight and cold plunges.
Took to his info voraciously as I had 'cured' myself from disease states 10 years ago, and had an 'idea' that what he was saying was possible.
1\. The disease state: Unknown name. More like a set of symptoms I just called "The fuzzies"
Symptoms (restless leg, numbness in right leg. Involuntary muscle movements in right hand, etc. )
3 doctors , an MRI and many many kinds of tests later...
No fixes from standardized healthcare. "We don't know..."
A personal trainer I knew had mentioned something about a great applied kinesiologist. "If it's structural he'll fix it"
He did fix it. Was better in about 2 months
Part of the fix was light therapy, as well as stretching, chiropractic manipulation, etc.

Four goals while I'm here:
Learn as much as I can
Get to be as 'superhuman' as possible (essentially optimizing my health for jiu jitsu and in general...)
Help son as much as I can
Has moderate dyslexia and dysgraphia
Has alpha-gal
Help my wife as much as I can
Has Hashimotos
More or less in remission

Me:
Currently trying to be 'superhuman'
Want to radically improve my cardio for brazilian jiu jitsu.
Hoping to push cold plunges colder and longer. Typically plunge 2-3 days a week. Two short 5-10 mins and one long.
Can do a 48 minute cold plunge in 48 degree water, totally submerged up to my neck.
Lately been doing the ice pack method outside. Pack my torso in 10-50 lbs of ice for 30-60 minutes. I could probably go longer but I get bored.
Looking to steepen the gradient here.
Get lots of sun!
See the sun come up, almost without fail. Sunrise is TINA in my household.
Out there when it's cloudy
Out unless it's raining really hard
5+ hours a day on the weekend
Bought a convertible - Top is down coming and going to work unless it's raining or below 25 degrees.
Ran a grounding mat to our patio (which is raised) so I can ground, drink coffee, and watch the sun come up. We do our morning bible study there and is a great way to start the day.
Bought a magnetico
More broadly, I experiment each month with different things
Need to bring blood testing/number tracking into the mix.
Currently working on getting our home to be a haven from nnEMFs. It's a big job and I just started!

Wife
During my wife's pregnancy with our youngest, she was diagnosed with Hashimoto's.
Did not tolerate the levothyroxin, etc. so had to do a lot of emergency studying to try to help her.
Speculated that there was a better way, as many others seems to just 'live with it' without much incident.
It's more or less in remission, though we've not tested in quite awhile.
How we 'cured'
diet changes really helped (No nightshades, no dairy)
Plenty of sleep/revamping circadian rhythms (indirectly)
And some over the counter supplements (Thyrothrophin and Drenamin, both from Standard Process)
Sunlight is TINA! She is out there with me in the morning every morning.
The last time she tested, her T antibodies tests were all just a little above normal, down from 100x too high. The doctor asked what she had done. She told the Dr. Dr was was silent a moment and said "I don't know why any of that is helping you. Keep it up."
She works from home in front of a window
We get nearly an hour of morning sun each day.
She also gets 30 minutes outside everyday around solar noon. It seems the 'blast' of UV is really good for her.
On the weekends we get more sun as well.
Often 90 minutes or more of evening sun and grounding
This fall we plan on getting her a convertible as well.

Son
9 yr old
Alpha-gal
Dyslexia
Joins us in the morning
Revamping his diet. He's really into carbs, but has been a good sport about changing things.
He even did a cold plunge with me recently!

Three questions
1\. Has anyone on here increased there cardio with cold plunging, etc.?
2\. How do I help my son with his dyslexia and alpha gal?
3\. Any insight on further reversing her Hashimoto's?

# Thread: Soul Doctor Nick - 68 y/o diagnosed with ALS here for Dr. Kruse Consult
