### Soul Doctor Nick — 2025-07-21T22:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/soul-doctor-nick-68-y-o-diagnosed-with-als-here-for-dr-kruse-consult.31353/post-356751>

Hello Dr. Kruse and the forums,

I've taken this amazing client through my consciousness experiential Soul Rebirth program, and I'm gifting him a consult with Dr. Kruse this Friday, July 25th.

I know my skills and limitations. We're working together because I know his family well, and he had already chosen to work outside of the centralized medical system. It didn't make any sense to him. And the most challenging part isn't working me with and learning how to trust himself - it's being with all the judgement of the family members who don't get why he won't just follow the program so that he can say he's doing the right thing, regardless of the outcome.

He had with our time together one of the top three experiences of his entire life - after marrying his beloved and the birth of his twin boys. He experienced the presence of all of his ancestors, including the ones who had ended their life early. Pure peace was his intention, and it was exactly what he received. He now has an anchor point, and is excited for what's to come next.

Attached are his latest testing results as well as his consult letters from his neurologist. Here for reference for our consult. What we're looking for is clarity on what is going on with him, and best practices for while he is still in Edmonton, Alberta, and he is motivated to make BIG changes. Including selling the home, travelling to El Salvador for a few months and figuring things out. And we want to optimize everything else.

He is likely coming for one more multi day session with his wife in late August early September, where we'll take them on a breath journey and beyond, and have access to multiple modalities including live blood analysis, EBOO, Hyperbaric O2 chambers, etc. along with consciousness expansion and being outside of the city and on the earth grounding and early morning sunlight. Looking forward to guidance from Dr. Kruse and the lovely people on this forum. Also, I'm just ordering a pallet of Litewater 5 PPM to have on hand if that can aid in the therapy.

Will also attach more files in the next post!

Much gratitude for the quick reply. and I've deleted the files here now.

# Thread: Stephanie's Health Pilgrimage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-22T22:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-356796>

I am a nature lover, classical pianist, and nature/landscape photographer trying to heal from pharmaceutical damage primarily due to a benzodiazepine taken in my 20s -- but I also took long-term antibiotics, Accutane, and birth control pills in my early teens and 20s that I think primed me to be easily damaged later -- and worsened my central nervous system. I did not take any covid vaccines, as I knew well enough about big pharma by that time. I am vehemently anti drug in general: never drank or smoked in my whole life: a teetotaler, had straight edge stickers on my car as a teen.

I have forsworn virtually all pharma for 10 years, except for Advil occasionally, but I don't even like taking that. However, menstrual cycle pain and side effects can be so horrible that I take a few Advil during that time. My goal is to eradicate Advil as well. I cannot tolerate supplements, though, or herbal medications so it's difficult to find an alternative. The benzodiazepines caused sensitivities to everything (except Advil) and now I can basically only use food and nature as medication.

Every day, I ground, swim in the ocean, walk the beaches and forests on my wonderful peninsula -- so I am in nature often. I eat a paleo based diet with the highest quality food and water that I can find. I also don't currently work as I am focusing on my health. I have perfect lab tests and have seen every specialist imaginable, even Environmental Illness doctors to see what I can do to get better. Though, I realized most of them are incompetent quacks so I gave up on doctors awhile ago -- but will occasionally use my conventional doctor for lab tests that I want done.

My goal is to eliminate any need for technology-based or government reliant living, maybe even live in an off grid, eco-village at some point. Modern living really isn't for me: I had to learn the hard way how destructive it is. I want to heal my central nervous system damage and am willing to do whatever it takes to accomplish this. I can't go to El Salvadore at the moment because my chemical sensitivities are so severe that I can't sit next to a bunch of people wearing cologne and perfume in the airports or on an airplane. All traveling must be done in my car and I am not even sure I could handle any hotels or air bnbs right now. That is the main thing I suffer from, though the sensitivities are getting progressively less severe as I heal, but I still have to avoid lots of places, people, and things because of the flares I get when trapped around chemicals.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-26T23:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357015>

When I was at my worst with my health about 6 years ago, I couldn't work and neither could my husband because he had to help me. We moved to a rental house on the marshes of Cape Cod where we had a perfect view of sunset. I saw one of the most beautiful wintery, snowy, fiery sunsets of my life there, and I took photos of it, shared it on my Instagram (CapeCodGirl4Life). The post became a big hit which I wasn't expecting. I then became addicted to photographing sunset photos, and I went to different beaches and locations every night, unfailingly, hunting the perfect sunset shot. I would post them on Instagram and my account began to grow fast, and I was featured on many hubs and magazines, etc. Photography then became a big part of my life where I would shoot moon scenes, full moon especially -- chase northern lights, shoot comets, etc.

The main point to sharing all of this is to remind myself that I saw exponential and rapid healing during this time period. I knew that getting out in nature and creating art was helping me, and I thought perhaps there was also something about watching sunset that was healing me. Now I know for sure, after studying Jack's work, that this is surely what happened. I remember that watching the northern lights also healed me almost instantaneously. I felt the power from that, especially last October when the lights were so strong that they happened from sunset until morning hours, visible to the naked eye.

Jack is right: Nature's lights are where it's at. All of health is connected to this.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-26T23:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357016>

I took this photo in June of the Hydrangeas and the best sunset Ive seen thusfar this Summer. This is how the healing happens: these colors, hues, frequencies -- and of course, the beauty.

Hi Stephanie,

I react to everything also but not in an anaphylaxis way so it's not life threatening.

Have you had your environment checked for EMF?

### VictorianGirl — 2025-07-27T16:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357033>

> [jaedyn said:](/goto/post?id=357030)
>
> Hi Stephanie,
>
> I react to everything also but not in an anaphylaxis way so it's not life threatening.
>
> I find it interesting that you had long term antibiotics and accutane, this is also a part of my history. I have issues with water too as you mentioned on the webinar, I drink purified water only now or I react.
>
> The chemical sensitivities are an absolute pain in the ass, I have to dodge then like the plague but can only imagine how hard it would be if they caused life threatening reactions.
>
> Did your issues start suddenly overnight or was it a gradual decline?
>
> Have you had your environment checked for EMF?

I wouldn't call mine anaphylaxis either, but depending on how severe the reaction is, it can last minutes, hours, days, or months -- and it will increase my sensitivities during that time to things. My symptoms are similar to many others who have central nervous system damage from pharma.

When I react to something, I immediately feel this weakness spread through my body, then I get neuropathy, sometimes I can feel as if I've been stung by a jellyfish, and then have neurological heaviness in muscles/neuro muscular problems -- trouble swallowing and other things. I could also describe the reactions as severe fibromyalgia symptoms, too.

What are your reactions like and how has it prevented you from doing things in life?

I'm getting better gradually but I had a major set back from taking a methylated B12 vitamin (204,000 times the daily value of B12), that was way too strong and it caused damage to me all over again and I'm trying to heal from that. That happened last year.

About the water, I drink mountain valley and Fiji. I'm scared of trying to deterium depleted water because anytime I try something strange or different, I don't know what the reaction is going to be. I even reacted to apple cider vinegar severely last year, which also makes me sound like Jordan Peterson because he has a reaction to that too and everyone called him crazy for it. He talks about it on Joe Rogan. I attribute his reaction to his benzo injury.

So I think because you and I took Accutane, we were screwed for life. It then primed us for being sensitive to everything. You were taking 200,000 IUs of synthetic vitamin A. I took it for months and then I developed scabs on my arms so I had to stop taking it immediately. I also took doxycycline for about 6 months to clear my skin, too. But then I started getting strange side effects from that, so had to stop it. What happened with you?

I would say my chemical sensitivities began pretty rapidly while taking benzodiazepines in my early 20s. How about you?

I have checked my environment with a safe and sound pro. Even though I live .2 miles from a cell phone tower, that wasn't the worst culprit. It was my AC which I have put faraday material over now, my cell phone, and wi Fi which I got rid of and now am hard wired in my apartment. Also my husband's gaming headphones with blue tooth were bad, too. I've turned off all signals in here.

Also my husband's gaming headphones with blue tooth were bad, too. I've turned off all signals in here.

Crazy what the CNS can do to someone, I've never been the same since it ramped up, much like yourself. Mine can also last upto weeks but unsure if it's ever persisted into months, at least obviously anyways as it's just a sea of symptoms most of the time.

I had to restrict down to eating only meat for years as everything else would cause a reaction. I can only eat a handful of foods now aswell, it triggers intense brain fog, fatigue, dizziness, mood issues and the list goes on and on. Unable to socialise anymore, smells instantly trigger symptoms and I lose all brain energy to socialise very quickly. Pretty much just in survival mode every day just to maintain full time work with no physical/neuro energy left over for anything else. My optimal Journal has all of this much more detailed if it's of interest.

Did the b12 trigger the CNS again and made you more reactive to everything in general or?

I react to apple cider vinegar aswell, not crazy at all. I'd imagine the DDW would be a very high quality product and would be less likely to cause issues,but I understand the body when so reactive doesn't care about that and can reject it. If you could get your hands on a single bottle and microdose it, it may give a lot of benefit as Jack mentioned on the webinar. DDW is on my to do list.

I took accutane for around 3 months I think and noticed depressive symptoms but this was when I was 22yrs old, after reading all the terrible things about it i stopped. I was on minocylcine for years due to cystic acne and had many rounds of antibiotics for tonsillitis and other things. I also had venlafaxine after I became suddenly ill and that absolutely ruined my libido and caused quite severe numbness and anhedonia. The minocycline/accutane possibly could be implicated in some of the intracranial issues I've had.

Mine started over night with all other symptoms, literally went out with friends one night and woke up to never be the same, that was 6 years ago.

Your AC had wifi capabilities? The SS pro only checks for RF, what about magnetic and electric fields? These can cause hypersensitivity aswell.

Jack said I had a brownout that night it all went haywire and to test a better environment out in far north Queensland for 4 weeks.

>
> Jack said I had a brownout that night it all went haywire and to test a better environment out in far north Queensland for 4 weeks.

I definitely want to read your journal. Where do I find it? I am new here -- do you have a link to it? I am relieved to know someone else here who has MCS. I am actually worried that my condition sounds pretty peculiar and crazy to others: I usually experience lots of gaslighting over it, even from family and especially from doctors. People think that MCS is a psychological condition when it most DEFINITELY isnt.

Let me know if you do decide to try the DDW. Is there a specific brand that you want to get? I would be curious to see if you feel anything after drinking it, or even if it helps.

To answer your question about the B12: I was doing amazingly well and exponentionally getting better after tapering off the benzodiazepine. But then I took the B12 a few years later, which was stupid of me because it was on a list of things not to take if you are benzo injured (I just thought maybe I was recovered enough to take supplements, but I guess not). I instantly felt as if I had brain damage and I only took a small piece of the B12: I had nightmarish symptoms, relentlessly: both physical and mental; even muscle convulsive episodes in the middle of the night for months. Then it turned into full blown life threatening MCS (I had chemical sensitivities before but not to a debilitating degree). It has been over a year and I am still damaged but am much better through sun, grounding, nature, diet, and avoiding ALL chemical exposures. I had to stop teaching piano because I could smell everyone that walked into my apartment. Now I am able to tolerate being around lots people as long as we are outside though. I can hang out with someone indoors as long as they have absolutely no fragrances on them or in their clothes.

So I cant take supplements, like, EVER AGAIN, ha. If I can't get my medicine from food and nature, then I don't even know what to say. I am now actually sensitive to radiation and can't get Xrays anymore. I tried to get bite wing xrays but even just a couple caused neuropathy and full body weakness. I also had a severe reaction to an MRI so cant go near those either. Also, when I go to the dentist, I can't do novacaine at all or even carbocaine, so all dental procedures must be done with no numbing whatsoever. Fortunately I found a dentist who is skilled at doing this and I felt no pain when he reconstructed a tooth for me a few months ago.

Yes my AC had wifi and though it claimed you could turn it off, the signal was still there bouncing around my entire house. It had the highest EMF readings of everything and I couldn't believe that I had used it for so long before I realized what was happening after using the safe and sound pro.

Oh, by the way, you should always keep activated charcoal on hand. That has saved my life on so many occasions, especially if I have consumed something that disagrees with me. It instantly adsorbs toxins in the body. It would actually stop episodic reactions in many cases. I think everyone needs to keep this on hand: I take it in my purse with me everywhere. Even if you had food poisoning, it can save your life.

Even if you had food poisoning, it can save your life.

I think if you click on my profile you should be able to find my posts, or go into the optimal Journal section on the main thread page and scroll through until my journal shows up.

I'm sorry to hear you've been gaslighted over your MCS, it's happened to me aswell but luckily only a few people here and there. It's hard if your family and friends do it, but if your doctors are you definitely haven't found the right one yet. Definitely not crazy, there's heaps of chronically ill people that suffer from it!

I think lite water is meant to be pretty decent! I'm able to drink Evian water, it's not super low deuterium but still less than average bottled water if that's worth trying?

Honestly I think our bodies are on such high alert knowing the damage that's been caused previously by pharma, that anything that resembles it even slightly will trigger all the crazy symptoms. There could have been an excipient in the benzo you had originally that was also in the b12 and triggered you. I've had to try a stupid amount of compounding pharmacies and excipients to be able to take cortisone and LDN. It's like my body even knows where it's been made and will react to the same compounding pharmacy.

Also very sensitive to EMF, if I go near a live switchboard, wifi, microwave or anything that is radiating I can instantly feel it, pretty insane. I feel zonked after any xrays ir MRIs aswell. EMF opens up the BBB, I guess we used to have more of a buffer but now our bodies are on high alert/don't have enough redox to stop the symptoms arising.

Thankyou I've tried it a few times but also react to it so I've had to ditch it. Do you notice any tingles or any odd symptoms when playing piano? I'm noticing it more now with electronic devices or phones.

### VictorianGirl — 2025-08-06T20:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephanies-health-pilgrimage.31362/post-357691>

I just read through your journal. I posted a reply about something because it stood out and was wondering why you are consuming any steroids at all with the condition that we have. I am also surprised your taking LDN because even that is no good for people with MCS. When was the last time you were pharmaceutical free?

Yeah I don't feel good around ANY manmade technology which is why I don't feel safe getting on an airplane and going to El Salvador (thats the only thing holding me back: I would have to rent an RV and drive down). I think the radiation would cause horrible side effects. When I went on a vacation to NH, I also reacted to driving up the White Mountains: I was having symptoms like I couldn't breathe and muscle weakness as I drove higher in elevation. That's another reason I am scared of airplanes. I haven't been on one in 6 years.

I have had trouble holding my cell phone and typing on my laptop: it seems to rev me up. Reading on screens also causes blepharitis of the eyes and eyelid swelling. I basically can't go near anything manmade anymore.

As for the digital piano: I don't particularly feel anything when playing that and my EMF reader wasnt really high around it, but it still could produce dirty electricity. I live an apartment though and wouldn't be able to play an acoustic instrument because of how loud they are. I don't really have a choice but to use a digital piano for piano lessons and practicing.

# Thread: Nathan Siles Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-siles-optimal-journal.31365/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nate Siles — 2025-07-23T00:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathan-siles-optimal-journal.31365/post-356804>

Hi Jack,
You've said clearly over the past year that glycolytic Warburg metabolism in the fovea and testes is a protective design—normal, not pathological. These immune privileged areas use glucose metabolism to shield against magnetically controlled free radicals and to avoid unnecessary proliferation.

But in my case, those exact areas—despite that built-in protection—were the first to go. Retinoblastoma at 8 months old, with tumors destroying my central vision almost immediately after detection. Then in 2015, non-seminoma testicular cancer—something far from conventional.

Lately I’ve been trying to zero in on what the environmental trigger or combination of factors might have been—especially in a 1992 world. What specific mitochondrial toxin or photonic mismatch could have bypassed that design in the retina? And again, in 2015, what got through in the testes?

It keeps raising the same question: if these areas are metabolically protected by design, why were they the first to break down in me? Is it that their sensitivity makes them early detectors of severe environmental mismatch? That would make sense in my case—but most others don’t follow that pattern.

So what made me different? What allowed those defenses to fail? I’m trying to understand what this says about the relationship between light, magnetism, mitochondrial control, and tissue-specific vulnerability.

Would love to hear your thoughts.
Nathan


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Clotilde — 2025-07-23T20:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clotildes-optimal-journal.31372/post-356868>

I off work on summer holidays until September with working plans to possibly not return in September. I was in Mexico on the 7th of July for 10 days. I spent my time mostly outdoors at the beach. I watched the sunrise at the ocean front on what looked like to me to be volcanic rocks. I definitely feel the positive responses within my body now that I am back. For example on my way to Mexico, during the flight as usual the nnEMF sensitivity effected my bladder (trips to the toilet every 30 minutes) feeling pressure and urgency to use the washroom. Also, my left leg felt like it had a pinch nerve. Sometime after I landed and I was outdoors it all stopped. I also had a lot of sunshine to recover from jet lag. I was overjoyed on my trip back home not to experiences those symptoms on the plain. My sensitive around WIFi also seems to have decreased.
As part of my health journey I would like to visit El Salvador. Possibly at the end of August. How is the weather at that time? I will post some questions on the All About El Salvador as I would need an AirB&B to stay at.

# Thread: Salt of the Earth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roxhupp — 2025-07-24T23:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356918>

Hello fellow warriors,
My name is Roxanne , I'm 54 and seeking enlightenment & truth.

I'm new here since June and catching up listening to all Q&A from december 2024 to now. Been following Uncle Jack for 1-2 years and applying some knowledge but knew something was missing so I'm jumping in with both feet.

Was procrastinating on starting my journal because there seems to be so much I want to share but decided to just take one bite at a time and get this moving forward. Also because I don't like sitting at computer (even with my blue blockers), I'm blessed to have time freedom to just be and prefer to be outside most of the day and have yet to get a daylight computer.

I joined for health freedom but have been going down rabbit hole of BTC so I can keep enjoying that time freedom. And of course this has sidetracked me as I want to move faster than I probably should at this point. Until I get trustable guidance on best way to rollover our 401K, ROTH & traditional IRA for which I'm still too young to start withdrawing. After hearing July Q&A , I think I should wait regarding Unchained capitol but want to make changes ASAP. Most are with Fidelity and wondering if I trade into BTC EFT is better then nothing at this point until I can figure out how to self store. Having a hard time swallowing this orange pill but determined to get it down.

I'm a brain warrior seeking optimal brain function and right now I have a difficult time comprehending and understanding things. Many of the blogs are like a 747 over my head and although it is hard to comprehend, what I like is the overall simplicity of sunrise to sunset. That resonates with me and I can easily align with it & follow my gut instinct on these truths. Even as a young teenager coming from a wealthy family, I'd offer to paint the fence outside just to be outside taking in the sun but needed to be active/productive and not just sit there and tan while my father hires contractors to do work I volunteered to do.

Growing up with money made me see things in a different light. I did not take it for granted and actually didn't value money because I could see that money didn't make my family happy. I love to give back and be of service and that's what makes my heart happy.

Like Uncle Jack mentioned about the wisdom learnt from talking with old age folks' is to live life with no regrets. And I have always been intuitive in that sense.

Grateful for all that has flown in my direction but never saved money, just lived in the moments and traveled the world with our kids, all over Europe & Japan. We pride ourselves with no debts except for mortgage , never lived beyond our means and only traveled if we could pay cash up front.

Now seeing how the economy is doing what we have is minimal and not up to my standards for being able to give back like I've always thought I would. Poor planning on my part but now awake to better potential future.

Recently an empty nester with no regrets as we have set up and paid for our children education and they can now be independant. Starting their lives with no debts. Now it's MY TIME to THRIVE.

For me it's always been about making people feel good and giving back. I've been told I was like the SALT OF THE EARTH because I love to season people with knowledge that I've learnt especially when it came to finding truth about health. (I will post my health history and journey in another optimal journal thread.)

I want to keep seasoning people but have lost my drive, passion & purpose. Still have a passion for traveling but now need to find a purpose aligned with that so I can reclaim my zest for life and keep serving.

Jesus sprinkles us like salt into the world **to prevent the erosion of kingdom values, to prevent the decay of society, to fight against the tide of inhumanity and oppression**. This is my mission.

Grateful to have found like minded savages that follow similar journeys. Like musketeers we must be ONE for ALL & ALL for ONE. xoxo

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-25T00:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356923>

### PIAZ — 2025-07-25T02:42:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356928>

> [roxhupp said:](/goto/post?id=356918)
>
> Hello fellow warriors,
> My name is Roxanne , I'm 54 and seeking enlightenment & truth.
>
> I'm new here since June and catching up listening to all Q&A from december 2024 to now. Been following Uncle Jack for 1-2 years and applying some knowledge but knew something was missing so I'm jumping in with both feet.
>
> Was procrastinating on starting my journal because there seems to be so much I want to share but decided to just take one bite at a time and get this moving forward. Also because I don't like sitting at computer (even with my blue blockers), I'm blessed to have time freedom to just be and prefer to be outside most of the day and have yet to get a daylight computer.
>
> I joined for health freedom but have been going down rabbit hole of BTC so I can keep enjoying that time freedom. And of course this has sidetracked me as I want to move faster than I probably should at this point. Until I get trustable guidance on best way to rollover our 401K, ROTH & traditional IRA for which I'm still too young to start withdrawing. After hearing July Q&A , I think I should wait regarding Unchained capitol but want to make changes ASAP. Most are with Fidelity and wondering if I trade into BTC EFT is better then nothing at this point until I can figure out how to self store. Having a hard time swallowing this orange pill but determined to get it down.
>
> I'm a brain warrior seeking optimal brain function and right now I have a difficult time comprehending and understanding things. Many of the blogs are like a 747 over my head and although it is hard to comprehend, what I like is the overall simplicity of sunrise to sunset. That resonates with me and I can easily align with it & follow my gut instinct on these truths. Even as a young teenager coming from a wealthy family, I'd offer to paint the fence outside just to be outside taking in the sun but needed to be active/productive and not just sit there and tan while my father hires contractors to do work I volunteered to do.
>
> Growing up with money made me see things in a different light. I did not take it for granted and actually didn't value money because I could see that money didn't make my family happy. I love to give back and be of service and that's what makes my heart happy.
>
> Like Uncle Jack mentioned about the wisdom learnt from talking with old age folks' is to live life with no regrets. And I have always been intuitive in that sense.
>
> Grateful for all that has flown in my direction but never saved money, just lived in the moments and traveled the world with our kids, all over Europe & Japan. We pride ourselves with no debts except for mortgage , never lived beyond our means and only traveled if we could pay cash up front.
>
> Now seeing how the economy is doing what we have is minimal and not up to my standards for being able to give back like I've always thought I would. Poor planning on my part but now awake to better potential future.
>
> Recently an empty nester with no regrets as we have set up and paid for our children education and they can now be independant. Starting their lives with no debts. Now it's MY TIME to THRIVE.
>
> For me it's always been about making people feel good and giving back. I've been told I was like the SALT OF THE EARTH because I love to season people with knowledge that I've learnt especially when it came to finding truth about health. (I will post my health history and journey in another optimal journal thread.)
>
> I want to keep seasoning people but have lost my drive, passion & purpose. Still have a passion for traveling but now need to find a purpose aligned with that so I can reclaim my zest for life and keep serving.
>
> Jesus sprinkles us like salt into the world **to prevent the erosion of kingdom values, to prevent the decay of society, to fight against the tide of inhumanity and oppression**. This is my mission.
>
> Grateful to have found like minded savages that follow similar journeys. Like musketeers we must be ONE for ALL & ALL for ONE. xoxo

Wonderful to meet you - and me too - now an empty nester finding it SO interesting to do things I never knew I wanted to do! I took the leap and moved my IRA into BTC - despite all family protestations - really like SWAN and their services (although I used another company) and never looked back - which is likely in keeping with your Life Philosophy too. Great community with good leadership and many wonderful results.

### PIAZ — 2025-07-25T17:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt-of-the-earth.31378/post-356956>

Hi again, Roxanne - Not sure how to privately message you... so here goes - take my opinions with a grain of salt  as I'm new (and yet wildly enthusiastic) to BTC. Vast galaxies to learn here. I used Unchained, on-boarding was idiot proof, and yet, just before you leap I would check out the other BTC company recommended often here (Swan) as I believe their fees are lower across the board. SWAN will offer a free consultation (I used Zoom) and uses Equity Trust as their IRA custodian, and Unchained uses Fortis bank. Just wire the money to them; that part couldn't be easier.... ESPECIALLY now, I would proceed slowly and methodically, take a few days to learn the lingo and basics. I was gung-ho to get it done but that was before, as you say, July 19. There was a special 1/2 price, one week only fee for onboarding with Unchained when I involved myself. Many, many folks here are considerably more knowledgable than I am, still in BTC Kindergarten myself. Tell me how to talk with you in person and I'll call you if you want. Not sure why I'm PIAZ here, it's PIA, haha

# Thread: Brain Warrior
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-warrior.31393/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roxhupp — 2025-07-29T04:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-warrior.31393/post-357121>

My optimal health journal,

Haplo I1a1

54 year old french Canadian grew up in Ottawa, Ontario latitude 45, moved to Southeastern Iowa on Mississippi River latitude 41 in 1998 on work visa to work in cardiopulmonary department.
I was always overweight yet very active. Figure skating, snowmobiling, alpin skiing in winter and swimming, camping in summer time.
Always had hard time studying in college. I did best if I could follow lecture/teaching with visual notes and had to write notes to retain information. Had swimmers hears and often difficulty comprehending what I heard but I pushed through because I had great drive to excel. Health was my passion so I became a Registered Respiratory Therapist RRT. Cross-trained into cardiology and obtained my credential as Registered Diagnostic Cardiac Sonographer in 2005. This is a success story because of my learning disability.
\-1997 had gallbladder removed. Which I now know has affected my digestion and gut health. SInce 2019 learnt of ox bile & digestive enzymes with meals to offset this.

\-2002-2004 C-sections 2 beautiful healthy baby girls now 20 & 22. Had gestational diabetes for 1st daughter but not with second. Reached an all time high weight of 285lbs with 5'2 height....

\--2005 put on SSRI for general anxiety. Then remembered advice from my elders...life is to short and kids grow up fast so I decided to stop working full time to be more present with my kids. And stopped using antidepressants. Preferred to apply principle thinking and take stressor away instead of using drugs.

\-2007 Gastric bypass laparoscopic and back at work in 7 days. I feel no pain and heal very easily. Felt like this was my only option to be fully active with my kids after years of unsuccessful yo-yo dieting. Had no regrets for 15 years until now knowing what I know. Been on B12 injetions ever since.

\-2010 Tympanoplasty of my left ear. which also had replacement of tiny bones of the middle ear (ossicles)...now I don't hear as well unless I put pressure behing ear to connect does bones. And this creates dissociation when in groups. I was once an extrovert and now have become an introvert, which is almost by choice because I have low tolerance for stupidity especially since Covid. Feel best when I'm secluded.

\-2015 diagnosed with OCD, then ADHD, then seasonal depression (which I knew deep down was BS since being canadian I love the outdoors) many drugs were tested and made me feel worse and again had deep instinct that they didn't know how to diagnose, they never even did any test... all was subjective not objective. So did some research and found Dr Daniel Amen book "Change your brain , Change your LIfe".
PS my father was on lithium diagnosed with bi-polar and I wanted to rule that out !
Maybe midlife crisis but I opted to take money out of 401K to get brain scans done intead of buying a red corvette. I became a BRAIN WARRIOR on a mission.
Findings of scans done at AMEN CLINIC in NYC. (see resting and concentration scans below). Wondering if this is the white matter Uncle Jack talks about.
\-Mild hypoperfusion of the anterior temporal lobes and right inferior orbital prefontal cortex.
\-Severely increased perfusion to the thalamus in both studies.
\-Moderate increase perfusion to basal ganglia bilaterally during concentration.
\-Mild hypoperfusion of the prefontal pole, occipital lobe and dorsal parietal cortex
\-MIld scalloping seen on both studies , more pronounced at rest.
\-Decreased internal cerebellar tracer activities seen at rest.
\-Increased activity in posterior cingulate cortex -finding correlates with superior intelligence and may protect from development of Alzheimers. ( Dr said only 10% of population have this increased white matter marking and he references ALbert Einstein as an example...LOL It did make me feel better. But explained my drive and curiosity to know and understand more even with my learning impairment and dysfunctional cognition. (Started on EPA/DHA supplements 3:2 ratio, Vit D3 with K2, SAMe, L-Tyrosine, Phosphatidyl Serine and Hyperzine A. ) and work on gut health, thyroid and lymes.

\-2016 AT the age of 45 had heart attack. 40% blockage of my LAD no stents needed. Figured it was probably due to all dental work and root canals I had done.
CAC not done to monitor so no prior to compare but had one done in 2023 and my score was 86. Hoping I'm in process of reversing damage done by working on my glucocalyx. Using Cardio Elite from Quick Silver Scientific labs.

\-2017 went back to school to study functional medicine, received my certification as traditional naturopathic doctor in 2020. I wanted to be in control of my health and my family, instead of being guided by others. During this time I found out I had hashimotos, lymes, mold toxicity, high arsenic . Never went for my board certification because I dropped out of program IIOM (International Institute of Original Medicine) never did my dissertation and wasn't aligned with what they wanted me to do as far as nutritional aspect with RBTI (Reams Biological Theory of Ionization). They follow seventh advent beliefs, I did not. I had started following Dr Georgia Edes from Ancestral Way of Eating and that resonated true to my soul. Started carnivore in 2019. Felt better doing that then all the oxylates from food, glyphosate etc.

\-2019 started focusing on my environment. Irradiate all mold in house, removed carpets, air doctor, water filtration system and now only drink distilled water structured with sea salt from France. and Use Quinton isotonic plasma water ( from my understanding it is the closest structure to amniotic fluid) at bedtime for calm parasympathetic system and use Quinton hypertonic in AM for busy days. This was part of my regiment to balance my adrenal glands since my adrenal burnout. Also had estrogen dominance so started on BHRT. I do regular coffee enemas, Sauna, EMR-TEK red light therapy, PEMF with SOTA and Silver Pulser inspired by Robert O Beckers work. ALso started having eye vision issues since doing on-line classes too much nnEMF.

\-2023 Ovarian cyst found on right Ovary. NO JABS EVER. But my husband did. I know about shedding but too late he did without my knowledge.
At removal pathology report found mucinous adenocarcinoma. I started high dose melatonin , B17, ivermectin, Transfer Factor (in PDR) . PETS scan came back clear and no other markers but it was too late they had already removed appendix during full hysterectomy, becasue they said that was usual origin of this type of cell but report was negative...High stress followed my cortisol went up, AIC went up so started working more directly on leptin /insulin resistance.
Leptin was 15.9 now 4.2,
Adiponectin was 19 now 14,
A1C was 6.1 now 5.5,
fasting glucose was 100 now 84,
hsCRP was 40 now 0.49,
cholesterol was 266 now 208,
LDL was 171 now 117,
triglyceride was 128 now 88,
HDL was 62 now 77,
APO B 120
homocysteine was 14 now 6.7,
fibrinogen still high at 441,
Vit D was 66 now 86...I'm always outdoors with good tan.
Thyroid panel TSH =2.4, FT3=2.7, TT3=97, RT3=8, FT4=1.9, TT4=6.2 TPO=0.9. on Levothyronine T4/Liothyronine T3 of 8 mcg/10 mcg
IgF1 =203
and all sex hormones within range pregnenolone 98, DHEA 106, SHBG 68 on 40mg progesterone, 0.1ml estradiol and high optimal testosterone not supplemented at this time. (Good libido)

\- 2024 Cognition started getting worse. eg...during a conversation someone mentioned and asparagus and my mind would stop to try and figure out what was an asparagus and I'd miss full conversation...this was scary that my brain could not compute an image or recognize what an asparagus was. Did some more testing and found Walsh protocol. I had copper toxicity Copper was 159 now 85, Ceruloplasmine was 39 now 21, Zinc plasma was 90 now 127, whole blood histamine was 39, so I seemed to be overmethylator probably from too many supplements. Stopped almost all supplements and retested histamine which was even lower at 36....which meant I am one of the 8% of actual over methylator. excessive activity of dopamine, norepinephrine and serotonin . therefore SAMe, st John's worth and methionine is not good fit for me. No wonder paxil, zoloft, prozac never worked well on me. Grateful I followed my gut instincts on those. SO now I started demethylating with folic acid, niacinamide, 4.5mg LDN (low dose naltraxone), N1O1, methylene blue low dose when doing IRT.
BRAIN WARRIOR BECAME A MITOCHONDRIAC

\-2025 Current issue needing iron transfusion with venofer , even while eating red meat daily and supplementing with 60mg chewable ferrosequel iron for better absorbtion due to gastric bypass. I cannot seem to find answer to=
low ferritin 4.4 now 49,
iron 17 now 87
%iron saturation was 4 ,
TIBC was 445 now 517,
Hgb 13.1 now 16.1,
Hct 45 now 51,
RBC was 5.7 now 6.3,
WBC was 7.1 now 5.4, ??? infection,
MCV 81, MCH 25 RDW 23, platelets 368
Vitamin A = 62.

I know I've left stuff out , my brain is like mush right now. Need to unplug off blue light!!!

Presently on magnesium, zinc, Creatine, hydrogen tablets , B12 injections, inositol, MCT oil , transfer factor, niacinamide, folic acid, dyhydroberberine, hemeplex iron, melatonin, CBD, MB, digestive enzymes with ox bile with meals , Serrapeptase and binders for heavy metals using CellCore a carbon technology company. And membrane mend from quicksilver scientific.
I want simple Vitality and aiming for less is more .

Have been looking at sunrise since June 2023. Have not been daily for about a year but now resuming and being consistent. Done some occasional CT but now following protocol for 1 week. After reading Épi-paleo RX book.

Started with the Quilt and reset my eating time frame . Since being carnivore I’m fully satiated and only eat once or twice a day per my instinct and often make myself eat to keep up with iron need .

I think this is a myelin issue not melanin. Again this comes from what I heard on podcast but have not read blogs associated with this. Part of my learning disability
Still trying to comprehend all the science of Fe2 vs Fe3.

I believe I need to start reading demyelination blogs.
Any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Hard to focus on where to start with 15 years of blogs.

Your Brain Warrior Roxanne

### roxhupp — 2025-08-07T21:36:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-warrior.31393/post-357797>

Update:
Been trying to catch up with the great information. Have read all demyelination blogs and always have ha ha moments while reading them but the next day I cannot seem to quilt the pieces together in my mind. I try to practice space repetition and reread to fully grasp but this slows me down from moving forward and getting to the missing piece of my puzzle. Even though I'm aiming to thrive with keeping it simple my mind has many squirrels....and I like to feed them.
I am exploring new ways to include fish in my diet. I've only ever really ate fish when traveling on coast when prepared fresh and local. I've tried some home recipes but never got the art of it. I do some canned tuna and sardines (but these make me gag). It's seems better when I air fry them but is this as good? Don't like oysters but will try again hoping to acquire the taste. Had been on Cod liver EFA supplements but stopped that, since science didn't prove to be of benefit. Any recommendations to get more DHA in ?
I believe I've reversed my leptin resistance per some of my bloodwork but still have neurological cognition impairment.
Doing morning sunrise about 30min, then mid afternoon sun for 2-3 hours and always eating supper outside at sunset. I am often in and out of pool during the day and have done cold showers in evenings now started doing cold packs on torso a few hours before bed. I do have amber & red light bulbs throughout the house in evening & blue blocking glasses. I usually liked to read before bed but with these low light it's straining my eyes. Unfortunately this lead me to watch Tv instead for distraction and unwind my mind from overthinking. I know this isn't optimal but so far it isn't affecting my sleep. 11pm seems to be best time for me to go to bed. If I go to bed between 9-10pm , I am up multiple times and restless.
Trying to find my N=1.

# Thread: Resistant husband got vertigo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resistant-husband-got-vertigo.31414/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sarahnile — 2025-08-01T00:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resistant-husband-got-vertigo.31414/post-357306>

My husband suddenly woke up with vertigo. It was in both ears and it was extremely traumatic for him. He's more open now, on the bright side. No more resistance to red light at night etc. I had to take care of him for about a week and it kind of messed up my routine...

In the mean time my lactate and pyruvate tests came back, can anyone interpret them?

**Keto acids, in serum**
The patient's keto acid profile has not been interpreted as indicative of a metabolic disease. It is recommended that the results be evaluated together with the patient's clinical condition and other laboratory findings, and that clinical and laboratory follow-up be carried out.

-   Pyruvic acid: 5.00 mg/L (Reference: <50 mg/L)
-   Keto-glutaric acid: <1.00 mg/L (Reference: <9 mg/L)
-   Acetoacetic acid: 16.00 mg/L (Reference: <20 mg/L)

**Lactate, plasma:** 7.6 mg/dL
Reference range: 4.5 – 19.8 mg/dL

# Thread: BioHacking BiPolar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jojoplachta — 2025-08-10T15:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/post-357931>

I apologize in advance for the length of this. I'm old. I'm BiPolar and probably Narcissistic (in the Greek mythology sense)

Family:

**Mother**: Outdoorsy DairyFarm kid in rural Nova Scotia, Canada Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full. She was red/blonde, very fair with white eyelashes, brows, blue eyes. She had regrown a fingertip that had been chopped mid nail at age 7 She was a Teacher so had summers off and pushed us to play outdoors til dark, took us to the beach on the regular and we often stayed all day, She got freckles, I got very tanned She had a small jaw and crooked crowded tooth placement She had her first (male)child who died at 6-9 months. I was the second born Inherited father's extremely dark eyes, he had black; I got reddish brown hair, olive skin, perfectly straight teeth Third (female)child is very blonde/fair like mother She became anorexic around 6yo, Bulimic as a teenager Mother had Gallbladder removed at 32 and was killed ina train/car crash at 34 (I was 12, sister 10) My Workaholic father was traumatized and family life became very difficult

**Maternal Grandmother**:

Dairy FarmHer, in Rural Nova Scotia (3rd generation) Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full Dark hair Dark eyes, Fair skin Got cataracts in 80's, debilitating arthritis in hands in her 60's, Had all teeth out and full dentures in her 40's, lived to be 95 with very bright mind til the end

**Father**: born to Polish immigrants very much traumatized by being sent to Canada to escape the starvation by Stalin during Holodomor, dairy farmer in Alberta Traumatized by abusive parents His father was angry and mother an extreme depressive and religious fanatic all her life. Left home at 16 Joined airforce at 18 He had olive skin, dark eyes, black hair, perfect teeth His only dental work was removal of a broken molar in his 70's I remember him being fun loving as a young child but extremely Depressed and/or Ragey after my Mothers death and finding out she was adulterous, he became a financially functioning alcoholic, but with violent rages, emotional and physical abuse and extreme mood swings

**Me**:

In utero and from birth until 3 1/2, I lived on a Dairy Farm in rural NovaScotia (11 miles from the AirForceBase) a vaginal birth in hospital, straight onto carnation instant milk/dextrin maltose formula and eventually raw cows milk, cream and lots of raw butter Cared for by my Maternal grandmother from 2 mos old when Mother went back to teaching.

At age 3 1/2 we moved to Trenton Ontario airforce base (literally 200 yards from the airplane runway living in a mobile home) Other than school, I was outdoors in nature from dawn til dusk on weekends and summer. Father took me snowmobiling in winter until my feet and fingers were near frostbitten. I was a behaviour problem to my mother, had sleep schedule resistance, very active, moody and strong willed, had frequent bronchitis, recurring tonsillitis and chronically chapped lips My adult teeth grew in straight but had 4-6 molars filled with amalgam by age 14 Wisdom teeth impacted and painful, removed at 18, 8 more fillings by 21

We had a colour tv as soon as available and I watched it sitting close every evening and Saturday mornings. I had trouble sleeping, and was sound sensitive and slept in the living room so I could be alone and have the radio on all night

At 10 we moved to 5 miles away from the airbase. I ran wild in nature,a horse kick at 12 broke my kneecap, had 3 surgeries by 16, I manipulated maximum Demerol and used it for emotional self medication, started smoking, drinking and marijuana at 11 Started menstruating at 12

At 13 my mother was killed in a car by a train which exposed her extramarital affair to my Father who was Traumatized My sister and I were moved in with Fathers "business partner" who years later I realized was his mistress. I was made responsible for 10 yo sister and mistresses 7, 4 and 6 month old sons. I developed chronic constipation, depression and clinical dry skin began Had 5 more Knee surgeries by age 19 Physical and emotional abuse from alcoholic depressed father. Lived in extreme anger and seething hatred Joined a religious cult and started experiencing intermittent mania

At 15 sent to psychiatrist by a school guidance councillor for major mood swings and religious fanaticism No parent was involved Diagnosed manic depressive Offered lithium which I declined in favour of keeping the "highs"

Forgot about it and went on with my "mercurial" life

At 18 Abandoned by "mistress turned stepmother" and her 3 children. I was dropped off at University, disowned by father and put on Student welfare/and arranged Government loans and worked restaurant overnight shifts to finish a Bachelor of Arts degree. Quit marijuana in first year because I had bad paranoia on it and a psychotic event with hashish Drank alcohol and smoked cigarettes heavily

At 20 Became torridly involved with a married man 21 years my senior w 4 kids After 4 years of a stressful affair, I lost a ton of weight, became severely hypoglycemic, was fainting all over the place Solved it with hospital low glycemic diet and lifetime of avoiding (or trying to) sugar and for a long time caffeine Married man moved in and we had a mostly On but also dramatically and stressfully Off Commonlaw relationship that lasted 21 years

At age 21 had emergency Pilonoidal cyst removal surgery
Suspected hypothyroidism but testing was deemed low normal Still constipated, very dry skin, low basal body temp to this day, low blood pressure

At 24 I moved to Toronto, became a Realtor Lots of parties w alcohol some cocaine, still smoked cigarettes and lots of money and depressive periods Had a giant brick of a car phone in 1985 and cell phone ever since

At 30 moved to NYC w same older man/commonlaw husband Joined a Mystical School and began doing spiritual work and meditation seeking relief from depression and angry rages mixed with manic partying Quit smoking and for months at a time and for about 5 years kept sober, sugar and caffeine free

Worked at a NYC nightclub Used ecstasy excessively recreationally and was living in the night Sleeping every other night Eventually using 1/4 tab ecstasy every other day for depression Manic phases started to become extreme irritability Started gaining weight Used Jenny Craig to lose 25lbs gained back immediately

1998 Husband returned to Canada

In the year 2000 (age 42) Did Atkins Diet at Atkins Centre of Complementary Medicine NYC Diagnosed Hypothyroid and started Natural Dessicated Thyroid Had amalgam fillings changed by Huggins Protocol and Chelation Therapy w dmsa/vit c IV, started on Bioidentical progesterone Tested thyroid releasing hormone because thyroid was intermittently normal and low and couldn't lose the weight

In December 2001 after 911 (lived 30 streets from ground zero and watched the 2nd plane hit) had major depression with suicidal ideation, Dr Atkins sent me to Psychopharmacologist where I got diagnosis of Bipolar (rapid cycling) ->Started Lamotragine 25 mg and Quetiapine (after a few sleep meds) For the first time I could focus enough to read without major difficulty Obsessively read about BiPolar trying to prove I wasn't and how to not take meds for life

Realized that sleep was key so I forced 10-6 sleep cycle using ambien Became intuitively aware that NYC was making me sick

In 2005 Age 47 moved back to Toronto. HATED it Went back into Real Estate Gained 35 lbs, at times gaining rapidly (a lb/day) for no reason Also had days with hypersensitive sense of smell Constant low grade depression, low energy, constipation, dizzy spells and brain fog Saw a PhD Biochemist Nutritionist who advised 1200 mg magnesium, 10,000 iu d3, Evening Primrise Oil, Citicholine, Inositol amongst other supplements that I took for years

Still taking Bioidentical progesterone, I added testosterone, Dhea, Oxytocin (for awhile), injectable HGH (for awhile) Added estrogen eventually

2009 Consulted with Amen Clinic Found a psychiatrist to order a Spect Scan (results below) hoping to manage the BP with nutraceuticals instead of meds Confirmed BiPolar Diagnosis and also PTSD OCD ADD Kept taking Lamotragine added Cymbalta (for depression and chronic pain)changed Quetiapene to Trazodone

On the personal front I met current husband whose DIL had just had his/our baby grandson We bonded and I provided full time childcare weekdays

In 2010 my mostly estranged Father died Engaged in a stressful estate settlement for 2 years becoming estranged from sister in the process (for two years after)

Married in 2011 Was mostly euthymic for a couple years Husband Retired from 37 years policing to fulfill my "travel in an Airstream" dream In 2012 I lost 70 lbs using Bernstein Weight Loss program injecting b12 and b6 every other day, on 900 calories locarb Lo fat subsistence protein and was pretty happy for a few years. Quit drinking for a year and a half.

I had a basal carcinoma removed from my nose (pretty much the only place I'd used sunscreen)I had also used sunglasses but hardly ever. Planning to rent my house and travel long term, we found out the foundation had turned to sand Managed a long, stressful and expensive Reno replacing basement under the house Started finding the stress of Real estate too much and having cycles of depression, irritability and rage Self medicating with alcohol didn't help. I semiretired to provide fulltime daycare for baby grandson until he was 4 Embraced my role as grandmother and enjoyed it

2017 Husband took a job he really wanted and I bought us a Beach House on Lake Erie and bnb'ed our Toronto home. Husband diagnosed with colon cancer 3 months after starting/moving. To "save his life", I obsessively researched the internet and fixated on Morley Robbins' magnesium group desperate to cure cancer with nutrition! I followed the Root Cause Protocol for 5 years, in that group I found Dr Jack. I quit the 10k iu vit d Id been taking for 3 years. I maxed beach living, sunsets and sunrises, and lived rural with very low nnemf Depression persisted and intermittent rage

2018 Had an accident in the water where several tendons/ligaments in both knees landed me in a wheel chair unable to strengthen one without reinjuring Eventually found a rehab program at hospital and devoted to achieving physical fitness through Aquafit and strength classes knowing I'd eventually need knee replacement

2020 Covid STRESS believing the pandemic threat for first few months Got COVID/or a flu? Test was negative and saw the public health incongruencies and then the WEF "plan" Avoided the vax and stopped complying! I gave up RealEstate completely.

Realized Jack was "the real thing" somewhere around that time and was living a mostly outside life tanned, gardened, canned and preserved food for the "expected food shortages" and did Aquafit

Was diagnosed with High Ocular pressure and monitored for glaucoma

Started 5 year taper off Cymbalta thinking it might be responsible for the rages. Realized a few years in it also might've been causing ocular pressure

Kept up Aquafit which had become the only exercise routine to ever give me a neurotransmitter high

Marriage started falling apart Caught him online cheating during pandemic

2022 We separated. He moved back to Toronto and after 10 months I sold beach house, I moved back in with him planning to divorce but doing couples therapy and having lots of episodic rages My knees were both painful with Osteoarthritis-but I was tanned, had darkened my separate bedroom, wore blueblockers and installed screen softwares, wifi timer, increased use of airplane mode and pointed my OCD at DIY Jack Kruse but living in Depression and Social Isolation except for my 3 grandchildren and daughter-in-law who were (and still are) enmeshed in a nasty and stressful (for all of us) divorce.

Jan 23 Had left knee replacement

May 23 Blown away by Jack's Tetragrammaton/Huberman podcast Resumed obsessive tanning, sunrise and outdoor Jack podcasts everyday

June 23 Fell and broke right ankle and severely tore right rotator cuff (bicep longhead severed, subscapularis and supraspinatus with major tears) Had double malleolus pin and plate repair surgery Wheelchair bound 100% for 2 months and mostly for 6 months Suffered extreme neglectful abandonment by husband Rehabbed by sitting in open sliding glass doors until I could drag myself downstairs to ground in sun in yard in bikini, read Leptin Rx and Jack's blogs and X posts Gained another 25lbs on top of the 50 extra I was already carrying

January '24 Had rotator cuff repair surgery Months of painful sleeplessness and physio rehab and massage and chiropractic which still continue as compensatory muscle and tendon stresses erupted

While Opthamologist was monitoring for Glaucoma and because I was experiencing blurred vision, Found retinopathy and optical Nerve Damage in left eye by MRI (Results below)

Still in Psychotherapy for extreme stress w/husband Started CT in October 24 and other principals from Jacks Water Muse X post and Vagus Nerve Rx and reduced Cortisol significantly Depression still extreme but rage returned to cycles of manageable irritability

Dec 24 Osteopathy starts bringing relief to shoulder pain and compensatory (I guess) right sacroilliac pain

Started Methylene Blue at .5mg First dose was awesome euphoria and energizing Gradually increased to 1.5 mg. Eventually found Jacks warnings about messing with it and it appeared to be causing rage at 1.5 mg Stopped it

Regular CT has decreased most of the rages

Did 7 years back taxes and refinanced to pay off 100k in taxes and took out 100 in equity from my house to buy bitcoin

Jan25 Saw something Jack said about not taking MB unless you see sunrise Since I was, I started it again and it definitely precipitated rage at only 1mg

Started having mild anxiety attacks Started tapering off the 50 mgTrazodone and adding CBD/CBN instead for sleep and chronic pain (I get some relief from PEA)I think the cbd has carried over into a calmer demeanor during the day too

Now I'm squirreling away bitcoin, researching El Salvador, (although I don't feel physically able to move most of the time)decluttering in hopes of moving, and bingeing Jack Kruse

**Current dissatisfactions**:

FAT, LOW ENERGY No motivation, NO JOY I crave carbs (although I've been really disciplined for several months now and seem to have successfully switched my eating window to between 8am and 3pm-4 with blood sugar more stabilized than it's been in 40ish years) Still Depressed (Dopamine must be low cause sometimes I don't even have motivation to even comply with my KrusePHD lessons and can't get myself to Aquafit class)Any waves of euthymic mood are few and far between and Mania is always irritability Predominant mood is irritable and detached or depressed and detached. I'm pretty socially isolated and in a constant battle against sedentary inertia. I have a drawer full of exit plan.

I've been in almost constant pain for many months and just a couple weeks ago seemingly unrelated to anything, the pain decreased by 80% (my guess is it's BP related and I'm grateful it's over but fear it's a new part of a cycle called Depression hurts) Muscle and tendon tears seem to keep happening, most recently my previously good shoulder without any conscious injury or strain and the odd time I get a weird seizing or cramping from the shin below my knee replacement, and eye twitches and above one side of my mouth (Since reading the demyelination series of blogs I wonder if that's why)

I just increased my thyroid meds (bloodwork showed low normal)(wondering if I should switch out NDT to Levothyroxine cause I read somewhere it can help depression in BP patients) And have had this itching of the skin on my shoulders and now arms for the last year and a half that slowly gets worse and doesn't really respond to any lotion, oil, except slightly I think to hypochlorous acid spray and castor oil? Not life threatening but super annoying and an indication of? I dunno

**Current Maintenance:**

AO Chiropractor adjusts c2 which rotates biweekly

Regular Chiro every two weeks

Osteopath once a week

Natural Dessicated Thyroid 60mg

Lamotragine (25mg) Trazodone (tapering off-9.5 mg currently)

Bioidentical hormones: prog, test, bi-est, Dhea, pregnenalone.

PEA for pain (3-4x400/day)

80/40 mg Cbd/Cqbn for sleep (get 1.5-2 hrs deep m, average 1:45 REM 7-9 total)

I never miss Sunrise with grounding, am still out there for uva and just added breath hold meditation last week Am tanning in spring summer and fall and have the doors open most of the time until deep winter, I do CT (but like 15-20 minutes at 50°-55°) Blue blockers an hour before bed (which is before sunset now and as soon as sunsets in winter)

I have Iris on my laptop and The BlueLight Diet filters on my phone, a DaylightCo tablet for reading Jack, Sunroof open when driving, Sliding glass doors open in house, Wifi off at night

I eat epiPaleo in season (75% compliance) and have been locarb for 45 years (with bipolar discipline)

Drink 1 cup of coffee in am

Self medicate w 2-3 hard seltzer daily

Marriage has started to have glimmers of supportive rather than just stressful It's hard to be decisive about ending a (second)marriage when MY brain is BP

**2009 SPECT results:**

mild decreased perfusion to the left parotid lobe, small perfusion defects high convexity, right infraorbital frontal, occipital lobe midline, left medial temporal lobe, left anteroinferior cerebellum and bilateral infratemporal lobes as well as scattered hyperfusion throughout the cortex and marked hyperfusion appreciated in the thalamus,, right basal ganglia and left frontal lobe anteriorly as well as the posterior cingulate gyrus with mild-moderate hyperfusion to the anterior cingulate gyrus and marked hypofusion to the cerebellum

**NeuroOpthomology Report**:

Followup for mild left optic neuropathy: Mitochondrial genomic sequencing was normal. Testing for heavy metals was normal as well (mild elevated Thallium)

Evidence of subtle left optic neuropathy with relative afferent pupillary defect, mild temporal pallor of the left optic disc which corresponded to thinning and pericapillary OCT.

Upon re-examination and pending MRI results, we would assume that she has had mild left non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy in the past

**2024-MRI:**

Tecnique: Multisequence multiplanar MRI imaging of the orbits with contrast

Diffusion imaging does not demonstrate any evidence of acute or subacute parenchymal brain ischemia.

The calibre of the optic nerves is symmetric. The optic chiasm is also symmetric in appearance. There is no abnormal signal or evidence of compressive lesion, the cavernous sinuses are clear. The orbital spaces are clear.

**Wondering** if I should switch to lithium? Or Lithium orotate?

If I should take the "miracle" Ivermectin? The expensive but likely helpful DDW? Get a magnetico ?

I do regular Blood donation. Wonder if it really is good for removing excess iron as per Morley Robbins?

I have to take Magnesium or I literally will not shit for more than a week, soooo its a solution but is it also a problem?

I also wonder if my cco is just permanently broken? Like, even when you sit in sunrise can you still be Warburg shifted?

Paid for a consult with Jack! Yay! Finally pulled the trigger! Can hardly wait but also bracing for a punch in the mouth I haven't considered.

Thanks for reading. The search for balance continues.

### Jojoplachta — 2026-03-07T21:32:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31472/post-368359>

Posting this reply from Jack to a new member Sara so I can read it over and over
[Jack Kruse said:](https://forum.jackkruse.com/goto/post?id=368264)
Change is excruciatingly difficult for low agency people because their biological system lacks dopamine creation in their frontal lobes due to a lack of sun. A low dopamine frontal lobe consistently overestimates the value of what they presently have while simultaneously underestimating the value of what they may gain by giving that up now. They do not understand opportunity cost in anything. Unless you are prepared to give up something valuable to you right now, you will never be able to change at all. You’ll remain forever in control of the things you cannot give up. Low dopamine = low agency and it binds us to this reality and keeps us unskilled in communicating with ourselves or nature. To become a decentralized thinker requires solar dopamine optimization.

[View attachment 34509](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/34509/)

This perspective on **agency and dopamine** as a function of light is a powerful lens for understanding why breaking the cycle of night shifts and metabolic disease feels impossible.
The image above is, Edgar Albert Guest’s poem "It Couldn't Be Done", is the anthem for the **High Agency** individual. But as I point out, "buckling in with a bit of a grin" requires the neurochemical fuel to do so. Sunlight provides that juice. Your spouse's pilot light is likely out as your is.

**1\. The Dopamine "Valuation" Error**
In a low-dopamine state (often driven by that "broken retinal clock"), the frontal lobe's **reward-prediction error** system glitches.

**Status Quo Bias:** When dopamine is low, the brain perceives the "effort" of change as a survival threat. It clings to the known (even if painful, like the night shift or chronic GERD) because it lacks the "chemical optimism" to visualize a better future state.

**Opportunity Cost Blindness:** You cannot calculate what you are losing (health, longevity, vision) because your brain is stuck in a low-power, "emergency" mode. It prioritizes the immediate paycheck or the comfort of habit over the long-term gain of solar-syncing.

**2\. Low Agency as a Biological Trap**
If the **RPE-SCN-Frontal Lobe** pathway isn't being "charged" by sunlight, the individual becomes biologically reactive rather than proactive.

**Communication Breakdown:** You mentioned being "unskilled in communicating with ourselves." This is because internal communication requires **GABAergic tone** (to quiet the noise) and **Dopaminergic drive** (to signal importance). Without light, the signal-to-noise ratio in the brain collapses.

**The Control Paradox:** We think we are "in control" of our routines, but as your text suggests, we are actually **controlled by the things we refuse to give up.** In a diabetic/night-shift context, this is often the artificial environment that provides a sense of false security while slowly draining the biological battery.

**3\. Decentralized Thinking = Solar Autonomy**
To become a "decentralized thinker" means moving away from a system that dictates your light/dark cycles (like a hospital night shift) and returning to the **source**.

**Solar Optimization:** Sunlight isn't just for Vitamin D; it's the specific stimulus that manufactures the dopamine needed for agency.

**The "Sacrifice":** Real change always requires giving up something valuable (perhaps the current work schedule or modern comforts) to reclaim the biological ability to **choose**.

The "thousands who tell you it cannot be done" are often those stuck in the same low-dopamine, low-agency loop. They are projecting their own biological limitations onto your potential.

# Thread: My Optimal Journal - Chris

### Chris Amen — 2025-08-16T20:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/post-358342>

I am 73 years old, 4’11” and have been careful what I eat my whole life as I am allergic to so many things, gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, peanuts, corn etc.

I live on 18 acres in Southern Oregon on the Rogue River. I have an organic garden with 30 fruit trees and grow a huge veggie garden and eat with the seasons. And I have done this for the last 35 years, even when I lived in Ojai, Calif. I mostly cook for myself because when I go out to eat, I often get sick.

My mother had me when she was 20 years old. In fairly good health, active but when I was 2 months old, she had appendicitis and that was the end of my nursing. She is still alive at 93 years old. I have struggled with eczema since I was a child but as long as I eat cleanly it is not a problem. I walk every morning at sunrise and sunset at least a mile. I work out with weights 3 times a week for the last 25 years. I struggle with my weight and have all my life. I have been up to 165 pounds for a small gal was way too much, but I am down to 118 now but it is difficult to keep my weight down.

I also have Osteoporosis even though I work out and am active. My labs are not bad but am pretty sure I am Leptin Resistant as I am hungry all the time.

I have signed up for a consultation with Dr. Kruse and am excited for the guidance. I have a lot of labs, MRI and a CT Cardio Score which was good.

I will put more information in this journal as I go.

### Chris Amen — 2025-08-16T21:02:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-chris.31514/post-358343>

I thought I should add some additional information. Since finding Dr. Kruse’s work, I have listened to over 20 podcasts. I have just completed the Webinar series for 2012 and listen to the last 3 PowWow’s. I am printing out the blogs and reading and putting them in a binder. Dr. Kruse is such a great teacher, but I have a long way to go to understand all of this. But am working on it.

I have had an EMF specialist out to my house to evaluate and mitigate problems in my home. Luckily all of my lights are incandescent. After dark, I try not to turn on the lights and if I need to, I have blue light blocker red lenses. I work many hours on the computer a day and have for years doing volunteer work for a non-profit organization that I have worked with over 30 years. I have cut that time down in half on all of my devices and have ordered a Daylight computer. In addition, I have put the Iris app on my computers. Added a blue light blocker to my TV and only watch with the red glasses.

I am working at eating Paleo and have doubled my protein intake and have been outside as much as I can and grounding as well. OK, I think this is a good beginning.

### joyfullyfefe — 2025-08-17T00:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/felicia%E2%80%99s-journal.31518/post-358351>

I did not take the jab, and didn’t comply with the masks in 2020. Became a student of jack’s 2 months ago. Ever since having my oldest son the day of the lockdown I was more drawn to be outside with him. I’m fortunate that we created a habit of spending time outside and grounding. It was like I became a mother and I was incredibly drawn back to nature.

I have four boys (5,4,2 and newborn). Everyone around me is struggling to get pregnant, has had a miscarriage, or has a child thats severely sick. I feel everyone around me is struggling with fertility and yet it seems like we can’t prevent ourselves from getting pregnant even when we’ve tried. This is not a complaint, but an observation.

When I learned about Jack something clicked, and I started catching sunrises last spring (2024). I can say with certainty that my post partum recovery was the easiest, in fact I didn’t deal with ANY post partum hair loss. My midwife is concerned that I’ve never had a child 2 years apart (which I guess is the appropriate window to fully recover and rebalance your hormones) and has been suggesting a vasectomy or hysterectomy but we likely will not work against nature. I’m 34 and my husband is 28.

Since my children are always with me, I have noticed that they have rarely, and I mean rarely have gotten sick since starting catching sunrises. None of them have any vaccinations. I’m looking forward to logging what our winter looks like (I usually struggle with a short amount of seasonal depression).
