# Thread: Zach's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ZachGray — 2025-08-18T01:43:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/post-358393>

First off, thank you Uncle Jack- I'm happy to be here.

My road to optimal begins in 2018.

I left my job in Vermont to be a volunteer track coach at the University of Georgia. I was 24 years old. Working for free was a "necessary" evil at the time to climb your way into the collegiate coaching ranks due to NCAA restrictions on coaching staff sizes. Those restrictions that have since been lifted.

In 2019 I enrolled in graduate school earning a small stipend from UGA's athletic department. Graduate work was a loophole to at least give coaches in my position some sort of income. I'm a very patient person and these decisions were made with my long-term professional goals in mind. Short-term finances were on the back burner, and to my detriment I've never been money motivated anyway. This mindset was a critical mistake.

The grad program was complete bullshit, just necessary for the stipend but as Covid derailed a timely graduation I ultimately chose to drop out in 2021 thus resigning from my position with the team. I left UGA with only a few thousand dollars to my name, depressed and unsure about what was next, but I was unvaccinated and grateful to have avoided the madness happening in other states (so I thought) despite pressure from the team & considerable pressure from my anchors (family & ex-GF). It was quite clear there was a massive PsyOp occuring and I was not going to be fooled...

**...until my financial burdens wore me down and I learned the hard way health & wealth are directly tied together.**

I ended up accepting a coaching position with the US Ski Team, which was based in the US but traveled significantly. Despite it now being the summer of 2022 and travel restrictions eroding, the jab was a requirement. I looked into faking a card but desperation ultimately made a fool out of me. I got my first jab in Athens, GA in July 2022 as a card was needed to enter US Ski & Snowboards training center in Utah.

All my principles had been thrown out at that moment, the self-betrayal felt like a greater injury than whatever the jab would potentially do to me. I got my second jab in Park City, Utah in late September before boarding a flight to Europe where fear again made a fool out of me.

Never again.

Fast forward to January 2025. I'm on a plane to Germany with a new client (I now work with a group of NFL players) and he recommended Jack's first podcast with Danny Jones. I didn't listen to that one until March, but I found your Nourish VT series and my life has changed profoundly for it.

**The Next Epoch**
Since discovering your work Jack, my focus of the past six months has been on the health side of the coin, not only for my own vitality but considering how paradigm shifting the biophysics world view is, I've had a lot of catching up to do (and still do) as it's directly related to my work & the athletes I serve.

Now that the guys are all back with their teams my energy has shifted heavily to the wealth side of the coin. I'm new to bitcoin but the road to financial sovereignty is under way and it's paved with satoshis.

BTC BTC BTC & REDOX REDOX REDOX.

That's where my head is at right now. Making my way through the blogs, I printed out about a hundred of them to give to the guys and read myself.

I currently don't have a home base, I'm writing this from Vermont. For the NFL offseason I'm in Los Angeles, but it looks like we'll start next offseason in Hawaii. Would love to eventually have a training group in Central America during the offseason. I'm planning a trip to ES this fall to scout it out. Very interested in the 1 & 2 bedroom units you mentioned on the Q&A.

**Vitamin D** as of Feb 2025: **40** (certainly much higher now, aiming for 80+ year round)
**Spike Level** as of Aug 14th: **2141**

Thanks again, and onward.

### ZachGray — 2025-08-25T18:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zachs-optimal-journal.31522/post-358715>

For further context. I got two hits of Moderna. How is progress going with getting samples of every lot?
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Prior to discovering Jacks work, I thought I had a fairly holistic perspective on fitness. The world of sport/recreation has been my life- my father is a coach, my mother is a PE teacher, and I'm a track coach/trainer by profession guided by as much as possible, first principles thinking (albeit with a poor knowledge base of physics & mathematics ironically). Fitness, as I assume most would agree here is not a certain physique, athletic feat, or hobby.

**_fitness_** by the biological definition is:
_the ability of an organism to survive & reproduce in its environment_

Pre-Uncle Jack, my fundamentals or non-negotiables of fitness were:

Base of Maslow's hierarchy (air, food, water, shelter, clothing, sex)
Needs based theory (autonomy, competency, relatedness).

In addition to those objective foundations, I include my personal barometers of health which are:

Equanimity
Curiosity, Creativity, Courage, Collaboration (Neil Turok's four C's of innovation)
The ability to do what I want with great joy (play of any sort)
Openness to new knowledge changing ones behavior/thinking (could fall under curiosity as well)

One of my goals as a coach has been to create an environment for clients/athletes that stimulates growth in all of the above areas as they would directly support whatever goals they have on their fitness journey or in their athletic career.

In addition to that, my first and foremost priority when working with anyone is **_do no harm._**
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Post-Uncle Jack, this is a period of daily iteration & revising my bedrock of fitness to integrate:

Light, Water, & Magnetism
Circadian Biology
Financial sovereignty (bitcoin)

Light for instance may very well replace many of my previous "fundamentals" given it encompasses all material things ie food, shelter, clothing, technology.

**_My current dilemma_** as a coach is that my first priority is **_do no harm._** In a post-covid world that looks a lot different given our current understanding of the short & long term impacts of the vaccines.

If an estimated 72% of the global population took the covid vaccine, that's around a 72% reduction in possible clients for a coach that is guided by the precautionary principle & do no harm _provided all that coach has to offer is an exercise prescription_. There is an unfortunate opportunity to help people navigate the new world as a GMO human and as one of those GMO humans, I'm quite invested in A. doing everything to potentially reverse it as irreversible as it appears to be & B. master the skills of living and hopefully reproducing as a GMO human. The latter begs the question of what are the risks to the next generation if their parent (s) are vaccinated if we're not already infertile?

**[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/35481/) ,** you mentioned on a Q&A you wouldn't even consider exercising unless spike levels were below 5,000. How did you land on that value? What is the range of values you're seeing in people? I'm still making my way through the covid blogs, so perhaps that is answered in one of those.

# Thread: Jack Kruse-inspired Costa Rican Retreat

### Beth Greer — 2025-08-25T21:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-inspired-costa-rican-retreat.31551/post-358728>

Two years ago, I had a very strong calling to move to Costa Rica. I am a New Yorker, then 30 years in N. California, then 2 years in S. Florida, thinking that was a better choice during the lockdowns. In 2023 a random visit to Coast Rica -- everything about it was a huge yes, but I didn’t know why at the time. It wasn’t until I discovered Jack’s work that I realized the reason: this land, this latitude, and this light were exactly what I needed to heal on the deepest level.

Now I’m creating an experiential retreat here so others can feel what it’s like to live fully immersed in these protocols. For 7 days (May 28-June 4, 2026) we’ll rise with equatorial sunlight at 9° latitude, ground in jungle rivers and waterfalls, experience cold thermogenesis, and nourish ourselves with organic animal- and wild seafood-based meals. All of it in a pristine rainforest sanctuary designed for true circadian alignment and mitochondrial repair.

This is a chance to practice quantum biology where it works best—under pure sun, surrounded by water and wild nature. If your mitochondria are craving a reset, I’d love for you to join me. Here's a link for more information. [https://supernaturalmom.com/retreat/](https://supernaturalmom.com/retreat/)

Jack, if you're reading this, please come as my guest (or stay at Shanti's home which is nearby the Retreat Center).

# Thread: Fellow Seeker

### Dave110 — 2025-08-30T16:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fellow-seeker.31570/post-358898>

Hello Uncle Jack, I really loved your recent post in your Journal entitled Echoes of the Shadow: A Journey of the Seeker;
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-358892](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-358892)

1\. What you wrote is what I aspire to, learning to see the inner darkness, to try to understand it, to extract light from that dark which diminishes that darkness and grows the light of inner wisdom, empathy, unity etc, the proof of your inner work having embraced the suck/chaos of any moment that triggers a negative response. Trouble is that negative response however small or big can sometimes be lightning fast then the remorse of that comes a second later. Its not easy to consistently respond without any negativity as your first response, especially when tired. Yet, I think circadian rhythm health helps to acquire a base level of equanimity from which to be able to explore these inner workings and to work upon them. An inner war to get to and maintain inner peace.

There are things in nature which point to this process metaphorically or as a living symbol of the human condition and potential, particularly the yearly cycle of Solstices and Equinoxes with their increasing light or increasing darkness. Various spiritual texts from many traditions alude to this process also.

I tend to think this process is the primary reason why creation exists (if it exists by design), that a person might choose to work through this process of a journey of enlightenment, evolving higher consciousness, overcoming inner darkness. Your wounds create your wisdom, moreso if you are actively seeking to learn from life as it happens, so you can hold that wisdom as an imprint on your soul and apply it in any given moment, and pass tests in the school of life, to one day graduate...

Does the above resonate Jack, what do you think?

2\. Do you see the sun as being something more than just a physical fireball in space which programs our biology, but also as something metaphysical or inter-dimensional with which a person can commune?

3\. Is there something the ancient peoples knew, who venerated the sun, which modern humanity does not or has forgotten?

4\. do you think reincarnation exists? There are stories of kids who remember their last life and then go meet the people who used to be in their life and can verify their memories are true.

5\. what do you think of out of body experiences or so called near death experiences where it seems the consciousness can exist outside of the physical body? People can relate what happened in the operating room when it was known they were clinically brain dead at that time.

6\. do you think astrology has any truth to it, that the planets really can exert a psychological effect on us or influence our personalities? What about physically effect us as well?

7\. I don't normally follow astrology but I have become aware there is a rare triangle alignment happening at the moment with Uranus, Neptune and Pluto which is said to bring about a cultural, scientific, spiritual renaissance, so it is very interesting you made such a spirutally seeking post at this time. Are you feeling that?

[https://jessicadavidson.co.uk/2023/11/13/the-uranus-neptune-pluto-triangle-2024-2027](https://jessicadavidson.co.uk/2023/11/13/the-uranus-neptune-pluto-triangle-2024-2027)

[https://jessicadavidson.co.uk/2024/04/15/outer-planets-triangle-2024-28-and-transiting-midpoints/](https://jessicadavidson.co.uk/2024/04/15/outer-planets-triangle-2024-28-and-transiting-midpoints/)

All the best!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carris%E2%80%99-optimal-journal.31576/post-358938>

Hello,
I am 48 yrs old residing in Chicago, Il.In recent years I’ve struggled with constipation, bloating, and losing weight
( want to lose 40-60 lbs$I also have trouble staying sleep and have eczema on my fingers and one toe. Several factors contribute to these issues but I’m not sure how to tackle them:
I work midnights ( police)
I have teen twins with special needs one on the autism spectrum the other with ADHD both have their challenges .
My gallbladder was removed in 2012 due to my ignorance and painful stones.
I have silver amalgam fillings since childhood and got root canals in recent years.

I was induced at 37 weeks had c section and epidural.

My mother is 72 and weaned off of benzodiazepines and other meds for anxiety but has lots of withdrawals and says that she is losing her eye sight.

My daughter on the autism spectrum is 13 but recently stopped talking and stopped drinking liquids. She also has what it appears to be bad eczema and has stomach pain that I can’t seem to help resolve even after seeing many doctors and getting an esophageal scope. She is always in distress which stresses me since I haven’t been able to help her.

My daughter with ADHD struggles in school, executive functioning ,and certain aspects of volleyball ( coaches don’t understand ). Her AIC is also high and she has keratosis Pilaris.

I have a 50 yr old brother on the autism spectrum.

# Thread: Post 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-1.31661/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ozzy1 — 2025-09-20T10:34:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-1.31661/post-359758>

Two years after spotify autoplayed the three part max podcast as i was sleeping at the time in my mums garage at the end of her garden and diving into jacks work, my mind can now recall words longer than three sylables so i feel like this is the right time to start journaling as we enter what i now hear people call the "fourth turning", and my dopamine has a habbit of being produced chaotically and i feel this will be grounding for me as society gets harder and harder to relate to and interact with. Jacks work imediately resonated me and the conspiritorial implications of the information where imediately apparant, mainly because i have had an interest in a more neo platonist theory revolving around light water and magnetism for a long time and i have read pretty much every bit of alternative information out there regarding this worlds nature. My reality and the reality of those around me are simulatounesly colliding and drifting further apart as the truth is slowly entering the cultural zeitgeist whilst I spend most of my days walking with all the information jack and the surounding ecosystem has given me fermenting in my mind and producing more and more personal eureka momments as my ability to connect dots improves more and more in a much more grounded fashion thanks to this biophysical framework that allows the esoteric to be much less up in the air metaphysically, sending me further away from the views of my peers even though i sound less and less crazy as time goes on and at this point comparative to my normie friends clairvoyant to the future.

I do not broach topics to people much anymore directly. When suggesting incadecent lights are healthier and 420-480 nm LEDS are killing you softly even when described in a less alarming fashion gives you incredulous looks than saying anything more alien than that is a fools errand and i am the black sheep of my family in a family where everyone has a story to tell. I have got quite good at leading horses to water but not forcing them to drink in a performative fashion where i mention things i have learned and say it in a way to be speculating in real time its implications and leave the dots to connect to their own minds. The facts said on their own are genuinely interesting to people who have a brain but i leave the implications or the inferences alone. Only proof of work- works. My uncle has a long list of maladies after retiring from the steel works, my mother recently had a knee operation and previously had a subaracnoid heamorage. She was suffering awfully in recovery so i gave her my emr tek and she had instant relief. She and my uncle bought one each afterwards and have been using it ever since. I bought a pack of incandecents for my uncles birthday, but he is entrapped in the energy saving propoganda perpetuated over the last 30 years.

I have good genes but i am incredibly porous. My hair was almost reflective as a child and my nicknames veered from casper to gollum and i have grey blue eyes. My fathers side is incredibly creative. His mother and father and my grandparents lived a heavy life of smoking and drinking and narcolepsy but nearly lived to their eighties. He was a well known drummer, her a model. Their house was nicknamed the jazz lamp for the after parties they hosted for travelling musicians where ray charles supposedly peed on the lamp post outside and sang my father happy birthday. On my mums side my great grandparents lived well into their ninetighes. That side of my family is ingeniuously practical. My great grandfather smoked 60 a day working in a furnace rolled by my great grandmother and bought 20 more on his way home and lived to 95 still working as a window cleaner up until his late eighties just for something to do. She got a letter from the queen on her birthday so i think im right in saying she lived until 100. My brother is in the top echaleon of the UK millitary. This colliding of the eccentric creative and the practical conservative is probably somewhat explinatory for gifts i have and the problems i have faced neurologically and physically.

I look around the dinner table and they are gone, their children and my grandparents also, dying in their late seventies to early eighties after several decades of illness. My mother is the strongest person i know, but she is struggling in her early 60s, and my uncle is slowly falling apart. Both have been jabbed. Then i look at myself knowing that I am lucky to be alive in my early thirties. Without jacks work I don't think i would be here. It is a strange feeling knowing i exist at least in part because of someone else i do not know and have never met, and i have everything i have been fighting to have since i was a child, which is nothing more than a place of my own with an aesthetic that is satisfying to me and what I like to call walking without expecation into the fantastical nature of the speculative unknown - without my final destination being an instution that doesn't add any creduality to a Curriculum Vitae where i spent more time than i would have preffered to in my twenties. Then I think about why and what caused all of this and who, and what needs to be done to its enablers and without getting too inflamatory i would do it with a heavy heart but i would not need tea breaks.

I will add more and more to my story as time goes on, because of the nature of my conditions in my twenties i have limited but sustainable resources given that allow me to be time rich and able to be outside most of the day and continue to heal. I hope it will come as some repayment for the help jacks podcasts and blogs have given me that my subscriptions are technically fabian funded and presumably imediately turned into bitcoin. Apologies for all the spelling and grammar mistakes

Hello all and Dr Kruse, I'll try and make this as short as possible :

History
\-Mother not close with for many years, heroin addiction and had to cut ties. No other known heath issues. Remember her liking tanning and the sun and fitness. I was probably born when she was about 20 years old. There was some issues she was on bedrest for maybe a month prior to c section (fraternal twin) and on meds but do not know the extent of it.
\-Grandmother recently died of cancer at 85 years old.
\-Father died few years ago of kidney cancer with history colon cancer

I was born and raised in New York and now live in Long Island with wife and 2 kids. Now 44yrs old
Firefighter until 2021
No covid jabs whole family
H haplotype from 23and me

\-2018 hemorrhagic cavernoma causing stroke and 2 partial sezeires. Required craniotomy . Was on depakote and leveteracitam for few years and now have been off all meds for few years no thanks to centralized docs
\-2021 vasectomy with bad experience artery cut spent few days in hospital
\-2022 half thyroid removed for NIFTP. Do not take thyroid pills
2025 colonoscopy 2 polyps removed
\-History of fatty liver and high enzymes. Drank alcohol heavily for many years due to tragedy and currently try to keep at minimum. Was currently seeing few doctors for possible autoimmune hepatitis diagnosis (labs came up positive) but the biopsy did not show it just some hemosiderosis and under 15% fatty. Never got any real answers (from centralize or functional medicine docs) I think I have it but keeping it at bay or just beginning

I have been following Dr Kruse for while. Have been doing cold exposure on and off for years. I was roped in to Peter Attia and that echo chamber plus functional medicine doctor. Was going to gym past year and half and doing cardio. My fitness never really improved and felt drained even with wearing blue light glasses. Started to develop sleep apnea despite this and being at lowest weight and eating mostly carnivore and getting sun(mostly afternoon)

CTJ moment:
Saw podcast with Dr Christopher Bronson. DJK was dropping info that was really resonating with me. The nnemf and blue light changing the oxidation state of iron and the heme proteins and red blood cells, and the resulting hypoxia (sleep apnea makes sense now!) and the resulting effects to the mitochondria( shifting away from oxidative phosphorylation). Especially if you do not see the sunrise you are unable to use the TCA cycle (again taking the mitochondria out of play) Its crazy you think your going to gym to improve mitochondria, and that pathway, yet you are doing the exact opposite.

\-Currently seeing sunrise for past few weeks, plenty of sun, adhering to circadian principles, quit gym, exercising outdoors, doing best to mitigate emf (ethernet cords and such)
\-Feel really good, best in a long time, sleep good, lido and energy up. stopped waking from sleep apnea
\-me and wife drinking 1 liter of 105ppm DDW day from hydro health

\-Would appreciate any advice on best way to move through the blogs (been going backward along with the q and a's)
\-any advice for new members appreciated from all
\-want to help son grinding teeth (Heard DJK mention brain or brainstem issue), and wife with migraines and neck pain (herniations)
\-Tried to get Strike app to get feet wet with Bitcoin but said not supported in NY. How about coinbase? (total newbie to bitcoin)

So much to ask but want to take time and really go through forum and q and a's

Thank you Dr Kruse for all your help.

# Thread: Advanced Sleep Phase
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eelefter99 — 2025-09-28T14:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/post-360115>

I get sleepy between 8 and 9pm. I wake between 3:30 and 4am. This is consistent, even if I go to bed later.

I push through the sleepiness and go to bed at 10pm.

I'm up with the sun. Spend most of my day outside. Use IRIS for when I use my computer indoors. Use only red light after 7pm. Wear blue blockers when I watch TV at night. Turn it off at 8:30 - 9pm.

My reading tells me that Advanced Sleep Phase is what's happening. The advice to address it is:
\- more bright light at night (until 9pm)
\- aim to wake between 5 and 6am
\- push back bedtime until 10:30 or 11pm.

Any thoughts about this?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-09-28T20:26:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/post-360118>

> [eelefter99 said:](/goto/post?id=360115)
>
> I get sleepy between 8 and 9pm. I wake between 3:30 and 4am. This is consistent, even if I go to bed later.
>
> I push through the sleepiness and go to bed at 10pm.
>
> I'm up with the sun. Spend most of my day outside. Use IRIS for when I use my computer indoors. Use only red light after 7pm. Wear blue blockers when I watch TV at night. Turn it off at 8:30 - 9pm.
>
> My reading tells me that Advanced Sleep Phase is what's happening. The advice to address it is:
> \- more bright light at night (until 9pm)
> \- aim to wake between 5 and 6am
> \- push back bedtime until 10:30 or 11pm.
>
> Any thoughts about this?

It is my understanding that “back in the day” before nnEMF and ALAN people would go to bed, (soon after sunset) sleep for a while, wake up, go to the kitchen (where the fire was still warm) chat with each other, write poetry, invent things . . . Then go back to bed for the “second” sleep. This time was called “between the sleeps” and is when many great works and inventions came from. If I remember correctly Einstein came up with E=MC2 in the middle of the night “between the sleeps”.

### eelefter99 — 2025-09-29T15:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/post-360158>

I think it took Einstein a decade or so of mulling and I couldn't find any record of his insight happening at night. But, I do appreciate the metaphor.

I am aware of biphasic sleep. If I were waking earlier, 2 or 3am, I could see how doing other stuff for an hour and returning to sleep might work but waking at 4am? An hour later, it's 5am. And the sun's coming up at 6:30ish right now. And I wouldn't just conk out at 5am, like switching off a light. Basically, by 5am -- I'm up for the day.

But biphasic sleep is certainly a viable way to sleep -- for anyone who might be reading this and having a similar but different experience than me: this might help them harness their natural cycle. For me, it's a bit too late in my cycle to return to a "second half" of sleep. At best, I'd get an hour or less of REM. Which, I take whenever I get the chance.

Thanks for the ideas.

Anyone else have any thoughts about introducing light until 9pm? Not a bright spotlight, but more than my usual red lights. I know it seems sacrilege to even think something like that, especially on this forum, but...now it's out there.

Does anyone have thoughts about this?

Many thanks.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-29T17:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/post-360159>

Have you tested your environment?

When I lived in Chernobyl, I would wake up at 3am almost daily no matter what time I fell asleep.

Turns out I had a pretty massive nnEMF problem. Once I fixed it I started sleeping through the night.

### eelefter99 — 2025-09-29T18:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-sleep-phase.31682/post-360160>

Thanks for the thoughtful reply. Yes, I have an EMF meter and all the readings in my sleeping environment as well as my home are on the lower (at worst) range.

I live in a wooded area in the Hudson Valley in New York. No overhead wires nearby. No Wi-Fi signal strong enough in my bedroom. No cell phone towers close enough to measure.

I’ve been living here over 10 years and this weird sleep pattern began a little less than a year ago. This coincides with nothing significant other than my discovery of Jack Kruse and my enthusiastic lifestyle changes: more sun and more cold.

Thanks.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Florian's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/florians-optimal-journal.31740/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Florian_CH — 2025-10-16T16:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/florians-optimal-journal.31740/post-361104>

Hello everyone,

I’m Florian, 28 years old, from Switzerland 47th latitude. I live in a small valley village with about 8,000 people.
I came across Jack about a year ago and have mainly been listening to podcasts. I got the Gold-Membership recently and started read blogs and watching webinars/old recordings.

History
My grandmother grew up as a typical farmer’s child and had very few health issues for most of her life. She had a stroke at age 70 and, now at 85, deals with slow-healing wounds, tooth loss, and scoliosis. The main powerlines of the valley run close to that village, but i do not know when they were built.

My mother had minor digestive issues and trouble sleeping, nothing was ever diagnosed. She’s now 65.

As for me, I had more health issues early on, which makes sense to me now, considering the environment I grew up in. I’m the youngest of four children, and my mother had me when she was 37.

When I was 4 years old, I broke my leg. After that, I decided being outside wasn’t safe or fun anymore. I stayed indoors most of the time, drawn to technology. My parents were going through a divorce, so no one pushed me to go outside. That trend continued, I always jumped on the next new thing. GameBoy → PlayStation → Smartphone → PC. Always indoors and always into the night.

At 10, I gained weight and got fat, my eyesight worsened, -2.5, and I started having digestive issues.

At 16, I began an IT apprenticeship. Within two years, my digestive discomfort turned into a bad case of ulcerative colitis. My brain function also declined. My grades dropped, and I eventually failed the apprenticeship. Around that time, I was also a semi-professional video gamer. I spent nearly all day in front of a computer with cable headphones on.

At 20, I was put on Remicade (Infliximab) and naively agreed to take it for about a year and a half. I eventually quit after managing to stay in remission from UC by eating only meat. However, it didn’t solve my underlying issues.
I then started a second three-year apprenticeship in a logistics office, hated it.

At 24, I worked a summer season in the Alps on a cable car station 2222m a.s.l. . I felt really good there, and my health seemed stable at the time.

25 - 27, I went back to an office job because it paid better. That didn’t go well. I developed sleep issues, waking up 10–20 times a night feeling agitated. I was stiff, dehydrated, and woke up with headaches, though I never felt thirsty during the day. My weight went from 78 → 104 kg (at 183 cm). I had infected wounds on my big toes that wouldn’t heal for an entire year. My brain fog was terrible. I could barely think and dreaded going to work every day.

In December 2024, I had an appendicitis surgery. It took me months to recover. After the surgery, I developed mild tinnitus, which led me to start paying more attention to nnEMF exposure. My surgical scars are healed now, but they start to hurt again if I’m exposed to too much nnEMF or get dehydrated.

Started making changes bit by bit this year:

-   Watching the sunrise every morning and keeping darkness after sunset
-   Eating seasonally and only during daylight hours
-   Kill-switch in my room/sleeping area
-   Sleeping under a Swiss Shield EMF canopy
-   Adding a Magnetico sleep pad
-   Eating daily seafood
-   Using blackout curtains at night
-   Always keeping my window open
-   Wearing grounding shoes when in the nearby forest
-   Switching from PC → laptop → daylight computer
-   Wearing blue-light blocking glasses after sunset (though I mostly stay in candlelight or darkness)
-   Started daily cold therapy (CT) on September 1st — about 1 hour after sunrise
-   1h walk at sunrise, tanning mid day if sun is out 2-3h + time in shade

\*\*Notes
Seafood was never a thing at home growing up
I quit all medication after stopping Remicade (the one exception being painkillers right after surgery)
No jabs

Progress so far:
Weight loss: 104 kg → 79 kg
CT tolerance: 10–20 minutes at 10°C
Built my first deep tan this year, never had a tan before in my life
Chronic wounds on toenails healed
Muscle strength improved
Finances are out of fiat
Addictive behaviors replaced with CT and sunlight
Brain fog and headaches gone
thinking improved
Seasonal allergies almost nonexistent this year

Work in Progress / Current issues:
Still get dehydrated at night. Dry eyes, nose, and muscle tension, feels fine during the day as long as there’s sun, worse now as nights get longer
Myopia: –3.0
Cavities on both upper wisdom teeth, one between first and second molars, my teeth seem to get weaker
Still get sunburns on my face, rest of the body doesn’t burn anymore
Hairloss
Job: My education ties me to office work in Switzerland, but I’m looking for something that gets me outdoors and ideally location-independent. Currently looking for work. Will be working back in the mountains or as a farming helper next chance I get.

### Mat.I — 2025-10-17T12:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/florians-optimal-journal.31740/post-361142>

Well done on the progress so far


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Aaron's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal.31769/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### amocchino1 — 2025-10-23T21:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal.31769/post-361500>

My health journey started when I was an infant I had **pyloric stenosis** and a whole lot of **eczema.** Throughout my childhood I would have eczema that would come and go but got worse as I was finishing high School and starting college. Every year I think I had antibiotics for sinus infections, or some sort of virus. I had mono in high school. The eczema post high school is what had me start looking at food and nutrition to fix my skin. It would usually manifest on my hands and back of the legs.

I grew up in Phoenix Arizona spending about a year and a half in Australia as a kid before the new millennium. I had a hard time adjusting to a new culture at that age and at some point I started developing **tics and other twitches** that was labeled **tourette's and OCD.** I also had a lot of fear as a small child, thinking that I was always going to be left somewhere by my mom, I never fully understood why? maybe my parents getting a divorce at such a young age? Some relationships in my 20s had a lot of codependent themes, I've wondered how that has played put in my nervous system and current symptoms - I know there's some work that says limbic retraining is important with mold, lyme, etc. Things like DNRS, Primal Trust (I've done Primal Trust).

I am currently 34. **I moved to Austin Texas in 2016** and about 2 years later my health started to decline with symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain specifically in my low back sometimes my knees and feet. My brain started malfunctioning - I would get such a dead to life feeling that I couldn't think straight. The only thing that helped was sitting down eating and drinking something, resting for a few minutes. Things progressed. When I first started noticing something was F'd up, I was on a plant based diet - kind of following the medical medium horse shit. I was hopeful it would help the eczema, nit really. Progression to mild NAFLD, was consuming a lot of seed oils for cooking, my Omega 3:6 ratio was awful. I switched to an animal-based diet following mostly Paul Saladino work after reading the carnivore code, this didn't change much - maybe blood sugar stability and I didn't feel as weak. I haven't had high liver enzymes since eating better.

I started to have pretty bad **frontal head pressure in 2019**, and I noticed it started only when I ATE - pretty immediately after eating like within seconds to minutes maybe even during eating? It's been a while now I don't really remember. **The head pressure is now constant.** The only time it improves is with sleep it feels a little bit better in the morning, OR if I put pressure on it, like squeeze my head basically. Next symptom that came on was some sort of "vestibular migraine" so I was told by a regular neurologist. I did an MRI - structurally unremarkable. The vestibular migraine was labeled because of the head pressure, but also this **MdDS** like sensation. It feels like **when you get off of an escalator and you feel like you are still moving.** It's also a **constant bobbing sensation when I walk.** Very unsettling. The neurologist prescribed some device called the Nerivio - didn't really help, and also and some other medications including SSRI's (I declined these). These neurological symptoms have been the most recent the last 3-4 years.

I was doing some breath work one day (around 2020/2021) and noticed this weird bag squeezing sensation in the epigastric area, the stomach organ itself I narrowed it down to. It's hard to explain, but it's almost like the stomach organ was making a sound like the bag was ringing itself out. Tension builds around hunger, and a stuck feeling occurs - if the stomach growls it brings relief, a lot of the time it doesn't growl, burping as a temporary relief and then sometimes pressing on it would help. It felt like there was a lot of gas stuck up there. History of a lot of heartburn and acid reflux as well. H pylori breath test was negative. Found functional medicine - Genova FX stool test, gut inflammation and high dysbiosis, thought maybe SIBO, did the Trio Smart test for SIBO - negative. Still didn't know what the heck was going on. I tried every type of diet: SCD, low FODMAP, Animal Based, plant based, cut out different things - gluten, dairy. No help. I also did an **upper endoscopy to check the organ and it was also unremarkable.** Currently, I am working with my 3rd functional medicine practitioner doing some functional neurology, not making much difference. The second FM practitioner suggested **mold.** We ran an **OAT - found candida followed up a year or two later ran a urinary mycotoxin test and found four different strains of mold while they were low. I've lived in 3 different houses since and symptoms haven't changed. I think my school was moldy.** I bought a house, roof leak, but fixed and remediated. The whole time since 2022 thinking mold, I've been using binders and antimicrobial herbs. Sometimes I would think I was having herx responses and clearing things, but maybe not because I still have symptoms. Nothing has moved the needle, and only progressed - mostly neurologically. Recently did Vibrant Wellness Neuro Zoomer Plus, biggest take away was past active EBV, and Strep A infection. No brain autoimmunity hooray.

September 2025 - I went to a naturopathic doctor specializing in frequency medicine. He used a bioscan and found **high levels of black mold as well as Lyme and other co-infections including Bartonella, babesia, borrelia.** Obviously being an acupuncturist, I'm into energetic medicine, but even this was a little wu for me. I haven't done any other follow-up for Lyme to confirm. I recently retook the **VCS test** and scored pretty high on some of the assessment questions for further investigation pointing towards biotoxin but the actual biotoxin portion for the pictures I did fine. I don't really want to spend the money on the igen-x Lyme test. I am using a distilled water tincutre that he charged to match my frequency and essentially kill the bugs... LOL, well see.

I had quite a few vaccines as a kid - regular schedule for kid born in 1991. **I did not** receive the COVID jab. I made the mistake of getting a Hep Vax and Typhoid for traveling to Peru in 2017, nada since.

Anyways, I've been a member for a little over a month or so here and obviously learning that mold and Lyme ia a nnEMF issue and Light issue, so I'm working on controlling the light in my house, as well as nnEMF. Challenging part is that I still work as a server in a restaurant, while I'm building up my acupuncture business, so it's been hard to get a good circadian rhythm and also ensure that I see the morning sunrise. I am typically not done working until midnight  My goal is to transition out of the restaurant as soon as possible and just focus on the acupuncture business, but it is taking a while. I've been wondering... would it be advantageous to get up and see the sunrise - even if that means I get 6 or less hours of sleep? then, take a nap after? LOL

I'm also concerned for just living in Austin Texas long-term. I know Jack says that Austin is a shit hole due to the sub 6G out here. I was considering maybe moving a little bit further out of town, more rural, and then commuting in to the city to work at the clinic - because at least at the clinic I work at I have some control over the nnEMF in the building. I recently did a consult with Anthony from EMF Safe. I got a body voltage meter and have been successful at mitigating nnEMF my sleep sanctuary. I am hoping to make the best choices for my health. I don't want to give up on my business here, but if its the only way for full recovery, then I am open to moving out of Texas. Maybe acupuncture in El Salvador? Costa Rica?

TIA for any of your thoughts and comments and reading my story! - Aaron

### rtd — 2025-10-23T21:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal.31769/post-361503>

> would it be advantageous to get up and see the sunrise - even if that means I get 6 or less hours of sleep? then, take a nap after?

From experience, yes. Grounded too

> I don't want to give up on my business here, but if its the only way for full recovery, then I am open to moving out of Texas. Maybe acupuncture in El Salvador? Costa Rica?

To me it makes sense to cut and run from TX and relocate. Think of all the resources and time you are and will be spending in TX that could be re-allocated. With that said, develop a business plan and life plan to map a path forward while you are in TX. Don't jump into a worse living/working situation.

I say all this with similar thoughts of uprooting. I do not have a business, and I view some of skill sets as very specific to niche industry. Gotta have a plan.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal.31769/post-361504>

Thanks rtd. I have a park near me that reads low nnEMF. Been wanting to go in the mornings. Are you using grounding tools or just bare feet on the ground?

### rtd — 2025-10-23T22:47:22+0300

> [amocchino1 said:](/goto/post?id=361508)

41.5218° N, 71.2835° W

The Leptin Protocol. Not a subset of it. Follow the protocol rules or its not the protocol. I know this because I was partially doing it and thinking I a was doing it (my early blog posts) but the proof is in the outcome. I still had positive results based upon how crappy I was when I started.

>
> I was doing some breath work one day (around 2020/2021) and noticed this weird bag squeezing sensation in the epigastric area, the stomach organ itself I narrowed it down to. It's hard to explain, but it's almost like the stomach organ was making a sound like the bag was ringing itself out. Tension builds around hunger, and a stuck feeling occurs - if the stomach growls it brings relief, a lot of the time it doesn't growl, burping as a temporary relief and then sometimes pressing on it would help. It felt like there was a lot of gas stuck up there. History of a lot of heartburn and acid reflux as well. H pylori breath test was negative. Found functional medicine - Genova FX stool test, gut inflammation and high dysbiosis, thought maybe SIBO, did the Trio Smart test for SIBO - negative. Still didn't know what the heck was going on. I tried every type of diet: SCD, low FODMAP, Animal Based, plant based, cut out different things - gluten, dairy. No help. I also did an **upper endoscopy to check the organ and it was also unremarkable.** Currently, I am working with my 3rd functional medicine practitioner doing some functional neurology, not making much difference. The second FM practitioner suggested **mold.** We ran an **OAT - found candida followed up a year or two later ran a urinary mycotoxin test and found four different strains of mold while they were low. I've lived in 3 different houses since and symptoms haven't changed. I think my school was moldy.** I bought a house, roof leak, but fixed and remediated. The whole time since 2022 thinking mold, I've been using binders and antimicrobial herbs. Sometimes I would think I was having herx responses and clearing things, but maybe not because I still have symptoms. Nothing has moved the needle, and only progressed - mostly neurologically. Recently did Vibrant Wellness Neuro Zoomer Plus, biggest take away was past active EBV, and Strep A infection. No brain autoimmunity hooray.
>
> September 2025 - I went to a naturopathic doctor specializing in frequency medicine. He used a bioscan and found **high levels of black mold as well as Lyme and other co-infections including Bartonella, babesia, borrelia.** Obviously being an acupuncturist, I'm into energetic medicine, but even this was a little wu for me. I haven't done any other follow-up for Lyme to confirm. I recently retook the **VCS test** and scored pretty high on some of the assessment questions for further investigation pointing towards biotoxin but the actual biotoxin portion for the pictures I did fine. I don't really want to spend the money on the igen-x Lyme test. I am using a distilled water tincutre that he charged to match my frequency and essentially kill the bugs... LOL, well see.
>
> I had quite a few vaccines as a kid - regular schedule for kid born in 1991. **I did not** receive the COVID jab. I made the mistake of getting a Hep Vax and Typhoid for traveling to Peru in 2017, nada since.
>
> Anyways, I've been a member for a little over a month or so here and obviously learning that mold and Lyme ia a nnEMF issue and Light issue, so I'm working on controlling the light in my house, as well as nnEMF. Challenging part is that I still work as a server in a restaurant, while I'm building up my acupuncture business, so it's been hard to get a good circadian rhythm and also ensure that I see the morning sunrise. I am typically not done working until midnight  My goal is to transition out of the restaurant as soon as possible and just focus on the acupuncture business, but it is taking a while. I've been wondering... would it be advantageous to get up and see the sunrise - even if that means I get 6 or less hours of sleep? then, take a nap after? LOL
>
> I'm also concerned for just living in Austin Texas long-term. I know Jack says that Austin is a shit hole due to the sub 6G out here. I was considering maybe moving a little bit further out of town, more rural, and then commuting in to the city to work at the clinic - because at least at the clinic I work at I have some control over the nnEMF in the building. I recently did a consult with Anthony from EMF Safe. I got a body voltage meter and have been successful at mitigating nnEMF my sleep sanctuary. I am hoping to make the best choices for my health. I don't want to give up on my business here, but if its the only way for full recovery, then I am open to moving out of Texas. Maybe acupuncture in El Salvador? Costa Rica?
>
> TIA for any of your thoughts and comments and reading my story! - Aaron

> [amocchino1 said:](/goto/post?id=361537)

fiat farm and get out! naps are great (read sex at dawn as jack suggests) see sunrise, go out of city and sunshine max when u can! I'm 2hrs from the canadian border, in cloud country not sunny inland, and sun maxed from spring to end of summer and had 67 vitamin D (fitz2) from sun alone, its fucking brutal in the cold long spring! How easy ya'll have it down south!! El Salvador and Costa rica both don't regulate acupuncture from what I've read online, but are you expecting the locals to see you? Paying what rates? etc. etc. Plan, prepare and stack and then move. We're seeing just the tip of Uncle Jacks escape! ### amocchino1 — 2026-04-12T23:57:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-optimal-journal.31769/post-370811>

Decided to move out of Austin. Hopefully will be out by the end of the year. It's too bad its such high tech here... A lot of wonderful decentralized minds, esp BTC. The food is great, found some local shop bringing in oysters from the Gulf. It seems Austin gets a lot of it except the tech and pop density destroyed the city. It was probably great here in the 90s.

I've done some more testing and found MARCoNS positive in nasal passages. I am really just interpreting this to tech abuse and lowered a-MSH now.

In terms of the head pressure, I am realizing it is all vagal and trigeminal based. After reading a lot of the blogs I am realizing I probably have a shit ton of deterium in my head and stomach - especially since the stomach fluttering issue, must be the vagal exhaust system getting gunked up. I am going to start drinking DDW while I transition out of here and also up the CT. Most of my ancestry is European or northern hemisphere based. Italian for at least half. I'm hoping enough CT keeping my gut in the cold will help a lot of this unwind. As for my head? The pressure is interesting. It seems if I squeeze my head in the temples the vestibular issue goes away... I think maybe dunking my head in ice every day would be good. Snorkel in there? LOL. But how long is the question? I learned carbs are a pretty much no. I get pimples on my back if I eat a lot of them, hmm more mass getting out. I tan very well, so makes me wonder if my Italian ancestry is more south. I love using the sun. Even with the pimples, or eczema getting out in the sun just helps it heal so much faster. So focusing on increased DHA, continued EpiPaleoRX, subtracting the carbs ( I also thought I could get away with this in Texas because some fruit does grow, apples, pears, etc.) add DDW.

I am able to cut back at the restaurant and take Fri nights off so I can at least get to bed early enough to get the AM sunrise. This has been the hardest thing to do consistently because of the schedule. I can get a lot of sun midday which is nice, I am hoping that it will offset the blue light at night in the restaurant. I think the latest I have the lights on me is 1130pm.

Plan is to get to ES, ASAP. I am wondering how long I will need to be out there. After, I will get back to AZ for a bit to reset with family. I am hoping since this is where I grew up I will feel better, too. Going to purchase a magnetico soon while I am still in Austin, so I can at least be working on this and then when I get back to AZ I will have that since there's none in AZ. I am hoping a permanent move to the tropics in a few years. Just need to figure out how.

# Thread: Optimal Journal 2
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-2.31828/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tierratrue — 2025-11-03T20:20:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-2.31828/post-362077>

For me mom strike looked like I stopped being everything to everybody. Stopped cleaning up after everyone, doing there laundry, dishes, making the perfect meal before I went off to work swing shift at 3pm so everyone could be taken care of. I gave no explanation. I just couldn't do it anymore. This was the beginning of my path to divorce. My husband was baffled, but I just couldn't explain. It was very inconvenient for him. He helped out around the house but didn't have the mom overachiever wound that I had. I had no energy for it. In hindsight, I think I myself was confused by it. I started taking walks in the woods, meditating more, just recharging my own battery from the full time nursing work and raising a family. I was still fully in going to soccer games and celebrating milestones but just couldn't overdo it. Near that time my father in law died. The grief that struck my husband went deep and long and I took over the kid and household roles best I could to support him, but it drove a deeper wedge between us as he isolated and processed. Life, work and kids went on business and usual and didn't allow for a lot of time to repair and work on this stuff. This was 2017 and patchy connection that still was wonderful at times lead to his desire for open relationship. This is where with my initial hard stop came to agree after doing some deep inner work and feeling what the emotions and facing jealousy was all about. I agreed eventually knowing that if I didn't it would likely happen without my consent. This devolved in more separation, isolation and he falling in love with someone else. Of course! Writing on the wall. Our divorce has been pretty easy and final Jan. 16th 2026 and I will be in El Salvador when it happens!

Covid came and my nursing profession blew up. Refusing the jab and standing hard for that for me kids worked. I luckily stayed employed due to a religious exemption letter. But after writing that I new it was over for me. I couldn't stand that kind of beg for a shitty job while the people at the top decided my fate. I quit in Nov. 2021. Went back to school for a Masters in Health and Human Performance Coaching and got my International Board Lactation Consulting Cert. Found per diem work in lactation and also working part time as a Life Insurance agent. Its a crazy fiat position, but gives people their life insurance without dying under certain qualifying illnesses and injuries. Especially with the epidemic of disease to come it does seem like a play on taking advantage of the current conditions in US and the onslaught of what is coming for those who do not take Jack's wake up call.

So here I am almost 10 years later. Still living in Chernobyl (Santa Cruz, CA) right next door to Silicon Valley. Working on a plan to buy property way south, El Salvador? My health is holding. I spend lots of days in sunlight and several days a week in the Pacific Ocean. Have been a practitioner of CT since 2018. I do not take this for granted and know a preventative next step is necessary.

PS I just read CT 7 and saw that prolactin is a circadian hormone! OMG mind blown. How this ties into its multiple uses I am so ready to learn.

# Thread: Stuart's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stuart31 — 2025-11-17T05:43:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-362728>

My health seems fairly decent for now at age 41 but the red flags have been around for quite some time that the clock is ticking. Currently living at the 52nd latitude in Saskatchewan, Canada with 5g towers all around. Though I'm a fair distance from the bulk of towers downtown and population density isn't out of control at around 300k.

I've had astigmatism and myopia since around 8 y/o (lots of tv/video games in the 80s and early 90s), consistently wore contact lenses every time I went out of the house since age 15. I had PRK surgery in 2014 but fortunately no trouble with mental health or serious side effects from it though I did have some severe dry eyes at night the first few years afterwards. Seems my whole life has been with my eyes shielded from UV light until now. I've been dealing with some minor eczema on/off for about 7 years. Other signs are that I've had hyperhidrosis since my teens, orthostatic hypertension at 25 years old and about 7 years ago I got tinnitus and hearing loss in one ear. I lost both parents to cancer early on. Mom at age 53 with colon cancer and dad more recently in 2020 at 74 after being through the meat grinder with prostate/colon cancer on/off for 15 years. I had a colonoscopy 2 years ago that showed the beginning of some polyps in the descending colon that were removed.

Overall, I feel well enough but these alarm bells are getting louder. I never took a covid shot which was about the hardest thing I've had to do living in a communist shithole that is BC at the time. Vaccine mandates meant being ostracized from my previous job as a chef (good riddance), family members, not being able to go out anywhere but for groceries for a year. That and listening to all the ignorant jabbed people rubbing how proud they were of getting their poison shots in everyone's faces. I’m so grateful I never took it but have noticed a big dip in my cardio endurance since covid.

I lived in the Rocky mountains for 15 years spending as much free time in the backcountry as possible. Maybe that's why I don't have more severe health problems already? Perhaps my skin was able to compensate with UV exposure that my eyes weren't getting at higher elevation and some CT in winter? Actually being out in the mountains make up the majority of times I could say I’ve truly felt good as an adult.

Since discovering Dr Jack's podcasts and blogs in the past 2 months I've been doing sunrise daily and wearing 98% blue blockers at night and 68% during the day except while working. I've also increased DHA with more seafood and taking fish oil. Remarkably the eczema on my hands seems to have cleared up already. I've just started mitigating nnEMF at home in the past 2 weeks by turning off circuits for my sleep area and living room during the day and switching over to ethernet instead of wifi. Also, I have noticed a small but significant boost in sleep quality, energy levels, mood and motivation from these changes but it seems like a drop in the bucket as to what it'd be like living in a proper sun environment with less nnEMF. If only I had found Dr Jack's work in the summer, I'd have been outside naked way more and a hell of a lot more tanned right now!

I have been planning to get out of Canada since the vaccine mandates as for me that was the last straw. On top of that, with about half of my fellow Canadians voting in a leader of the WEF it all seems too far gone. With all I’ve been learning from Dr Jack and considering my own health situation this plan of jumping ship will have to be ASAP. 5-10 years is a realistic timeline to get out with my current financial situation unless I can figure something else out. 5-10 years may not be soon enough with the political climate what it is. But that's the way she goes.

Mitigating at home seems to bring nnEMF into just a slightly concerning range. Optimistic that mitigation at home while making trips down South 2-3 times/year can buy some more time and avoid a cancer diagnosis like my parents or something else.

### AndyShannStock — 2026-01-27T02:26:02+0200

Hi Stuart, I’m a fellow Canadian and am looking to connect with other members who have or are working on getting out. Where are you at with this trajectory so far?

Hi! Nice to meet you. I've been pretty silent on here lately. I got back from my first trip to El Salvador 2 weeks ago. Next trip to ES will be for one month in June and likely 3 months next winter with subsequently longer trips until I transition my business completely.

### stuart31 — 2026-02-21T14:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-367275>

I’m back from my first trip to El Salvador. It was only for 2 weeks but it was enough to get some good information, start a solar callous and boost vitamin D levels to get through the rest of winter. The first week I was very cautious with UVB exposure taking breaks every hour (would highly recommend the Dminder app for those new to the tropical sun like me) but the second week I got a bit more bold with increasing mid day sun exposure the eczema started to return on my forearms. It’s interesting how the distribution of the eczema follows acupuncture channels. My best guess is:

1.  Sub optimal vitamin d levels plus increased photoelectric effect equals unchecked electron flow along fascial networks into the cellular environment and mitochondrial IMM causing huge ROS production and inflammation
2.  The increased amount of semiconductive currents from melanin produced in my skin only after the first week (particularly forearms where I have the most pre-existing solar callus) overpowered the insufficient intracellular water from CCO through poor heteroplasmy/broken respiratory proteins again causing massive inflammation in the skin

Good news:

-   Leptin resistance seems to have improved. I lost 10 lbs in ES and carb cravings have decreased and stayed down
-   A great start to a tan going. The “How to build a solar callous” blog really works cause I typically burn really bad before getting any tan (fitzpatrick 1)
-   after getting home energy levels and mood were greatly improved
-   constipation was completely gone from day one in ES with no supplementation at all…this is truly amazing
-   returning to my low quantum yield environment at home the eczema went away immediately

Been back for 2 weeks now. Concerns for me now:

-   Energy levels are trending back down to the lower side
-   The more I shift towards a seasonal epi-paleo high protein/fat diet I’m tending back towards constipation. Have to take fiber supplements and magnesium to have normal BM’s

I’m currently waiting to get blood work results from my first round of optimal labs:

\- Highly sensitive CRP
\- Complete vitamin D panel
\- Full thyroid panel
\- Salivary cortisol / melatonin
\- VAP or NMR analysis cholesterol panel
\- Omega 3/6 serum test

Will have to do functional medicine to get all of the required labs. My doctor and the lab were profoundly confused by the tests I requested. I have suspicions about what the results will be but we’ll see.

Next trip to ES will be for one month in June. I'll be offering Acu treatments to mitos and those interested. Looking for a clinic/treatment space if anyone has suggestions. Preferably something “bringing the outside inside”. The hygiene of an indoor clinic space but with minimal nnEMF and artificial lighting.

I’ve been silent on the forums as am undertaking reading Jack’s recommended books as well as the blogs from the very beginning which has been enlightening and at times frustrating.. With the rate new blogs are coming out, If I don’t keep up with reading, I’ll fall behind.. Jack sure does come at us fast. I’m currently on Cold thermogenesis 2. At least the older blogs are a lot shorter! There’s a surprising amount of biochemistry which is actually great for me to learn. The TCM education I got did cover basic western medical physiology/pathology/pharmacology but was very centralized and disappointing. What Dr Kruse has been laying out over the years is just the foundation for education as a health practitioner I was looking for. There’s far too much pseudoscience and magical thinking in Chinese Medicine diagnosis and treatment from what I’ve seen. I’m excited to get to the tensegrity and QE series as I believe these will have a lot more insights specific to acupuncture practice and the relevant biophysics. I hope to increase the efficacy of what I do to truly help others reach optimal health, **prevent disease** and those with chronic illness, autoimmunity, cancer, etc.

The business plan for now is one month in June in Atami, then 3 months and subsequently longer trips until transitioning my business full time to ES.

### stuart31 — 2026-02-24T16:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-367561>

One more weird thing I forgot to mention in the last post. When I got back home, I noticed three stray white hairs growing out of my left ear around the tragus that were legit 2 inches long. never had this happen before! The left ear is where I have hearing loss & tinnitus. Could my body be trying to get more internal melanin from the sun this way?

### stuart31 — 2026-05-11T18:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-373745>

Health update:
I’ve been back from my first trip to El Salvador for 3 months. Continued to get fat over winter but with the return of the light in spring, weight is much easier to manage.

Current environmental focus:

-   No ALAN. 30 minutes grounding and AM Sunrise viewing. All through winter/spring I have been getting up 30 minutes before sunrise with no alarm. Just get up and go outside regardless of temp.
-   Eating lots of seafood/DHA. Primary sources are from salmon, sardines and raw oysters multiple times per week
-   Breakfast 30 min after rising. Eating smaller meals towards the end of the day
-   I was going hard with CT in Jan-March but have been slacking. Got my compression shirt so good to go for some more to thin my rhino skin
-   Maximum AM sun exposure after sunrise. I always have mornings off so this is a huge asset

For lab work, getting comprehensive testing done in Canada would cost $2k just for thyroid, sex, melatonin/cortisol hormone & lipid panels. A lot of money for just one datapoint. I decided it would be wiser to put that money towards more trips South ASAP instead. The one useful metric I was able to get from my doc was HS CRP at 0.8 in February.

No significant health changes for better or worse. Biggest wins are improved sleep and bowel movements. Bristol 4's or close to every morning about an hour after my big ass breakfast. Sleepwise over winter for a couple weeks I was waking around 2-3am and would fall asleep fairly shortly after. That has stopped now and sleep has been solid through the night. Out like a light almost immediately as my head hits the pillow and up with the sun. Before last year finding Dr Jack's work, my sleep was not terrible but very sporadic. It’s embarrassing to say how vague my understanding and implementation of circadian biology and light exposure were even just last year.

Planning to do 3 months in El Salvador at the end of the year and apply for temporary residency. After that I'll have to make a more concrete long term plan for where to live. I need more information to make a sound decision. There's nothing keeping me anchored in my current city which is probably my best asset. My biggest consideration for moving is my mtDNA haplotype “I”.

The best option within Canada is the west coast but even that does not seem good enough for "I" haplotype sun requirement. This was a big shock to me being 80% Scandinavian, I'd assumed my MT's were highly uncoupled but it is only the maternal side that matters. My understanding of “I” being from the N lineage but not as distant as the more northern H, J, U, T groups. Hence, “I” would have mid-level engines with mid-level uncoupling ability. Do I have this right? Do my engines require at least mid-latitude sun? Of course a combination of strong magnetism with minimal declination is a given in the current times.

**MtDNA haplotype**: I
**Goals**: Optimal health to 80+. Reverse familial predisposition for heart disease and colon/prostate cancer. Prevent neurodegenerative disease
**Location**: Latitude 52N @ 500m elevation

### stuart31 — 2026-05-11T18:34:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-373751>

Reading the blogs/forum updates. This has become my obsession outside of work. It actually adds to what I do in “alternative” healthcare so leaves me inspired every day. Trying to read the new blogs as they come out on the same day just to keep from falling behind. There have been some knowledge bombs on the forum so trying to stay on top of those. I've read the blogs from the start in 2011 up to the EMF series with some scattered other blogs out of interest. From here on I've decided to start reading by series instead of straight up chronological. Next series I've printed out and ready to go are:

EMF
Quantum Biology
Energy & Epigenetics

The older blogs have undercut my whole understanding of health and disease. Namely the CT & Brain-gut series. I found these refreshing compared to the newer blogs as they take the evolutionary perspective from rodents to primates and finally humans and help put the wide variety of things Jack teaches into context as to what health and disease really are all about. The new information about the archean era and earth's geomagnetic sphere currently I find easy to get caught in the weeds of what exactly is happening and what it all entails and feel kind of lost at times. Though it is stretching my brain to its limits trying to understand what it all means and what are the implications. This feels like a good thing. I'll get the most out of this low voltage environment while still here.

It truly blows my mind how all this wide breadth of knowledge connects. Factor X epigenetic adaptations followed by taking on genetics from viruses in the East African Rift. The catch 22 of our leaky guts allowed us to adapt and rapidly uptake nutrients to build a big brain but also gave us a weakness of autoimmunity. The vagus nerve as the controller of the digital (SCN) and analog (gut) systems. Jack's insights from Brain-gut are all synonymous with the recent blogs on how the vagus also runs the isotopic fractionator Y axis. Doesn't this all just break down to the Y axis and deuterium in the gut?

On growing a massive human brain. Humans evolved and were able to thrive in Africa with minimal access to carbohydrates because of tons of access to DHA and iodine from the ocean. Where is the only other place on earth we see animals with a complex CNS comparable to humans..Dolphins and whales eating a shit ton of DHA in the ocean. Where do we find tons of DHA in humans? In the brain and eyes. When does this become a problem? Blue light in the northern winter wrecking DHA is ruining circadian biology causing diabetes, bad hormone panels, leptin resistance. Well isn't that all just a breakdown of the X axis and the pivot to the Y axis? Are we back to the vagus nerve controller? Hell to the YES!

What else is all this isotopic fractionation of these 3 axes dependent on? Spinning of these vortexes, generating woodward lift (Z-axis) and centrifuges heavy mass to the outside of the vortices needs strong/stable magnetism on earth that is failing at an increasing rate. The delicate balance of deuterium in our earthly water tables flushed by season/latitude and apparently our place in the galaxy is now overflowing like a clogged toilet.

It's the “quantum” piece I really feel is missing from my current understanding. Most of my understanding is in the classical physics realm. I've read the basic stuff on the forum about electron spin and chirality in melanin etc but it feels very surface level. I think that is about to change soon.

As far as business, reading the blogs has made my understanding of disease and communicating to patients so much easier. This is what I was hoping to find going to TCM and post-graduate education. A universal model of disease. And I've just scratched the surface of what's to be learned. I understand in this environment my tools to help people are limited but I can at least educate people about why and how their environment sucks and help mitigate some of their aches & pains. Thank you Dr Kruse.

### DavidL — 2026-05-12T06:49:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuarts-optimal-journal.31891/post-373806>

The blogs and forum should help you massively in your field when patients come to see you with "pain, stiffness, soreness, and injury". I am a bit envious of the medical professionals here like yourself, I think you can make a huge impact on your patients with this knowledge. As a fellow Saskie kid, hopefully the snow is gone forever for this year. I am back in Saskatchewan for 3 days this weekend, hopefully the weather is good!
