# Thread: Jack Lehman's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lehmans-optimal-journal.31892/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ikkyuman — 2025-11-17T21:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lehmans-optimal-journal.31892/post-362750>

Optimal Health Journal Nov 15, 2025

My name: Jack Lehman age 78
Born: Aug 23, 1947 in Jersey City, NJ
Both my parents were angerholics. My father was also an alcoholic and had PTSD from his 10 months of combat in Patton’s 4th Armored Division. He was abusive, using both his fists and his feet on me. From the beatings I learned that I’d rather be a hammer than a nail.

Got my bell wrung playing grammar school football - 1st concussion?
Started smoking cigarettes at age 8, stealing some from his pack. It was a way to say “fuck you” to my father.
Pleurisy at age 14.
High School football: broken metatarsal, broken index finger - 2nd concussion
Inspiration: I flunked Algebra and the principle took my report card and changed the F to D saving me from summer school. I was getting drunk, stealing, getting detention - I was angry and felt trapped. The Marist Brothers that ran the HS looked like they knew something that I didn’t. Started talking to them - signed up to go to Marist Prep in Cold Spring, NY as a junior. (Spent 7 years in the order, 4 with vows.) I thereby 1. escaped my father’s abuse and 2. I enjoyed hunting and fishing and playing ice hockey,

’69 - Went to Paris with $ from student loans.
’72 - Obtained a Maîtrise es Lettres (MA) in Philosophy at the Sorbonne in Paris. Wrote my
thesis on Nietzsche - a kindred soul and mentor.
’72 - Sailed from San Francisco to Yokohama on a Russian freighter. Spent 1 yr in Tokyo. Took
Sho Dan (black belt) in Karate. Taught English, French and Italian to survive.
’74 - Went overland from Italy to India on public buses - 6 months.
Lived in India for 7 yrs and 2 more in Japan.
’75 - Introduced to J Krishnamurti/ spent 21 days doing Vipassana in foothills of Himalayas.
Started getting free of what I call “The Dao Domination”
([https://www.buzzsprout.com/1554039/8691095](https://www.buzzsprout.com/1554039/8691095)), started being present.
Amoebic Dysentery, lot of it.
Met Rajneesh in Poona - 10 yrs living in his commune in India and then Oregon. Wild,
celebratory and informative years. Taught marshal arts, practiced tantra.
’78 - 3rd concussion - my teacher knocked me out - jaw broken in 2 places
’81 - Lived in the Oregon commune: drove heavy machinery without proper hearing protection
Tinnitus; aching joints. Bought my first horse. Still have horses - love to hunt elk with
them.
’84 - Moved to Santa Fe, NM - still here.
12 years of “Men’s Work”
MA in Counseling Psychology - Licensed Psychotherapist since ’96.
“Nonviolent Communication” - I learned from the founder, Marshall Rosenberg, the
nature of anger, it’s purpose and how to work with it so that it doesn’t eat your lunch.
I became a certified trainer in NVC in 2006 - still teaching it. IMO, it’s the best tool for
getting free of conditioning.

’86 - All mercury filings removed
’96 - First bout of Tansient Global Amnesia - onset experience just after working out at the
gymn - Cat scan at the ER.
2010 - Yellow fever vaccination that I could not find a way to avoid.
Aajonus Vonderplanitz - started eating raw eggs, raw meat, butter & cheese
2012 - Mohs surgery on right forehead for some kind of skin cancer
2014 - Neuroma in left foot from much dancing tango Buenos Aires in narrow shoes - still a
nuisance no.
2015 - 6 broken ribs when my horse and I went over backwards from a 20’ embankment. At
the bottom his ass was resting comfortably on my chest - airlifted out of the wilderness.
“Arthritis” in the base of both thumb.
Glenohumoral joint pain
Frequent straining of the muscles and tendons of right forearm
2016 - Hip replacement
Have eaten mostly elk, some deer, 1 bear and 1 oryx that I have killed in last 10-12
years.
2020 - Got covid mid February 2020 while visiting my daughter in Davis. CA. Spent 3 nights in
a motel and then flew back to Santa Fe. Took NO JAB. Stopped dancing t
though  hoping to avoid the shedding of spike protein.
2003 Arthritis now in base of right thumb too.
2024 - Spent 5 weeks in El Tunco - disappointed to not meet any JK members. Like the place.
Couldn’t catch any fish. I tried often from the shore in the early morning and
at sunset. Anybody know how to fish from the shore in ES?
Have a ticket to ES for Dec. 5
2025 - Tore my left achilles tendon (partial tear) while bucking up a hung up pine tree.
Surgery on Sept 15. In PT now. I have booked a flight to ES for Dec 5. I will see the
surgeon on 19th hoping to get the ok to travel.

Recap of Current Body Issues

Cataracts - Diagnosed about 2 years ago.
I gleaned from Jack’s writing on cataracts that the brain, by increasing opacity
of the lens is trying to protect itself from nnEMF, blue light and that the treatment
ls to limit exposure to the latter and to increase the amount and quality of sunlight.
So I consistently observe the sunrise and sun gaze every day. Take many breaks
every day to allow sunlight into my eyes. I wear blue blockers, never sunglasses
or sunscreen. I go to bed within an hour or so of sunset. I only have
Incandescent bulbs in my house. I do some (not as much) grounding daily
but do live on the 2nd floor of my barn. I have grounding mats but haven’t yet
sunk grounding rods in the dirt to ground them directly.
Floaters - More in LT eye than the Rt one. Rt eye dominant.

Connective Tissue pain - thumbs, trigger finger, Glenohumoral joints, right forearm
Neuroma - left foot

Numbness - right heel area - Lack of energy flowing to the numb area due to my titanium
hip?

Transient Global Amnesia - 1st episode was harsh because I thought that my brain was
permanently damaged. I knew my name but not much else. Short term
memory was totally gone. My fear was catastrophic. But the next day I
was normal. So during the consequent episodes I had little fear. I rode
them out. One, around 12 yrs ago was so severe that I couldn’t teach a
the workshop when I arrived at the venue.
My mother did die from a stroke so I would like to get Jack’s
counsel about my GTA. Anyone want to comment on this?
Torn achilles -healing up - is this accident related to a systemic weakness in a connective
Issue? If so what is causing it and how to mitigate it?
I have a ticket to ES for Dec. 5. I hope my surgeon will ok my date.
Would like to find a place not too far from the beach with low EMF etc.
Does anyone know how to go about finding it?

I don’t know my haplotype and am not sure how to find out. My father is about 90% Irish.
My mother is 100% Italian, Her people are from Caserta, just north of Naples.

Mother’s History

Born: Aug 16, 1920
Died: 2005 - stroke, few months after my father died.
Had 4 children and 1 miscarriage
Mitral Valve Prolapse diagnosis - Took Coumadin - misdiagnosis? Took Coumadin.
Varicose veins
Osteoarthritis in knees
Migraines
Diverticulitis
Removal of skin cancer on nose

Maternal Grandmother 100% Italian

Born 1901 Died 1984? From heart problems Had 5 children.
Severe osteoarthritis
Bad migraines
Triple Bipass Surgery
Died from some heart condition methinks.

### Ikkyuman — 2025-11-18T02:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lehmans-optimal-journal.31892/post-362759>

Hi Piaz, I hope to meet you in ES! Have you been to interesting places?

### PIAZ — 2025-11-18T02:53:43+0200

Yes, when younger. In response to finding a place near the beach - you can read the blogs for names and locations - and also go to Booking.com - the Mitos, (I am told) seem to find each other at certain beaches. I just read about the high number of venomous snakes in ES. Cheery topic.

### Ikkyuman — 2025-11-30T17:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lehmans-optimal-journal.31892/post-363395>

I found a 2 bedroom house in Atami. I will be there on Dec 6. You can whatsapp me at + 1 505-988-5464 cheers, Jack

# Thread: Gabriel's Hidden Path: A Privacy Junkie's Quest for Optimal Living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-15T04:04:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363892>

Hi everyone,

My name is Gabriel, but here I'm PrivacyJunkie. My username aligns with my work as a cybersecurity and risk advisor for a big global firm. I've spent my entire career in IT, and honestly, it's a double-edged sword. Without awareness of tech's downsides, we all pay a heavy price eventually. Turning 48 soon, and I'm grateful: no known acute or chronic health issues holding me back. In my personal life, I teach people how to build real digital resilience.

About a year ago, everything shifted. I watched Dr. Jack Kruse's interview on the Danny Jones podcast, and it cracked open a Pandora's box. The past 12 months have been an intense, accelerated rewiring of my life.

Here's what's transformed:

-   My relationship with the sun: I used to avoid it (not from mainstream "sun is evil" dogma, just... hiding for whatever reason). Now? I've built a serious solar callus through rigorous, strategic exposure. My skin tells the story.
-   Natural light/dark cycles: Morning sunlight rituals, daytime exposure, and a dialed-in sleep schedule aligned with nature.
-   nnEMF awareness: Deep dive into non-native electromagnetic fields, researching, measuring, and mitigating RF, AC electric, and magnetic fields everywhere in my environment.
-   Food and eating: Less overall intake thanks to consistent UV exposure, almost entirely seasonal/local, and heavy on DHA-rich sources.
-   Mindset and psychology: Ruthlessly questioning old beliefs, rethinking my purpose, social interactions—and seeing how these physical changes have reshaped my inner world.

There are probably more shifts I haven't listed, but you get the idea.

How I learn and explore: Mostly through Dr. Jack Kruse's content (videos, interviews, podcasts, X posts, and now his blog posts, starting from the beginning and catching up). Plus independent research, books, and papers.

Books I've finished (planning to reread many annually, no particular order):

-   The Body Electric
-   Cross Currents
-   The Invisible Rainbow
-   Going Somewhere
-   Epi-Paleo Rx
-   Power, Sex, and Suicide
-   Electromagnetism & Life (currently reading)
-   Various EMF books on measurement, education, and mitigation
-   Some key papers by Allan Frey
-   Please feel free to suggest anything that's worth reading in your opinion that aligns with the topic

(Still hunting for an affordable copy of _Light in Shaping Life_ by Roeland van Wijk—open to tips!)

My aim?
Keep optimising my own life and share what works with those around me. That said, I know not everyone's ready for ideas that upend everything they've been told. When people resist hard, like they're drowning and trying to drag you down, sometimes you have to prioritize your own oxygen.

I'd love to connect with anyone on a similar path: sharing experiences, discussing ideas, brainstorming experiments, or just exploring these rabbit holes together.

Looking forward to the conversations!

### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-16T07:20:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363932>

Hey everyone,

Hope you're all doing well!

I'm dealing with severe flight anxiety that's really intense, I've got two long flights coming up in about 2 months (roughly 10 hours each), and I'm already having panic attacks just thinking about it.
Flying has always been a huge challenge for me in the past, even though I've managed it before.

I've tried lorazepam + propranolol, and it helps a bit but feels like it's just on the edge, not quite enough.
I'm thinking I might need something a bit stronger and longer-lasting to make it bearable.

Reaching out to see if anyone here faces similar issues and what meds (or combos) have worked for you to knock down the anxiety hard enough to get through longer flights.

Thanks so much in advance for any advice or stories!

### Jacks — 2025-12-16T20:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363948>

I went through a period of time when I also had a difficult time flying due to anxiety, especially on long flights. I used alcohol to numb it back then and watched movies continuously to mentally block out the fact I was in a metal tube miles above the ground without having control and being stuck in a tight seat with little personal space! I tried Kava tea a couple of times and that helped but that can have some side effects. I turned to breathing exercises that actually really helped me - such as box breathing etc. So that is what I would recommend. Also meditation practice helps. Drinking salty water I have found helps with anxiety too.

That phase of anxiety with flying appears to have passed for me now and I don't have issues any more. Now I just read a book and listen to music. I still have a difficult time sleeping on planes. I have found the newer Airbus (double decker) planes are a lot quieter and more comfortable than the 747s and I try to upgrade if possible to have more personal space.

### privacyjunkie — 2025-12-16T20:11:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gabriels-hidden-path-a-privacy-junkies-quest-for-optimal-living.32010/post-363950>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=363948)
>
> I went through a period of time when I also had a difficult time flying due to anxiety, especially on long flights. I used alcohol to numb it back then and watched movies continuously to mentally block out the fact I was in a metal tube miles above the ground without having control and being stuck in a tight seat with little personal space! I tried Kava tea a couple of times and that helped but that can have some side effects. I turned to breathing exercises that actually really helped me - such as box breathing etc. So that is what I would recommend. Also meditation practice helps. Drinking salty water I have found helps with anxiety too.
>
> That phase of anxiety with flying appears to have passed for me now and I don't have issues any more. Now I just read a book and listen to music. I still have a difficult time sleeping on planes. I have found the newer Airbus (double decker) planes are a lot quieter and more comfortable than the 747s and I try to upgrade if possible to have more personal space.

I appreciate your response Jacks. I do have a bit of balance issues, maybe some sort of vertigo, which amplifies everything. I am preparing heavily for this journey from the circadian perspective. Building a lot of melanin in my skin, very rigorous day/night routine, and a lot of grounding.
I also support this training/preparation with DHA rich foods and high quality structured spring water.

# Thread: Metal vs Bioabsorbable implants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-vs-bioabsorbable-implants.32080/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JAUS — 2026-01-04T06:28:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-vs-bioabsorbable-implants.32080/post-364630>

Hey All,
I’ll soon be undergoing ACL reconstruction which uses a tendon graft for the new ACL, secured in place by two fixation screws. I’ve heard Dr Kruse mention in previous podcasts that metal implants may have biological consequences relating to EMF and mitochondrial function. I’m unsure how much of that concern translates to a peripheral joint like the knee.
Metal interference screws are most commonly used but there is an option to use screws made from biodegradable polymers. I can't seem to find any blog posts about metallic implants, does anyone have any insight as to how these materials might differ from a mitochondrial or healing perspective?

Thanks in advance.

### Jacks — 2026-01-04T06:51:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-vs-bioabsorbable-implants.32080/post-364645>

I can't answer your question specifically, but I did have an ACL reconstruction about 35 years ago - same tendon graft method - and it has held up really well and continued most activities afterwards - but I still have metal screws in my bones where the graft is attached. I have considered having them removed due to not wanting metal in my body. I definitely notice that the ACL leg has more minor issues in general compared to my other one, but I'm still not convinced that having another surgery to remove them would outweigh the risk, at the moment. Perhaps in the future I may consider it. However, if I had to choose again, I would probably go for the biodegradeable material option, given the choice, and then I there is no issue with having metal screws in the body long term.

Also I have wondered recently if my knee would have been fine without the surgery. Hard to tell though, but recently (this year) I injured the same knee twice causing it to swell up massively and become slightly unstable (but no pain), but I just iced it and got it in the sun and the swelling and instability issues went away pretty quickly and was back to normal in no time and able to hike like normal. I know the current surgery method (orthoscopic) is probably better than what was done for me - I still have two long scars, but have you considered trying to heal it without surgery?

### flavin0 — 2026-01-07T05:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/metal-vs-bioabsorbable-implants.32080/post-364899>

Any object can be approximated as roughly a stick (a dipole) upon which electrons go back and forth. If the electrons go back and forth periodically (in a regularly repeating way like a sine wave) then you have an antenna. The length of this antenna as well as the resonant frequencies of the atoms/molecules comprising the screw dictate what electromagnetic waves will resonate. For example, an ideal antenna for FM radio frequencies is a perfect multiple of the FM radio wavelength. For example, a quarter wavelength antenna would be a metal rod that is about as long as a quarter of the wavelength of the desired wavelength.

If I had to speculate, the worst case would be having a literal conductive screw into your body, and it would basically amplify a rather specific range of the electromagnetic spectrum in your environment the same way the antenna in your smartphone hooks up to the rest of the phone. For the most part, metals are probably going to be more electromagnetically active than polymers which are basically plastic which is basically not conductive.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Craig’s Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craig%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.32097/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cberg — 2026-01-06T23:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craig%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.32097/post-364886>

Optimal Journal
Birth to 18
\-Born and raised in southern Oregon

Health problems
\-took Accutane for bad acne
\-at least 2 concussion playing football

Age 19-21

\-Lived in Maine and Vermont
Health Problems

\-I went from 200 to 250 at one point and got back down to 220 4 months after hitting highest weight.
\- No other health issues besides being fat during this time

Age 22-26
lived in southeastern Idaho for 4 years undergrad in Exercise Physiology

Health problems
\- Lost the weight, got back to 200 lbs but hurt back working out.
\-Had a couple flare ups of low back in the 4 years in Idaho but managed with chiropractor
\-Took 1 more round of Accutane

Age 27-32

Lived in Utah- Clinical Doctorate in Audiology

Health Problems

\-When first moved to Utah got a necrotizing pneumonia and was in hospital for 6 days, managed with antibiotics, no know issues since then
\-Started wearing contacts
\-Started having more back issues for about 1.5 years then ended up with herniated disc L5-S1 on the right side. Looking back I attribute part of this to watching a lot of shows on my laptop with it plugged in and it laying on my chest for sometimes up to 3 hours a night.
\-Dealt with back pain for 3 years with ibuprofen and chiropractor but was pretty miserable. All while doing class, doing clinic, and working as research assistant and TA. My down time once again was spent watching shows on laptop for multiple hours a night most nights.
\-Finally started losing feeling in right foot, so decided on surgery, laminectomy and discectomy. 4 months later had the same surgery after re herniating the same disc
\-Graduated about the time I healed up from second surgery.
\-Worked for 1 more year in Utah, with minimal back problems managed with occasional chiropractic visits

Age 32 to present
Moved back to Oregon- joined and became part owner of audiology practice

\-occasional back problems managed with chiropractic visits and cranial sacral massage.
\-Got married and had 2 kids (6 and 7 years old currently) and weight has fluctuated between 215 and 235. Currently about 220
\-Worst of all succumbed to the pressure and got 2 Pfizer covid jabs
\-About 2 years ago heard Jack in a podcast and have been making changes to lifestyle since

### cberg — 2026-01-12T22:48:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craig%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.32097/post-365222>

Jack, I was on the Pow Wow yesterday but did not realize I was signed in under wife's name and so Christy read my question since she thought I was not on the call. Anyways you answered part of my question but you froze and I could not hear the rest of your answer. My question was about DDW water for my 7 year old son with ADHD and ODD. I got the part were you said yes to DDW would/could help. I know getting him the sun is the best thing from you blog on ADHD. The part that cut was you talking about heme protein (my reading list for the week includes your recent blog series and others on heme protein).

Some history on my son. No known complications at birth. Did have a tonged tie that was not diagnosed for 2 months which lead to problems latching, breast feeding, and low weight. Was able to put on a lot weight once he was on formula after tongue tie was taken care of. Unfortunately had normal vaccine schedule for first two years of life then we stopped all vaccines. He has always been active and destructive since about 2 years of age. Since then he has constantly been getting into trouble at various thing (gymnastics, swim, ji jitsu, and school). We put him a private Catholic school and he barley made it through kindergarten and was ultimately kicked out of the school 2 months into 1st grade. Put him in another private school and got kicked out after 2.5 months even after reluctantly putting him on methylphenidate which helped some. My wife stopped working part time as a nurse to home school him but that was a disaster. This year we have him in public school ( not the best but homeschool was pretty terrible). Tried a few different meds with bad to slightly better results. I will say he does okay when medication is in his system. Without meds he making random noises, very aggressive, very defiant/stubborn and very sloppy work. With meds in system he is mostly focused and has really neat work. At school he still has to sit in desk away from everyone else but is at least not in as much trouble as previous years. I feel bad because he mentions his brain sometimes goes crazy and does not feel good.

Ultimate goal is to get him off meds but also allow him to function. Somethings we have already done is we had done:
\- Had Anthony with EMF safe to get readings and installed his device to turn off electricity in rooms while we sleep
\- Sold me Tesla to reduce EMF exposure
\-Keep Wifi off most of the day
\- Getting him outside more often
\- Changed diet to higher protein and fat diet. Cut out most processed carbs and food
\- Limited screen time

Things we are wanting try:
\-DDW
\-Getting to EL Salvador for about a month ( don't know if that would be long enough time help)

Anyone else have any suggestions on things we should implement or any of Jacks blogs that would be helpful?

# Thread: Matt's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-optimal-journal.32115/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MattB99 — 2026-01-10T01:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matts-optimal-journal.32115/post-365070>

Hello. I am so happy to have found Uncle Jack's online resources and am looking forward to connecting with this community. I wish I could have found this information sooner (beginning to understand why I didn't). I've been listening obsessively to Uncle Jack's podcasts on youtube for the last few months and am now cramming as much blog and webinar content into my cranium as my limited bandwidth and comprehension will allow.

**Location**
I live in south eastern British Columbia in a town called Kimberley. It is a small town, about 8,000 people, at relatively high elevation, ~1,100m (3,650ft) near the 49th lattitude. It is on the edge the tectonic plate west of the Rocky Mountains. There are probaly pros and cons to living here. Look forward to hearing your thoughts. Mentally preparing for exile.
PROS:
\- hotsprings
\- low population density
\- hot springs
\- good spring water near my house
\- high altitude (potential UVA in winter)
CONS:
\- living near a mine site (geopathic stress? / X factors)
\- high altitude
\- high latitude
\- far from ocean
\- Canadian politics / brainwashed population

**Maternal History**
My maternal grandmother was a nurse. She lived until her early 80s and died of cancer. My mother was also a nurse. My understanding was she had difficulty having children before I was born - 2 miscarriages that I am aware of. I was the first born, by cesarian weighing almost 10 pounds. I was born with a lymphatic defect in my neck which required surgery at 13 months. I have one sister who was also born by cesarian, also large. She had no congenital defects. I had one other sister who was born with an enlarged heart. She passed away at 13 months.

**Health History**
My neck surgery damaged some nerves in my neck causing some muscle atrophy and scoliosis. The death of my sister was emotionally traumatic to me and my family. I have had anxiety as long as I can remember. Luckily my family was outdoor oriented. We did lot of outdoor activities when I was a kid which probably compensated somewhat for my compromised maternal epigenetics and the trauma of losing a sister when I was 5.
I had my first episode of major depression half way through my university career in 2009. I went to the doctor and started taking some depression/anxiety meds. I was able to return to university and complete my degree but relapsed again shortly after. I moved home after my relapse, recovered a bit, started working again and then relapsed again with a major suicidal obsession. I admited myselft to the hospital for a month. I was assigned a psychiatrist and took every on and off label pharmaceutical he could think of. I was then diagnosed with dythymia or treatment resistant depression. I eventually gave up on doctors and prescriptions and went down the naturpathic/functional medicine path without much sucess. I came out of my major depression but have been slogging along in an apathetic, anhedonic, low-agency state ever since. I have had a lot of cavities as well over the past 10 years.

**Current Health Challenges**
Persistant depression
Cavity/tooth infection
Tinnitus (started recently after tooth infection)

**Current Rx**
1hr walk at daybreak until after sunrise in the nearby forest.
nnEMF search and destroy mission: Hard wiring internet devices Trifield2 meter -> kill MAG/ELEC/RF. Swapping out all LEDs for incandesants.
BAB: ~100g Salmon with eggs and animal protein for breakfast. Butter in coffee.
Cold baths, 55F for 5 mins. Working up from here.
Ketogenic, local, seasonal diet as much as possible.
Walk at sunset.
Regular outside breaks during the day.
Lights out after dark as soon as possible. Need to black out my bedroom windows to block LED street lamps and neighbours' lights. Currently using a sleep mask.
Buying BTC

**Next Health Focused Actions**
Working on finding friends. Have lost most of them and not initiated any new freindships over the years due to depression, apathy, low dopamine.
Saving up for magnetico matress.
Saving up for tropical vacation.
Saving up for EMF consult.
Looking for outdoor low nnEMF work enviroment.

# Thread: Cold and Companionship
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-companionship.32171/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2026-01-22T00:27:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-and-companionship.32171/post-365704>

Yesterday, January 20, I did 3 "floats" in California's cold Pacific Ocean. The sea life is prolific here in Malibu. I will swim out a bit and then just float with my head down for 20 minutes observing a sea of fish--huge schools of tiny fish, with clusters of larger fish below, along with the usual sitings of giant sea bass, leopard sharks, rays, halibut and sheepshead. Occasionally I will dive down deeper to see the lobsters and geribaldis hiding under the rock shelves.

When I go with my husband we do more vigorous swims. His presence stimulates higher levels of activation in me. He loves to workout. He says he needs to swim fast to build his body heat because he doesn't have the fat layer like I have. Its easy for me to keep pace with him because I float like an otter probably in part because of eating like a polar bear--starting with a filet of salmon (raw/sushi grade) just after sunrise. Before I started doing this the 30 minutes in the cold would stress me out. But now I digest for about an hour and then when we do our morning swims I am more relaxed. I feel as if I have an inner furnace running at a slow burn inside me and my husband can swim as long as he wants and I will be fine at his side.

The companionship is the best part. Every swim is an adventure and we try to find places that we can swim without the drag of swimwear and so the fascia can move unrestricted. We eat at different times of day but also I haven't missed a sunrise in 8 years. Then I couldn't digest fat but now my it seems my CCO furnace thrives on fat.

In the past he has typically missed the sunrise, skipped breakfast and stayed up later till around 11:00 winding down the day on the dopamine rush of the screen with a bowl of ice cream. But when I returned from Costa Rica with MRSA I just told him I needed a companion to live and I wanted it to be him. He carved out time for intimacy pre dawn and now the past 5 months he hasn't missed a sunrise either. So he is charging up in the morning with his dopamine now (plus endorphins, oxytocin...). He naturally is going to bed earlier, sometimes even with me. Maybe one day he'll even have breakfast with me.

But it's the companionship that is healing me. It's second to my light environment, albeit the high nnEMF/flouride/chem trail environment of California. My focus is on my patterns within this environment. (My choices: nature's rhythms in good environment without my companion and family or a avatar patterns in a challenging environment with my companion and family). Hopefully one day we will be able to spend more time together in the stronger red light of Costa Rica but for now his work is the here and I need to be with him. (I went off the chlorine dioxide and just keep it on hand in case of emergencies.)

For years now, whether I am in Costa Rica or here in Malibu I am outside all day, minimally clothed, unless it's too blustery or raining. In both places I have an outdoor area on the grass that measures low emfs on my meters. But with the typical clouds from the marine layer in Malibu it's much colder. Every day I feel more adapted to the cold. I love living outside and cringe when I have to go into altered light environments. Although our evening lights are incandescent, and I am hardly ever under blue light, I usually retire early after a few family games and good conversation. I sleep in a room with blackout curtains and an emf canopy, and drink deuterium depleted water. I'm thankful to be able to do this.

# Thread: Consistent mG rise toward midday (EMF-390)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### urko — 2026-02-03T17:00:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366292>

Hi everyone, I wanted to share some magnetic field measurements I’ve been taking with a **GQ EMF-390**, since I’m seeing a really consistent pattern depending on the time of day.

I’m located in **The Gambia**, around **13°N latitude**, in a coastal area. I’m mentioning this in case latitude, local grid load patterns, or regional infrastructure makes any difference when interpreting the daily changes I’m seeing in the magnetic field readings.

## Measurements in the same spot (same location, different times)​

All readings below were taken **in the exact same place**, just at different times:

| Time |
Reading (mG)​

 |
| --- | --- |
| 07:38 |

0.7–0.8​

 |
| 09:29 |

0.9​

 |
| 10:51 |

1.5​

 |
| 12:58 |

2.0 (highest)​

 |
| 19:23 |

1.4​

 |

What surprised me is that **the next morning I measured again and got basically the same results at roughly the same times**, with the same “ramp up” toward midday.

## Beach measurement (no obvious AC nearby, some RF present)​

I also took the meter to the **beach**, where there’s **no obvious nearby AC/power infrastructure**, but there is some **RF** around (not especially strong). At **16:30** it was reading **2.0 mG** there as well.

## What I’m trying to figure out​

-   Does it make sense for **magnetic field (mG)** levels to climb steadily toward midday and then drop later, like this?
-   Any ideas what could explain **2.0 mG at the beach** if there’s no obvious electrical infrastructure nearby?
-   For people who use this meter (or similar): do you prefer **peak vs. average**, and is it better to do a longer continuous log to see whether these are brief spikes vs. a sustained level?

If it helps, I can share more details about how I measured (height off the ground, distance to walls/outlets, whether my phone was nearby, etc.).

I attach some pictures of the measurements.

* * *

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-03T20:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366312>

I believe the GQ is a single axis meter for magnetic . . . So you have to turn the meter on all 3 axis and then take the highest reading. Once you find the highest reading position put the meter down, what does it read when you are not touching it? Physical contact with a meter can sometimes affect readings significantly.

Outside humidity can affect electric and magnetic field readings also.
One morning (misty humid conditions) I got high electric and magnetic field readings upto 100ft from a power line.
That afternoon (air was now dry) I had to get within 20ft before I got a reading.

Average reading is meaningless. Peak is the important one.

### urko — 2026-02-03T21:13:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366315>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=366312)
>
> I believe the GQ is a single axis meter for magnetic . . . So you have to turn the meter on all 3 axis and then take the highest reading. Once you find the highest reading position put the meter down, what does it read when you are not touching it? Physical contact with a meter can sometimes affect readings significantly.
>
> Outside humidity can affect electric and magnetic field readings also.
> One morning (misty humid conditions) I got high electric and magnetic field readings upto 100ft from a power line.
> That afternoon (air was now dry) I had to get within 20ft before I got a reading.
>
> Average reading is meaningless. Peak is the important one.

I have a picture where I left it on the towel with no human interaction. Here we are on the dry season and at the morning is the moment where the moisture is the highest and the lowest reading. I can tell you that there are no peaks from the measurements. I'm wondering why the EMF is higher at daytime and going low to night time when the moisture is higher.

We believe there is something happening here that local people are doing like generators, or some military stuff. there are military here and 3 RF antennas on the village which is 2.5 km far away from us.

There is also geoengineering like cloud HAARP patterns but we have not seen any flight spraying, but could be boats doing stuff too...

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-04T03:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366336>

RF antennas will affect the RF readings . . . NOT the magnetic.
When you say “the EMF is higher at daytime” that does not help.
EMF is electric and/or magnetic and/or RF. They are all different, you need to be specific.
Magnetic is normally VERY local (within feet) but I have No idea what affect HAARP would have at distance.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-04T03:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consistent-mg-rise-toward-midday-emf-390.32222/post-366337>

Mag fields are governed by Ampres law.. Your patterns observed are due to demand (i.e. usage peaks). No mystery here.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal-va-living-wife-and-two-kids.32236/post-366497>

I am starting an optimal health journal after speaking with Dr. Kruse. I’m very thankful for the conversation [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) as it really shifted my perspective and immediately pointed me in the right direction and got me to stop asking questions that don’t matter. I genuinely had never even considered moving, but now that is all I can think about. Upon further investigation and speaking with my wife, we had decided on three states: Florida, Alabama, or Georgia. With me being a contractor, I did further research on the three states, and although Florida was our prime choice, roofing contractors don’t do great there because of crappy insurance policies due to all the storms and apparently a lot of fraud. I actually worked for a roofing company a few years ago that had to close their office in Florida because of the difficulty there. We have decided on Alabama and we are making a plan within the next 2 to 3 years to head down there and I am wondering if near somewhere south of Montgomery Alabama is a safe place to live? We are wanting to do somewhere near Montgomery because of the available businesses and church proximity. I am spending a lot of money on EMF proofing our house as our new baby was born yesterday. I will come back with questions and concerns during this time but one question I had was how much deuterium depleted water should I be giving my family? I purchased 10ppm.

She mentioned Orange Beach AL, Tybee Island GA and Savannah GA. The Texas coast in our opinion is not great. # Thread: Gregg's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grclark_w — 2026-02-13T13:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/post-366816>

My name is Gregg. I have been here for about 8 months, and it is time to start my journal.

I’m a 53-year-old male, living at 38 ° latitude in Kansas for most of my life. I am a Fitzpatrick Skin-Type II. I am awaiting my haplotype results and will post as soon as I receive them.

I very much resonate with Jack’s work. I like his attitude. I like his approach. I am amazed by his knowledge. I have worked with semiconductors my whole career, so his work naturally aligns with how I think.

**Family History –** I was close to all my family growing up, and have remained close as an adult.

**Maternal

Great-Grandfather**

Died at 103, mostly due to old age

**Grandmother**

Died of cancer in 2006 at 79 years of age

**Grandfather**

Died at 85 in 2008. I would say mostly due to the loss of a lifelong spouse, he just decided he was done.

**Mother**

Diagnosed with Krones disease when she was 35 years old, but still alive and doing well at the age of 75.

**Paternal History

Grandmother**

Passed away at the age of 90, mostly due to old age.

**Grandfather**

Died due to diabetes complications at 88 years of age

**Father**

Alive and kicking and going strong at the age of 76

**Brief History**
I grew up in a rural area in eastern Kansas. As a kid, I spent almost all my time outside. Once I went to college and started my career, I was inside most of the time. I got married when I was 21. We had our first child immediately. We had a second child four years later. Upon graduating from college, we moved to Austin, Texas, for six years. Then we moved back to eastern Kansas and have been here ever since.

In 2001, I started my own business designing reactor protection systems for commercial nuclear power plants. I sold my first business in 2009. Then, in 2010, I had the opportunity to design reactor protection systems for the new small modular reactors. So, I started another company and ultimately sold it in 2021.

**Past 6 years**

We started experimenting with low-carb diets in the 2000s. I think I dove into the low-carb/keto in 2015/2016, then got into intermittent fasting with a 6/18 window. I did that for a couple of years, probably from 2017 to 2020. I also started lifting less and riding a bike more. I got into “gravel grinding”, riding on the gravel roads around Coffey County. I then started riding my bike with a trainer in the winter months, using Zwift. In late 2019 and early 2020, I was riding hard. I would push myself very hard for 60-90 minutes doing ZWIFT races. Then, in February of 2020, I had a sort of catastrophic moment. Here is the write-up I sent to our naturopathic physician. This was the new patient write-up I provided to him.

“I have been having a lot of various ongoing symptoms that I cannot figure out the cause of. It started Sunday, 2/23/2020. I had been awake early and did an intense ride. Afterwards, I had a very bad dizzy, lightheaded spell, we called 911, and by the time the first responders got here, I had a Blood Pressure of 160/120. They took me to the hospital and monitored me. My blood pressure came back down to the 120/70 range after being in the hospital for 4-5 hours. I stayed the night and went home the next morning. I felt pretty good the rest of that week. They performed a nuclear stress test on Friday, 2/28, and all results looked very good. Then, on the following Saturday, 2/29, around 6 PM, I experienced an adrenaline rush that lasted just over an hour. It is worth noting that, in the lead-up to the Nuclear Stress Test, I stopped drinking coffee on Tuesday, 2/25. I had more adrenaline rushes over the next week. I resumed drinking coffee on Wednesday, 3/4, and slept reasonably well by Thursday evening, 3/5. Before that, I would get an adrenaline rush Episode when I was trying to go to sleep. When I had the adrenaline rush episodes, my BP would spike to the 160/120 range and then subside back down to the 130-140/85-80 range. Since coffee resumed, I have not had any adrenaline rushes.

I spent the rest of 2020 and 2021 being very careful with myself. I reduced my training intensity, cut back on biking, and lifted only in short superset sessions to maintain some muscle.

At the same time, from 2017 to 2021, my business was growing rapidly. I would push myself hard to make everything possible for the business.

In 2020/2021, I pretty much went on a fresh-foods diet. Lots of fruit, vegetables, and meat, no alcohol.

In December 2021, I sold my business. This was a big emotional moment: on the one hand, lots of relief; on the other, lots of unknowns about the future. We enjoyed the holidays that year. I was more relaxed than I had been in years. After the holidays, it hit me… I don’t want to be an employee for this company that just bought me out. I realized how much I hate working for someone. Then, at the end of January, my right ear started ringing and kept ringing for the rest of that year. In the spring of 2022, we decided to buy a lovely house in the Texas Hill Country, outside New Braunfels, TX, as a second home. We spent the early part of 2022 looking for a house and bought one in April 2022. We spent most of 2022 down there, but my ear ringing kept getting worse. My overall health fell off a cliff in the fall of 2022, so much so that I was only getting 1-3 hours of poor sleep a night. There were months that I felt like someone had pumped me full of medium-strength adrenaline; I just could not relax and could not sleep. I finally began to extricate myself from this difficult situation in February 2023. I began to improve by the summer of 2023 and enjoyed that summer. However, my right ear still rings significantly to this day. I also got a weird, full-head feeling headache off and on for most of 2022. It was not a sinus-type headache; it was an overall pressure in the head. And to this day, I have a “regular” schedule of 2 days or so of feeling low energy, and ear ringing goes up. Then I will feel good for 3-5 days, with lots of energy and the ringing way down. It’s like something clicks on and off within me on a 4 good/2 bad sort of schedule.

**Past year**

I have been under a lot of stress again this past year. My wife of 32 years was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer in April 2025, and I lost her in late September 2025. I put my health journey on hold and focused on her. Knowing what I know now, if we could go back 2 years, she would still be here…

Now it is time for me to focus on myself, essentially for the first time since before I got married and had kids.

### grclark_w — 2026-02-14T16:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greggs-journal.32256/post-366881>

I am going to start adding detail to my journal with observations I have made about myself and my "condition," as well as thoughts I have that tie into the multitude of material I have been reading from Dr. Kruse. I will also list the actions I have taken.

**Observations:**

1) When I have my 2 days or so of bad, I experience the increased ringing amplitude first, followed by loss of energy, and eventually, I will get the feeling of inflammation in the right ear area. It is typically in this order. When the feeling of inflammation comes on, I will notice that I get "popping" noises in that area, which seem to be pressure relief events. I can also press on areas around my ear to vary the frequency and amplitude. This leads me to believe that the pressure of the surrounding areas is playing a major role.

2) I bought a darkfield microscope a couple of years ago. I haven't used darkfield much, but I have used normal mode to view my live blood. What I notice on the "bad" days is a rouleaux formation of the blood. I think this implies a low Zeta Potential, or the inability of the RBCs to repel one another.

3) Another tie into this, not surprisingly, is mild sleep apnea. I have noticed that when I fall asleep on the couch, I often open my mouth and breathe deeply. This then causes an almost instant increase in the ringing amplitude.

**Thoughts:**

1) I am somewhat all over the map with my thoughts. I have read a lot of Dr. Kruse's work, and much of it I can relate to directly. I am just trying to distill this information down into a logical collection.

2) Hypoxia is a key to this. Reading Hypoxia #22 reveals many aspects to me. Then I read CPC #49, and I think about Tinnitus as a sensory processing disorder... I just don't think that is all that I have going on. Hypoxia, at large, seems to tie into everything I feel on this persistent, periodic basis.

3) Given the persistence and periodicity of my "condition", there must be a life cycle going on. I say this because I have altered everything I can think of, and the cycle persists. For me, this leads me to my gut and makes me think of a podcast where Dr. Kruse talked about a normal gut turning over every 24-48 hours and a "bad" gut turning over many days. In my case, 7-8 days.

4) Dehydrated melanin? Leads me to thoughts on deuterium. I have been consuming DDW by mixing a 10PPM DDW solution with Icelandic water to achieve 90-100PPM DDW. The cycle still persists, regardless of drinking primarily DDW or not.

**Actions:**

1) I have watched sunrises consistently since I started following Dr. Kruse. My typical routine is to go out to my yard (I live in a rural area with no close neighbors) and watch the sunrise naked, like the Spinx, while doing planks and such.

2) I have increased my consumption of DHA from seafood significantly.

3) I am outside again almost all day. No pressures of making a living for me, so I get to choose what I do with my time.

4) I sleep in the dark; my house has only incandescent lighting. I go to dark mode when the sun sets, with only a little deep red turned on.

5) I use a Daylight tablet now and blue-blocking glasses plus Iris when using my normal computer screen.

6) DDW consumption as mentioned above

7) I raise and consume my own grass finished needle free beef. I can't get a much better sunlight bar code than that

8) Trying to eat seasonal and local as much as possible

9) No sunglasses for several years now

10) Minimize my exposure to nnEMF, no wifi in the home, all hardwired. Minimize my use of the mobile phone, never have it to my head. Absolutely no Bluetooth shit. (Interesting side note: I was one of the leads on a team at Motorola Semiconductor that implemented the first functional Bluetooth radio.) I was mostly involved in the baseband and software stack development, so I was not around all the intense RF side development.)

# Thread: TROPICS ALL DAY EXPERIMENT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropics-all-day-experiment.32267/>

### Wippe — 2026-02-17T21:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropics-all-day-experiment.32267/post-367030>

What's up mito gang!

It's your boy from 62N latitude Finland, but now been inside the twenties for 5 months straight. First time in my life in the tropics.

All the people still arguing Q&A after Q&A with Uncle Jack about mitohacking their hellhole high latitude environment, this one is for you guys. And obviously it was Dr. Kruse who planted the seed for all of this so can't thank you enough! But now, little backstory before we get to the good stuff..

Since my teenage years (I'm 32 now) I was always searching for ways to feel better because I was pretty high level athlete and wanted to perform better. That lead me to all the wrong places like it does for many young people on social media still lacking big time on their knowledge and first principles thinking + being blue light toxic + no mentors/teachers who could have pulled me out of my mistakes faster. Now I understand those wrong places where put there by design by the Rockefeller medicine & MKULTRA programs. I was 4 years high carb vegan in Finland while training very hard almost daily in blue lit gyms 90% of the time. Very very bad mistake young bucko. This 4 year period depleted my body completely and my redox was worse than dogshit when I realized I had made a huge mistake with my diet. I changed to very animal based diet which suited Finland pretty well and started to immediately feel better and gain my strength back. I think I gained about 8kg of mass in less than a month. I kept getting better and better pretty fast, but I still felt like I had lost something in me permanently with that 4 year massive mistake. I could train very hard again, although my body felt more brittle now. So I could make decent progress if I was smart how I trained. Still training indoors under blue lights 80-90% of the year. Circadian rhythm getting worse and worse as I noticed I needed more & more sleep to be able to recover from my hard training sessions. Life felt pretty allright at this time. There were whispers but they were not very loud so I did not pay too much attention to them. This was before 5G & COVID BS started to roll in in Finland.

Suddenly started to look how I could feel & perform better again. Tried this & that supplement. Tried this & that wacky diet protocol again. The wrong places, they were there waiting on my ass. I could still train quite hard and could make progress but things didn't feel right at all anymore. I needed so much sleep and pretty much was completely useless all the other times of the day expect when I started to pump myself full of caffeine to get ready for my daily training session. My circadian rhythm was atrocious during this time. Going to sleep 2-3am and waking up sometimes 1-2pm. This went on for 1-2 years. Life was losing it's magic slowly but surely. The whispers were getting pretty fucking loud at this time. Then, I tore both of my hamstrings at the same time doing pretty heavy deadlift. Same day, one important relationship in my life ended. That day was the punch in the mouth my ass desperately needed. Next few months were the darkest time of my life. Only time in my life I have felt completely empty inside. I started to spend most of my time outside in nature. Stopped training. Stopped to care about many other superfluous things. Started to look for answers in new places (there were not many more places left to be honest). I really didn't put two and two together before I got into Uncle Jack's work heavy late 2024. On surface level I knew most of the stuff about 5G & nnEMF's, but did I do anything about it? No (expect when I intuitively started to hang out with Mother Earth a lot more again). The light, water & magnetism stuff was new to me and blowing my mind one podcast at a time. It was February 2025 in Finland when we got first nice sunny day, still cold af but I was naked on a frozen lake ice fishing while listening Uncle Jack the whole day. I started to really see how the pieces fit together in a completely new way. The food guru paradigm was being destroyed big time in my mind.

That summer was the best summer in years for me in Finland. Spend most of it in the family cottage my grandfather build on a lake. No wifis, no 5G, can turn the electricity off when I want to, middle of a forest, on a lake with food fire old school Finnish sauna. Thanks grandpa, you were the best. During the summer became a member for the first time to get access to all the Q&A's, old webinar and blogs. Still going back with them almost daily (now on January 2019 webinars/Q&A's). Although I felt pretty good compared to how I had been feeling last couple of years, I knew there was a lot more to be had. The magic hadn't come back full on yet and I knew it. I knew I had to go experience the power of tropics for the first time in my life that upcoming winter...

And next time we are going to get to the tropics and little spoiler alert, Uncle Jack ain't joking around when he says tropics are where the true healing is at

Stay juicy

### Wippe — 2026-02-20T21:18:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropics-all-day-experiment.32267/post-367247>

**The Power Of The Tropics - Costa Rica Chronicles 1**

After eye bleeding (did not have blue blockers), tinnitus inducing but fairly manageable 24h travel from Finland to San Jose Costa Rica, first thing I noticed when I popped out of the airport was "this place smells funky af". The sun had just set when I got out so I just wanted to get to my AirBnB, try to get some rest and be ready at the sunrise to start my experiment.

I don't think I got any sleep that night. The air travel, massive circadian mismatch and the excitement what kind of new world was waiting for me tomorrow kept me tossing & turning the whole night. I was up 5am ready to get this party started. Walked to the bus stop while soaking in my first sunrise this close to the Creator and was amazed by every little new thing I was encountering. The plant life, bunch of birds I have never seen before, lizards, the crazy electrical wiring in the neighborhoods, all these little family shops and breakfast places that were opening up, the crazy traffic.. I kinda loved it all already. Everything felt way more lively & chaotic in a good way compared to Finland where everybody just feels like robots acting out their programs these days. When the street light turns red but there are no cars to be seen, most Finnish people still wait to the light to turn green again. Not in San Jose Costa Rica. Everybody kinda had their own thing going on from the vehicles they drive to the way they act & be. Everything was very full on already at 6am.

My bus came 6.30am and I was already surprised how strong the sun felt at that time. Felt like there was electricity in the air. The bus rider was this good looking happy-go-lucky dude, blasting local music on max volume the whole time and he seemed to know everybody. The driving was super chaotic and I loved it. Everything felt so lively.

I started to lay my eyes to the absolutely stunning landscape of Costa Rica for the first time. The mountains, the humongous tropical trees, the canyons. The ferry ride from Puntarenas to Nicoya Peninsula was the moment when I really felt the tropical sunlight for the first time. Totally new level of power. I saw massive tortoise on the way and when the ferry got closer to Nicoya it felt like I was entering into Jurassic Park. Now I was truly getting in the jungle for the first time. Remember being little emotional at that time as I was just so amazed how powerful the nature felt to me and how beautiful it was there.

The bus ride from the ferry port to Santa Teresa was a memorable one. I had never seen nature like this before. Kept constantly thinking "this looks like nature on steroids". There was also a lot of animals everywhere. Cows, chickens, dogs, birds.. loved to see that. But when it really hit me like a ton of bricks is when I walked thru the jungle and finally had my feet touching the white sand beach, eyes locked in on the crystal blue waves of the Pacific Ocean.

Those beaches at Santa Teresa were absolute perfection. So grandiose, so pristine, picture perfect, I felt like I had entered heaven. On top of that the sun was now beaming at it's full 9th north latitude power and everything in my being was yelling "YES!". Life was about to be a whole lot different from now on...

### Wippe — 2026-02-24T21:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropics-all-day-experiment.32267/post-367592>

**The Power Of The Tropics - Costa Rica Chronicles 2**

Looking back, Santa Teresa was the perfect place to start my tropics all day experiment. There are quite a few American expats and Israelis (o ou) who have brought money & infrastructure to the area so you have your fancy waters and other western world conveniences available in very well stocked supermarkets & health food stores, but it's still pretty low key naturally pristine spot with low population density especially during the off season (May - October). If I just walked a bit north from the main beach, I pretty much had my own massive private picture perfect tropical paradise beach where I could make love to the Creator Adam & Eve style. That felt so damn good. And going south from the main beach to Mal Pais, on low tide, there are these natural pools that form on the rocks that were absolutely amazing for gathering free electrons, ground and enjoy amazing jungle views under the tropical sun.

One time this group of middle aged Israeli women came to the same pool as I was enjoying the morning, one of them said "don't be bothered by us, we are going to do a ritual here, but you can be at peace right there", then got here titties out and they started their ritual of some kind. Didn't really understand what the ritual was about but doing it the ladies out must have made it more effective.

Other time I was enjoying the sunrise at the beach, these two middle aged American marketers came to talk to me. First thing he says to me with very intensive eye contact "How do you feel?", which I responded "Pretty damn great. Enjoying the sunrise, the most magical time of the day". Then we started to talk about sun/health in general and I started to drop some Black Swan wisdom to these guys and the other guy was repeating "human photosynthesis.. that's what it's all about!". The other guy with very intense energy started to also take pictures of me and my eyes saying "you have such a beautiful blue eyes". They had this usual corporate marketer type energy trying make everything into some kind of profitable miraculous product which I hate but it was still very fun & weird experience sharing the power of sun to these guys. At the end they were all about it and wanted to get a consult from me the following day but I wasn't really feeling to take any money from these guys. I just said start doing the sunrise & sunset everyday, get good local food made by photosynthesis, be outside all day with as little clothes as possible, "you guys already are in absolutely magical spot so take full advantage of it". Don't think they fully got it but it was a new kind way to spend sunrise for sure.

Last days there I also found the local fisherman and got some fresh FRESH yellowfin tuna for 8-9$/kg which was pretty amazing deal I thought.. DHA UP!

It was super easy to get into perfect daily rhythm there due the time zone change, waking up when it started to get bright outside (10-20 minutes before sunrise) and getting to bed very soon after it got dark. I didn't experience any jet lag really. I was very surprised how quickly the sun got strong there. Already at 7.30-8am the sun felt stronger than ever in Finland. I had very nice tan going on already from Finland and my skin could handle the sun pretty well I thought. I did peel massively from my back on day 3 and 4 + got bunch of white spots = deuterium dumping all the stuff my body couldn't in Finland. After that I have not peeled at all from my back, just gotten deeper and deeper tan.

I felt I was on cloud 9 the whole first week. Saw white faced monkeys, howler monkeys, all kinds of birds, massive iguanas, fireflies when the sun started to go down, absolute stunner sunsets to the Pacific ocean, spectacular lightning shows when it started to get dark, the food was amazing, met cool people.. **pura vida** was so strong. This was supposed to be the rainiest time of the year there but it did not pour rain once during the day in the time while I was there. In Finland I could be in the sun literally the whole time it was out so basically 15-16h a day and still feel I wanted more.. In Santa Teresa few hours in the morning, little sun breaks in the middle of the day and then again couple hours leading to the sunset and that felt more than enough sun for me. The abundance of life force was so cool. There are few towers there but they are pretty few and far between. Also like I pointed out, this time of the year the population density is not an issue. Great waves for surfing if you are into that, which means not the best beach for swimming.

I was off to fantastic start to my experiment!

### Wippe — 2026-02-27T23:39:51+0200

### Wippe — 2026-03-01T22:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropics-all-day-experiment.32267/post-367883>

**The Power Of The Tropics - Costa Rica Chronicles 3**

After what already felt like life-changing week one, I started my journey to even more lowkey location at the tip of the Nicoya Peninsula called Cabuya. I decided to walk there from Santa Teresa thru a rather mountainous small dirt road thru the jungle. It was about 8km journey and I started it straight away after finishing my raw tuna sashimi breakfast at 6am so it wouldn't be so hot & humid, but like I said already, it gets hot & humid quickly here. I was also carrying about 20kg of my travel belongings. It didn't took long before my bags where completely drenched in sweat. I can't remember the last time I had sweated that much without the help of a sauna. Some of the long rather steep hills really got my heart pounding out of my chest.

Most of the journey I was there all by myself in the middle of a massive jungle. There were few really cool streams coming from the mountains along the way that I stopped by to immerse my sweaty self into the cold water to cool down and refresh a bit. I felt so alive. The journey took me about 4-5 hours in total and even thou it did get fairly tough at times, I was loving every second of it.

Once I got to Cabuya, I still had couple hours to kill before I could check into my AirBnB so I made my way to this highly rated coffee/chocolate shop run by this amazing Italian guy who had moved to Costa Rica 15-20 years ago. On my way there I already noticed huge difference from Santa Teresa.. No more supermarkets or American gringos driving their ATV's from the beach to their mansions on the hills. Only extremely friendly local people living in their modest homes in this absolutely spectacular land where everything grows middle of jungle mountains and pacific ocean.

When I got to the coffee shop that was also in the middle of the jungle (everything there is), absolutely drenched in sweat, first thing they did was give me free samples of some of the chocolate they have and glass of cold water. In my state of exhaustion & euphoria is tasted like the best chocolate I have ever had and the cold water was hitting all the right spots. Then I ordered very strong chocolate milk where they use 90% dark chocolate with a cup of coffee that was also infused with the same chocolate. When my drinks arrived, two scarlet macaws just landed in the massive three I was sitting under. This was the first time I had seen these glorious birds in the wild. I started to sip my hot drinks and they brought me a small piece of cake they had just baked, also free of charge. The hot chocolate was out of this world good as was the coffee/chocolate drink. The best I have ever had. The exhaustion started to shift into pure state of bliss. What a life I thought.

With a bit more pep in my step again and brain completely in the state of bliss from the chocolate/coffee/whole experience, I climbed one more steep hill into my cabin which was surrounded by the lush rainforest. There were a gang of howler monkeys welcoming me into the property. They were eating jocote fruit in the trees. I also later tried the jocote fruit for the first time and it was delicious mix of sweet, tard & juicy. Reminded me of mango mixed with a tard cherry. The property had a small stream with a waterfall on it so I hopped into the cold water and felt immense connection with everything. The magic had returned big time into my being. This was the best I had felt in a long time and that feeling became the theme for my week long stay in Cabuya (and for the whole experiment thus far spoiler alert)...

The next day I tried to find the local fisherman for that DHA and stumbled upon a local drunk who was actually a really nice guy who could speak little English. He took me to the fisherman's house and introduced me to him. I bought the drunk small drink for helping me out, he was very happy lol. The seafood turned out to be some of the best seafood I have ever had. The tuna, marlin and red snapper were all fantastic and they had octopus too that I sadly didn't get a chance to try. Their prices were killer as well, 7-10$/kg depending on the fish.

Everybody in the town were always smiling and came to say hello or "PURA VIDA!" as they say in Costa Rica to me. Everybody had animals and fruit trees on their property. Dogs, chickens, ducks, cows, goats, pigs, cats everywhere. Least amount of cellphones I had seen in years. Children happy playing in the streets, rivers and beaches. No cell towers.

One day I was walking and saw two macaws flying just over my head while there was a older gentleman approaching me with a machete on one hand, bag of limes in the other. He came to say pura vida and started to ask questions in Spanish that I did not understand. He didn't understand any English so we could only communicate some very basic things but he had very friendly and wise presence. After few minutes we bumped fists and he gave me the whole bag full of limes. I was surprised how welcoming and nice everybody were. I felt like I had gone back in time few decades there.

I explored the national park there which was the oldest one in Costa Rica. It had 5km trail that goes thru the jungle that leads to this very excluded beach. One day I hitchhiked to Montezuma to visit the waterfall and the beaches. Everyday I did CT in the little waterfall. I tried noni fruit which some people say is the worst tasting fruit in the world and I would have to agree. Smells like blue cheese but tastes like spicy ass. I saw grasshoppers and spiders the size of my hand there. Massive bullet ants.

One time I went under the jocote trees where the howler monkeys were once again enjoying the fruits and started to pound my chest to them. The whole tribe started to howl at me extremely loudly and I got massive chills. Super fun. This was the most magical week I have had in a long time. Everyday I couldn't wait to get out of my bed to see the sunrise and see what the day brings. Completely at peace. The world build by the money masters & technocrats seemed so distant there.

Two weeks in Costa Rica had already done more for me than any other health experiment I had ever done in Finland. Only thing comparable was the times I was really falling in love with girl. The needle had moved big time. Cabuya was pure magic.

### GaryB — 2026-02-19T14:11:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garys-journal-journey-2026-and-beyond.32278/post-367142>

I was introduced to Uncle Jack (Dr. John Kruse) at the end of 2025 by a friend simply tracking who Satoshi Nakamoto was.

I'm not going to go back in history. I will start from today.

Wake up, take DHA/EPA supplement.

Today's sunrise was at Mermaid beach in Nakagusuku village in Okinawa, Japan. I've been there every morning this year but only since the begining of February I started walking into the water to watch the sunrise and I've learned something.

Like this morning, when I stand with my bare feet in the sand, dead coral and sea weed ticking my feet, as usual before the sun rises here come the birds. The cranes and the ducks flying by immediately before sunrise and this morning at 0701 more and more birds flying between my perfect view of the rising sun exactly as it rises. I've watched many sunrises and sunsets, but this year is different - I follow Uncle Jack's advice.

Thirty minutes pass - I'm with a friend who I've started dragging to the beach to be a withness of what I was experiencing. I'm staring at the sun, I am a circult between a cathode and anode. Today, as usual when the sunrise is pefectly visible, after 30 minutes, the pineal gland starts buzzing. I close my eyes and "voom, voom, voom" my entire head is buzzing as a chorus of a millon angels. The sun is going blink, blink, bling with my eyes closed in syncronicity with the buzzing. "Do you feel it?" I ask my friend who has been attending the mitochondria party with me. "Yes, I feel it." comes the reply as usual. "OK, we're done here, let's go grab breaky on the west side of the island and then hit the beach."

After a nice breakfast salad at Vongo & Anchor (sinister & dexter), in American Village (dexter & sinister) off to Araha Beach to get one hour of perfect sun, 30 minutes of sun on the back, 30 minutes on the front. My friend walked home and I rode my bicycle went home and worked remote. After work, I went to watch the sunset, a beautiful sunset on Sunset Beach. Read Uncle Jack's latest blog which seems to have been triggered by the best question in 5 years form the Q&A the other day. Obviously wearing 400 to 550nm non-polarized blue blocking lenses.

A perfect TINA day.

### GaryB — 2026-02-21T02:41:37+0200

This morning and yesterday morning were great days for watching the sunrise at Mermaid beach, feet in the ocean, just being between the cathode and anode. Started naming the ducks yesterday as they fly in perfect timing with the rising sun. Yesterday the ducks. had sound effects of a solo jet taking off towards the sun from MCAS Futenma. This morning the sun was not visible until peaking from the clouds 15 minutes after sunrise but yesterday was perfectly clear. Regardless, with feet in the ocean at around the 25 minute mark the blinking starts and my entire body seems to vibe with it.

Standing on the sand at Mermaid beach in the water you realize the sand gets washed away from under your feet and you are standing on coral. Its like natural acupuncture and feet on fire all day long in a good way. Yesterday there was no sun in the evenings and I had to work at the office. No chance to sit out in the sun or watch the sunset as it was cloudy so I played darts indoors with 400-500nm blockers for a couple of hours after work walking from my house in Araha Beach down the sea wall to Sunabe Seawall and back.

Today has great sunlight and layed on Araha Beach sunning for over an hour and then on my bicycle to ride up some hills - it was the best ride I've ever had on that course. I hope to ride hills 2 or 3 times a week. I always pay attention to transformers and power lines and avoid riding directly under power lines as I've used EF meter so always avoid walking or riding directly under them.

I notice I'm losing weight because I don't feel like eating in the evenings anymore at all. No more night time cravings. Had a big breakfast this morning with lots of local greens.

Today's supplements: DHA/EPA, 1 mullein extract tablet bringing sunlight to my lungs (I wish Uncle Jack would supplement with mullein when he's got lung issues - it makes coughing exponentially less and exponentially more mucus production). Also this morning 3 drops of MB (not droppers, not pipets - 3 drops).

Crutches: I use Aires Tech around my neck, tagging my phone, and a big one in my house. I definitely feel a difference.

Observations: Since unplugging my refrigerator life just seems better. I only had frozen strawberries, ice, and some fermented organic non pasteurized kraut in it anyway and I had noticed it has a very large EF. I use Apple MacBook and since getting an external wired keyboard and wired mouse - always had blue blocker on screen I feel less electrified. I wonder if those lines in my fingernails will go away as I think previously working on a Macbook while it was charging or even my cell phone was cause. I have more clarity and I will only charge my Macbook or iphone isolated in a far corner of my apartment,, basically where all the appliances are at sans the washing machine which remains unplugged unless in use.

### MTA321 — 2026-02-23T16:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garys-journal-journey-2026-and-beyond.32278/post-367454>

"DHA controls the inflammatory process and epigenetic switch position so they work well when they are in the Sn-2 position on
lipoproteins. If they are in Sn-1 or 3, their function is radically different. DHA cannot control epigenetic switch signaling
when they are in another position on the glycerol backbone. DHA is also more resistant to oxidation in the Sn-2 position.
DHA, it turns out, has to be bound to the Sn-2 position on the glycerol backbone to get into the brain. 3-D molecular
specificity is key to its proper function. It is also key to the correct ketotic template. It is also why fish/krill oil supplements
can never replace food as the best source... I do not believe any one in ancestral health even knows
that DHA must be in the SN-2 position to work well in humans. Taking a supplement never puts it in this position."

### GaryB — 2026-02-23T21:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garys-journal-journey-2026-and-beyond.32278/post-367488>

> [MTA321 said:](/goto/post?id=367454)
>
> "DHA controls the inflammatory process and epigenetic switch position so they work well when they are in the Sn-2 position on
> lipoproteins. If they are in Sn-1 or 3, their function is radically different. DHA cannot control epigenetic switch signaling
> when they are in another position on the glycerol backbone. DHA is also more resistant to oxidation in the Sn-2 position.
> DHA, it turns out, has to be bound to the Sn-2 position on the glycerol backbone to get into the brain. 3-D molecular
> specificity is key to its proper function. It is also key to the correct ketotic template. It is also why fish/krill oil supplements
> can never replace food as the best source... I do not believe any one in ancestral health even knows
> that DHA must be in the SN-2 position to work well in humans. Taking a supplement never puts it in this position."

Yoshihachi sushi restaurant has excellent Saba (Mackerel) and I see Yoshi often, a good friend. Not really worried here, I find the supplement makes the "blink, blink, blink" and the "voom, voom, voom" more intense. Also from my research DHA does not have to be bound to the sn-2 position on the glycerol backbone in dietary triacylglycerols (such as those common in algal oil supplements or fish oil, where DHA is frequently enriched or predominant at sn-2) to support overall DHA functions or absorption. However, for efficient brain enrichment, DHA uptake primarily occurs via the Mfsd2a transporter at the blood-brain barrier, which specifically recognizes lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC)-bound DHA (not intact TAG or free DHA). In LPC-DHA, the fatty acid can be at either the sn-1 or sn-2 position, with multiple studies showing no major difference in brain DHA enrichment when administered orally—likely due to acyl migration, equivalent uptake efficiency by Mfsd2a, or post-absorption remodeling. Dietary DHA from standard TAG forms (including algal oils with high sn-2 DHA content) or natural phosphatidylcholine (PC, typically with sn-2 DHA) is less efficient for brain enrichment because it undergoes hydrolysis to free DHA or absorption as TAG/remodeled lipids, bypassing optimal Mfsd2a transport. Specialized LPC forms (from enzymatic preparations or certain phospholipid-rich sources) are superior for direct brain delivery, though strict sn-2 specificity in the final LPC molecule is not required. I believe this is why Joe Rogan's Alpha Brain supplement works for him. I'll let you know when I decide to stop this supplementation, only doing now because I notice it makes the sunrises more intense for me.
